# Introduction - Flet

 

[Skip to content](https://docs.flet.dev/#flet-app-example)

Introduction
============

Flet is a framework that allows building web, desktop and mobile applications in Python without prior experience in frontend development.

Flet app example[#](https://docs.flet.dev/#flet-app-example "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Below is a simple "Counter" app, with a text field and two buttons to increment and decrement the counter value:

counter.py

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/#__codelineno-0-1) import flet as ft [](https://docs.flet.dev/#__codelineno-0-2)[](https://docs.flet.dev/#__codelineno-0-3) def main(page: ft.Page):     [](https://docs.flet.dev/#__codelineno-0-4)     page.title = "Flet counter example"    [](https://docs.flet.dev/#__codelineno-0-5)     page.vertical_alignment = ft.MainAxisAlignment.CENTER [](https://docs.flet.dev/#__codelineno-0-6)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/#__codelineno-0-7)     input = ft.TextField(value="0", text_align=ft.TextAlign.RIGHT, width=100) [](https://docs.flet.dev/#__codelineno-0-8)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/#__codelineno-0-9)     def minus_click(e):        [](https://docs.flet.dev/#__codelineno-0-10)         input.value = str(int(input.value) - 1) [](https://docs.flet.dev/#__codelineno-0-11)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/#__codelineno-0-12)     def plus_click(e):        [](https://docs.flet.dev/#__codelineno-0-13)         input.value = str(int(input.value) + 1) [](https://docs.flet.dev/#__codelineno-0-14)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/#__codelineno-0-15)     page.add(        [](https://docs.flet.dev/#__codelineno-0-16)         ft.Row(            [](https://docs.flet.dev/#__codelineno-0-17)             alignment=ft.MainAxisAlignment.CENTER,            [](https://docs.flet.dev/#__codelineno-0-18)             controls=[                [](https://docs.flet.dev/#__codelineno-0-19)                 ft.IconButton(ft.Icons.REMOVE, on_click=minus_click),                [](https://docs.flet.dev/#__codelineno-0-20)                 input,                [](https://docs.flet.dev/#__codelineno-0-21)                 ft.IconButton(ft.Icons.ADD, on_click=plus_click),            [](https://docs.flet.dev/#__codelineno-0-22)             ],        [](https://docs.flet.dev/#__codelineno-0-23)         )    [](https://docs.flet.dev/#__codelineno-0-24)     ) [](https://docs.flet.dev/#__codelineno-0-25)[](https://docs.flet.dev/#__codelineno-0-26) ft.run(main)`

To run the app, [install `flet`](https://docs.flet.dev/getting-started/installation/)
:

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/#__codelineno-1-1) pip install 'flet[all]'`

then launch the app:

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/#__codelineno-2-1) flet run counter.py`

This will open the app in a native OS window - what a nice alternative to Electron! 🙂

[![](https://docs.flet.dev/assets/getting-started/counter-app/macos.png)](https://docs.flet.dev/assets/getting-started/counter-app/macos.png)

To run the same app as a web app use `--web` option with `flet run` command:

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/#__codelineno-3-1) flet run --web counter.py`

[![](https://docs.flet.dev/assets/getting-started/counter-app/safari.png)](https://docs.flet.dev/assets/getting-started/counter-app/safari.png)

Back to top

# BaseAd - Flet

 

[Skip to content](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/basead/#flet_ads.base_ad.BaseAd)

BaseAd
======

Inherits: `[BaseControl](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basecontrol/#flet.BaseControl) `

Base class for all Ad controls.

This class defines shared ad request and lifecycle event properties for concrete ad controls/services.

Raises:

*   `FletUnsupportedPlatformException` –
    
    When using this control on a web and/or non-mobile platform.
    

Properties

*   **`[request](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/basead/#flet_ads.base_ad.BaseAd.request) `**(`[AdRequest](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/types/adrequest/#flet_ads.types.AdRequest) `) –
    
    Targeting information used to fetch an Ad.
    
*   **`[unit_id](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/basead/#flet_ads.base_ad.BaseAd.unit_id) `**(`[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str) `) –
    
    Ad unit ID for this ad.
    

Events

*   **`[on_click](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/basead/#flet_ads.base_ad.BaseAd.on_click) `**(`[ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[BaseAd](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/basead/#flet_ads.base_ad.BaseAd) ] | None`) –
    
    Called when this ad is clicked.
    
*   **`[on_close](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/basead/#flet_ads.base_ad.BaseAd.on_close) `**(`[ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[BaseAd](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/basead/#flet_ads.base_ad.BaseAd) ] | None`) –
    
    Called when the full screen view has been closed. You should restart
    
*   **`[on_error](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/basead/#flet_ads.base_ad.BaseAd.on_error) `**(`[ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[BaseAd](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/basead/#flet_ads.base_ad.BaseAd) ] | None`) –
    
    Called when an ad request failed.
    
*   **`[on_impression](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/basead/#flet_ads.base_ad.BaseAd.on_impression) `**(`[ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[BaseAd](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/basead/#flet_ads.base_ad.BaseAd) ] | None`) –
    
    Called when an impression occurs on this ad.
    
*   **`[on_load](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/basead/#flet_ads.base_ad.BaseAd.on_load) `**(`[ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[BaseAd](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/basead/#flet_ads.base_ad.BaseAd) ] | None`) –
    
    Called when this ad is loaded successfully.
    
*   **`[on_open](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/basead/#flet_ads.base_ad.BaseAd.on_open) `**(`[ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[BaseAd](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/basead/#flet_ads.base_ad.BaseAd) ] | None`) –
    
    Called when this ad opens up.
    

Properties[#](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/basead/#flet_ads.base_ad.BaseAd-properties "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### request `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/basead/#flet_ads.base_ad.BaseAd.request "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/basead/#__codelineno-0-1) request: [AdRequest](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/types/adrequest/#flet_ads.types.AdRequest)  = [field](https://docs.python.org/3/library/dataclasses.html#dataclasses.field "<code>dataclasses.field</code>") (     [](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/basead/#__codelineno-0-2)     default_factory=lambda: [AdRequest](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/types/adrequest/#flet_ads.types.AdRequest) () [](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/basead/#__codelineno-0-3) )`

Targeting information used to fetch an Ad.

### unit\_id `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/basead/#flet_ads.base_ad.BaseAd.unit_id "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/basead/#__codelineno-0-1) unit_id: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)`

Ad unit ID for this ad.

Events[#](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/basead/#flet_ads.base_ad.BaseAd-events "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### on\_click `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/basead/#flet_ads.base_ad.BaseAd.on_click "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/basead/#__codelineno-0-1) on_click: [ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[BaseAd](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/basead/#flet_ads.base_ad.BaseAd) ] | None = None`

Called when this ad is clicked.

### on\_close `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/basead/#flet_ads.base_ad.BaseAd.on_close "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/basead/#__codelineno-0-1) on_close: [ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[BaseAd](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/basead/#flet_ads.base_ad.BaseAd) ] | None = None`

Called when the full screen view has been closed. You should restart anything paused while handling [`on_open`](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/basead/#flet_ads.base_ad.BaseAd.on_open)
.

### on\_error `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/basead/#flet_ads.base_ad.BaseAd.on_error "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/basead/#__codelineno-0-1) on_error: [ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[BaseAd](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/basead/#flet_ads.base_ad.BaseAd) ] | None = None`

Called when an ad request failed.

Event handler argument [`data`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/event/#flet.Event.data)
 property contains information about the error.

### on\_impression `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/basead/#flet_ads.base_ad.BaseAd.on_impression "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/basead/#__codelineno-0-1) on_impression: [ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[BaseAd](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/basead/#flet_ads.base_ad.BaseAd) ] | None = None`

Called when an impression occurs on this ad.

### on\_load `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/basead/#flet_ads.base_ad.BaseAd.on_load "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/basead/#__codelineno-0-1) on_load: [ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[BaseAd](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/basead/#flet_ads.base_ad.BaseAd) ] | None = None`

Called when this ad is loaded successfully.

### on\_open `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/basead/#flet_ads.base_ad.BaseAd.on_open "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/basead/#__codelineno-0-1) on_open: [ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[BaseAd](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/basead/#flet_ads.base_ad.BaseAd) ] | None = None`

Called when this ad opens up.

A full screen view/overlay is presented in response to the user clicking on an ad. You may want to pause animations and time sensitive interactions.

# Ads - Flet

 

[Skip to content](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/#ads)

Ads[#](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/#ads "Permanent link")

========================================================

Displaying Google Ads in [Flet](https://flet.dev/)
 apps.

It is powered by the [google\_mobile\_ads](https://pub.dev/packages/google_mobile_ads)
 Flutter package.

Platform Support[#](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/#platform-support "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Platform | Windows | macOS | Linux | iOS | Android | Web |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Supported | ❌   | ❌   | ❌   | ✅   | ✅   | ❌   |

Usage[#](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/#usage "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------

To use ads controls add `flet-ads` package to your project dependencies:

uvpip

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/#__codelineno-0-1) uv add flet-ads`

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/#__codelineno-1-1) pip install flet-ads  # (1)!`

1.  After this, you will have to manually add this package to your `requirements.txt` or `pyproject.toml`.

Requirements[#](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/#requirements "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------

The below sections show the required configurations for each platform.

### Android[#](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/#android "Permanent link")

A valid [AdMob app ID](https://support.google.com/admob/answer/7356431)
 is required to be specified, otherwise the app might crash on launch or behave unexpectedly.

`flet build``pyproject.toml`

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/#__codelineno-2-1) flet build apk \   [](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/#__codelineno-2-2)   --android-meta-data com.google.android.gms.ads.APPLICATION_ID="ca-app-pub-xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx~yyyyyyyyyy"`

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/#__codelineno-3-1) [tool.flet.android.meta_data] [](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/#__codelineno-3-2) "com.google.android.gms.ads.APPLICATION_ID" = "ca-app-pub-xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx~yyyyyyyyyy"`

See also:

*   [setting Android permissions](https://docs.flet.dev/publish/android/#permissions)
    
*   [AdMob app ID for testing purposes](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/#test-values)
    

### iOS[#](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/#ios "Permanent link")

A valid [AdMob app ID](https://support.google.com/admob/answer/7356431)
 is required to be specified, otherwise the app might crash on launch or behave unexpectedly.

`flet build``pyproject.toml`

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/#__codelineno-4-1) flet build ipa \   [](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/#__codelineno-4-2)   --info-plist GADApplicationIdentifier="ca-app-pub-xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx~yyyyyyyyyy"`

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/#__codelineno-5-1) [tool.flet.ios.info] [](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/#__codelineno-5-2) GADApplicationIdentifier = "ca-app-pub-xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx~yyyyyyyyyy"`

See also:

*   [setting iOS permissions](https://docs.flet.dev/publish/ios/#permissions)
    
*   [AdMob app ID for testing purposes](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/#test-values)
    

### Test Values[#](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/#test-values "Permanent link")

AdMob [provides](https://developers.google.com/admob/flutter/banner#always_test_with_test_ads)
 app and ad unit IDs for testing purposes.

*   **AdMob app ID**: `"ca-app-pub-3940256099942544~3347511713"`
*   [`BannerAd.unit_id`](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/bannerad/#flet_ads.banner_ad.BannerAd)
    *   **Android**: `"ca-app-pub-3940256099942544/9214589741"`
    *   **iOS**: `"ca-app-pub-3940256099942544/2435281174"`
*   [`InterstitialAd.unit_id`](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/interstitialad/#flet_ads.interstitial_ad.InterstitialAd)
    *   **Android**: `"ca-app-pub-3940256099942544/1033173712"`
    *   **iOS**: `"ca-app-pub-3940256099942544/4411468910"`

**They are not meant to be used in production.**

Example[#](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/#example "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/#__codelineno-6-1) import flet as ft [](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/#__codelineno-6-2) import flet_ads as fta # BannerAd - Flet

 

[Skip to content](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/bannerad/#flet_ads.banner_ad.BannerAd)

BannerAd
========

Displays a banner ad.

Test IDs

AdMob [provides](https://developers.google.com/admob/flutter/banner#always_test_with_test_ads)
 unit IDs for testing purposes. Set [`unit_id`](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/basead/#flet_ads.base_ad.BaseAd.unit_id)
 to the appropriate value based on the platform you're testing on:

*   Android: `"ca-app-pub-3940256099942544/9214589741"`
*   iOS: `"ca-app-pub-3940256099942544/2435281174"`

Remember to replace them in production.

Raises:

*   `FletUnsupportedPlatformException` –
    
    When this control is used on a web and/or non-mobile platform.
    

Inherits: `[LayoutControl](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/layoutcontrol/#flet.LayoutControl) `, `[BaseAd](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/basead/#flet_ads.base_ad.BaseAd) `

Events

*   **`[on_paid](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/bannerad/#flet_ads.banner_ad.BannerAd.on_paid) `**(`[ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[PaidAdEvent](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/types/paidadevent/#flet_ads.types.PaidAdEvent) [[BannerAd](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/bannerad/#flet_ads.banner_ad.BannerAd) ]] | None`) –
    
    Called when this ad is estimated to have earned money.
    
*   **`[on_will_dismiss](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/bannerad/#flet_ads.banner_ad.BannerAd.on_will_dismiss) `**(`[ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[BannerAd](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/bannerad/#flet_ads.banner_ad.BannerAd) ] | None`) –
    
    Called before dismissing a full screen view.
    

Examples[#](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/bannerad/#examples "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Example 1[#](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/bannerad/#example-1 "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/bannerad/#__codelineno-0-1) import flet as ft [](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/bannerad/#__codelineno-0-2) import flet_ads as fta Events[#](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/bannerad/#flet_ads.banner_ad.BannerAd-events "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### on\_paid `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/bannerad/#flet_ads.banner_ad.BannerAd.on_paid "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/bannerad/#__codelineno-0-1) on_paid: (     [](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/bannerad/#__codelineno-0-2)     [ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[PaidAdEvent](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/types/paidadevent/#flet_ads.types.PaidAdEvent) [[BannerAd](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/bannerad/#flet_ads.banner_ad.BannerAd) ]] | None [](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/bannerad/#__codelineno-0-3) ) = None`

Called when this ad is estimated to have earned money.

Available for allowlisted accounts only.

### on\_will\_dismiss `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/bannerad/#flet_ads.banner_ad.BannerAd.on_will_dismiss "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/bannerad/#__codelineno-0-1) on_will_dismiss: [ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[BannerAd](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/bannerad/#flet_ads.banner_ad.BannerAd) ] | None = None`

Called before dismissing a full screen view.

Note

Only available on iOS.

# Overview - Flet

 

[Skip to content](https://docs.flet.dev/api-reference/#api-reference)

API Reference[#](https://docs.flet.dev/api-reference/#api-reference "Permanent link")

======================================================================================

*   [Controls](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/)
     - UI building blocks with properties, events, and usage examples.
*   [Services](https://docs.flet.dev/services/)
     - Device and platform capabilities such as sensors, storage, and permissions.
*   [CLI](https://docs.flet.dev/cli/)
     - `flet` commands for creating, running, packaging, and debugging apps.
*   [Types](https://docs.flet.dev/types/)
     - Core types, enums, events, exceptions, and utilities shared across the SDK.
*   [Built-in binary Python packages for Android and iOS](https://docs.flet.dev/reference/binary-packages-android-ios/)
     - Prebuilt wheels available from `https://pypi.flet.dev` for mobile targets.
*   [Environment Variables](https://docs.flet.dev/reference/environment-variables/)
     - Runtime configuration toggles for apps and the CLI.

# InterstitialAd - Flet

 

[Skip to content](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/interstitialad/#flet_ads.interstitial_ad.InterstitialAd)

InterstitialAd
==============

Displays a full-screen interstitial ad.

Note

Each instance is allowed to be shown (using [`show()`](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/interstitialad/#flet_ads.interstitial_ad.InterstitialAd.show)
) only once. To show another ad, create a new instance. Reusing an already-shown instance will result in errors or unexpected behavior.

Test IDs

AdMob [provides](https://developers.google.com/admob/flutter/banner#always_test_with_test_ads)
 unit IDs for testing purposes. Set [`unit_id`](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/basead/#flet_ads.base_ad.BaseAd.unit_id)
 to the appropriate value based on the platform you're testing on:

*   Android: `"ca-app-pub-3940256099942544/1033173712"`
*   iOS: `"ca-app-pub-3940256099942544/4411468910"`

Remember to replace them in production.

Raises:

*   `FletUnsupportedPlatformException` –
    
    When using this control on a web and/or non-mobile platform.
    

Inherits: `[Service](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/service/#flet.Service) `, `[BaseAd](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/basead/#flet_ads.base_ad.BaseAd) `

Methods

*   **`[show](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/interstitialad/#flet_ads.interstitial_ad.InterstitialAd.show) `** –
    
    Present the loaded interstitial ad as a full-screen overlay.
    

Examples[#](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/interstitialad/#examples "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Example 1[#](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/interstitialad/#example-1 "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/interstitialad/#__codelineno-0-1) import flet as ft [](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/interstitialad/#__codelineno-0-2) import flet_ads as fta [](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/interstitialad/#__codelineno-0-3)[](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/interstitialad/#__codelineno-0-4) # Test ad unit IDs [](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/interstitialad/#__codelineno-0-5) ids = {     [](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/interstitialad/#__codelineno-0-6)     ft.PagePlatform.ANDROID: {        [](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/interstitialad/#__codelineno-0-7)         "banner": "ca-app-pub-3940256099942544/6300978111",        [](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/interstitialad/#__codelineno-0-8)         "interstitial": "ca-app-pub-3940256099942544/1033173712",    [](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/interstitialad/#__codelineno-0-9)     },    [](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/interstitialad/#__codelineno-0-10)     ft.PagePlatform.IOS: {        [](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/interstitialad/#__codelineno-0-11)         "banner": "ca-app-pub-3940256099942544/2934735716",        [](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/interstitialad/#__codelineno-0-12)         "interstitial": "ca-app-pub-3940256099942544/4411468910",    [](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/interstitialad/#__codelineno-0-13)     }, [](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/interstitialad/#__codelineno-0-14) } [](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/interstitialad/#__codelineno-0-15) [](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/interstitialad/#__codelineno-0-16)[](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/interstitialad/#__codelineno-0-17) def main(page: ft.Page):     [](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/interstitialad/#__codelineno-0-18)     page.appbar = ft.AppBar(        [](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/interstitialad/#__codelineno-0-19)         adaptive=True,        [](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/interstitialad/#__codelineno-0-20)         title="Mobile Ads Playground",        [](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/interstitialad/#__codelineno-0-21)         bgcolor=ft.Colors.LIGHT_BLUE_300,    [](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/interstitialad/#__codelineno-0-22)     )    [](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/interstitialad/#__codelineno-0-23)     page.horizontal_alignment = ft.CrossAxisAlignment.CENTER    [](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/interstitialad/#__codelineno-0-24)     page.scroll = ft.ScrollMode.AUTO [](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/interstitialad/#__codelineno-0-25)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/interstitialad/#__codelineno-0-26)     def show_new_interstitial_ad(): Methods[#](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/interstitialad/#flet_ads.interstitial_ad.InterstitialAd-methods "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### show `async` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/interstitialad/#flet_ads.interstitial_ad.InterstitialAd.show "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/interstitialad/#__codelineno-0-1) show()`

Present the loaded interstitial ad as a full-screen overlay.

The ad must be loaded before this method is called.

# Audio - Flet

 

[Skip to content](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#audio)

Audio[#](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#audio "Permanent link")

==============================================================

Allows playing audio in [Flet](https://flet.dev/)
 apps.

Platform Support[#](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#platform-support "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Platform | Windows | macOS | Linux | iOS | Android | Web |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Supported | ✅   | ✅   | ✅   | ✅   | ✅   | ✅   |

Usage[#](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#usage "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------

To use `Audio` control add `flet-audio` package to your project dependencies:

uvpip

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#__codelineno-0-1) uv add flet-audio`

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#__codelineno-1-1) pip install flet-audio  # (1)!`

1.  After this, you will have to manually add this package to your `requirements.txt` or `pyproject.toml`.

Linux requirements

To play audio on Linux (or [WSL](https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/wsl/about)
) you need to install [`GStreamer`](https://github.com/GStreamer/gstreamer)
 library.

To install the minimal set of GStreamer libs on Ubuntu/Debian, run:

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#__codelineno-2-1) sudo apt install libgtk-3-dev libgstreamer1.0-dev libgstreamer-plugins-base1.0-dev`

To install the full set:

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#__codelineno-3-1) sudo apt install \   [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#__codelineno-3-2)   libgstreamer1.0-dev libgstreamer-plugins-base1.0-dev libgstreamer-plugins-bad1.0-dev \  [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#__codelineno-3-3)   gstreamer1.0-plugins-base gstreamer1.0-plugins-good gstreamer1.0-plugins-bad \  [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#__codelineno-3-4)   gstreamer1.0-plugins-ugly gstreamer1.0-libav gstreamer1.0-doc gstreamer1.0-tools \  [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#__codelineno-3-5)   gstreamer1.0-x gstreamer1.0-alsa gstreamer1.0-gl gstreamer1.0-gtk3 \  [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#__codelineno-3-6)   gstreamer1.0-qt5 gstreamer1.0-pulseaudio`

If you receive `error while loading shared libraries: libgstapp-1.0.so.0`, it means `GStreamer` is not installed in your WSL environment. Install the full set of GStreamer libs, as shown above.

See [this guide](https://gstreamer.freedesktop.org/documentation/installing/on-linux.html?gi-language=c)
 for installing on other Linux distributions.

Examples[#](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#examples "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------

### Basic example[#](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#basic-example "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#__codelineno-4-1) import flet as ft [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#__codelineno-4-2) import flet_audio as fta [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#__codelineno-4-3)     async def pause():        [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#__codelineno-4-12)         await audio.pause() [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#__codelineno-4-13)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#__codelineno-4-14)     async def resume():        [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#__codelineno-4-15)         await audio.resume() [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#__codelineno-4-16)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#__codelineno-4-17)     async def release():        [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#__codelineno-4-18)         await audio.release() [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#__codelineno-4-19)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#__codelineno-4-20)     def set_volume(value: float):        [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#__codelineno-4-21)         audio.volume += value [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#__codelineno-4-22)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#__codelineno-4-23)     def set_balance(value: float):        [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#__codelineno-4-24)         audio.balance += value [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#__codelineno-4-25)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#__codelineno-4-26)     async def seek_2s():        [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#__codelineno-4-27)         await audio.seek(ft.Duration(seconds=2)) [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#__codelineno-4-28)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#__codelineno-4-29)     async def get_duration():        [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#__codelineno-4-30)         duration = await audio.get_duration()        [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#__codelineno-4-31)         print("Duration:", duration) [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#__codelineno-4-32)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#__codelineno-4-33)     async def on_get_current_position():        [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#__codelineno-4-34)         position = await audio.get_current_position()        [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#__codelineno-4-35)         print("Current position:", position) [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#__codelineno-4-36)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#__codelineno-4-37)     audio = fta.Audio(        [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#__codelineno-4-38)         src=url,        [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#__codelineno-4-39)         autoplay=False,        [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#__codelineno-4-40)         volume=1, print("Duration changed:", e.duration),        [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#__codelineno-4-45)         on_position_change=lambda e: print("Position changed:", e.position),        [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#__codelineno-4-46)         on_state_change=lambda e: print("State changed:", e.state),        [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#__codelineno-4-47)         on_seek_complete=lambda _: print("Seek complete"),    [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#__codelineno-4-48)     ) [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#__codelineno-4-49)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#__codelineno-4-50)     page.add(        [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#__codelineno-4-51)         ft.Button("Play", on_click=play),        [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#__codelineno-4-52)         ft.Button("Pause", on_click=pause),        [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#__codelineno-4-53)         ft.Button("Resume", on_click=resume),        [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#__codelineno-4-54)         ft.Button("Release", on_click=release),        [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#__codelineno-4-55)         ft.Button("Seek 2s", on_click=seek_2s),        [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#__codelineno-4-56)         ft.Row(            [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#__codelineno-4-57)             controls=[            ft.Button("Volume up", on_click=lambda _: set_volume(0.1)),            [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#__codelineno-4-60)             ]        [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#__codelineno-4-61)         ),        [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#__codelineno-4-62)         ft.Row(            [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#__codelineno-4-63)             controls=[                [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#__codelineno-4-64)                 ft.Button("Balance left", on_click=lambda _: set_balance(-0.1)),                [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#__codelineno-4-65)                 ft.Button("Balance right", on_click=lambda _: set_balance(0.1)),            [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#__codelineno-4-66)             ]        [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#__codelineno-4-67)         ),        [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#__codelineno-4-68)         ft.Button("Get duration", on_click=get_duration), [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#__codelineno-4-71) [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#__codelineno-4-72)[](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#__codelineno-4-73) ft.run(main)`

Description[#](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#description "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------

Inherits: `[Service](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/service/#flet.Service) `

A control to simultaneously play multiple audio sources.

Properties

*   **`[autoplay](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#flet_audio.Audio.autoplay) `**(`[bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool) `) –
    
    Starts playing audio as soon as audio control is added to a page.
    
*   **`[balance](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#flet_audio.Audio.balance) `**(`[Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number) `) –
    
    Defines the stereo balance.
    
*   **`[playback_rate](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#flet_audio.Audio.playback_rate) `**(`[Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number) `) –
    
    Defines the playback rate.
    
*   **`[release_mode](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#flet_audio.Audio.release_mode) `**(`[ReleaseMode](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/types/releasemode/#flet_audio.ReleaseMode) `) –
    
    Defines the release mode.
    
*   **`[src](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#flet_audio.Audio.src) `**(`[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)  | [bytes](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#bytes)  | None`) –
    
    The audio source.
    
*   **`[volume](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#flet_audio.Audio.volume) `**(`[Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number) `) –
    
    Sets the volume (amplitude).
    

Events

*   **`[on_duration_change](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#flet_audio.Audio.on_duration_change) `**(`[EventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.EventHandler) [[AudioDurationChangeEvent](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/types/audiodurationchangeevent/#flet_audio.AudioDurationChangeEvent) ] | None`) –
    
    Fires as soon as audio duration is available
    
*   **`[on_loaded](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#flet_audio.Audio.on_loaded) `**(`[ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[Audio](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#flet_audio.Audio) ] | None`) –
    
    Fires when an audio is loaded/buffered.
    
*   **`[on_position_change](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#flet_audio.Audio.on_position_change) `**(`[EventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.EventHandler) [[AudioPositionChangeEvent](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/types/audiopositionchangeevent/#flet_audio.AudioPositionChangeEvent) ] | None`) –
    
    Fires when audio position is changed.
    
*   **`[on_seek_complete](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#flet_audio.Audio.on_seek_complete) `**(`[ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[Audio](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#flet_audio.Audio) ] | None`) –
    
    Fires on seek completions.
    
*   **`[on_state_change](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#flet_audio.Audio.on_state_change) `**(`[EventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.EventHandler) [[AudioStateChangeEvent](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/types/audiostatechangeevent/#flet_audio.AudioStateChangeEvent) ] | None`) –
    
    Fires when audio player state changes.
    

Methods

*   **`[get_current_position](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#flet_audio.Audio.get_current_position) `** –
    
    Get the current position of the audio playback.
    
*   **`[get_duration](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#flet_audio.Audio.get_duration) `** –
    
    Get audio duration of the audio playback.
    
*   **`[pause](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#flet_audio.Audio.pause) `** –
    
    Pauses the audio that is currently playing.
    
*   **`[play](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#flet_audio.Audio.play) `** –
    
    Starts playing audio from the specified `position`.
    
*   **`[release](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#flet_audio.Audio.release) `** –
    
    Releases the resources associated with this media player.
    
*   **`[resume](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#flet_audio.Audio.resume) `** –
    
    Resumes the audio that has been paused or stopped.
    
*   **`[seek](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#flet_audio.Audio.seek) `** –
    
    Moves the cursor to the desired position.
    

Properties[#](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#flet_audio.Audio-properties "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### autoplay `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#flet_audio.Audio.autoplay "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#__codelineno-0-1) autoplay: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)  = False`

Starts playing audio as soon as audio control is added to a page.

Note

Autoplay works in desktop, mobile apps and Safari browser, but doesn't work in Chrome/Edge.

### balance `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#flet_audio.Audio.balance "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#__codelineno-0-1) balance: [Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  = 0.0`

Defines the stereo balance.

*   `-1` - The left channel is at full volume; the right channel is silent.
*   `1` - The right channel is at full volume; the left channel is silent.
*   `0` - Both channels are at the same volume.

### playback\_rate `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#flet_audio.Audio.playback_rate "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#__codelineno-0-1) playback_rate: [Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  = 1.0`

Defines the playback rate.

Should ideally be set when creating the constructor.

Note

*   iOS and macOS have limits between `0.5x` and `2x`.
*   Android SDK version should be 23 or higher.

### release\_mode `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#flet_audio.Audio.release_mode "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#__codelineno-0-1) release_mode: [ReleaseMode](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/types/releasemode/#flet_audio.ReleaseMode)  = [RELEASE](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/types/releasemode/#flet_audio.ReleaseMode.RELEASE)`

Defines the release mode.

### src `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#flet_audio.Audio.src "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#__codelineno-0-1) src: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)  | [bytes](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#bytes)  | None = None`

The audio source.

It can be one of the following: - A URL or local [asset file](https://docs.flet.dev/cookbook/assets)
 path; - A base64 string; - Raw bytes.

Note

[Here](https://github.com/bluefireteam/audioplayers/blob/main/troubleshooting.md#supported-formats--encodings)
 is a list of supported audio formats.

### volume `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#flet_audio.Audio.volume "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#__codelineno-0-1) volume: [Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  = 1.0`

Sets the volume (amplitude). It's value ranges between `0.0` (mute) and `1.0` (maximum volume). Intermediate values are linearly interpolated.

Events[#](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#flet_audio.Audio-events "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### on\_duration\_change `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#flet_audio.Audio.on_duration_change "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#__codelineno-0-1) on_duration_change: (     [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#__codelineno-0-2)     [EventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.EventHandler) [[AudioDurationChangeEvent](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/types/audiodurationchangeevent/#flet_audio.AudioDurationChangeEvent) ] | None [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#__codelineno-0-3) ) = None`

Fires as soon as audio duration is available (it might take a while to download or buffer it).

### on\_loaded `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#flet_audio.Audio.on_loaded "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#__codelineno-0-1) on_loaded: [ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[Audio](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#flet_audio.Audio) ] | None = None`

Fires when an audio is loaded/buffered.

### on\_position\_change `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#flet_audio.Audio.on_position_change "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#__codelineno-0-1) on_position_change: (     [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#__codelineno-0-2)     [EventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.EventHandler) [[AudioPositionChangeEvent](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/types/audiopositionchangeevent/#flet_audio.AudioPositionChangeEvent) ] | None [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#__codelineno-0-3) ) = None`

Fires when audio position is changed. Will continuously update the position of the playback every 1 second if the status is playing.

Can be used for a progress bar.

### on\_seek\_complete `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#flet_audio.Audio.on_seek_complete "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#__codelineno-0-1) on_seek_complete: [ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[Audio](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#flet_audio.Audio) ] | None = None`

Fires on seek completions. An event is going to be sent as soon as the audio seek is finished.

### on\_state\_change `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#flet_audio.Audio.on_state_change "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#__codelineno-0-1) on_state_change: (     [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#__codelineno-0-2)     [EventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.EventHandler) [[AudioStateChangeEvent](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/types/audiostatechangeevent/#flet_audio.AudioStateChangeEvent) ] | None [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#__codelineno-0-3) ) = None`

Fires when audio player state changes.

Methods[#](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#flet_audio.Audio-methods "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### get\_current\_position `async` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#flet_audio.Audio.get_current_position "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#__codelineno-0-1) get_current_position() -> [Duration](https://docs.flet.dev/types/duration/#flet.Duration)  | None`

Get the current position of the audio playback.

Returns:

*   `[Duration](https://docs.flet.dev/types/duration/#flet.Duration)  | None` –
    
    The current position of the audio playback.
    

### get\_duration `async` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#flet_audio.Audio.get_duration "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#__codelineno-0-1) get_duration() -> [Duration](https://docs.flet.dev/types/duration/#flet.Duration)  | None`

Get audio duration of the audio playback.

It will be available as soon as the audio duration is available (it might take a while to download or buffer it if file is not local).

Returns:

*   `[Duration](https://docs.flet.dev/types/duration/#flet.Duration)  | None` –
    
    The duration of audio playback.
    

### pause `async` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#flet_audio.Audio.pause "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#__codelineno-0-1) pause()`

Pauses the audio that is currently playing.

If you call [`resume()`](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#flet_audio.Audio.resume)
 later, the audio will resume from the point that it has been paused.

### play `async` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#flet_audio.Audio.play "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#__codelineno-0-1) play(position: [DurationValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.DurationValue)  = 0)`

Starts playing audio from the specified `position`.

Parameters:

*   **`position`** (`[DurationValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.DurationValue) `, default: `0` ) –
    
    The position to start playback from.
    

### release `async` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#flet_audio.Audio.release "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#__codelineno-0-1) release()`

Releases the resources associated with this media player. These are going to be fetched or buffered again as soon as you change the source or call [`resume()`](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#flet_audio.Audio.resume)
.

### resume `async` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#flet_audio.Audio.resume "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#__codelineno-0-1) resume()`

Resumes the audio that has been paused or stopped.

### seek `async` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#flet_audio.Audio.seek "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/audio/#__codelineno-0-1) seek(position: [DurationValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.DurationValue) )`

Moves the cursor to the desired position.

Parameters:

*   **`position`** (`[DurationValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.DurationValue) `) –
    
    The position to seek/move to.
    

Back to top

---

# AudioRecorder - Flet

 

[Skip to content](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#audio-recorder)

Audio Recorder[#](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#audio-recorder "Permanent link")

=========================================================================================

Allows recording audio in [Flet](https://flet.dev/)
 apps.

It is based on the [record](https://pub.dev/packages/record)
 Flutter package.

Platform Support[#](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#platform-support "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Platform | Windows | macOS | Linux | iOS | Android | Web |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Supported | ✅   | ✅   | ✅   | ✅   | ✅   | ✅   |

Usage[#](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#usage "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------

To use `AudioRecorder` service add `flet-audio-recorder` package to your project dependencies:

uvpip

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#__codelineno-0-1) uv add flet-audio-recorder`

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#__codelineno-1-1) pip install flet-audio-recorder  # (1)!`

1.  After this, you will have to manually add this package to your `requirements.txt` or `pyproject.toml`.

Requirements[#](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#requirements "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The below sections show the required configurations for each platform.

### Android[#](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#android "Permanent link")

Configuration to be made to access the microphone:

*   [`android.permission.RECORD_AUDIO`](https://developer.android.com/reference/android/Manifest.permission#RECORD_AUDIO)
    : Allows audio recording.
*   [`android.permission.WRITE_EXTERNAL_STORAGE`](https://developer.android.com/reference/android/Manifest.permission#WRITE_EXTERNAL_STORAGE)
     (optional): Allows saving your recordings in public folders.
*   [`android.permission.MODIFY_AUDIO_SETTINGS`](https://developer.android.com/reference/android/Manifest.permission#MODIFY_AUDIO_SETTINGS)
     (optional): Allows using bluetooth telephony device like headset/earbuds.

`flet build``pyproject.toml`

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#__codelineno-2-1) flet build apk \   [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#__codelineno-2-2)   --android-permissions android.permission.RECORD_AUDIO=true \  [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#__codelineno-2-3)   --android-permissions android.permission.WRITE_EXTERNAL_STORAGE=true \  [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#__codelineno-2-4)   --android-permissions android.permission.MODIFY_AUDIO_SETTINGS=true`

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#__codelineno-3-1) [tool.flet.android.permission] [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#__codelineno-3-2) "android.permission.RECORD_AUDIO" = true [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#__codelineno-3-3) "android.permission.WRITE_EXTERNAL_STORAGE" = true [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#__codelineno-3-4) "android.permission.MODIFY_AUDIO_SETTINGS" = true`

See also:

*   [setting Android permissions](https://docs.flet.dev/publish/android/#permissions)
    
*   [Audio formats sample rate hints](https://developer.android.com/guide/topics/media/media-formats#audio-formats)
    

### iOS[#](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#ios "Permanent link")

Configuration to be made to access the microphone:

*   [`NSMicrophoneUsageDescription`](https://developer.apple.com/documentation/BundleResources/Information-Property-List/NSMicrophoneUsageDescription)
    : Required for recording audio.

`flet build``pyproject.toml`

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#__codelineno-4-1) flet build ipa \   [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#__codelineno-4-2)   --info-plist NSMicrophoneUsageDescription="Some message to describe why you need this permission..."`

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#__codelineno-5-1) [tool.flet.ios.info] [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#__codelineno-5-2) NSMicrophoneUsageDescription = "Some message to describe why you need this permission..."`

See also:

*   [setting iOS permissions](https://docs.flet.dev/publish/ios/#permissions)
    .

### macOS[#](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#macos "Permanent link")

Configuration to be made to access the microphone:

*   [`NSMicrophoneUsageDescription`](https://developer.apple.com/documentation/BundleResources/Information-Property-List/NSMicrophoneUsageDescription)
    : Allows recording audio.
*   [`com.apple.security.device.audio-input`](https://developer.apple.com/documentation/BundleResources/Entitlements/com.apple.security.device.audio-input)
    : Allows recording audio using the built-in microphone and accessing audio input using Core Audio.

`flet build``pyproject.toml`

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#__codelineno-6-1) flet build macos \   [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#__codelineno-6-2)   --info-plist NSMicrophoneUsageDescription="Some message to describe why you need this permission..." \  [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#__codelineno-6-3)   --macos-entitlements com.apple.security.device.audio-input=true`

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#__codelineno-7-1) [tool.flet.macos.info] [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#__codelineno-7-2) NSMicrophoneUsageDescription = "Some message to describe why you need this permission..." [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#__codelineno-7-3)[](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#__codelineno-7-4) [tool.flet.macos.entitlement] [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#__codelineno-7-5) "com.apple.security.device.audio-input" = true`

See also:

*   [setting macOS permissions](https://docs.flet.dev/publish/macos/#permissions)
    
*   [setting macOS entitlements](https://docs.flet.dev/publish/macos/#entitlements)
    

### Linux[#](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#linux "Permanent link")

The following dependencies are required (and widely available on your system):

*   `parecord`: Used for audio input.
*   `pactl`: Used for utility methods like getting available devices.
*   [`ffmpeg`](https://ffmpeg.org/)
    : Used for encoding and output.

On Ubuntu 24.04.3 LTS, you can install them using:

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#__codelineno-8-1) sudo apt install pulseaudio-utils ffmpeg`

### Cross-platform[#](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#cross-platform "Permanent link")

Additionally/alternatively, you can make use of our predefined cross-platform `microphone` [permission bundle](https://docs.flet.dev/publish/#predefined-cross-platform-permission-bundles)
:

`flet build``pyproject.toml`

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#__codelineno-9-1) flet build <target_platform> --permissions microphone`

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#__codelineno-10-1) [tool.flet] [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#__codelineno-10-2) permissions = ["microphone"]`

Example[#](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#example "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#__codelineno-11-1) import flet as ft [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#__codelineno-11-2) import flet_audio_recorder as far [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#__codelineno-11-3) [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#__codelineno-11-4)[](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#__codelineno-11-5) def main(page: ft.Page):     [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#__codelineno-11-6)     page.horizontal_alignment = ft.CrossAxisAlignment.CENTER    [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#__codelineno-11-7)     page.appbar = ft.AppBar(title=ft.Text("Audio Recorder"), center_title=True) [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#__codelineno-11-8)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#__codelineno-11-9)     path = "test-audio-file.wav" [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#__codelineno-11-10)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#__codelineno-11-11)     def show_snackbar(message):        [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#__codelineno-11-12)         page.show_dialog(ft.SnackBar(ft.Text(message))) [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#__codelineno-11-13)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#__codelineno-11-14)     async def handle_recording_start(e: ft.Event[ft.Button]):        [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#__codelineno-11-15)         show_snackbar("Starting recording...")        [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#__codelineno-11-16)         await recorder.start_recording(output_path=path) [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#__codelineno-11-17)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#__codelineno-11-18)     async def handle_recording_stop(e: ft.Event[ft.Button]):        [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#__codelineno-11-19)         output_path = await recorder.stop_recording()        [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#__codelineno-11-20)         show_snackbar(f"Stopped recording. Output Path: {output_path}")        [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#__codelineno-11-21)         if page.web and output_path is not None:            [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#__codelineno-11-22)             await page.launch_url(output_path) [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#__codelineno-11-23)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#__codelineno-11-24)     async def handle_list_devices(e: ft.Event[ft.Button]):        [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#__codelineno-11-25)         o = await recorder.get_input_devices()        [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#__codelineno-11-26)         show_snackbar(f"Input Devices: {', '.join([f'{d.id}:{d.label}' for d in o])}") [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#__codelineno-11-27)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#__codelineno-11-28)     async def handle_has_permission(e: ft.Event[ft.Button]):        [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#__codelineno-11-29)         try:            [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#__codelineno-11-30)             status = await recorder.has_permission()            [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#__codelineno-11-31)             show_snackbar(f"Audio Recording Permission status: {status}")        [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#__codelineno-11-32)         except Exception as e:            [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#__codelineno-11-33)             show_snackbar(f"Error checking permission: {e}") [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#__codelineno-11-34)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#__codelineno-11-35)     async def handle_pause(e: ft.Event[ft.Button]):        [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#__codelineno-11-36)         print(f"isRecording: {await recorder.is_recording()}")        [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#__codelineno-11-37)         if await recorder.is_recording():            [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#__codelineno-11-38)             await recorder.pause_recording() [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#__codelineno-11-39)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#__codelineno-11-40)     async def handle_resume(e: ft.Event[ft.Button]):        [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#__codelineno-11-41)         print(f"isPaused: {await recorder.is_paused()}")        [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#__codelineno-11-42)         if await recorder.is_paused():            [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#__codelineno-11-43)             await recorder.resume_recording() [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#__codelineno-11-44)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#__codelineno-11-45)     async def handle_audio_encoder_test(e: ft.Event[ft.Button]):        [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#__codelineno-11-46)         print(await recorder.is_supported_encoder(far.AudioEncoder.WAV)) [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#__codelineno-11-47)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#__codelineno-11-48)     recorder = far.AudioRecorder(        [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#__codelineno-11-49)         configuration=far.AudioRecorderConfiguration(encoder=far.AudioEncoder.WAV),        [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#__codelineno-11-50)         on_state_change=lambda e: print(f"State Changed: {e.data}"),    [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#__codelineno-11-51)     ) [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#__codelineno-11-52)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#__codelineno-11-53)     page.add(        [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#__codelineno-11-54)         ft.Button(content="Start Audio Recorder", on_click=handle_recording_start),        [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#__codelineno-11-55)         ft.Button(content="Stop Audio Recorder", on_click=handle_recording_stop),        [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#__codelineno-11-56)         ft.Button(content="List Devices", on_click=handle_list_devices),        [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#__codelineno-11-57)         ft.Button(content="Pause Recording", on_click=handle_pause),        [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#__codelineno-11-58)         ft.Button(content="Resume Recording", on_click=handle_resume),        [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#__codelineno-11-59)         ft.Button(content="WAV Encoder Support", on_click=handle_audio_encoder_test),        [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#__codelineno-11-60)         ft.Button(            [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#__codelineno-11-61)             content="Get Audio Recording Permission Status",            [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#__codelineno-11-62)             on_click=handle_has_permission,        [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#__codelineno-11-63)         ),    [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#__codelineno-11-64)     ) [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#__codelineno-11-65) [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#__codelineno-11-66)[](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#__codelineno-11-67) ft.run(main)`

Description[#](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#description "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Inherits: `[Service](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/service/#flet.Service) `

A control that allows you to record audio from your device.

This control can record audio using different audio encoders and also allows configuration of various audio recording parameters such as noise suppression, echo cancellation, and more.

Properties

*   **`[configuration](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#flet_audio_recorder.AudioRecorder.configuration) `**(`[AudioRecorderConfiguration](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/types/audiorecorderconfiguration/#flet_audio_recorder.AudioRecorderConfiguration) `) –
    
    The default configuration of the audio recorder.
    

Events

*   **`[on_state_change](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#flet_audio_recorder.AudioRecorder.on_state_change) `**(`[EventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.EventHandler) [[AudioRecorderStateChangeEvent](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/types/audiorecorderstatechangeevent/#flet_audio_recorder.AudioRecorderStateChangeEvent) ] | None`) –
    
    Event handler that is called when the state of the audio recorder changes.
    

Methods

*   **`[cancel_recording](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#flet_audio_recorder.AudioRecorder.cancel_recording) `** –
    
    Cancels the current audio recording.
    
*   **`[get_input_devices](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#flet_audio_recorder.AudioRecorder.get_input_devices) `** –
    
    Retrieves the available input devices for recording.
    
*   **`[has_permission](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#flet_audio_recorder.AudioRecorder.has_permission) `** –
    
    Checks if the app has permission to record audio.
    
*   **`[is_paused](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#flet_audio_recorder.AudioRecorder.is_paused) `** –
    
    Checks whether the audio recorder is currently paused.
    
*   **`[is_recording](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#flet_audio_recorder.AudioRecorder.is_recording) `** –
    
    Checks whether the audio recorder is currently recording.
    
*   **`[is_supported_encoder](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#flet_audio_recorder.AudioRecorder.is_supported_encoder) `** –
    
    Checks if the given audio encoder is supported by the recorder.
    
*   **`[pause_recording](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#flet_audio_recorder.AudioRecorder.pause_recording) `** –
    
    Pauses the ongoing audio recording.
    
*   **`[resume_recording](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#flet_audio_recorder.AudioRecorder.resume_recording) `** –
    
    Resumes a paused audio recording.
    
*   **`[start_recording](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#flet_audio_recorder.AudioRecorder.start_recording) `** –
    
    Starts recording audio and saves it to the specified output path.
    
*   **`[stop_recording](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#flet_audio_recorder.AudioRecorder.stop_recording) `** –
    
    Stops the audio recording and optionally returns the path to the saved file.
    

Properties[#](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#flet_audio_recorder.AudioRecorder-properties "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### configuration `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#flet_audio_recorder.AudioRecorder.configuration "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#__codelineno-0-1) configuration: [AudioRecorderConfiguration](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/types/audiorecorderconfiguration/#flet_audio_recorder.AudioRecorderConfiguration)  = [field](https://docs.python.org/3/library/dataclasses.html#dataclasses.field "<code>dataclasses.field</code>") (     [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#__codelineno-0-2)     default_factory=lambda: [AudioRecorderConfiguration](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/types/audiorecorderconfiguration/#flet_audio_recorder.AudioRecorderConfiguration) () [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#__codelineno-0-3) )`

The default configuration of the audio recorder.

Events[#](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#flet_audio_recorder.AudioRecorder-events "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### on\_state\_change `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#flet_audio_recorder.AudioRecorder.on_state_change "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#__codelineno-0-1) on_state_change: (     [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#__codelineno-0-2)     [EventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.EventHandler) [[AudioRecorderStateChangeEvent](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/types/audiorecorderstatechangeevent/#flet_audio_recorder.AudioRecorderStateChangeEvent) ] | None [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#__codelineno-0-3) ) = None`

Event handler that is called when the state of the audio recorder changes.

Methods[#](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#flet_audio_recorder.AudioRecorder-methods "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### cancel\_recording `async` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#flet_audio_recorder.AudioRecorder.cancel_recording "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#__codelineno-0-1) cancel_recording()`

Cancels the current audio recording.

### get\_input\_devices `async` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#flet_audio_recorder.AudioRecorder.get_input_devices "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#__codelineno-0-1) get_input_devices() -> [list](https://docs.python.org/3/glossary.html#term-list) [[InputDevice](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/types/inputdevice/#flet_audio_recorder.InputDevice) ]`

Retrieves the available input devices for recording.

Returns:

*   `[list](https://docs.python.org/3/glossary.html#term-list) [[InputDevice](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/types/inputdevice/#flet_audio_recorder.InputDevice) ]` –
    
    A list of available input devices.
    

### has\_permission `async` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#flet_audio_recorder.AudioRecorder.has_permission "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#__codelineno-0-1) has_permission() -> [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)`

Checks if the app has permission to record audio.

Returns:

*   `[bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool) ` –
    
    `True` if the app has permission, `False` otherwise.
    

### is\_paused `async` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#flet_audio_recorder.AudioRecorder.is_paused "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#__codelineno-0-1) is_paused() -> [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)`

Checks whether the audio recorder is currently paused.

Returns:

*   `[bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool) ` –
    
    `True` if the recorder is paused, `False` otherwise.
    

### is\_recording `async` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#flet_audio_recorder.AudioRecorder.is_recording "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#__codelineno-0-1) is_recording() -> [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)`

Checks whether the audio recorder is currently recording.

Returns:

*   `[bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool) ` –
    
    `True` if the recorder is currently recording, `False` otherwise.
    

### is\_supported\_encoder `async` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#flet_audio_recorder.AudioRecorder.is_supported_encoder "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#__codelineno-0-1) is_supported_encoder(encoder: [AudioEncoder](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/types/audioencoder/#flet_audio_recorder.AudioEncoder) ) -> [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)`

Checks if the given audio encoder is supported by the recorder.

Parameters:

*   **`encoder`** (`[AudioEncoder](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/types/audioencoder/#flet_audio_recorder.AudioEncoder) `) –
    
    The audio encoder to check.
    

Returns:

*   `[bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool) ` –
    
    `True` if the encoder is supported, `False` otherwise.
    

### pause\_recording `async` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#flet_audio_recorder.AudioRecorder.pause_recording "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#__codelineno-0-1) pause_recording()`

Pauses the ongoing audio recording.

### resume\_recording `async` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#flet_audio_recorder.AudioRecorder.resume_recording "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#__codelineno-0-1) resume_recording()`

Resumes a paused audio recording.

### start\_recording `async` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#flet_audio_recorder.AudioRecorder.start_recording "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#__codelineno-0-1) start_recording(     [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#__codelineno-0-2)     output_path: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)  | None = None,    [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#__codelineno-0-3)     configuration: [AudioRecorderConfiguration](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/types/audiorecorderconfiguration/#flet_audio_recorder.AudioRecorderConfiguration)  | None = None, [](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#__codelineno-0-4) ) -> [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)`

Starts recording audio and saves it to the specified output path.

If not on the web, the `output_path` parameter must be provided.

Parameters:

*   **`output_path`** (`[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)  | None`, default: `None` ) –
    
    The file path where the audio will be saved. It must be specified if not on web.
    
*   **`configuration`** (`[AudioRecorderConfiguration](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/types/audiorecorderconfiguration/#flet_audio_recorder.AudioRecorderConfiguration)  | None`, default: `None` ) –
    
    The configuration for the audio recorder. If `None`, the `AudioRecorder.configuration` will be used.
    

Returns:

*   `[bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool) ` –
    
    `True` if recording was successfully started, `False` otherwise.
    

Raises:

*   `[ValueError](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ValueError) ` –
    
    If `output_path` is not provided on platforms other than web.
    

### stop\_recording `async` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#flet_audio_recorder.AudioRecorder.stop_recording "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/audio_recorder/#__codelineno-0-1) stop_recording() -> [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)  | None`

Stops the audio recording and optionally returns the path to the saved file.

Returns:

*   `[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)  | None` –
    
    The file path where the audio was saved or `None` if not applicable.
    

Back to top

# Overview - Flet

 

[Skip to content](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/#charts)

Charts[#](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/#charts "Permanent link")

=================================================================

Interactive chart controls powered by [flet-charts](https://pypi.org/project/flet-charts/)
 let you display data as bar, line, pie, scatter and plotly visualisations directly in your Flet apps.

It is built on top of the [fl\_chart](https://pub.dev/packages/fl_chart)
 Flutter package and ships with helper classes for axis labels, tooltips and more.

Platform Support[#](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/#platform-support "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Platform | Windows | macOS | Linux | iOS | Android | Web |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Supported | ✅   | ✅   | ✅   | ✅   | ✅   | ✅   |

Usage[#](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/#usage "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------

Add `flet-charts` to your project dependencies:

uvpip

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/#__codelineno-0-1) uv add flet-charts`

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/#__codelineno-1-1) pip install flet-charts  # (1)!`

1.  After this, you will have to manually add this package to your `requirements.txt` or `pyproject.toml`.

Available Charts[#](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/#available-charts "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   [`BarChart`](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/)
    
*   [`CandlestickChart`](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/candlestickchart/)
    
*   [`LineChart`](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/linechart/)
    
*   [`MatplotlibChart`](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/matplotlibchart/)
    
*   [`PieChart`](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/piechart/)
    
*   [`PlotlyChart`](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/)
    
*   [`RadarChart`](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/)
    
*   [`ScatterChart`](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/scatterchart/)
    

Back to top

---

# Camera - Flet

 

[Skip to content](https://docs.flet.dev/camera/#camera)

Camera[#](https://docs.flet.dev/camera/#camera "Permanent link")

=================================================================

Display a live camera preview, capture photos and videos, and stream camera frames directly in your Flet apps.

Powered by the [camera](https://pub.dev/packages/camera)
 Flutter package.

Platform Support[#](https://docs.flet.dev/camera/#platform-support "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Platform | iOS | Android | Web | Windows | macOS | Linux |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Supported | ✅   | ✅   | ✅   | ❌   | ❌   | ❌   |

Usage[#](https://docs.flet.dev/camera/#usage "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------

Add the `flet-camera` package to your project dependencies:

uvpip

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/camera/#__codelineno-0-1) uv add flet-camera`

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/camera/#__codelineno-1-1) pip install flet-camera  # (1)!`

1.  After this, you will have to manually add this package to your `requirements.txt` or `pyproject.toml`.

Permissions

Request camera (and microphone if recording video with audio) permissions on mobile platforms before initializing the control. You can use [`PermissionHandler`](https://docs.flet.dev/permission_handler/#flet_permission_handler.PermissionHandler)
 to prompt the user. You can also use [predefined cross-platform permission bundles](https://docs.flet.dev/publish/#predefined-cross-platform-permission-bundles)
 for camera and microphone permissions.

Requirements[#](https://docs.flet.dev/camera/#requirements "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------

The below sections show the required configurations for each platform.

### Android[#](https://docs.flet.dev/camera/#android "Permanent link")

Configuration to be made to access the camera and optionally the microphone:

*   [`android.permission.CAMERA`](https://developer.android.com/reference/android/Manifest.permission#CAMERA)
    : Allows camera usage.
*   [`android.permission.RECORD_AUDIO`](https://developer.android.com/reference/android/Manifest.permission#RECORD_AUDIO)
     (optional): Allows video recording with audio.

`flet build``pyproject.toml`

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/camera/#__codelineno-2-1) flet build apk \   [](https://docs.flet.dev/camera/#__codelineno-2-2)   --android-permissions android.permission.CAMERA=true \  [](https://docs.flet.dev/camera/#__codelineno-2-3)   --android-permissions android.permission.RECORD_AUDIO=true`

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/camera/#__codelineno-3-1) [tool.flet.android.permission] [](https://docs.flet.dev/camera/#__codelineno-3-2) "android.permission.CAMERA" = true [](https://docs.flet.dev/camera/#__codelineno-3-3) "android.permission.RECORD_AUDIO" = true`

See also:

*   [setting Android permissions](https://docs.flet.dev/publish/android/#permissions)
    

### iOS[#](https://docs.flet.dev/camera/#ios "Permanent link")

Configuration to be made to access the camera and optionally the microphone:

*   [`NSCameraUsageDescription`](https://developer.apple.com/documentation/BundleResources/Information-Property-List/NSCameraUsageDescription)
    : Required for camera usage.
*   [`NSMicrophoneUsageDescription`](https://developer.apple.com/documentation/BundleResources/Information-Property-List/NSMicrophoneUsageDescription)
     (optional): Required only for video recording with audio. For example, when `enable_audio` parameter of [`Camera.initialize`](https://docs.flet.dev/camera/#flet_camera.Camera.initialize)
     is set to `True` (default).

`flet build``pyproject.toml`

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/camera/#__codelineno-4-1) flet build ipa \   [](https://docs.flet.dev/camera/#__codelineno-4-2)   --info-plist NSCameraUsageDescription="Some message to describe why you need this permission..." \  [](https://docs.flet.dev/camera/#__codelineno-4-3)   --info-plist NSMicrophoneUsageDescription="Some message to describe why you need this permission..."`

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/camera/#__codelineno-5-1) [tool.flet.ios.info] [](https://docs.flet.dev/camera/#__codelineno-5-2) NSCameraUsageDescription = "Some message to describe why you need this permission..." [](https://docs.flet.dev/camera/#__codelineno-5-3) NSMicrophoneUsageDescription = "Some message to describe why you need this permission..."`

See also:

*   [setting iOS permissions](https://docs.flet.dev/publish/ios/#permissions)
    

### Cross-platform[#](https://docs.flet.dev/camera/#cross-platform "Permanent link")

Additionally/alternatively, you can make use of our predefined cross-platform `camera` (and optionally `microphone`) [permission bundles](https://docs.flet.dev/publish/#predefined-cross-platform-permission-bundles)
:

`flet build``pyproject.toml`

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/camera/#__codelineno-6-1) flet build <target_platform> --permissions camera microphone`

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/camera/#__codelineno-7-1) [tool.flet] [](https://docs.flet.dev/camera/#__codelineno-7-2) permissions = ["camera", "microphone"]`

Example[#](https://docs.flet.dev/camera/#example "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------
