Description[#](https://docs.flet.dev/camera/#description "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------

Inherits: `[LayoutControl](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/layoutcontrol/#flet.LayoutControl) `

A control that provides camera preview and capture capabilities.

Properties

*   **`[content](https://docs.flet.dev/camera/#flet_camera.Camera.content) `**(`[Control](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control)  | None`) –
    
    Optional child to overlay on top of the camera preview.
    
*   **`[preview_enabled](https://docs.flet.dev/camera/#flet_camera.Camera.preview_enabled) `**(`[bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool) `) –
    
    Whether the preview surface is shown.
    

Events

*   **`[on_state_change](https://docs.flet.dev/camera/#flet_camera.Camera.on_state_change) `**(`[EventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.EventHandler) [[CameraStateEvent](https://docs.flet.dev/camera/types/camerastateevent/#flet_camera.CameraStateEvent) ] | None`) –
    
    Fires when the camera controller state changes.
    
*   **`[on_stream_image](https://docs.flet.dev/camera/#flet_camera.Camera.on_stream_image) `**(`[EventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.EventHandler) [[CameraImageEvent](https://docs.flet.dev/camera/types/cameraimageevent/#flet_camera.CameraImageEvent) ] | None`) –
    
    Fires when an image frame is available while streaming.
    

Methods

*   **`[get_available_cameras](https://docs.flet.dev/camera/#flet_camera.Camera.get_available_cameras) `** –
    
    Lists the available camera devices on the current platform.
    
*   **`[get_exposure_offset_step_size](https://docs.flet.dev/camera/#flet_camera.Camera.get_exposure_offset_step_size) `** –
    
    Returns the smallest increment supported for exposure offset changes.
    
*   **`[get_max_exposure_offset](https://docs.flet.dev/camera/#flet_camera.Camera.get_max_exposure_offset) `** –
    
    Maximum exposure offset supported by the current camera.
    
*   **`[get_max_zoom_level](https://docs.flet.dev/camera/#flet_camera.Camera.get_max_zoom_level) `** –
    
    Maximum zoom level supported by the current camera.
    
*   **`[get_min_exposure_offset](https://docs.flet.dev/camera/#flet_camera.Camera.get_min_exposure_offset) `** –
    
    Minimum exposure offset supported by the current camera.
    
*   **`[get_min_zoom_level](https://docs.flet.dev/camera/#flet_camera.Camera.get_min_zoom_level) `** –
    
    Minimum zoom level supported by the current camera.
    
*   **`[initialize](https://docs.flet.dev/camera/#flet_camera.Camera.initialize) `** –
    
    Initializes a new camera controller for the given description.
    
*   **`[lock_capture_orientation](https://docs.flet.dev/camera/#flet_camera.Camera.lock_capture_orientation) `** –
    
    Locks capture orientation to the specified device orientation.
    
*   **`[pause_preview](https://docs.flet.dev/camera/#flet_camera.Camera.pause_preview) `** –
    
    Pauses the camera preview.
    
*   **`[pause_video_recording](https://docs.flet.dev/camera/#flet_camera.Camera.pause_video_recording) `** –
    
    Pauses an active video recording.
    
*   **`[prepare_for_video_recording](https://docs.flet.dev/camera/#flet_camera.Camera.prepare_for_video_recording) `** –
    
    Prepares the capture session for video recording.
    
*   **`[resume_preview](https://docs.flet.dev/camera/#flet_camera.Camera.resume_preview) `** –
    
    Resumes the camera preview.
    
*   **`[resume_video_recording](https://docs.flet.dev/camera/#flet_camera.Camera.resume_video_recording) `** –
    
    Resumes a paused video recording.
    
*   **`[set_description](https://docs.flet.dev/camera/#flet_camera.Camera.set_description) `** –
    
    Switches to another camera description.
    
*   **`[set_exposure_mode](https://docs.flet.dev/camera/#flet_camera.Camera.set_exposure_mode) `** –
    
    Changes the exposure mode.
    
*   **`[set_exposure_offset](https://docs.flet.dev/camera/#flet_camera.Camera.set_exposure_offset) `** –
    
    Sets exposure offset in EV units.
    
*   **`[set_exposure_point](https://docs.flet.dev/camera/#flet_camera.Camera.set_exposure_point) `** –
    
    Sets the exposure metering point.
    
*   **`[set_flash_mode](https://docs.flet.dev/camera/#flet_camera.Camera.set_flash_mode) `** –
    
    Changes the flash mode.
    
*   **`[set_focus_mode](https://docs.flet.dev/camera/#flet_camera.Camera.set_focus_mode) `** –
    
    Changes the focus mode.
    
*   **`[set_focus_point](https://docs.flet.dev/camera/#flet_camera.Camera.set_focus_point) `** –
    
    Sets the focus metering point.
    
*   **`[set_zoom_level](https://docs.flet.dev/camera/#flet_camera.Camera.set_zoom_level) `** –
    
    Applies the provided zoom level.
    
*   **`[start_image_stream](https://docs.flet.dev/camera/#flet_camera.Camera.start_image_stream) `** –
    
    Begins streaming camera image frames.
    
*   **`[start_video_recording](https://docs.flet.dev/camera/#flet_camera.Camera.start_video_recording) `** –
    
    Starts capturing video.
    
*   **`[stop_image_stream](https://docs.flet.dev/camera/#flet_camera.Camera.stop_image_stream) `** –
    
    Stops streaming camera image frames.
    
*   **`[stop_video_recording](https://docs.flet.dev/camera/#flet_camera.Camera.stop_video_recording) `** –
    
    Stops video recording and returns the recorded bytes.
    
*   **`[supports_image_streaming](https://docs.flet.dev/camera/#flet_camera.Camera.supports_image_streaming) `** –
    
    Indicates whether image streaming is supported on the current platform.
    
*   **`[take_picture](https://docs.flet.dev/camera/#flet_camera.Camera.take_picture) `** –
    
    Captures a still image and returns the encoded bytes.
    
*   **`[unlock_capture_orientation](https://docs.flet.dev/camera/#flet_camera.Camera.unlock_capture_orientation) `** –
    
    Unlocks the capture orientation.

### initialize `async` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/camera/#flet_camera.Camera.initialize "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/camera/#__codelineno-0-1) initialize(     [](https://docs.flet.dev/camera/#__codelineno-0-2)     description: [CameraDescription](https://docs.flet.dev/camera/types/cameradescription/#flet_camera.CameraDescription) ,    [](https://docs.flet.dev/camera/#__codelineno-0-3)     resolution_preset: [ResolutionPreset](https://docs.flet.dev/camera/types/resolutionpreset/#flet_camera.ResolutionPreset) ,    [](https://docs.flet.dev/camera/#__codelineno-0-4)     enable_audio: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)  = True,    [](https://docs.flet.dev/camera/#__codelineno-0-5)     fps: [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int)  | None = None,    [](https://docs.flet.dev/camera/#__codelineno-0-6)     video_bitrate: [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int)  | None = None,    [](https://docs.flet.dev/camera/#__codelineno-0-7)     audio_bitrate: [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int)  | None = None,    [](https://docs.flet.dev/camera/#__codelineno-0-8)     image_format_group: [ImageFormatGroup](https://docs.flet.dev/camera/types/imageformatgroup/#flet_camera.ImageFormatGroup)  | None = None, [](https://docs.flet.dev/camera/#__codelineno-0-9) )`

Initializes a new camera controller for the given description.

Parameters:

*   **`description`** (`[CameraDescription](https://docs.flet.dev/camera/types/cameradescription/#flet_camera.CameraDescription) `) –
    
    Camera device to bind to.
    
*   **`resolution_preset`** (`[ResolutionPreset](https://docs.flet.dev/camera/types/resolutionpreset/#flet_camera.ResolutionPreset) `) –
    
    Desired resolution preset.
    
*   **`enable_audio`** (`[bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool) `, default: `True` ) –
    
    Whether audio is enabled for recordings.
    
*   **`fps`** (`[int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int)  | None`, default: `None` ) –
    
    Optional target frames per second.
    
*   **`video_bitrate`** (`[int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int)  | None`, default: `None` ) –
    
    Optional video bitrate.
    
*   **`audio_bitrate`** (`[int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int)  | None`, default: `None` ) –
    
    Optional audio bitrate.
    
*   **`image_format_group`** (`[ImageFormatGroup](https://docs.flet.dev/camera/types/imageformatgroup/#flet_camera.ImageFormatGroup)  | None`, default: `None` ) –
    
    Optional image format group override.

# MatplotlibChart - Flet

 

[Skip to content](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/matplotlibchart/#flet_charts.matplotlib_chart.MatplotlibChart)

MatplotlibChart
===============

Displays a [Matplotlib](https://matplotlib.org/)
 chart.

To display a Matplotlib figure with a built-in toolbar UI, use [`MatplotlibChartWithToolbar`](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/matplotlibchartwithtoolbar/#flet_charts.matplotlib_chart_with_toolbar.MatplotlibChartWithToolbar)
.

Warning

This control requires the [`matplotlib`](https://matplotlib.org/)
 Python package to be installed.

See this [installation guide](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/#installation)
 for more information.

Inherits: `[GestureDetector](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/gesturedetector/#flet.GestureDetector) `

Properties

*   **`[figure](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/matplotlibchart/#flet_charts.matplotlib_chart.MatplotlibChart.figure) `**(`Figure`) –
    
    Matplotlib figure to draw - an instance of
    

Events

*   **`[on_message](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/matplotlibchart/#flet_charts.matplotlib_chart.MatplotlibChart.on_message) `**(`[EventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.EventHandler) [MatplotlibChartMessageEvent] | None`) –
    
    The event is triggered on figure message update.
    
*   **`[on_toolbar_buttons_update](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/matplotlibchart/#flet_charts.matplotlib_chart.MatplotlibChart.on_toolbar_buttons_update) `**(`[EventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.EventHandler) [MatplotlibChartToolbarButtonsUpdateEvent] | None`) –
    
    Triggers when toolbar buttons status is updated.
    

Methods

*   **`[back](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/matplotlibchart/#flet_charts.matplotlib_chart.MatplotlibChart.back) `** –
    
    Goes back to the previous view.
    
*   **`[download](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/matplotlibchart/#flet_charts.matplotlib_chart.MatplotlibChart.download) `** –
    
    Downloads the current figure in the specified format.
    
*   **`[forward](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/matplotlibchart/#flet_charts.matplotlib_chart.MatplotlibChart.forward) `** –
    
    Goes forward to the next view.
    
*   **`[home](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/matplotlibchart/#flet_charts.matplotlib_chart.MatplotlibChart.home) `** –
    
    Resets the view to the original state.
    
*   **`[pan](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/matplotlibchart/#flet_charts.matplotlib_chart.MatplotlibChart.pan) `** –
    
    Activates the pan tool.
    
*   **`[send_binary](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/matplotlibchart/#flet_charts.matplotlib_chart.MatplotlibChart.send_binary) `** –
    
    Sends a binary message to the front end.
    
*   **`[send_json](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/matplotlibchart/#flet_charts.matplotlib_chart.MatplotlibChart.send_json) `** –
    
    Sends a JSON message to the front end.
    
*   **`[send_message](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/matplotlibchart/#flet_charts.matplotlib_chart.MatplotlibChart.send_message) `** –
    
    Sends a message to the figure's canvas manager.
    
*   **`[zoom](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/matplotlibchart/#flet_charts.matplotlib_chart.MatplotlibChart.zoom) `** –
    
    Activates the zoom tool.
    

Examples[#](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/matplotlibchart/#examples "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Bar chart[#](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/matplotlibchart/#bar-chart "Permanent link")

Based on an official [Matplotlib example](https://matplotlib.org/stable/gallery/lines_bars_and_markers/bar_colors.html#sphx-glr-gallery-lines-bars-and-markers-bar-colors-py)
.

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/matplotlibchart/#__codelineno-0-1) import matplotlib.pyplot as plt [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/matplotlibchart/#__codelineno-0-2)[](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/matplotlibchart/#__codelineno-0-3) import flet as ft [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/matplotlibchart/#__codelineno-0-4) import flet_charts as fch [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/matplotlibchart/#__codelineno-0-5) [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/matplotlibchart/#__codelineno-0-6)[](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/matplotlibchart/#__codelineno-0-7) def main(page: ft.Page):     [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/matplotlibchart/#__codelineno-0-8)     fig, ax = plt.subplots() [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/matplotlibchart/#__codelineno-0-9)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/matplotlibchart/#__codelineno-0-10)     fruits = ["apple", "blueberry", "cherry", "orange"]    [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/matplotlibchart/#__codelineno-0-11)     counts = [40, 100, 30, 55]    [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/matplotlibchart/#__codelineno-0-12)     bar_labels = ["red", "blue", "_red", "orange"]    [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/matplotlibchart/#__codelineno-0-13)     bar_colors = ["tab:red", "tab:blue", "tab:red", "tab:orange"] [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/matplotlibchart/#__codelineno-0-14)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/matplotlibchart/#__codelineno-0-15)     ax.bar(fruits, counts, label=bar_labels, color=bar_colors) [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/matplotlibchart/#__codelineno-0-16)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/matplotlibchart/#__codelineno-0-17)     ax.set_ylabel("fruit supply")    [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/matplotlibchart/#__codelineno-0-18)     ax.set_title("Fruit supply by kind and color")    [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/matplotlibchart/#__codelineno-0-19)     ax.legend(title="Fruit color") [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/matplotlibchart/#__codelineno-0-20)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/matplotlibchart/#__codelineno-0-21)     page.add(fch.MatplotlibChart(figure=fig, expand=True)) [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/matplotlibchart/#__codelineno-0-22) [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/matplotlibchart/#__codelineno-0-23)[](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/matplotlibchart/#__codelineno-0-24) if __name__ == "__main__":     [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/matplotlibchart/#__codelineno-0-25)     ft.run(main)`

[![bar_chart.png](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images-charts/examples/golden/macos/matplotlib_chart/bar_chart.png)](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images-charts/examples/golden/macos/matplotlib_chart/bar_chart.png)

Properties[#](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/matplotlibchart/#flet_charts.matplotlib_chart.MatplotlibChart-properties "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### figure `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/matplotlibchart/#flet_charts.matplotlib_chart.MatplotlibChart.figure "Permanent link")

Methods[#](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/matplotlibchart/#flet_charts.matplotlib_chart.MatplotlibChart-methods "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### back [#](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/matplotlibchart/#flet_charts.matplotlib_chart.MatplotlibChart.back "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/matplotlibchart/#__codelineno-0-1) back()`

Goes back to the previous view.

### download [#](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/matplotlibchart/#flet_charts.matplotlib_chart.MatplotlibChart.download "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/matplotlibchart/#__codelineno-0-1) download(format) -> [bytes](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#bytes)`

Downloads the current figure in the specified format. Args: format (str): The format to download the figure in (e.g., 'png', 'jpg', 'svg', etc.). Returns: bytes: The figure image in the specified format as a byte array.

### forward [#](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/matplotlibchart/#flet_charts.matplotlib_chart.MatplotlibChart.forward "Permanent link")

### pan [#](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/matplotlibchart/#flet_charts.matplotlib_chart.MatplotlibChart.pan "Permanent link")

Activates the pan tool.

### send\_binary [#](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/matplotlibchart/#flet_charts.matplotlib_chart.MatplotlibChart.send_binary "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/matplotlibchart/#__codelineno-0-1) send_binary(blob)`

Sends a binary message to the front end.

### send\_json [#](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/matplotlibchart/#flet_charts.matplotlib_chart.MatplotlibChart.send_json "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/matplotlibchart/#__codelineno-0-1) send_json(content)`

Sends a JSON message to the front end.

### send\_message [#](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/matplotlibchart/#flet_charts.matplotlib_chart.MatplotlibChart.send_message "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/matplotlibchart/#__codelineno-0-1) send_message(message)`

Sends a message to the figure's canvas manager.

### zoom [#](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/matplotlibchart/#flet_charts.matplotlib_chart.MatplotlibChart.zoom "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/matplotlibchart/#__codelineno-0-1) zoom()`

Activates the zoom tool.

Back to top

# PlotlyChart - Flet

 

[Skip to content](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#flet_charts.plotly_chart.PlotlyChart)

PlotlyChart
===========

Displays a [Plotly](https://plotly.com/python/)
 chart.

Warning

This control requires the [`plotly`](https://plotly.com/python/)
 and [`kaleido`](https://github.com/plotly/Kaleido)
 Python packages to be installed.

See this [installation guide](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/#installation)
 for more information.

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-0-1) import plotly.express as px [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-0-2)[](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-0-3) data_canada = px.data.gapminder().query("country == 'Canada'") [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-0-4) fig = px.bar(data_canada, x="year", y="pop") [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-0-5) fch.PlotlyChart(figure=fig, expand=True)`

[![PlotlyChart](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images-charts/examples/golden/macos/plotly_chart/image_for_docs.png)](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images-charts/examples/golden/macos/plotly_chart/image_for_docs.png)

Bar chart based on official [Plotly example](https://plotly.com/python/bar-charts)

Inherits: `[Container](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/container/#flet.Container) `

Properties

*   **`[figure](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#flet_charts.plotly_chart.PlotlyChart.figure) `**(`Figure`) –
    
    Plotly figure to draw.
    
*   **`[original_size](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#flet_charts.plotly_chart.PlotlyChart.original_size) `**(`[bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool) `) –
    
    Whether to display this chart in original size.
    

Examples[#](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#examples "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Example 1[#](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#example-1 "Permanent link")

Based on an official [Plotly example](https://plotly.com/python/line-charts)
.

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-0-1) import flet_charts as fch [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-0-2) import plotly.express as px [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-0-3)[](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-0-4) import flet as ft [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-0-5) [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-0-6)[](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-0-7) def main(page: ft.Page):     [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-0-8)     df = px.data.gapminder().query("continent=='Oceania'")    [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-0-9)     fig = px.line(df, x="year", y="lifeExp", color="country") [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-0-10)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-0-11)     page.add(fch.PlotlyChart(figure=fig, expand=True)) [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-0-12) [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-0-13)[](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-0-14) if __name__ == "__main__":     [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-0-15)     ft.run(main)`

[![example_1.png](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images-charts/examples/golden/macos/plotly_chart/example_1.png)](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images-charts/examples/golden/macos/plotly_chart/example_1.png)

### Example 2[#](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#example-2 "Permanent link")

Based on an official [Plotly example](https://plotly.com/python/bar-charts)
.

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-1-1) import flet_charts as fch [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-1-2) import plotly.express as px [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-1-3)[](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-1-4) import flet as ft [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-1-5) [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-1-6)[](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-1-7) def main(page: ft.Page):     [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-1-8)     df = px.data.gapminder().query("continent == 'Oceania'")    [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-1-9)     fig = px.bar(        [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-1-10)         df,        [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-1-11)         x="year",        [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-1-12)         y="pop",        [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-1-13)         hover_data=["lifeExp", "gdpPercap"],        [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-1-14)         color="country",        [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-1-15)         labels={"pop": "population of Canada"},        [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-1-16)         height=400,    [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-1-17)     ) [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-1-18)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-1-19)     page.add(fch.PlotlyChart(figure=fig, expand=True)) [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-1-20) [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-1-21)[](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-1-22) if __name__ == "__main__":     [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-1-23)     ft.run(main)`

[![example_2.png](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images-charts/examples/golden/macos/plotly_chart/example_2.png)](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images-charts/examples/golden/macos/plotly_chart/example_2.png)

### Example 3[#](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#example-3 "Permanent link")

Based on an official [Plotly example](https://plotly.com/python/pie-charts)
.

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-2-1) import flet_charts as fch [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-2-2) import plotly.graph_objects as go [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-2-3)[](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-2-4) import flet as ft [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-2-5) [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-2-6)[](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-2-7) def main(page: ft.Page):     [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-2-8)     labels = ["Oxygen", "Hydrogen", "Carbon_Dioxide", "Nitrogen"]    [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-2-9)     values = [4500, 2500, 1053, 500] [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-2-10)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-2-11)     fig = go.Figure(data=[go.Pie(labels=labels, values=values)]) [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-2-12)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-2-13)     page.add(fch.PlotlyChart(figure=fig, expand=True)) [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-2-14) [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-2-15)[](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-2-16) if __name__ == "__main__":     [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-2-17)     ft.run(main)`

[![example_3.png](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images-charts/examples/golden/macos/plotly_chart/example_3.png)](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images-charts/examples/golden/macos/plotly_chart/example_3.png)

### Example 4[#](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#example-4 "Permanent link")

Based on an official [Plotly example](https://plotly.com/python/box-plots)
.

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-3-1) import flet_charts as fch [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-3-2) import plotly.graph_objects as go [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-3-3)[](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-3-4) import flet as ft [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-3-5) [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-3-6)[](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-3-7) def main(page: ft.Page):     [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-3-8)     x = [        [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-3-9)         "day 1",        [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-3-10)         "day 1",        [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-3-11)         "day 1",        [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-3-12)         "day 1",        [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-3-13)         "day 1",        [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-3-14)         "day 1",        [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-3-15)         "day 2",        [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-3-16)         "day 2",        [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-3-17)         "day 2",        [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-3-18)         "day 2",        [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-3-19)         "day 2",        [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-3-20)         "day 2",    [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-3-21)     ] [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-3-22)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-3-23)     fig = go.Figure() [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-3-24)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-3-25)     fig.add_trace(        [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-3-26)         go.Box(            [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-3-27)             y=[0.2, 0.2, 0.6, 1.0, 0.5, 0.4, 0.2, 0.7, 0.9, 0.1, 0.5, 0.3],            [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-3-28)             x=x,            [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-3-29)             name="kale",            [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-3-30)             marker_color="#3D9970",        [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-3-31)         )    [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-3-32)     )    [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-3-33)     fig.add_trace(        [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-3-34)         go.Box(            [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-3-35)             y=[0.6, 0.7, 0.3, 0.6, 0.0, 0.5, 0.7, 0.9, 0.5, 0.8, 0.7, 0.2],            [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-3-36)             x=x,            [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-3-37)             name="radishes",            [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-3-38)             marker_color="#FF4136",        [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-3-39)         )    [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-3-40)     )    [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-3-41)     fig.add_trace(        [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-3-42)         go.Box(            [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-3-43)             y=[0.1, 0.3, 0.1, 0.9, 0.6, 0.6, 0.9, 1.0, 0.3, 0.6, 0.8, 0.5],            [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-3-44)             x=x,            [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-3-45)             name="carrots",            [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-3-46)             marker_color="#FF851B",        [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-3-47)         )    [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-3-48)     ) [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-3-49)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-3-50)     fig.update_layout(        [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-3-51)         yaxis_title="normalized moisture",        [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-3-52)         boxmode="group",  # group together boxes of the different traces    [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-3-53)     ) [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-3-54)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-3-55)     page.add(fch.PlotlyChart(figure=fig, expand=True)) [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-3-56) [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-3-57)[](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-3-58) if __name__ == "__main__":     [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-3-59)     ft.run(main)`

Properties[#](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#flet_charts.plotly_chart.PlotlyChart-properties "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### figure `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#flet_charts.plotly_chart.PlotlyChart.figure "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-0-1) figure: Figure = [field](https://docs.python.org/3/library/dataclasses.html#dataclasses.field "<code>dataclasses.field</code>") (metadata={'skip': True})`

Plotly figure to draw.

The value is an instance of [`plotly.graph_objects.Figure`](https://plotly.com/python-api-reference/generated/plotly.graph_objects.Figure.html)
.

### original\_size `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#flet_charts.plotly_chart.PlotlyChart.original_size "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/plotlychart/#__codelineno-0-1) original_size: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)  = False`

Whether to display this chart in original size.

Set to `False` for it to fit it's configured bounds.

# BarChart - Flet

 

[Skip to content](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#flet_charts.bar_chart.BarChart)

BarChart
========

Draws a bar chart.

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#__codelineno-0-1) fch.BarChart(     [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#__codelineno-0-2)     border=ft.Border.all(1, ft.Colors.GREY_400),    [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#__codelineno-0-3)     groups=[        [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#__codelineno-0-4)         fch.BarChartGroup(            [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#__codelineno-0-5)             x=0,            [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#__codelineno-0-6)             rods=[                [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#__codelineno-0-7)                 fch.BarChartRod(                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#__codelineno-0-8)                     from_y=0,                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#__codelineno-0-9)                     to_y=40,                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#__codelineno-0-10)                     color=ft.Colors.BLUE_GREY_200,                [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#__codelineno-0-11)                 ),            [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#__codelineno-0-12)             ],        [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#__codelineno-0-13)         ),        [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#__codelineno-0-14)         ...,    [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#__codelineno-0-15)     ], [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#__codelineno-0-16) )`

[![BarChart](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images-charts/examples/golden/macos/bar_chart/image_for_docs.png)](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images-charts/examples/golden/macos/bar_chart/image_for_docs.png)

Basic bar chart

Inherits: `[LayoutControl](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/layoutcontrol/#flet.LayoutControl) `

Properties

*   **`[animation](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#flet_charts.bar_chart.BarChart.animation) `**(`[AnimationValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.AnimationValue) `) –
    
    Controls chart implicit animation.
    
*   **`[baseline_y](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#flet_charts.bar_chart.BarChart.baseline_y) `**(`[Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None`) –
    
    Baseline value for Y axis.
    
*   **`[bgcolor](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#flet_charts.bar_chart.BarChart.bgcolor) `**(`[ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None`) –
    
    Background color of the chart.
    
*   **`[border](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#flet_charts.bar_chart.BarChart.border) `**(`[Border](https://docs.flet.dev/types/border/#flet.Border)  | None`) –
    
    The border around the chart.
    
*   **`[bottom_axis](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#flet_charts.bar_chart.BarChart.bottom_axis) `**(`[ChartAxis](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/types/chartaxis/#flet_charts.chart_axis.ChartAxis)  | None`) –
    
    The appearance of the bottom axis, its title and labels.
    
*   **`[group_alignment](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#flet_charts.bar_chart.BarChart.group_alignment) `**(`[MainAxisAlignment](https://docs.flet.dev/types/mainaxisalignment/#flet.MainAxisAlignment) `) –
    
    The alignment of the bar [`groups`](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#flet_charts.bar_chart.BarChart.groups)
     within this chart.
    
*   **`[group_spacing](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#flet_charts.bar_chart.BarChart.group_spacing) `**(`[Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number) `) –
    
    An amount of space between bar [`groups`](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#flet_charts.bar_chart.BarChart.groups)
    .
    
*   **`[groups](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#flet_charts.bar_chart.BarChart.groups) `**(`[list](https://docs.python.org/3/glossary.html#term-list) [[BarChartGroup](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/types/barchartgroup/#flet_charts.bar_chart_group.BarChartGroup) ]`) –
    
    The list of [`BarChartGroup`](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/types/barchartgroup/#flet_charts.bar_chart_group.BarChartGroup)
    s to draw.
    
*   **`[horizontal_grid_lines](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#flet_charts.bar_chart.BarChart.horizontal_grid_lines) `**(`[ChartGridLines](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/types/chartgridlines/#flet_charts.types.ChartGridLines)  | None`) –
    
    Controls drawing of chart's horizontal lines.
    
*   **`[interactive](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#flet_charts.bar_chart.BarChart.interactive) `**(`[bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool) `) –
    
    Enables automatic tooltips when hovering chart bars.
    
*   **`[left_axis](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#flet_charts.bar_chart.BarChart.left_axis) `**(`[ChartAxis](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/types/chartaxis/#flet_charts.chart_axis.ChartAxis)  | None`) –
    
    The appearance of the left axis, its title and labels.
    
*   **`[max_y](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#flet_charts.bar_chart.BarChart.max_y) `**(`[Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None`) –
    
    The maximum displayed value for Y axis.
    
*   **`[min_y](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#flet_charts.bar_chart.BarChart.min_y) `**(`[Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None`) –
    
    The minimum displayed value for Y axis.
    
*   **`[right_axis](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#flet_charts.bar_chart.BarChart.right_axis) `**(`[ChartAxis](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/types/chartaxis/#flet_charts.chart_axis.ChartAxis)  | None`) –
    
    The appearance of the right axis, its title and labels.
    
*   **`[tooltip](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#flet_charts.bar_chart.BarChart.tooltip) `**(`[BarChartTooltip](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/types/barcharttooltip/#flet_charts.bar_chart.BarChartTooltip)  | None`) –
    
    The tooltip configuration for this chart.
    
*   **`[top_axis](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#flet_charts.bar_chart.BarChart.top_axis) `**(`[ChartAxis](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/types/chartaxis/#flet_charts.chart_axis.ChartAxis)  | None`) –
    
    The appearance of the top axis, its title and labels.
    
*   **`[vertical_grid_lines](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#flet_charts.bar_chart.BarChart.vertical_grid_lines) `**(`[ChartGridLines](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/types/chartgridlines/#flet_charts.types.ChartGridLines)  | None`) –
    
    Controls drawing of chart's vertical lines.
    

Events

*   **`[on_event](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#flet_charts.bar_chart.BarChart.on_event) `**(`[EventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.EventHandler) [[BarChartEvent](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/types/barchartevent/#flet_charts.bar_chart.BarChartEvent) ] | None`) –
    
    Called when an event occurs on this chart, such as a click or hover.
    

Examples[#](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#examples "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Example 2[#](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#example-2 "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#__codelineno-1-1) import flet_charts as fch [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#__codelineno-1-2)[](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#__codelineno-1-3) import flet as ft [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#__codelineno-1-4) [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#__codelineno-1-5)[](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#__codelineno-1-6) class CustomRod(fch.BarChartRod):     [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#__codelineno-1-7)     def __init__(self, y: float, hovered: bool = False):        [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#__codelineno-1-8)         super().__init__()        [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#__codelineno-1-9)         self.hovered = hovered        [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#__codelineno-1-10)         self.y = y        [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#__codelineno-1-11)         self.width = 22        [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#__codelineno-1-12)         self.color = ft.Colors.WHITE        [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#__codelineno-1-13)         self.bg_to_y = 20        [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#__codelineno-1-14)         self.bg_color = ft.Colors.GREEN_300 [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#__codelineno-1-15)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#__codelineno-1-16)     def before_update(self):        [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#__codelineno-1-17)         super().before_update()        [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#__codelineno-1-18)         self.to_y = self.y + 0.5 if self.hovered else self.y        [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#__codelineno-1-19)         self.color = ft.Colors.YELLOW if self.hovered else ft.Colors.WHITE        [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#__codelineno-1-20)         self.border_side = (            [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#__codelineno-1-21)             ft.BorderSide(width=1, color=ft.Colors.RED)            [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#__codelineno-1-22)             if self.hovered            [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#__codelineno-1-23)             else ft.BorderSide(width=1, color=ft.Colors.BLUE)        [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#__codelineno-1-24)         ) [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#__codelineno-1-25) [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#__codelineno-1-26)[](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#__codelineno-1-27) def main(page: ft.Page):     [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#__codelineno-1-28)     def on_chart_event(e: fch.BarChartEvent):        [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#__codelineno-1-29)         if e.type == fch.ChartEventType.POINTER_HOVER:            [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#__codelineno-1-30)             for group_index, group in enumerate(chart.groups):                [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#__codelineno-1-31)                 for rod_index, rod in enumerate(group.rods):                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#__codelineno-1-32)                     rod.hovered = (                        [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#__codelineno-1-33)                         e.group_index == group_index and e.rod_index == rod_index                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#__codelineno-1-34)                     )            [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#__codelineno-1-35)             chart.update() [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#__codelineno-1-36)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#__codelineno-1-37)     chart = fch.BarChart(        [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#__codelineno-1-38)         on_event=on_chart_event,        [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#__codelineno-1-39)         interactive=True,        [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#__codelineno-1-40)         groups=[            [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#__codelineno-1-41)             fch.BarChartGroup(x=0, rods=[CustomRod(5)]),            [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#__codelineno-1-42)             fch.BarChartGroup(x=1, rods=[CustomRod(6.5)]),            [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#__codelineno-1-43)             fch.BarChartGroup(x=2, rods=[CustomRod(15)]),            [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#__codelineno-1-44)             fch.BarChartGroup(x=3, rods=[CustomRod(7.5)]),            [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#__codelineno-1-45)             fch.BarChartGroup(x=4, rods=[CustomRod(9)]),            [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#__codelineno-1-46)             fch.BarChartGroup(x=5, rods=[CustomRod(11.5)]),            [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#__codelineno-1-47)             fch.BarChartGroup(x=6, rods=[CustomRod(6)]),        [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#__codelineno-1-48)         ],        [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#__codelineno-1-49)         bottom_axis=fch.ChartAxis(            [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#__codelineno-1-50)             labels=[                [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#__codelineno-1-51)                 fch.ChartAxisLabel(value=0, label=ft.Text("M", color=ft.Colors.BLUE)),                [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#__codelineno-1-52)                 fch.ChartAxisLabel(value=1, label=ft.Text("T", color=ft.Colors.YELLOW)), Properties[#](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#flet_charts.bar_chart.BarChart-properties "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### animation `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#flet_charts.bar_chart.BarChart.animation "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#__codelineno-0-1) animation: [AnimationValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.AnimationValue)  = [field](https://docs.python.org/3/library/dataclasses.html#dataclasses.field "<code>dataclasses.field</code>") (     [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#__codelineno-0-2)     default_factory=lambda: [Animation](https://docs.flet.dev/types/animation/#flet.Animation) (        [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#__codelineno-0-3)         duration=[Duration](https://docs.flet.dev/types/duration/#flet.Duration) (milliseconds=150), curve=[LINEAR](https://docs.flet.dev/types/animationcurve/#flet.AnimationCurve.LINEAR)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#__codelineno-0-4)     ) [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#__codelineno-0-5) )`

Controls chart implicit animation.

### baseline\_y `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#flet_charts.bar_chart.BarChart.baseline_y "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#__codelineno-0-1) baseline_y: [Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None = None`

Baseline value for Y axis.

### bgcolor `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#flet_charts.bar_chart.BarChart.bgcolor "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#__codelineno-0-1) bgcolor: [ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None = None`

Background color of the chart.

### border `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#flet_charts.bar_chart.BarChart.border "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#__codelineno-0-1) border: [Border](https://docs.flet.dev/types/border/#flet.Border)  | None = None`

The border around the chart.

### bottom\_axis `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#flet_charts.bar_chart.BarChart.bottom_axis "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#__codelineno-0-1) bottom_axis: [ChartAxis](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/types/chartaxis/#flet_charts.chart_axis.ChartAxis)  | None = None`

The appearance of the bottom axis, its title and labels.

### group\_alignment `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#flet_charts.bar_chart.BarChart.group_alignment "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#__codelineno-0-1) group_alignment: [MainAxisAlignment](https://docs.flet.dev/types/mainaxisalignment/#flet.MainAxisAlignment)  = [SPACE_EVENLY](https://docs.flet.dev/types/mainaxisalignment/#flet.MainAxisAlignment.SPACE_EVENLY)`

The alignment of the bar [`groups`](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#flet_charts.bar_chart.BarChart.groups)
 within this chart.

If set to [`MainAxisAlignment.CENTER`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/mainaxisalignment/#flet.MainAxisAlignment.CENTER)
, the space between the `groups` can be specified using [`group_spacing`](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#flet_charts.bar_chart.BarChart.group_spacing)
.

### group\_spacing `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#flet_charts.bar_chart.BarChart.group_spacing "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#__codelineno-0-1) group_spacing: [Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  = 16.0`

An amount of space between bar [`groups`](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#flet_charts.bar_chart.BarChart.groups)
.

### groups `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#flet_charts.bar_chart.BarChart.groups "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#__codelineno-0-1) groups: [list](https://docs.python.org/3/glossary.html#term-list) [[BarChartGroup](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/types/barchartgroup/#flet_charts.bar_chart_group.BarChartGroup) ] = [field](https://docs.python.org/3/library/dataclasses.html#dataclasses.field "<code>dataclasses.field</code>") (default_factory=[list](https://docs.python.org/3/glossary.html#term-list) )`

The list of [`BarChartGroup`](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/types/barchartgroup/#flet_charts.bar_chart_group.BarChartGroup)
s to draw.

### horizontal\_grid\_lines `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#flet_charts.bar_chart.BarChart.horizontal_grid_lines "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#__codelineno-0-1) horizontal_grid_lines: [ChartGridLines](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/types/chartgridlines/#flet_charts.types.ChartGridLines)  | None = None`

Controls drawing of chart's horizontal lines.

### interactive `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#flet_charts.bar_chart.BarChart.interactive "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#__codelineno-0-1) interactive: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)  = True`

Enables automatic tooltips when hovering chart bars.

### left\_axis `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#flet_charts.bar_chart.BarChart.left_axis "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#__codelineno-0-1) left_axis: [ChartAxis](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/types/chartaxis/#flet_charts.chart_axis.ChartAxis)  | None = None`

The appearance of the left axis, its title and labels.

### max\_y `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#flet_charts.bar_chart.BarChart.max_y "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#__codelineno-0-1) max_y: [Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None = None`

The maximum displayed value for Y axis.

### min\_y `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#flet_charts.bar_chart.BarChart.min_y "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#__codelineno-0-1) min_y: [Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None = None`

The minimum displayed value for Y axis.

### right\_axis `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#flet_charts.bar_chart.BarChart.right_axis "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#__codelineno-0-1) right_axis: [ChartAxis](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/types/chartaxis/#flet_charts.chart_axis.ChartAxis)  | None = None`

The appearance of the right axis, its title and labels.

### tooltip `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#flet_charts.bar_chart.BarChart.tooltip "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#__codelineno-0-1) tooltip: [BarChartTooltip](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/types/barcharttooltip/#flet_charts.bar_chart.BarChartTooltip)  | None = [field](https://docs.python.org/3/library/dataclasses.html#dataclasses.field "<code>dataclasses.field</code>") (     [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#__codelineno-0-2)     default_factory=lambda: [BarChartTooltip](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/types/barcharttooltip/#flet_charts.bar_chart.BarChartTooltip) () [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#__codelineno-0-3) )`

The tooltip configuration for this chart.

If set to `None`, tooltips will not shown throughout this chart.

### top\_axis `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#flet_charts.bar_chart.BarChart.top_axis "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#__codelineno-0-1) top_axis: [ChartAxis](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/types/chartaxis/#flet_charts.chart_axis.ChartAxis)  | None = None`

The appearance of the top axis, its title and labels.

### vertical\_grid\_lines `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#flet_charts.bar_chart.BarChart.vertical_grid_lines "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#__codelineno-0-1) vertical_grid_lines: [ChartGridLines](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/types/chartgridlines/#flet_charts.types.ChartGridLines)  | None = None`

Controls drawing of chart's vertical lines.

Events[#](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#flet_charts.bar_chart.BarChart-events "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### on\_event `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#flet_charts.bar_chart.BarChart.on_event "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/barchart/#__codelineno-0-1) on_event: [EventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.EventHandler) [[BarChartEvent](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/types/barchartevent/#flet_charts.bar_chart.BarChartEvent) ] | None = None`

Called when an event occurs on this chart, such as a click or hover.

# RadarChart - Flet

 

[Skip to content](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#flet_charts.radar_chart.RadarChart)

RadarChart
==========

A radar chart made of multiple datasets.

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#__codelineno-0-1) fch.RadarChart(     [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#__codelineno-0-2)     titles=[fch.RadarChartTitle(text="winter"), ...],    [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#__codelineno-0-3)     radar_shape=fch.RadarShape.CIRCLE,    [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#__codelineno-0-4)     data_sets=[        [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#__codelineno-0-5)         fch.RadarDataSet(            [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#__codelineno-0-6)             fill_color=ft.Colors.with_opacity(0.2, ft.Colors.BLUE_GREY_700),            [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#__codelineno-0-7)             entries=[fch.RadarDataSetEntry(130), ...],        [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#__codelineno-0-8)         ),        [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#__codelineno-0-9)         ...,    [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#__codelineno-0-10)     ], [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#__codelineno-0-11) )`

[![RadarChart](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images-charts/examples/golden/macos/radar_chart/image_for_docs.png)](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images-charts/examples/golden/macos/radar_chart/image_for_docs.png)

Basic radar chart

Inherits: `[LayoutControl](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/layoutcontrol/#flet.LayoutControl) `

Properties

*   **`[animation](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#flet_charts.radar_chart.RadarChart.animation) `**(`[AnimationValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.AnimationValue) `) –
    
    Controls the implicit animation applied when updating this chart.
    
*   **`[border](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#flet_charts.radar_chart.RadarChart.border) `**(`[Border](https://docs.flet.dev/types/border/#flet.Border)  | None`) –
    
    The border drawn around this chart.
    
*   **`[center_min_value](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#flet_charts.radar_chart.RadarChart.center_min_value) `**(`[bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool) `) –
    
    Whether minimum entry values should be positioned at the center of this chart.
    
*   **`[data_sets](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#flet_charts.radar_chart.RadarChart.data_sets) `**(`[list](https://docs.python.org/3/glossary.html#term-list) [[RadarDataSet](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/types/radardataset/#flet_charts.radar_data_set.RadarDataSet) ]`) –
    
    A list of [`RadarDataSet`](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/types/radardataset/#flet_charts.radar_data_set.RadarDataSet)
     controls rendered on the chart.
    
*   **`[grid_border_side](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#flet_charts.radar_chart.RadarChart.grid_border_side) `**(`[BorderSide](https://docs.flet.dev/types/borderside/#flet.BorderSide) `) –
    
    The style of the radar grid lines.
    
*   **`[interactive](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#flet_charts.radar_chart.RadarChart.interactive) `**(`[bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool) `) –
    
    Enables touch interactions and event notifications.
    
*   **`[long_press_duration](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#flet_charts.radar_chart.RadarChart.long_press_duration) `**(`[DurationValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.DurationValue)  | None`) –
    
    The duration before a long-press event fires.
    
*   **`[radar_bgcolor](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#flet_charts.radar_chart.RadarChart.radar_bgcolor) `**(`[ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue) `) –
    
    The background color of the radar area.
    
*   **`[radar_border_side](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#flet_charts.radar_chart.RadarChart.radar_border_side) `**(`[BorderSide](https://docs.flet.dev/types/borderside/#flet.BorderSide) `) –
    
    The outline drawn around the radar area.
    
*   **`[radar_shape](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#flet_charts.radar_chart.RadarChart.radar_shape) `**(`[RadarShape](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/types/radarshape/#flet_charts.radar_chart.RadarShape) `) –
    
    The shape of the radar area.
    
*   **`[tick_border_side](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#flet_charts.radar_chart.RadarChart.tick_border_side) `**(`[BorderSide](https://docs.flet.dev/types/borderside/#flet.BorderSide) `) –
    
    The style of the tick rings.
    
*   **`[tick_count](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#flet_charts.radar_chart.RadarChart.tick_count) `**(`[Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number) `) –
    
    Number of tick rings drawn from the centre to the edge.
    
*   **`[ticks_text_style](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#flet_charts.radar_chart.RadarChart.ticks_text_style) `**(`[TextStyle](https://docs.flet.dev/types/textstyle/#flet.TextStyle)  | None`) –
    
    The text style used to draw tick labels.
    
*   **`[title_position_percentage_offset](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#flet_charts.radar_chart.RadarChart.title_position_percentage_offset) `**(`[Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number) `) –
    
    Defines the relative distance of titles from the chart center.
    
*   **`[title_text_style](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#flet_charts.radar_chart.RadarChart.title_text_style) `**(`[TextStyle](https://docs.flet.dev/types/textstyle/#flet.TextStyle)  | None`) –
    
    The text style applied to titles around this chart.
    
*   **`[titles](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#flet_charts.radar_chart.RadarChart.titles) `**(`[list](https://docs.python.org/3/glossary.html#term-list) [[RadarChartTitle](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/types/radarcharttitle/#flet_charts.radar_chart.RadarChartTitle) ]`) –
    
    The titles shown around this chart, matching the number of entries per set.
    
*   **`[touch_spot_threshold](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#flet_charts.radar_chart.RadarChart.touch_spot_threshold) `**(`[Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number) `) –
    
    The radius (in logical pixels) used to detect nearby entries for touches.
    

Events

*   **`[on_event](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#flet_charts.radar_chart.RadarChart.on_event) `**(`[EventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.EventHandler) [[RadarChartEvent](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/types/radarchartevent/#flet_charts.radar_chart.RadarChartEvent) ] | None`) –
    
    Called when the chart is interacted with.
    

Examples[#](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#examples "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Example 1[#](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#example-1 "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#__codelineno-0-1) import flet as ft [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#__codelineno-0-2) import flet_charts as fch [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#__codelineno-0-3) [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#__codelineno-0-4)[](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#__codelineno-0-5) def main(page: ft.Page):     [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#__codelineno-0-6)     page.title = "Radar chart"    [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#__codelineno-0-7)     page.padding = 20    [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#__codelineno-0-8)     # page.vertical_alignment = page.horizontal_alignment = "center"    [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#__codelineno-0-9)     page.theme_mode = ft.ThemeMode.LIGHT [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#__codelineno-0-10)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#__codelineno-0-11)     categories = ["macOS", "Linux", "Windows"] [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#__codelineno-0-12)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#__codelineno-0-13)     page.add(        [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#__codelineno-0-14)         fch.RadarChart(            [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#__codelineno-0-15)             expand=True,            [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#__codelineno-0-16)             titles=[fch.RadarChartTitle(text=label) for label in categories],            [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#__codelineno-0-17)             center_min_value=True,            [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#__codelineno-0-18)             tick_count=4,            [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#__codelineno-0-19)             ticks_text_style=ft.TextStyle(size=20, color=ft.Colors.ON_SURFACE),            [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#__codelineno-0-20)             title_text_style=ft.TextStyle(                [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#__codelineno-0-21)                 size=24, weight=ft.FontWeight.BOLD, color=ft.Colors.ON_SURFACE            [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#__codelineno-0-22)             ),            [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#__codelineno-0-23)             on_event=lambda e: print(e.type),            [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#__codelineno-0-24)             data_sets=[                [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#__codelineno-0-25)                 fch.RadarDataSet(                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#__codelineno-0-26)                     fill_color=ft.Colors.with_opacity(0.2, ft.Colors.DEEP_PURPLE),                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#__codelineno-0-27)                     border_color=ft.Colors.DEEP_PURPLE,                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#__codelineno-0-28)                     entry_radius=4,                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#__codelineno-0-29)                     entries=[                        [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#__codelineno-0-30)                         fch.RadarDataSetEntry(300),                        [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#__codelineno-0-31)                         fch.RadarDataSetEntry(50),                        [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#__codelineno-0-32)                         fch.RadarDataSetEntry(250),                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#__codelineno-0-33)                     ],                [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#__codelineno-0-34)                 ),                [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#__codelineno-0-35)                 fch.RadarDataSet(                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#__codelineno-0-36)                     fill_color=ft.Colors.with_opacity(0.15, ft.Colors.PINK),                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#__codelineno-0-37)                     border_color=ft.Colors.PINK,                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#__codelineno-0-38)                     entry_radius=4,                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#__codelineno-0-39)                     entries=[                        [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#__codelineno-0-40)                         fch.RadarDataSetEntry(250),                        [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#__codelineno-0-41)                         fch.RadarDataSetEntry(100),                        [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#__codelineno-0-42)                         fch.RadarDataSetEntry(200),                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#__codelineno-0-43)                     ],                [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#__codelineno-0-44)                 ),                [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#__codelineno-0-45)                 fch.RadarDataSet(                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#__codelineno-0-46)                     fill_color=ft.Colors.with_opacity(0.12, ft.Colors.CYAN),                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#__codelineno-0-47)                     border_color=ft.Colors.CYAN,                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#__codelineno-0-48)                     entry_radius=4,                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#__codelineno-0-49)                     entries=[                        [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#__codelineno-0-50)                         fch.RadarDataSetEntry(200),                        [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#__codelineno-0-51)                         fch.RadarDataSetEntry(150),                        [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#__codelineno-0-52)                         fch.RadarDataSetEntry(50),                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#__codelineno-0-53)                     ],                [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#__codelineno-0-54)                 ),            [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#__codelineno-0-55)             ],        [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#__codelineno-0-56)         )    [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#__codelineno-0-57)     ) [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#__codelineno-0-58) [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#__codelineno-0-59)[](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#__codelineno-0-60) if __name__ == "__main__":     [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#__codelineno-0-61)     ft.run(main)`

[![example_1.png](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images-charts/examples/golden/macos/radar_chart/example_1.png)](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images-charts/examples/golden/macos/radar_chart/example_1.png)

Properties[#](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#flet_charts.radar_chart.RadarChart-properties "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### animation `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#flet_charts.radar_chart.RadarChart.animation "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#__codelineno-0-1) animation: [AnimationValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.AnimationValue)  = [field](https://docs.python.org/3/library/dataclasses.html#dataclasses.field "<code>dataclasses.field</code>") (     [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#__codelineno-0-2)     default_factory=lambda: [Animation](https://docs.flet.dev/types/animation/#flet.Animation) (        [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#__codelineno-0-3)         duration=[Duration](https://docs.flet.dev/types/duration/#flet.Duration) (milliseconds=150), curve=[LINEAR](https://docs.flet.dev/types/animationcurve/#flet.AnimationCurve.LINEAR)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#__codelineno-0-4)     ) [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#__codelineno-0-5) )`

Controls the implicit animation applied when updating this chart.

### border `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#flet_charts.radar_chart.RadarChart.border "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#__codelineno-0-1) border: [Border](https://docs.flet.dev/types/border/#flet.Border)  | None = None`

The border drawn around this chart.

### center\_min\_value `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#flet_charts.radar_chart.RadarChart.center_min_value "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#__codelineno-0-1) center_min_value: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)  = False`

Whether minimum entry values should be positioned at the center of this chart.

### data\_sets `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#flet_charts.radar_chart.RadarChart.data_sets "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#__codelineno-0-1) data_sets: [list](https://docs.python.org/3/glossary.html#term-list) [[RadarDataSet](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/types/radardataset/#flet_charts.radar_data_set.RadarDataSet) ] = [field](https://docs.python.org/3/library/dataclasses.html#dataclasses.field "<code>dataclasses.field</code>") (default_factory=[list](https://docs.python.org/3/glossary.html#term-list) )`

A list of [`RadarDataSet`](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/types/radardataset/#flet_charts.radar_data_set.RadarDataSet)
 controls rendered on the chart.

### grid\_border\_side `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#flet_charts.radar_chart.RadarChart.grid_border_side "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#__codelineno-0-1) grid_border_side: [BorderSide](https://docs.flet.dev/types/borderside/#flet.BorderSide)  = [field](https://docs.python.org/3/library/dataclasses.html#dataclasses.field "<code>dataclasses.field</code>") (     [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#__codelineno-0-2)     default_factory=lambda: [BorderSide](https://docs.flet.dev/types/borderside/#flet.BorderSide) (width=2.0) [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#__codelineno-0-3) )`

The style of the radar grid lines.

### interactive `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#flet_charts.radar_chart.RadarChart.interactive "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#__codelineno-0-1) interactive: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)  = True`

Enables touch interactions and event notifications.

### long\_press\_duration `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#flet_charts.radar_chart.RadarChart.long_press_duration "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#__codelineno-0-1) long_press_duration: [DurationValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.DurationValue)  | None = None`

The duration before a long-press event fires.

### radar\_bgcolor `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#flet_charts.radar_chart.RadarChart.radar_bgcolor "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#__codelineno-0-1) radar_bgcolor: [ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  = [TRANSPARENT](https://docs.flet.dev/types/colors/#flet.Colors.TRANSPARENT)`

The background color of the radar area.

### radar\_border\_side `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#flet_charts.radar_chart.RadarChart.radar_border_side "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#__codelineno-0-1) radar_border_side: [BorderSide](https://docs.flet.dev/types/borderside/#flet.BorderSide)  = [field](https://docs.python.org/3/library/dataclasses.html#dataclasses.field "<code>dataclasses.field</code>") (     [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#__codelineno-0-2)     default_factory=lambda: [BorderSide](https://docs.flet.dev/types/borderside/#flet.BorderSide) (width=2.0) [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#__codelineno-0-3) )`

The outline drawn around the radar area.

### radar\_shape `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#flet_charts.radar_chart.RadarChart.radar_shape "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#__codelineno-0-1) radar_shape: [RadarShape](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/types/radarshape/#flet_charts.radar_chart.RadarShape)  = [POLYGON](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/types/radarshape/#flet_charts.radar_chart.RadarShape.POLYGON)`

The shape of the radar area.

### tick\_border\_side `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#flet_charts.radar_chart.RadarChart.tick_border_side "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#__codelineno-0-1) tick_border_side: [BorderSide](https://docs.flet.dev/types/borderside/#flet.BorderSide)  = [field](https://docs.python.org/3/library/dataclasses.html#dataclasses.field "<code>dataclasses.field</code>") (     [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#__codelineno-0-2)     default_factory=lambda: [BorderSide](https://docs.flet.dev/types/borderside/#flet.BorderSide) (width=2.0) [](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#__codelineno-0-3) )`

The style of the tick rings.

### tick\_count `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#flet_charts.radar_chart.RadarChart.tick_count "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#__codelineno-0-1) tick_count: [Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  = 1`

Number of tick rings drawn from the centre to the edge.

Must be greater than or equal to `1`.

Raises:

*   `[ValueError](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ValueError) ` –
    
    If set to a value less than `1`.
    

### ticks\_text\_style `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#flet_charts.radar_chart.RadarChart.ticks_text_style "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#__codelineno-0-1) ticks_text_style: [TextStyle](https://docs.flet.dev/types/textstyle/#flet.TextStyle)  | None = None`

The text style used to draw tick labels.

### title\_position\_percentage\_offset `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#flet_charts.radar_chart.RadarChart.title_position_percentage_offset "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#__codelineno-0-1) title_position_percentage_offset: [Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  = 0.2`

Defines the relative distance of titles from the chart center.

*   `0` draws titles near the inside edge of each section.
*   `1` draws titles near the outside edge of each section.

Must be between `0` and `1` (inclusive).

Raises:

*   `[ValueError](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ValueError) ` –
    
    If set to a value less than `0` or greater than `1`.
    

### title\_text\_style `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#flet_charts.radar_chart.RadarChart.title_text_style "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#__codelineno-0-1) title_text_style: [TextStyle](https://docs.flet.dev/types/textstyle/#flet.TextStyle)  | None = None`

The text style applied to titles around this chart.

### titles `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#flet_charts.radar_chart.RadarChart.titles "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#__codelineno-0-1) titles: [list](https://docs.python.org/3/glossary.html#term-list) [[RadarChartTitle](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/types/radarcharttitle/#flet_charts.radar_chart.RadarChartTitle) ] = [field](https://docs.python.org/3/library/dataclasses.html#dataclasses.field "<code>dataclasses.field</code>") (default_factory=[list](https://docs.python.org/3/glossary.html#term-list) )`

The titles shown around this chart, matching the number of entries per set.

### touch\_spot\_threshold `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#flet_charts.radar_chart.RadarChart.touch_spot_threshold "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#__codelineno-0-1) touch_spot_threshold: [Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  = 10`

The radius (in logical pixels) used to detect nearby entries for touches.

Events[#](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#flet_charts.radar_chart.RadarChart-events "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### on\_event `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#flet_charts.radar_chart.RadarChart.on_event "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/radarchart/#__codelineno-0-1) on_event: [EventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.EventHandler) [[RadarChartEvent](https://docs.flet.dev/charts/types/radarchartevent/#flet_charts.radar_chart.RadarChartEvent) ] | None = None`

Called when the chart is interacted with.
