# FilledIconButton - Flet

 

[Skip to content](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/fillediconbutton/#flet.FilledIconButton)

FilledIconButton
================

A filled variant of [`IconButton`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#flet.IconButton)
.

Filled icon buttons have higher visual impact and should be used for high emphasis actions, such as turning off a microphone or camera.

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/fillediconbutton/#__codelineno-0-1) ft.FilledIconButton(icon=ft.Icons.CHECK)`

[![FilledIconButton](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/material/golden/macos/filled_icon_button/image_for_docs.png)](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/material/golden/macos/filled_icon_button/image_for_docs.png)

Basic FilledIconButton

Inherits: `[IconButton](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#flet.IconButton) `

Back to top

---

# CircleAvatar - Flet

 

[Skip to content](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/circleavatar/#flet.CircleAvatar)

CircleAvatar
============

A circle that represents a user.

If [`foreground_image_src`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/circleavatar/#flet.CircleAvatar.foreground_image_src)
 fails then [`background_image_src`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/circleavatar/#flet.CircleAvatar.background_image_src)
 is used, and if this also fails, then [`bgcolor`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/circleavatar/#flet.CircleAvatar.bgcolor)
 is used.

Example:

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/circleavatar/#__codelineno-0-1) ft.CircleAvatar(     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/circleavatar/#__codelineno-0-2)     content=ft.Text("AB"),    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/circleavatar/#__codelineno-0-3)     bgcolor=ft.Colors.PRIMARY,    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/circleavatar/#__codelineno-0-4)     color=ft.Colors.ON_PRIMARY, [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/circleavatar/#__codelineno-0-5) )`

[![CircleAvatar](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/material/golden/macos/circle_avatar/image_for_docs.png)](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/material/golden/macos/circle_avatar/image_for_docs.png)

Basic CircleAvatar

Inherits: `[LayoutControl](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/layoutcontrol/#flet.LayoutControl) `

Properties

*   **`[background_image_src](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/circleavatar/#flet.CircleAvatar.background_image_src) `**(`[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)  | [bytes](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#bytes)  | None`) –
    
    The source (local asset file or URL) of the background image in the circle.
    
*   **`[bgcolor](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/circleavatar/#flet.CircleAvatar.bgcolor) `**(`[ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None`) –
    
    The color with which to fill the circle.
    
*   **`[color](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/circleavatar/#flet.CircleAvatar.color) `**(`[ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None`) –
    
    The default color for text in this avatar.
    
*   **`[content](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/circleavatar/#flet.CircleAvatar.content) `**(`[StrOrControl](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.StrOrControl)  | None`) –
    
    The content of this avatar.
    
*   **`[foreground_image_src](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/circleavatar/#flet.CircleAvatar.foreground_image_src) `**(`[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)  | [bytes](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#bytes)  | None`) –
    
    The source (local asset file or URL) of the foreground image in the circle.
    
*   **`[max_radius](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/circleavatar/#flet.CircleAvatar.max_radius) `**(`[Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None`) –
    
    The maximum size of the avatar, expressed as the radius (half the diameter).
    
*   **`[min_radius](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/circleavatar/#flet.CircleAvatar.min_radius) `**(`[Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None`) –
    
    The minimum size of the avatar, expressed as the radius (half the diameter).
    
*   **`[radius](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/circleavatar/#flet.CircleAvatar.radius) `**(`[Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None`) –
    
    The size of the avatar, expressed as the radius (half the diameter).
    

Events

*   **`[on_image_error](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/circleavatar/#flet.CircleAvatar.on_image_error) `**(`[ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[CircleAvatar](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/circleavatar/#flet.CircleAvatar) ] | None`) –
    
    Called when an error occurs while loading the [`background_image_src`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/circleavatar/#flet.CircleAvatar.background_image_src)
     or [`foreground_image_src`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/circleavatar/#flet.CircleAvatar.foreground_image_src)
    .
    

Examples[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/circleavatar/#examples "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[Live example](https://flet-controls-gallery.fly.dev/displays/circleavatar)

### User avatars[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/circleavatar/#user-avatars "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/circleavatar/#__codelineno-0-1) import flet as ft [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/circleavatar/#__codelineno-0-2) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/circleavatar/#__codelineno-0-3)[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/circleavatar/#__codelineno-0-4) def main(page: ft.Page):     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/circleavatar/#__codelineno-0-5)     page.add(        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/circleavatar/#__codelineno-0-6)         # a "normal" avatar with background image        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/circleavatar/#__codelineno-0-7)         ft.CircleAvatar(            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/circleavatar/#__codelineno-0-8)             foreground_image_src="https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/5041459?s=88&v=4",            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/circleavatar/#__codelineno-0-9)             content=ft.Text("FF"),        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/circleavatar/#__codelineno-0-10)         ),        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/circleavatar/#__codelineno-0-11)         # avatar with failing foreground image and fallback text        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/circleavatar/#__codelineno-0-12)         ft.CircleAvatar(            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/circleavatar/#__codelineno-0-13)             foreground_image_src="https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/_5041459?s=88&v=4",            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/circleavatar/#__codelineno-0-14)             content=ft.Text("FF"),        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/circleavatar/#__codelineno-0-15)         ),        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/circleavatar/#__codelineno-0-16)         # avatar with icon, aka icon with inverse background        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/circleavatar/#__codelineno-0-17)         ft.CircleAvatar(content=ft.Icon(ft.Icons.ABC)),        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/circleavatar/#__codelineno-0-18)         # avatar with icon and custom colors        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/circleavatar/#__codelineno-0-19)         ft.CircleAvatar(            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/circleavatar/#__codelineno-0-20)             content=ft.Icon(ft.Icons.WARNING_ROUNDED),            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/circleavatar/#__codelineno-0-21)             color=ft.Colors.YELLOW_200,            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/circleavatar/#__codelineno-0-22)             bgcolor=ft.Colors.AMBER_700,        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/circleavatar/#__codelineno-0-23)         ),        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/circleavatar/#__codelineno-0-24)         # avatar with online status        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/circleavatar/#__codelineno-0-25)         ft.Stack(            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/circleavatar/#__codelineno-0-26)             width=40,            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/circleavatar/#__codelineno-0-27)             height=40,            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/circleavatar/#__codelineno-0-28)             controls=[                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/circleavatar/#__codelineno-0-29)                 ft.CircleAvatar(                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/circleavatar/#__codelineno-0-30)                     foreground_image_src="https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/5041459?s=88&v=4"                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/circleavatar/#__codelineno-0-31)                 ),                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/circleavatar/#__codelineno-0-32)                 ft.Container(                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/circleavatar/#__codelineno-0-33)                     content=ft.CircleAvatar(bgcolor=ft.Colors.GREEN, radius=5),                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/circleavatar/#__codelineno-0-34)                     alignment=ft.Alignment.BOTTOM_LEFT,                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/circleavatar/#__codelineno-0-35)                 ),            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/circleavatar/#__codelineno-0-36)             ],        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/circleavatar/#__codelineno-0-37)         ),    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/circleavatar/#__codelineno-0-38)     ) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/circleavatar/#__codelineno-0-39) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/circleavatar/#__codelineno-0-40)[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/circleavatar/#__codelineno-0-41) ft.run(main)`

[![user-avatars](https://docs.flet.dev/examples/controls/circle_avatar/media/user_avatars.png)](https://docs.flet.dev/examples/controls/circle_avatar/media/user_avatars.png)

Properties[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/circleavatar/#flet.CircleAvatar-properties "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### background\_image\_src `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/circleavatar/#flet.CircleAvatar.background_image_src "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/circleavatar/#__codelineno-0-1) background_image_src: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)  | [bytes](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#bytes)  | None = None`

The source (local asset file or URL) of the background image in the circle. Changing the background image will cause the avatar to animate to the new image.

If this avatar is to have the user's initials, use [`content`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/circleavatar/#flet.CircleAvatar.content)
 instead.

Typically used as a fallback image for [`foreground_image_src`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/circleavatar/#flet.CircleAvatar.foreground_image_src)
.

### bgcolor `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/circleavatar/#flet.CircleAvatar.bgcolor "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/circleavatar/#__codelineno-0-1) bgcolor: [ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None = None`

The color with which to fill the circle.

Changing the background color will cause this avatar to animate to the new color.

### color `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/circleavatar/#flet.CircleAvatar.color "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/circleavatar/#__codelineno-0-1) color: [ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None = None`

The default color for text in this avatar.

Defaults to the primary text theme color if no [`bgcolor`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/circleavatar/#flet.CircleAvatar.bgcolor)
 is specified.

### content `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/circleavatar/#flet.CircleAvatar.content "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/circleavatar/#__codelineno-0-1) content: [StrOrControl](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.StrOrControl)  | None = None`

The content of this avatar.

Typically a [`Text`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/text/#flet.Text)
 control.

Tip

If this avatar is to have an image, use [`background_image_src`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/circleavatar/#flet.CircleAvatar.background_image_src)
 instead.

### foreground\_image\_src `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/circleavatar/#flet.CircleAvatar.foreground_image_src "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/circleavatar/#__codelineno-0-1) foreground_image_src: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)  | [bytes](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#bytes)  | None = None`

The source (local asset file or URL) of the foreground image in the circle.

Fallbacks to [`background_image_src`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/circleavatar/#flet.CircleAvatar.background_image_src)
.

Typically used as profile image.

### max\_radius `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/circleavatar/#flet.CircleAvatar.max_radius "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/circleavatar/#__codelineno-0-1) max_radius: [Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None = None`

The maximum size of the avatar, expressed as the radius (half the diameter). If set to a non `None` value, then [`radius`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/circleavatar/#flet.CircleAvatar.radius)
 must be `None` (default).

Defaults to `float('inf')` i.e. infinity.

Raises:

*   `[ValueError](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ValueError) ` –
    
    If it is not greater than or equal to `0.0`.
    
*   `[ValueError](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ValueError) ` –
    
    If it is set while [`radius`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/circleavatar/#flet.CircleAvatar.radius)
     is set.
    

### min\_radius `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/circleavatar/#flet.CircleAvatar.min_radius "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/circleavatar/#__codelineno-0-1) min_radius: [Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None = None`

The minimum size of the avatar, expressed as the radius (half the diameter). If set to a non `None` value, then [`radius`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/circleavatar/#flet.CircleAvatar.radius)
 must be `None` (default).

Defaults to `0.0`.

Raises:

*   `[ValueError](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ValueError) ` –
    
    If it is not greater than or equal to `0.0`.
    
*   `[ValueError](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ValueError) ` –
    
    If it is set while [`radius`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/circleavatar/#flet.CircleAvatar.radius)
     is set.
    

### radius `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/circleavatar/#flet.CircleAvatar.radius "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/circleavatar/#__codelineno-0-1) radius: [Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None = None`

The size of the avatar, expressed as the radius (half the diameter).

If set to a non `None` value, then neither [`min_radius`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/circleavatar/#flet.CircleAvatar.min_radius)
 nor [`max_radius`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/circleavatar/#flet.CircleAvatar.max_radius)
 may be specified.

Raises:

*   `[ValueError](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ValueError) ` –
    
    If it is not greater than or equal to `0`.
    
*   `[ValueError](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ValueError) ` –
    
    If it is set while [`min_radius`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/circleavatar/#flet.CircleAvatar.min_radius)
     or [`max_radius`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/circleavatar/#flet.CircleAvatar.max_radius)
     is set.
    

Events[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/circleavatar/#flet.CircleAvatar-events "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### on\_image\_error `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/circleavatar/#flet.CircleAvatar.on_image_error "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/circleavatar/#__codelineno-0-1) on_image_error: [ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[CircleAvatar](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/circleavatar/#flet.CircleAvatar) ] | None = (     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/circleavatar/#__codelineno-0-2)     None [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/circleavatar/#__codelineno-0-3) )`

Called when an error occurs while loading the [`background_image_src`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/circleavatar/#flet.CircleAvatar.background_image_src)
 or [`foreground_image_src`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/circleavatar/#flet.CircleAvatar.foreground_image_src)
.

The [`data`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/event/#flet.Event.data)
 property of the event handler argument is a string whose value is either `"background"` or `"foreground"` indicating the error's origin.

Back to top

---

# BottomAppBar - Flet

 

[Skip to content](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/bottomappbar/#flet.BottomAppBar)

BottomAppBar
============

A material design bottom app bar.

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/bottomappbar/#__codelineno-0-1) ft.BottomAppBar(     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/bottomappbar/#__codelineno-0-2)     bgcolor=ft.Colors.SURFACE_CONTAINER_LOW,    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/bottomappbar/#__codelineno-0-3)     content=ft.Row(        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/bottomappbar/#__codelineno-0-4)         alignment=ft.MainAxisAlignment.SPACE_AROUND,        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/bottomappbar/#__codelineno-0-5)         controls=[            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/bottomappbar/#__codelineno-0-6)             ft.IconButton(ft.Icons.MENU),            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/bottomappbar/#__codelineno-0-7)             ft.IconButton(ft.Icons.SEARCH),            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/bottomappbar/#__codelineno-0-8)             ft.IconButton(ft.Icons.SETTINGS),        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/bottomappbar/#__codelineno-0-9)         ],    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/bottomappbar/#__codelineno-0-10)     ), [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/bottomappbar/#__codelineno-0-11) )`

[![BottomAppBar](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/material/golden/macos/bottom_app_bar/image_for_docs.png)](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/material/golden/macos/bottom_app_bar/image_for_docs.png)

Basic BottomAppBar

Inherits: `[LayoutControl](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/layoutcontrol/#flet.LayoutControl) `

Properties

*   **`[bgcolor](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/bottomappbar/#flet.BottomAppBar.bgcolor) `**(`[ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None`) –
    
    The fill color to use for this app bar.
    
*   **`[border_radius](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/bottomappbar/#flet.BottomAppBar.border_radius) `**(`[BorderRadiusValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.BorderRadiusValue)  | None`) –
    
    The border radius to apply when clipping and painting this app bar.
    
*   **`[clip_behavior](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/bottomappbar/#flet.BottomAppBar.clip_behavior) `**(`[ClipBehavior](https://docs.flet.dev/types/clipbehavior/#flet.ClipBehavior)  | None`) –
    
    Defines how the [`content`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/bottomappbar/#flet.BottomAppBar.content)
     of this app bar should be clipped.
    
*   **`[content](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/bottomappbar/#flet.BottomAppBar.content) `**(`[Control](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control)  | None`) –
    
    The content of this bottom app bar.
    
*   **`[elevation](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/bottomappbar/#flet.BottomAppBar.elevation) `**(`[Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None`) –
    
    The z-coordinate at which to place this bottom app bar relative to its parent. It controls the size of the shadow below this app bar.
    
*   **`[notch_margin](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/bottomappbar/#flet.BottomAppBar.notch_margin) `**(`[Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number) `) –
    
    The margin between the [`FloatingActionButton`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#flet.FloatingActionButton)
     and this app bar's notch.
    
*   **`[padding](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/bottomappbar/#flet.BottomAppBar.padding) `**(`[PaddingValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.PaddingValue)  | None`) –
    
    Empty space to inscribe inside a container decoration (background, border).
    
*   **`[shadow_color](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/bottomappbar/#flet.BottomAppBar.shadow_color) `**(`[ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None`) –
    
    The color of the shadow below this app bar.
    
*   **`[shape](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/bottomappbar/#flet.BottomAppBar.shape) `**(`[NotchShape](https://docs.flet.dev/types/notchshape/#flet.NotchShape)  | None`) –
    
    The notch that is made for the floating action button.
    

Examples[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/bottomappbar/#examples "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[Live example](https://flet-controls-gallery.fly.dev/navigation/bottomappbar)

### Notched `FloatingActionButton`[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/bottomappbar/#notched-floatingactionbutton "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/bottomappbar/#__codelineno-0-1) import flet as ft [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/bottomappbar/#__codelineno-0-2) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/bottomappbar/#__codelineno-0-3)[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/bottomappbar/#__codelineno-0-4) def main(page: ft.Page):     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/bottomappbar/#__codelineno-0-5)     page.vertical_alignment = ft.MainAxisAlignment.CENTER    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/bottomappbar/#__codelineno-0-6)     page.horizontal_alignment = ft.CrossAxisAlignment.CENTER [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/bottomappbar/#__codelineno-0-7)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/bottomappbar/#__codelineno-0-8)     page.floating_action_button = ft.FloatingActionButton(        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/bottomappbar/#__codelineno-0-9)         icon=ft.Icons.ADD,        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/bottomappbar/#__codelineno-0-10)         shape=ft.CircleBorder(),    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/bottomappbar/#__codelineno-0-11)     )    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/bottomappbar/#__codelineno-0-12)     page.floating_action_button_location = ft.FloatingActionButtonLocation.CENTER_DOCKED [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/bottomappbar/#__codelineno-0-13)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/bottomappbar/#__codelineno-0-14)     page.bottom_appbar = ft.BottomAppBar( shape=ft.CircularRectangleNotchShape(),        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/bottomappbar/#__codelineno-0-17)         content=ft.Row( [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/bottomappbar/#__codelineno-0-28) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/bottomappbar/#__codelineno-0-29)[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/bottomappbar/#__codelineno-0-30) if __name__ == "__main__":     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/bottomappbar/#__codelineno-0-31)     ft.run(main)`

ft.IconButton(icon=ft.Icons.MENU, icon_color=ft.Colors.WHITE),                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/bottomappbar/#__codelineno-1-14)                 ft.Container(expand=True), [![border_radius.png](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/material/golden/macos/bottom_app_bar/border_radius.png)](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/material/golden/macos/bottom_app_bar/border_radius.png)

Properties[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/bottomappbar/#flet.BottomAppBar-properties "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### bgcolor `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/bottomappbar/#flet.BottomAppBar.bgcolor "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/bottomappbar/#__codelineno-0-1) bgcolor: [ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None = None`

The fill color to use for this app bar.

If `None`, [`BottomAppBarTheme.bgcolor`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/bottomappbartheme/#flet.BottomAppBarTheme.bgcolor)
 is used; if that is also `None`, then defaults to [`ColorScheme.surface`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/colorscheme/#flet.ColorScheme.surface)
.

### border\_radius `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/bottomappbar/#flet.BottomAppBar.border_radius "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/bottomappbar/#__codelineno-0-1) border_radius: [BorderRadiusValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.BorderRadiusValue)  | None = None`

The border radius to apply when clipping and painting this app bar.

### clip\_behavior `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/bottomappbar/#flet.BottomAppBar.clip_behavior "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/bottomappbar/#__codelineno-0-1) clip_behavior: [ClipBehavior](https://docs.flet.dev/types/clipbehavior/#flet.ClipBehavior)  | None = None`

Defines how the [`content`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/bottomappbar/#flet.BottomAppBar.content)
 of this app bar should be clipped.

If `None`, defaults to: - [`ClipBehavior.ANTI_ALIAS`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/clipbehavior/#flet.ClipBehavior.ANTI_ALIAS)
 if [`border_radius`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/bottomappbar/#flet.BottomAppBar.border_radius)
 is set and not equal to [`BorderRadius.all(0)`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/borderradius/#flet.BorderRadius.all)
; - Else [`ClipBehavior.NONE`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/clipbehavior/#flet.ClipBehavior.NONE)
.

### content `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/bottomappbar/#flet.BottomAppBar.content "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/bottomappbar/#__codelineno-0-1) content: [Control](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control)  | None = None`

The content of this bottom app bar.

### elevation `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/bottomappbar/#flet.BottomAppBar.elevation "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/bottomappbar/#__codelineno-0-1) elevation: [Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None = None`

The z-coordinate at which to place this bottom app bar relative to its parent. It controls the size of the shadow below this app bar.

If `None`, [`BottomAppBarTheme.elevation`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/bottomappbartheme/#flet.BottomAppBarTheme.elevation)
 is used; if that is also `None`, then defaults to `3`.

Raises:

*   `[ValueError](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ValueError) ` –
    
    If it is not greater than or equal to `0`.
    

### notch\_margin `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/bottomappbar/#flet.BottomAppBar.notch_margin "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/bottomappbar/#__codelineno-0-1) notch_margin: [Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  = 4.0`

The margin between the [`FloatingActionButton`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#flet.FloatingActionButton)
 and this app bar's notch.

Note

Has effect only if [`shape`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/bottomappbar/#flet.BottomAppBar.shape)
 is not `None`.

### padding `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/bottomappbar/#flet.BottomAppBar.padding "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/bottomappbar/#__codelineno-0-1) padding: [PaddingValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.PaddingValue)  | None = None`

Empty space to inscribe inside a container decoration (background, border).

If `None`, [`BottomAppBarTheme.padding`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/bottomappbartheme/#flet.BottomAppBarTheme.padding)
 is used; if that is also `None`, then defaults to `Padding.symmetric(vertical=12.0, horizontal=16.0)`.

### shadow\_color `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/bottomappbar/#flet.BottomAppBar.shadow_color "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/bottomappbar/#__codelineno-0-1) shadow_color: [ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None = None`

The color of the shadow below this app bar.

If `None`, [`BottomAppBarTheme.shadow_color`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/bottomappbartheme/#flet.BottomAppBarTheme.shadow_color)
 is used; if that is also `None`, then defaults to [`Colors.TRANSPARENT`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/colors/#flet.Colors.TRANSPARENT)
.

### shape `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/bottomappbar/#flet.BottomAppBar.shape "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/bottomappbar/#__codelineno-0-1) shape: [NotchShape](https://docs.flet.dev/types/notchshape/#flet.NotchShape)  | None = None`

The notch that is made for the floating action button.

If `None`, [`BottomAppBarTheme.shape`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/bottomappbartheme/#flet.BottomAppBarTheme.shape)
 is used; if that is also `None`, then the shape will be rectangular with no notch.

Back to top

# Color pickers - Flet

 

[Skip to content](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/#color-pickers)

Color pickers[#](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/#color-pickers "Permanent link")

=====================================================================================

Multiple color picker controls built on Flutter's [`flutter_colorpicker`](https://pub.dev/packages/flutter_colorpicker)
 package.

Usage[#](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/#usage "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------

Add `flet-color-pickers` to your project dependencies:

uvpip

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/#__codelineno-0-1) uv add flet-color-pickers`

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/#__codelineno-1-1) pip install flet-color-pickers  # (1)!`

1.  After this, you will have to manually add this package to your `requirements.txt` or `pyproject.toml`.

Available color pickers[#](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/#available-color-pickers "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   [ColorPicker](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/colorpicker/)
    
*   [BlockPicker](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/blockpicker/)
    
*   [HueRingPicker](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/hueringpicker/)
    
*   [MaterialPicker](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/materialpicker/)
    
*   [MultipleChoiceBlockPicker](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/multiplechoiceblockpicker/)
    
*   [SlidePicker](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/slidepicker/)

# CupertinoActivityIndicator - Flet

 

[Skip to content](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinoactivityindicator/#flet.CupertinoActivityIndicator)

CupertinoActivityIndicator
==========================

An iOS-style activity indicator that spins clockwise.

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinoactivityindicator/#__codelineno-0-1) ft.CupertinoActivityIndicator(     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinoactivityindicator/#__codelineno-0-2)     radius=30,    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinoactivityindicator/#__codelineno-0-3)     color=ft.CupertinoColors.DARK_BACKGROUND_GRAY, [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinoactivityindicator/#__codelineno-0-4) )`

[![CupertinoActivityIndicator](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/cupertino/golden/macos/cupertino_activity_indicator/image_for_docs.png)](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/cupertino/golden/macos/cupertino_activity_indicator/image_for_docs.png)

Basic CupertinoActivityIndicator

Inherits: `[LayoutControl](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/layoutcontrol/#flet.LayoutControl) `

Properties

*   **`[animating](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinoactivityindicator/#flet.CupertinoActivityIndicator.animating) `**(`[bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool) `) –
    
    Whether this indicator is running its animation.
    
*   **`[color](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinoactivityindicator/#flet.CupertinoActivityIndicator.color) `**(`[ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None`) –
    
    Defines the color of this indicator.
    
*   **`[progress](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinoactivityindicator/#flet.CupertinoActivityIndicator.progress) `**(`[Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None`) –
    
    Determines the percentage of spinner ticks that will be shown.
    
*   **`[radius](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinoactivityindicator/#flet.CupertinoActivityIndicator.radius) `**(`[Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number) `) –
    
    The radius of this indicator.
    

Examples[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinoactivityindicator/#examples "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[Live example](https://flet-controls-gallery.fly.dev/displays/cupertinoactivityindicator)

### Basic Example[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinoactivityindicator/#basic-example "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinoactivityindicator/#__codelineno-0-1) import flet as ft [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinoactivityindicator/#__codelineno-0-2) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinoactivityindicator/#__codelineno-0-3)[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinoactivityindicator/#__codelineno-0-4) def main(page: ft.Page):     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinoactivityindicator/#__codelineno-0-5)     page.add(        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinoactivityindicator/#__codelineno-0-6)         ft.CupertinoActivityIndicator(            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinoactivityindicator/#__codelineno-0-7)             animating=True,            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinoactivityindicator/#__codelineno-0-8)             color=ft.Colors.RED,            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinoactivityindicator/#__codelineno-0-9)             radius=50,        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinoactivityindicator/#__codelineno-0-10)         )    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinoactivityindicator/#__codelineno-0-11)     ) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinoactivityindicator/#__codelineno-0-12) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinoactivityindicator/#__codelineno-0-13)[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinoactivityindicator/#__codelineno-0-14) ft.run(main)`

[![basic](https://docs.flet.dev/examples/controls/cupertino_activity_indicator/media/basic.png)](https://docs.flet.dev/examples/controls/cupertino_activity_indicator/media/basic.png)

Properties[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinoactivityindicator/#flet.CupertinoActivityIndicator-properties "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### animating `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinoactivityindicator/#flet.CupertinoActivityIndicator.animating "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinoactivityindicator/#__codelineno-0-1) animating: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)  = True`

Whether this indicator is running its animation.

Note

Has no effect if [`progress`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinoactivityindicator/#flet.CupertinoActivityIndicator.progress)
 is not `None`.

### color `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinoactivityindicator/#flet.CupertinoActivityIndicator.color "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinoactivityindicator/#__codelineno-0-1) color: [ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None = None`

Defines the color of this indicator.

### progress `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinoactivityindicator/#flet.CupertinoActivityIndicator.progress "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinoactivityindicator/#__codelineno-0-1) progress: [Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None = None`

Determines the percentage of spinner ticks that will be shown.

Typical usage would display all ticks, however, this allows for more fine-grained control such as during pull-to-refresh when the drag-down action shows one tick at a time as the user continues to drag down.

Note

If not `None`, then [`animating`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinoactivityindicator/#flet.CupertinoActivityIndicator.animating)
 will be ignored.

Raises:

*   `[ValueError](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ValueError) ` –
    
    If it is not between `0.0` and `1.0`, inclusive.
    

### radius `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinoactivityindicator/#flet.CupertinoActivityIndicator.radius "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinoactivityindicator/#__codelineno-0-1) radius: [Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  = 10`

The radius of this indicator.

Raises:

*   `[ValueError](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ValueError) ` –
    
    If it is not strictly greater than `0`.
    

Back to top

# CupertinoContextMenuAction - Flet

 

[Skip to content](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinocontextmenuaction/#flet.CupertinoContextMenuAction)

CupertinoContextMenuAction
==========================

Inherits: `[AdaptiveControl](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/adaptivecontrol/#flet.AdaptiveControl) `

A cupertino context menu action.

Typically used as a child of [`CupertinoContextMenu.actions`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinocontextmenu/#flet.CupertinoContextMenu.actions)
.

Properties

*   **`[content](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinocontextmenuaction/#flet.CupertinoContextMenuAction.content) `**(`[StrOrControl](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.StrOrControl) `) –
    
    The content of this action button.
    
*   **`[default](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinocontextmenuaction/#flet.CupertinoContextMenuAction.default) `**(`[bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool) `) –
    
    Whether this action should receive the style of an emphasized, default action.
    
*   **`[destructive](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinocontextmenuaction/#flet.CupertinoContextMenuAction.destructive) `**(`[bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool) `) –
    
    Whether this action should receive the style of a destructive action.
    
*   **`[trailing_icon](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinocontextmenuaction/#flet.CupertinoContextMenuAction.trailing_icon) `**(`[IconData](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.IconData)  | None`) –
    
    An icon to display at the right of the [`content`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinocontextmenuaction/#flet.CupertinoContextMenuAction.content)
     control.
    

Events

*   **`[on_click](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinocontextmenuaction/#flet.CupertinoContextMenuAction.on_click) `**(`[ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[CupertinoContextMenuAction](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinocontextmenuaction/#flet.CupertinoContextMenuAction) ] | None`) –
    
    Called when this action button is clicked.
    

Properties[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinocontextmenuaction/#flet.CupertinoContextMenuAction-properties "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### content `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinocontextmenuaction/#flet.CupertinoContextMenuAction.content "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinocontextmenuaction/#__codelineno-0-1) content: [StrOrControl](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.StrOrControl)`

The content of this action button.

Raises:

*   `[ValueError](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ValueError) ` –
    
    If it is neither a string nor a visible `Control`.
    

### default `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinocontextmenuaction/#flet.CupertinoContextMenuAction.default "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinocontextmenuaction/#__codelineno-0-1) default: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)  = False`

Whether this action should receive the style of an emphasized, default action.

### destructive `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinocontextmenuaction/#flet.CupertinoContextMenuAction.destructive "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinocontextmenuaction/#__codelineno-0-1) destructive: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)  = False`

Whether this action should receive the style of a destructive action.

### trailing\_icon `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinocontextmenuaction/#flet.CupertinoContextMenuAction.trailing_icon "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinocontextmenuaction/#__codelineno-0-1) trailing_icon: [IconData](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.IconData)  | None = None`

An icon to display at the right of the [`content`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinocontextmenuaction/#flet.CupertinoContextMenuAction.content)
 control.

Events[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinocontextmenuaction/#flet.CupertinoContextMenuAction-events "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### on\_click `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinocontextmenuaction/#flet.CupertinoContextMenuAction.on_click "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinocontextmenuaction/#__codelineno-0-1) on_click: (     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinocontextmenuaction/#__codelineno-0-2)     [ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[CupertinoContextMenuAction](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinocontextmenuaction/#flet.CupertinoContextMenuAction) ] | None [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinocontextmenuaction/#__codelineno-0-3) ) = None`

Called when this action button is clicked.

# Divider - Flet

 

[Skip to content](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/divider/#flet.Divider)

Divider
=======

A thin horizontal line (divider), with padding on either side.

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/divider/#__codelineno-0-1) ft.Column(     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/divider/#__codelineno-0-2)     width=240,    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/divider/#__codelineno-0-3)     spacing=10,    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/divider/#__codelineno-0-4)     controls=[        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/divider/#__codelineno-0-5)         ft.Text("Section A", weight=ft.FontWeight.W_600),        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/divider/#__codelineno-0-6)         ft.Divider(),        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/divider/#__codelineno-0-7)         ft.Text("Section B"),    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/divider/#__codelineno-0-8)     ], [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/divider/#__codelineno-0-9) )`

[![Divider](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/material/golden/macos/divider/image_for_docs.png)](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/material/golden/macos/divider/image_for_docs.png)

Basic Divider

Inherits: `[Control](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control) `

Properties

*   **`[color](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/divider/#flet.Divider.color) `**(`[ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None`) –
    
    The color to use when painting the line.
    
*   **`[height](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/divider/#flet.Divider.height) `**(`[Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None`) –
    
    The divider's height extent. The divider itself is always drawn as a horizontal line that is centered within the height specified by this value.
    
*   **`[leading_indent](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/divider/#flet.Divider.leading_indent) `**(`[Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None`) –
    
    The amount of empty space to the leading edge of the divider.
    
*   **`[radius](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/divider/#flet.Divider.radius) `**(`[BorderRadiusValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.BorderRadiusValue)  | None`) –
    
    The border radius of the divider.
    
*   **`[thickness](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/divider/#flet.Divider.thickness) `**(`[Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None`) –
    
    The thickness of the line drawn within the divider.
    
*   **`[trailing_indent](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/divider/#flet.Divider.trailing_indent) `**(`[Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None`) –
    
    The amount of empty space to the trailing edge of the divider.
    

Examples[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/divider/#examples "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[Live example](https://flet-controls-gallery.fly.dev/layout/divider)

### Basic Example[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/divider/#basic-example "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/divider/#__codelineno-0-1) import flet as ft [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/divider/#__codelineno-0-2) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/divider/#__codelineno-0-3)[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/divider/#__codelineno-0-4) def main(page: ft.Page):     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/divider/#__codelineno-0-5)     page.add(        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/divider/#__codelineno-0-6)         ft.Column(            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/divider/#__codelineno-0-7)             spacing=0,            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/divider/#__codelineno-0-8)             expand=True,            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/divider/#__codelineno-0-9)             controls=[                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/divider/#__codelineno-0-10)                 ft.Container(                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/divider/#__codelineno-0-11)                     expand=True,                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/divider/#__codelineno-0-12)                     bgcolor=ft.Colors.AMBER,                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/divider/#__codelineno-0-13)                     alignment=ft.Alignment.CENTER,                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/divider/#__codelineno-0-14)                 ),                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/divider/#__codelineno-0-15)                 ft.Divider(),                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/divider/#__codelineno-0-16)                 ft.Container(                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/divider/#__codelineno-0-17)                     expand=True,                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/divider/#__codelineno-0-18)                     bgcolor=ft.Colors.PINK,                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/divider/#__codelineno-0-19)                     alignment=ft.Alignment.CENTER,                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/divider/#__codelineno-0-20)                 ),                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/divider/#__codelineno-0-21)                 ft.Divider(height=1, color=ft.Colors.WHITE),                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/divider/#__codelineno-0-22)                 ft.Container(                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/divider/#__codelineno-0-23)                     expand=True,                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/divider/#__codelineno-0-24)                     bgcolor=ft.Colors.BLUE_300,                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/divider/#__codelineno-0-25)                     alignment=ft.Alignment.CENTER,                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/divider/#__codelineno-0-26)                 ),                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/divider/#__codelineno-0-27)                 ft.Divider(height=9, thickness=3),                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/divider/#__codelineno-0-28)                 ft.Container(                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/divider/#__codelineno-0-29)                     expand=True,                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/divider/#__codelineno-0-30)                     bgcolor=ft.Colors.DEEP_PURPLE_200,                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/divider/#__codelineno-0-31)                     alignment=ft.Alignment.CENTER,                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/divider/#__codelineno-0-32)                 ),            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/divider/#__codelineno-0-33)             ],        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/divider/#__codelineno-0-34)         ),    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/divider/#__codelineno-0-35)     ) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/divider/#__codelineno-0-36) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/divider/#__codelineno-0-37)[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/divider/#__codelineno-0-38) ft.run(main)`

[![basic](https://docs.flet.dev/examples/controls/divider/media/basic.png)](https://docs.flet.dev/examples/controls/divider/media/basic.png)

Properties[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/divider/#flet.Divider-properties "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### color `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/divider/#flet.Divider.color "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/divider/#__codelineno-0-1) color: [ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None = None`

The color to use when painting the line.

If `None`, [`DividerTheme.color`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/dividertheme/#flet.DividerTheme.color)
 is used.

### height `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/divider/#flet.Divider.height "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/divider/#__codelineno-0-1) height: [Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None = None`

The divider's height extent. The divider itself is always drawn as a horizontal line that is centered within the height specified by this value.

If `None`, [`DividerTheme.space`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/dividertheme/#flet.DividerTheme.space)
 is used. If that's is also `None`, defaults to `16.0`.

Raises:

*   `[ValueError](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ValueError) ` –
    
    If [`height`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/divider/#flet.Divider.height)
     is negative.
    

### leading\_indent `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/divider/#flet.Divider.leading_indent "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/divider/#__codelineno-0-1) leading_indent: [Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None = None`

The amount of empty space to the leading edge of the divider.

If `None`, [`DividerTheme.leading_indent`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/dividertheme/#flet.DividerTheme.leading_indent)
 is used. If that's is also `None`, defaults to `0.0`.

Raises:

*   `[ValueError](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ValueError) ` –
    
    If it is not greater than or equal to `0`.
    

### radius `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/divider/#flet.Divider.radius "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/divider/#__codelineno-0-1) radius: [BorderRadiusValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.BorderRadiusValue)  | None = None`

The border radius of the divider.

### thickness `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/divider/#flet.Divider.thickness "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/divider/#__codelineno-0-1) thickness: [Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None = None`

The thickness of the line drawn within the divider.

A divider with a thickness of `0.0` is always drawn as a line with a height of exactly one device pixel.

If `None`, [`DividerTheme.thickness`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/dividertheme/#flet.DividerTheme.thickness)
 is used. If that is also `None`, defaults to `0.0`.

Raises:

*   `[ValueError](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ValueError) ` –
    
    If [`thickness`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/divider/#flet.Divider.thickness)
     is negative.
    

### trailing\_indent `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/divider/#flet.Divider.trailing_indent "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/divider/#__codelineno-0-1) trailing_indent: [Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None = None`

The amount of empty space to the trailing edge of the divider.

If `None`, [`DividerTheme.trailing_indent`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/dividertheme/#flet.DividerTheme.trailing_indent)
 is used. If that is also `None`, defaults to `0.0`.

Raises:

*   `[ValueError](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ValueError) ` –
    
    If it is not greater than or equal to `0`.
    

Back to top

# DragTarget - Flet

 

[Skip to content](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/dragtarget/#examples)

DragTarget
==========

Examples[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/dragtarget/#examples "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------

See [these](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/draggable/#examples)
.

Inherits: `[Control](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control) `

A control that completes drag operation when a [`Draggable`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/draggable/#flet.Draggable)
 control is dropped.

When a `Draggable` is dragged on top of a `DragTarget`, the `DragTarget` is asked whether it will accept the data the `Draggable` is carrying. The `DragTarget` will accept incoming drag if it belongs to the same `group` as `Draggable`. If the user does drop the `Draggable` on top of the `DragTarget` (and the `DragTarget` has indicated that it will accept the `Draggable`'s data), then the `DragTarget` is asked to accept the `Draggable`'s data.

Properties

*   **`[content](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/dragtarget/#flet.DragTarget.content) `**(`[Control](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control) `) –
    
    The content of this control.
    
*   **`[group](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/dragtarget/#flet.DragTarget.group) `**(`[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str) `) –
    
    The group this target belongs to.
    

Events

*   **`[on_accept](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/dragtarget/#flet.DragTarget.on_accept) `**(`[EventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.EventHandler) [[DragTargetEvent](https://docs.flet.dev/types/dragtargetevent/#flet.DragTargetEvent) ] | None`) –
    
    Called when the user does drop an acceptable (same [`group`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/dragtarget/#flet.DragTarget.group)
    ) draggable on this target.
    
*   **`[on_leave](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/dragtarget/#flet.DragTarget.on_leave) `**(`[EventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.EventHandler) [[DragTargetLeaveEvent](https://docs.flet.dev/types/dragtargetleaveevent/#flet.DragTargetLeaveEvent) ] | None`) –
    
    Called when a draggable leaves this target.
    
*   **`[on_move](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/dragtarget/#flet.DragTarget.on_move) `**(`[EventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.EventHandler) [[DragTargetEvent](https://docs.flet.dev/types/dragtargetevent/#flet.DragTargetEvent) ] | None`) –
    
    Called when a draggable moves within this target.
    
*   **`[on_will_accept](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/dragtarget/#flet.DragTarget.on_will_accept) `**(`[EventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.EventHandler) [[DragWillAcceptEvent](https://docs.flet.dev/types/dragwillacceptevent/#flet.DragWillAcceptEvent) ] | None`) –
    
    Called when a draggable is dragged on this target.
    

Properties[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/dragtarget/#flet.DragTarget-properties "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### content `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/dragtarget/#flet.DragTarget.content "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/dragtarget/#__codelineno-0-1) content: [Control](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control)`

The content of this control.

Raises:

*   `[ValueError](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ValueError) ` –
    
    If it is not visible.
    

### group `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/dragtarget/#flet.DragTarget.group "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/dragtarget/#__codelineno-0-1) group: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)  = 'default'`

The group this target belongs to.

Note

For a `DragTarget` to accept an incoming drop from a [`Draggable`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/draggable/#flet.Draggable)
, they must both be in the same `group`.

Events[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/dragtarget/#flet.DragTarget-events "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### on\_accept `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/dragtarget/#flet.DragTarget.on_accept "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/dragtarget/#__codelineno-0-1) on_accept: [EventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.EventHandler) [[DragTargetEvent](https://docs.flet.dev/types/dragtargetevent/#flet.DragTargetEvent) ] | None = None`

Called when the user does drop an acceptable (same [`group`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/dragtarget/#flet.DragTarget.group)
) draggable on this target.

Use `page.get_control(e.src_id)` to retrieve Control reference by its ID.

### on\_leave `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/dragtarget/#flet.DragTarget.on_leave "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/dragtarget/#__codelineno-0-1) on_leave: [EventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.EventHandler) [[DragTargetLeaveEvent](https://docs.flet.dev/types/dragtargetleaveevent/#flet.DragTargetLeaveEvent) ] | None = None`

Called when a draggable leaves this target.

### on\_move `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/dragtarget/#flet.DragTarget.on_move "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/dragtarget/#__codelineno-0-1) on_move: [EventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.EventHandler) [[DragTargetEvent](https://docs.flet.dev/types/dragtargetevent/#flet.DragTargetEvent) ] | None = None`

Called when a draggable moves within this target.

### on\_will\_accept `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/dragtarget/#flet.DragTarget.on_will_accept "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/dragtarget/#__codelineno-0-1) on_will_accept: [EventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.EventHandler) [[DragWillAcceptEvent](https://docs.flet.dev/types/dragwillacceptevent/#flet.DragWillAcceptEvent) ] | None = (     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/dragtarget/#__codelineno-0-2)     None [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/dragtarget/#__codelineno-0-3) )`

Called when a draggable is dragged on this target.

Back to top

# FletApp - Flet

 

[Skip to content](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/fletapp/#flet.FletApp)

FletApp
=======

Inherits: `[LayoutControl](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/layoutcontrol/#flet.LayoutControl) `

Renders another Flet app in the current app, similar to HTML IFrame, but for Flet.

Properties

*   **`[app_error_message](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/fletapp/#flet.FletApp.app_error_message) `**(`[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)  | None`) –
    
    Template message to display when the app fails to load.
    
*   **`[app_startup_screen_message](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/fletapp/#flet.FletApp.app_startup_screen_message) `**(`[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)  | None`) –
    
    Message to display on the app startup screen.
    
*   **`[args](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/fletapp/#flet.FletApp.args) `**(`[dict](https://docs.python.org/3/reference/expressions.html#dict) [[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str) , [Any](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "<code>typing.Any</code>") ] | None`) –
    
    Optional dictionary of arguments to pass to the Flet app.
    
*   **`[force_pyodide](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/fletapp/#flet.FletApp.force_pyodide) `**(`[bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool) `) –
    
    Whether to force the use of Pyodide.
    
*   **`[reconnect_interval_ms](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/fletapp/#flet.FletApp.reconnect_interval_ms) `**(`[int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int)  | None`) –
    
    Delay, in milliseconds, between reconnection attempts.
    
*   **`[reconnect_timeout_ms](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/fletapp/#flet.FletApp.reconnect_timeout_ms) `**(`[int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int)  | None`) –
    
    Total time to try reconnecting.
    
*   **`[show_app_startup_screen](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/fletapp/#flet.FletApp.show_app_startup_screen) `**(`[bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool) `) –
    
    Whether to show the app startup screen.
    
*   **`[url](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/fletapp/#flet.FletApp.url) `**(`[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)  | None`) –
    
    Flet app URL, e.g. `http://localhost:8550` or `flet.sock`.
    

Events

*   **`[on_error](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/fletapp/#flet.FletApp.on_error) `**(`[ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[FletApp](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/fletapp/#flet.FletApp) ] | None`) –
    
    Called when a connection or any unhandled error occurs.
    

Properties[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/fletapp/#flet.FletApp-properties "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### app\_error\_message `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/fletapp/#flet.FletApp.app_error_message "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/fletapp/#__codelineno-0-1) app_error_message: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)  | None = None`

Template message to display when the app fails to load. Use `{message}` placeholder to include the error message and `{details}` to include error details.

### app\_startup\_screen\_message `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/fletapp/#flet.FletApp.app_startup_screen_message "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/fletapp/#__codelineno-0-1) app_startup_screen_message: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)  | None = None`

Optional dictionary of arguments to pass to the Flet app.

### force\_pyodide `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/fletapp/#flet.FletApp.force_pyodide "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/fletapp/#__codelineno-0-1) force_pyodide: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)  = False`

Whether to force the use of Pyodide.

### reconnect\_interval\_ms `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/fletapp/#flet.FletApp.reconnect_interval_ms "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/fletapp/#__codelineno-0-1) reconnect_interval_ms: [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int)  | None = None`

Delay, in milliseconds, between reconnection attempts.

### reconnect\_timeout\_ms `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/fletapp/#flet.FletApp.reconnect_timeout_ms "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/fletapp/#__codelineno-0-1) reconnect_timeout_ms: [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int)  | None = None`

Total time to try reconnecting.

### show\_app\_startup\_screen `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/fletapp/#flet.FletApp.show_app_startup_screen "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/fletapp/#__codelineno-0-1) show_app_startup_screen: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)  = False`

Whether to show the app startup screen.

### url `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/fletapp/#flet.FletApp.url "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/fletapp/#__codelineno-0-1) url: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)  | None = None`

Flet app URL, e.g. `http://localhost:8550` or `flet.sock`.

Events[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/fletapp/#flet.FletApp-events "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### on\_error `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/fletapp/#flet.FletApp.on_error "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/fletapp/#__codelineno-0-1) on_error: [ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[FletApp](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/fletapp/#flet.FletApp) ] | None = None`

# OutlinedIconButton - Flet

 

[Skip to content](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/outlinediconbutton/#examples)

OutlinedIconButton
==================

Examples[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/outlinediconbutton/#examples "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

See `IconButton` [examples](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#examples)
.

Inherits: `[IconButton](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#flet.IconButton) `

An outlined variant of [`IconButton`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#flet.IconButton)
.

Outlined icon buttons are medium-emphasis buttons. They're useful when an icon button needs more emphasis than a standard icon button but less than a filled or filled tonal icon button.

Back to top

.

Inherits: `[Control](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control) `

Radio buttons let people select a single option from two or more choices.

Properties

*   **`[content](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/radiogroup/#flet.RadioGroup.content) `**(`[Control](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control) `) –

*   **`[value](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/radiogroup/#flet.RadioGroup.value) `**(`[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)  | None`) –
    
    Current value of the RadioGroup.
    

Events

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The content of the RadioGroup.

Typically a list of `Radio` controls nested in a container control, e.g. `Column`, `Row`.

Raises:

*   `[ValueError](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ValueError) ` –

# DropdownOption - Flet

 

[Skip to content](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/dropdownoption/#flet.DropdownOption)

DropdownOption
==============

Inherits: `[Control](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control) `

Represents an item in a dropdown.

Properties

*   **`[content](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/dropdownoption/#flet.DropdownOption.content) `**(`[Control](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control)  | None`) –
    
    A `Control` to display in this option. If not specified, `text` will be used as fallback, else `text` will be ignored.
    
*   **`[key](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/dropdownoption/#flet.DropdownOption.key) `**(`[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)  | None`) –
    
    Option's key.
    
*   **`[leading_icon](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/dropdownoption/#flet.DropdownOption.leading_icon) `**(`[IconDataOrControl](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.IconDataOrControl)  | None`) –
    
    An optional icon to display before the content or text.
    
*   **`[style](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/dropdownoption/#flet.DropdownOption.style) `**(`[ButtonStyle](https://docs.flet.dev/types/buttonstyle/#flet.ButtonStyle)  | None`) –
    
    Customizes this menu item's appearance.
    
*   **`[text](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/dropdownoption/#flet.DropdownOption.text) `**(`[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)  | None`) –
    
    Option's display text.
    
*   **`[trailing_icon](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/dropdownoption/#flet.DropdownOption.trailing_icon) `**(`[IconDataOrControl](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.IconDataOrControl)  | None`) –
    
    An optional icon to display after the content or text.
    

Properties[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/dropdownoption/#flet.DropdownOption-properties "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### content `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/dropdownoption/#flet.DropdownOption.content "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/dropdownoption/#__codelineno-0-1) content: [Control](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control)  | None = None`

A `Control` to display in this option. If not specified, `text` will be used as fallback, else `text` will be ignored.

### key `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/dropdownoption/#flet.DropdownOption.key "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/dropdownoption/#__codelineno-0-1) key: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)  | None = None`

If not specified [`text`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/dropdownoption/#flet.DropdownOption.text)
 will be used as fallback.

Raises:

*   `[ValueError](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ValueError) ` –

---

# AnimatedSwitcher - Flet

 

[Skip to content](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/animatedswitcher/#flet.AnimatedSwitcher)

AnimatedSwitcher
================

Used to switch between controls with an animation.

Inherits: `[LayoutControl](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/layoutcontrol/#flet.LayoutControl) `

Properties

*   **`[content](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/animatedswitcher/#flet.AnimatedSwitcher.content) `**(`[Control](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control) `) –
    
    The content to display.
    
*   **`[duration](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/animatedswitcher/#flet.AnimatedSwitcher.duration) `**(`[DurationValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.DurationValue) `) –
    
    The duration of the transition from the old [`content`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/animatedswitcher/#flet.AnimatedSwitcher.content)
     to the new one.
    
*   **`[reverse_duration](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/animatedswitcher/#flet.AnimatedSwitcher.reverse_duration) `**(`[DurationValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.DurationValue) `) –
    
    The duration of the transition from the new [`content`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/animatedswitcher/#flet.AnimatedSwitcher.content)
     to the old one.
    
*   **`[switch_in_curve](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/animatedswitcher/#flet.AnimatedSwitcher.switch_in_curve) `**(`[AnimationCurve](https://docs.flet.dev/types/animationcurve/#flet.AnimationCurve) `) –
    
    The animation curve to use when transitioning in a new [`content`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/animatedswitcher/#flet.AnimatedSwitcher.content)
    .
    
*   **`[switch_out_curve](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/animatedswitcher/#flet.AnimatedSwitcher.switch_out_curve) `**(`[AnimationCurve](https://docs.flet.dev/types/animationcurve/#flet.AnimationCurve) `) –
    
    The animation curve to use when transitioning an old [`content`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/animatedswitcher/#flet.AnimatedSwitcher.content)
     out.
    
*   **`[transition](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/animatedswitcher/#flet.AnimatedSwitcher.transition) `**(`[AnimatedSwitcherTransition](https://docs.flet.dev/types/animatedswitchertransition/#flet.AnimatedSwitcherTransition) `) –
    
    An animation type to transition between new and old [`content`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/animatedswitcher/#flet.AnimatedSwitcher.content)
    .
    

Examples[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/animatedswitcher/#examples "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[Live example](https://flet-controls-gallery.fly.dev/animations/animated_switcher)

### Animated switching between two containers with scale effect[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/animatedswitcher/#animated-switching-between-two-containers-with-scale-effect "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/animatedswitcher/#__codelineno-0-1) import flet as ft [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/animatedswitcher/#__codelineno-0-2) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/animatedswitcher/#__codelineno-0-3)[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/animatedswitcher/#__codelineno-0-4) def main(page: ft.Page):     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/animatedswitcher/#__codelineno-0-5)     c1 = ft.Container(        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/animatedswitcher/#__codelineno-0-6)         content=ft.Text("Hello!", theme_style=ft.TextThemeStyle.HEADLINE_MEDIUM),        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/animatedswitcher/#__codelineno-0-7)         alignment=ft.Alignment.CENTER,        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/animatedswitcher/#__codelineno-0-8)         width=200,        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/animatedswitcher/#__codelineno-0-9)         height=200,        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/animatedswitcher/#__codelineno-0-10)         bgcolor=ft.Colors.GREEN,    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/animatedswitcher/#__codelineno-0-11)     )    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/animatedswitcher/#__codelineno-0-12)     c2 = ft.Container(        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/animatedswitcher/#__codelineno-0-13)         content=ft.Text("Bye!", size=50),        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/animatedswitcher/#__codelineno-0-14)         alignment=ft.Alignment.CENTER,        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/animatedswitcher/#__codelineno-0-15)         width=200,        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/animatedswitcher/#__codelineno-0-16)         height=200,        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/animatedswitcher/#__codelineno-0-17)         bgcolor=ft.Colors.YELLOW,    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/animatedswitcher/#__codelineno-0-18)     )    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/animatedswitcher/#__codelineno-0-19)     switcher = ft.AnimatedSwitcher(        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/animatedswitcher/#__codelineno-0-20)         content=c1,        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/animatedswitcher/#__codelineno-0-21)         transition=ft.AnimatedSwitcherTransition.SCALE,        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/animatedswitcher/#__codelineno-0-22)         duration=500,        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/animatedswitcher/#__codelineno-0-23)         reverse_duration=100,        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/animatedswitcher/#__codelineno-0-24)         switch_in_curve=ft.AnimationCurve.BOUNCE_OUT,        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/animatedswitcher/#__codelineno-0-25)         switch_out_curve=ft.AnimationCurve.BOUNCE_IN,    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/animatedswitcher/#__codelineno-0-26)     ) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/animatedswitcher/#__codelineno-0-27)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/animatedswitcher/#__codelineno-0-28)     def scale(e):        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/animatedswitcher/#__codelineno-0-29)         switcher.content = c2 if switcher.content == c1 else c1        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/animatedswitcher/#__codelineno-0-30)         switcher.transition = ft.AnimatedSwitcherTransition.SCALE
