--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### content `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/contextmenu/#flet.ContextMenu.content "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/contextmenu/#__codelineno-0-1) content: [Control](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control)`

The child control that listens for mouse interaction.

Raises:

*   `[ValueError](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ValueError) ` –
    
    If not visible.
    

### items `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/contextmenu/#flet.ContextMenu.items "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/contextmenu/#__codelineno-0-1) items: [list](https://docs.python.org/3/glossary.html#term-list) [[PopupMenuItem](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/popupmenubutton/#flet.PopupMenuItem) ] = [field](https://docs.python.org/3/library/dataclasses.html#dataclasses.field "<code>dataclasses.field</code>") (default_factory=[list](https://docs.python.org/3/glossary.html#term-list) )`

A list of menu items to display in the context menu, when [`open()`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/contextmenu/#flet.ContextMenu.open)
 is called.

### primary\_items `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/contextmenu/#flet.ContextMenu.primary_items "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/contextmenu/#__codelineno-0-1) primary_items: [list](https://docs.python.org/3/glossary.html#term-list) [[PopupMenuItem](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/popupmenubutton/#flet.PopupMenuItem) ] = [field](https://docs.python.org/3/library/dataclasses.html#dataclasses.field "<code>dataclasses.field</code>") (     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/contextmenu/#__codelineno-0-2)     default_factory=[list](https://docs.python.org/3/glossary.html#term-list) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/contextmenu/#__codelineno-0-3) )`

A list of menu items to display in the context menu, for primary (usually left) mouse button actions.

These items are displayed when the corresponding [`primary_trigger`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/contextmenu/#flet.ContextMenu.primary_trigger)
 is activated.

### primary\_trigger `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/contextmenu/#flet.ContextMenu.primary_trigger "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/contextmenu/#__codelineno-0-1) primary_trigger: [ContextMenuTrigger](https://docs.flet.dev/types/contextmenutrigger/#flet.ContextMenuTrigger)  | None = None`

Defines a trigger mode for the display of [`primary_items`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/contextmenu/#flet.ContextMenu.primary_items)
.

If set to `None`, the trigger is disabled.

### secondary\_items `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/contextmenu/#flet.ContextMenu.secondary_items "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/contextmenu/#__codelineno-0-1) secondary_items: [list](https://docs.python.org/3/glossary.html#term-list) [[PopupMenuItem](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/popupmenubutton/#flet.PopupMenuItem) ] = [field](https://docs.python.org/3/library/dataclasses.html#dataclasses.field "<code>dataclasses.field</code>") (     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/contextmenu/#__codelineno-0-2)     default_factory=[list](https://docs.python.org/3/glossary.html#term-list) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/contextmenu/#__codelineno-0-3) )`

A list of menu items to display in the context menu for secondary (usually right) mouse button actions.

These items are displayed when the corresponding [`secondary_trigger`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/contextmenu/#flet.ContextMenu.secondary_trigger)
 is activated.

### secondary\_trigger `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/contextmenu/#flet.ContextMenu.secondary_trigger "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/contextmenu/#__codelineno-0-1) secondary_trigger: [ContextMenuTrigger](https://docs.flet.dev/types/contextmenutrigger/#flet.ContextMenuTrigger)  | None = [DOWN](https://docs.flet.dev/types/contextmenutrigger/#flet.ContextMenuTrigger.DOWN)`

Defines a trigger mode for the display of [`secondary_items`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/contextmenu/#flet.ContextMenu.secondary_items)
.

If set to `None`, the trigger is disabled.

### tertiary\_items `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/contextmenu/#flet.ContextMenu.tertiary_items "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/contextmenu/#__codelineno-0-1) tertiary_items: [list](https://docs.python.org/3/glossary.html#term-list) [[PopupMenuItem](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/popupmenubutton/#flet.PopupMenuItem) ] = [field](https://docs.python.org/3/library/dataclasses.html#dataclasses.field "<code>dataclasses.field</code>") (     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/contextmenu/#__codelineno-0-2)     default_factory=[list](https://docs.python.org/3/glossary.html#term-list) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/contextmenu/#__codelineno-0-3) )`

A list of menu items to display in the context menu for tertiary (usually middle) mouse button actions.

These items are displayed when the corresponding [`tertiary_trigger`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/contextmenu/#flet.ContextMenu.tertiary_trigger)
 is activated.

### tertiary\_trigger `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/contextmenu/#flet.ContextMenu.tertiary_trigger "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/contextmenu/#__codelineno-0-1) tertiary_trigger: [ContextMenuTrigger](https://docs.flet.dev/types/contextmenutrigger/#flet.ContextMenuTrigger)  | None = [DOWN](https://docs.flet.dev/types/contextmenutrigger/#flet.ContextMenuTrigger.DOWN)`

Defines a trigger mode for the display of [`tertiary_items`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/contextmenu/#flet.ContextMenu.tertiary_items)
.

If set to `None`, the trigger is disabled.

Events[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/contextmenu/#flet.ContextMenu-events "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### on\_dismiss `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/contextmenu/#flet.ContextMenu.on_dismiss "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/contextmenu/#__codelineno-0-1) on_dismiss: [EventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.EventHandler) [[ContextMenuDismissEvent](https://docs.flet.dev/types/contextmenudismissevent/#flet.ContextMenuDismissEvent) ] | None = (     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/contextmenu/#__codelineno-0-2)     None [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/contextmenu/#__codelineno-0-3) )`

Fires when the menu is dismissed without a selection, or when an attempt is made to open the menu but no items are available.

### on\_select `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/contextmenu/#flet.ContextMenu.on_select "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/contextmenu/#__codelineno-0-1) on_select: [EventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.EventHandler) [[ContextMenuSelectEvent](https://docs.flet.dev/types/contextmenuselectevent/#flet.ContextMenuSelectEvent) ] | None = (     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/contextmenu/#__codelineno-0-2)     None [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/contextmenu/#__codelineno-0-3) )`

Fires when a context menu item is selected.

Methods[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/contextmenu/#flet.ContextMenu-methods "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### open `async` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/contextmenu/#flet.ContextMenu.open "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/contextmenu/#__codelineno-0-1) open(     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/contextmenu/#__codelineno-0-2)     global_position: [OffsetValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.OffsetValue)  | None = None,    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/contextmenu/#__codelineno-0-3)     local_position: [OffsetValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.OffsetValue)  | None = None, [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/contextmenu/#__codelineno-0-4) ) -> None`

Opens the context menu programmatically, and displays [`items`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/contextmenu/#flet.ContextMenu.items)
.

Parameters:

*   **`global_position`** (`[OffsetValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.OffsetValue)  | None`, default: `None` ) –
    
    A global coordinate describing where the menu should appear. If omitted, `local_position` or the center of the [`content`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/contextmenu/#flet.ContextMenu.content)
     is used.
    
*   **`local_position`** (`[OffsetValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.OffsetValue)  | None`, default: `None` ) –
    
    A local coordinate relative to the [`content`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/contextmenu/#flet.ContextMenu.content)
    . When provided without `global_position`, the coordinate is translated to global space automatically.
    

Back to top

---

# ExpansionPanelList - Flet

 

[Skip to content](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#flet.ExpansionPanelList)

ExpansionPanelList
==================

A material expansion panel list that lays out its children and animates expansions.

Example:

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#__codelineno-0-1) ft.ExpansionPanelList(     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#__codelineno-0-2)     width=400,    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#__codelineno-0-3)     controls=[        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#__codelineno-0-4)         ft.ExpansionPanel(            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#__codelineno-0-5)             header=ft.Text("Details"),            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#__codelineno-0-6)             content=ft.Text("More information here"),            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#__codelineno-0-7)             expanded=True,        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#__codelineno-0-8)         ),        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#__codelineno-0-9)         ft.ExpansionPanel(            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#__codelineno-0-10)             header=ft.Text("History"),            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#__codelineno-0-11)             content=ft.Text("View previous updates"),        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#__codelineno-0-12)         ),    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#__codelineno-0-13)     ], [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#__codelineno-0-14) )`

[![ExpansionPanelList](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/material/golden/macos/expansion_panel_list/image_for_docs.png)](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/material/golden/macos/expansion_panel_list/image_for_docs.png)

Basic ExpansionPanelList

Inherits: `[LayoutControl](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/layoutcontrol/#flet.LayoutControl) `, `[ScrollableControl](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/scrollablecontrol/#flet.ScrollableControl) `

Properties

*   **`[controls](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#flet.ExpansionPanelList.controls) `**(`[list](https://docs.python.org/3/glossary.html#term-list) [[ExpansionPanel](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanel/#flet.ExpansionPanel) ]`) –
    
    A list of panels to display.
    
*   **`[divider_color](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#flet.ExpansionPanelList.divider_color) `**(`[ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None`) –
    
    The color of the divider when [`ExpansionPanel.expanded`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanel/#flet.ExpansionPanel.expanded)
     is `False`.
    
*   **`[elevation](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#flet.ExpansionPanelList.elevation) `**(`[Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number) `) –
    
    Defines the elevation of the [`controls`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#flet.ExpansionPanelList.controls)
    , when expanded.
    
*   **`[expand_icon_color](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#flet.ExpansionPanelList.expand_icon_color) `**(`[ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None`) –
    
    The color of the icon.
    
*   **`[expanded_header_padding](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#flet.ExpansionPanelList.expanded_header_padding) `**(`[PaddingValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.PaddingValue) `) –
    
    Defines the padding around the header when expanded.
    
*   **`[spacing](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#flet.ExpansionPanelList.spacing) `**(`[Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None`) –
    
    The size of the gap between the [`controls`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#flet.ExpansionPanelList.controls)
    s when expanded.
    

Events

*   **`[on_change](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#flet.ExpansionPanelList.on_change) `**(`[EventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.EventHandler) [[ExpansionPanelListChangeEvent](https://docs.flet.dev/types/expansionpanellistchangeevent/#flet.ExpansionPanelListChangeEvent) ] | None`) –
    
    Called when an item of [`controls`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#flet.ExpansionPanelList.controls)
     is expanded or collapsed.
    

Examples[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#examples "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[Live example](https://flet-controls-gallery.fly.dev/layout/expansionpanellist)

### Basic Example[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#basic-example "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#__codelineno-0-1) import flet as ft [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#__codelineno-0-2) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#__codelineno-0-3)[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#__codelineno-0-4) def main(page: ft.Page):     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#__codelineno-0-5)     page.theme_mode = ft.ThemeMode.LIGHT [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#__codelineno-0-6)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#__codelineno-0-7)     def handle_change(e: ft.Event[ft.ExpansionPanelList]):        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#__codelineno-0-8)         print(f"change on panel with index {e.data}") [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#__codelineno-0-9)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#__codelineno-0-10)     def handle_delete(e: ft.Event[ft.IconButton]):        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#__codelineno-0-11)         icon_button = e.control        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#__codelineno-0-12)         tile = icon_button.parent        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#__codelineno-0-13)         panel = tile.parent [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#__codelineno-0-14)        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#__codelineno-0-15)         panel_list.controls.remove(panel)        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#__codelineno-0-16)         page.update() [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#__codelineno-0-17)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#__codelineno-0-18)     panel_list = ft.ExpansionPanelList(        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#__codelineno-0-19)         expand_icon_color=ft.Colors.AMBER,        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#__codelineno-0-20)         elevation=8,        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#__codelineno-0-21)         divider_color=ft.Colors.AMBER,        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#__codelineno-0-22)         on_change=handle_change,        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#__codelineno-0-23)         controls=[            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#__codelineno-0-24)             ft.ExpansionPanel(                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#__codelineno-0-25)                 # has no header and content - placeholders will be used                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#__codelineno-0-26)                 bgcolor=ft.Colors.BLUE_400,                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#__codelineno-0-27)                 expanded=True,            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#__codelineno-0-28)             ),        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#__codelineno-0-29)         ],    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#__codelineno-0-30)     ) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#__codelineno-0-31)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#__codelineno-0-32)     colors = [        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#__codelineno-0-33)         ft.Colors.GREEN_500,        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#__codelineno-0-34)         ft.Colors.BLUE_800,        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#__codelineno-0-35)         ft.Colors.RED_800,    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#__codelineno-0-36)     ] [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#__codelineno-0-37) bgcolor = colors[i % len(colors)]        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#__codelineno-0-40)         panel_list.controls.append(            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#__codelineno-0-41)             ft.ExpansionPanel(                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#__codelineno-0-42)                 bgcolor=bgcolor,                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#__codelineno-0-43)                 header=ft.ListTile(title=ft.Text(f"Panel {i}"), bgcolor=bgcolor), [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#__codelineno-0-45)                     bgcolor=bgcolor, [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#__codelineno-0-48)                     trailing=ft.IconButton(                        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#__codelineno-0-49)                         icon=ft.Icons.DELETE,                        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#__codelineno-0-50)                         on_click=handle_delete,                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#__codelineno-0-51)                     ),                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#__codelineno-0-52)                 ),            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#__codelineno-0-53)             )        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#__codelineno-0-54)         ) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#__codelineno-0-55)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#__codelineno-0-56)     page.add(panel_list) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#__codelineno-0-57) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#__codelineno-0-58)[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#__codelineno-0-59) ft.run(main)`

### Scrolling[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#scrolling "Permanent link")

`ExpansionPanelList` supports scrolling through its [`scroll`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/scrollablecontrol/#flet.ScrollableControl.scroll)
 property.

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#__codelineno-1-1) import flet as ft [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#__codelineno-1-2) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#__codelineno-1-3)[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#__codelineno-1-4) def main(page: ft.Page):     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#__codelineno-1-5)     page.add(        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#__codelineno-1-6)         ft.SafeArea(            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#__codelineno-1-7)             expand=True,            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#__codelineno-1-8)             content=ft.ExpansionPanelList(                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#__codelineno-1-9)                 expand=True,                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#__codelineno-1-10)                 scroll=ft.ScrollMode.ALWAYS,                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#__codelineno-1-11)                 spacing=8,                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#__codelineno-1-12)                 controls=[                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#__codelineno-1-13)                     ft.ExpansionPanel(                        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#__codelineno-1-14)                         can_tap_header=True,                        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#__codelineno-1-15)                         header=ft.ListTile(title=ft.Text(f"Panel {i}")),                        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#__codelineno-1-16)                         content=ft.ListTile(                            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#__codelineno-1-17)                             title=ft.Text(f"Details for panel {i}"),                            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#__codelineno-1-18)                             subtitle=ft.Text(                                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#__codelineno-1-19)                                 "This content can be expanded or collapsed."                            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#__codelineno-1-20)                             ),                        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#__codelineno-1-21)                         ),                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#__codelineno-1-22)                     )                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#__codelineno-1-23)                     for i in range(1, 41)                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#__codelineno-1-24)                 ],            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#__codelineno-1-25)             ),        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#__codelineno-1-26)         ),    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#__codelineno-1-27)     ) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#__codelineno-1-28) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#__codelineno-1-29)[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#__codelineno-1-30) ft.run(main)`

Properties[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#flet.ExpansionPanelList-properties "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### controls `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#flet.ExpansionPanelList.controls "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#__codelineno-0-1) controls: [list](https://docs.python.org/3/glossary.html#term-list) [[ExpansionPanel](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanel/#flet.ExpansionPanel) ] = [field](https://docs.python.org/3/library/dataclasses.html#dataclasses.field "<code>dataclasses.field</code>") (default_factory=[list](https://docs.python.org/3/glossary.html#term-list) )`

A list of panels to display.

### divider\_color `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#flet.ExpansionPanelList.divider_color "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#__codelineno-0-1) divider_color: [ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None = None`

The color of the divider when [`ExpansionPanel.expanded`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanel/#flet.ExpansionPanel.expanded)
 is `False`.

### elevation `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#flet.ExpansionPanelList.elevation "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#__codelineno-0-1) elevation: [Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  = 2`

Defines the elevation of the [`controls`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#flet.ExpansionPanelList.controls)
, when expanded.

Raises:

*   `[ValueError](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ValueError) ` –
    
    If it is not greater than or equal to `0`.
    

### expand\_icon\_color `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#flet.ExpansionPanelList.expand_icon_color "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#__codelineno-0-1) expand_icon_color: [ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None = None`

The color of the icon.

Defaults to [`Colors.BLACK_54`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/colors/#flet.Colors.BLACK_54)
 in light theme mode and [`Colors.WHITE_60`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/colors/#flet.Colors.WHITE_60)
 in dark theme mode.

### expanded\_header\_padding `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#flet.ExpansionPanelList.expanded_header_padding "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#__codelineno-0-1) expanded_header_padding: [PaddingValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.PaddingValue)  = [field](https://docs.python.org/3/library/dataclasses.html#dataclasses.field "<code>dataclasses.field</code>") (     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#__codelineno-0-2)     default_factory=lambda: [symmetric](https://docs.flet.dev/types/padding/#flet.Padding.symmetric) (vertical=16.0) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#__codelineno-0-3) )`

Defines the padding around the header when expanded.

### spacing `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#flet.ExpansionPanelList.spacing "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#__codelineno-0-1) spacing: [Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None = None`

The size of the gap between the [`controls`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#flet.ExpansionPanelList.controls)
s when expanded.

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#__codelineno-0-1) on_change: (     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#__codelineno-0-2)     [EventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.EventHandler) [[ExpansionPanelListChangeEvent](https://docs.flet.dev/types/expansionpanellistchangeevent/#flet.ExpansionPanelListChangeEvent) ] | None [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/expansionpanellist/#__codelineno-0-3) ) = None`

# GridView - Flet

 

[Skip to content](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/gridview/#flet.GridView)

GridView
========

A scrollable, 2D array of controls.

It is very effective for large lists (thousands of items). Prefer it over wrapping [`Column`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#flet.Column)
s or [`Row`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/row/#flet.Row)
s for smooth scrolling.

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/gridview/#__codelineno-0-1) ft.GridView(     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/gridview/#__codelineno-0-2)     width=180,    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/gridview/#__codelineno-0-3)     runs_count=2,    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/gridview/#__codelineno-0-4)     spacing=8,    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/gridview/#__codelineno-0-5)     controls=[        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/gridview/#__codelineno-0-6)         ft.Container(            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/gridview/#__codelineno-0-7)             width=50, height=50, bgcolor=ft.Colors.PRIMARY, border_radius=8        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/gridview/#__codelineno-0-8)         ),        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/gridview/#__codelineno-0-9)         ft.Container(            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/gridview/#__codelineno-0-10)             width=50, height=50, bgcolor=ft.Colors.SECONDARY, border_radius=8        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/gridview/#__codelineno-0-11)         ),        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/gridview/#__codelineno-0-12)         ft.Container(            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/gridview/#__codelineno-0-13)             width=50, height=50, bgcolor=ft.Colors.TERTIARY, border_radius=8        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/gridview/#__codelineno-0-14)         ),        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/gridview/#__codelineno-0-15)         ft.Container(            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/gridview/#__codelineno-0-16)             width=50, height=50, bgcolor=ft.Colors.ERROR, border_radius=8        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/gridview/#__codelineno-0-17)         ),    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/gridview/#__codelineno-0-18)     ], [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/gridview/#__codelineno-0-19) )`

[![GridView](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/core/golden/macos/grid_view/image_for_docs.png)](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/core/golden/macos/grid_view/image_for_docs.png)

Basic GridView

Inherits: `[LayoutControl](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/layoutcontrol/#flet.LayoutControl) `, `[ScrollableControl](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/scrollablecontrol/#flet.ScrollableControl) `, `[AdaptiveControl](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/adaptivecontrol/#flet.AdaptiveControl) `

Properties

*   **`[build_controls_on_demand](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/gridview/#flet.GridView.build_controls_on_demand) `**(`[bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool) `) –
    
    TBD
    
*   **`[cache_extent](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/gridview/#flet.GridView.cache_extent) `**(`[Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None`) –
    
    Items that fall in the cache area (area before or after the visible area that are about to become visible when the user scrolls) are laid out even though they are not (yet) visible on screen.
    
*   **`[child_aspect_ratio](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/gridview/#flet.GridView.child_aspect_ratio) `**(`[Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number) `) –
    
    The ratio of the cross-axis to the main-axis extent of each child.
    
*   **`[clip_behavior](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/gridview/#flet.GridView.clip_behavior) `**(`[ClipBehavior](https://docs.flet.dev/types/clipbehavior/#flet.ClipBehavior)  | None`) –
    
    The content will be clipped (or not) according to this option.
    
*   **`[controls](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/gridview/#flet.GridView.controls) `**(`[list](https://docs.python.org/3/glossary.html#term-list) [[Control](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control) ]`) –
    
    A list of controls to display inside grid.
    
*   **`[horizontal](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/gridview/#flet.GridView.horizontal) `**(`[bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool) `) –
    
    Whether to layout the grid items horizontally.
    
*   **`[max_extent](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/gridview/#flet.GridView.max_extent) `**(`[int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int)  | None`) –
    
    The maximum width or height of the grid item.
    
*   **`[padding](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/gridview/#flet.GridView.padding) `**(`[PaddingValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.PaddingValue)  | None`) –
    
    The amount of space by which to inset the children.
    
*   **`[reverse](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/gridview/#flet.GridView.reverse) `**(`[bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool) `) –
    
    Whether the scroll view scrolls in the reading direction.
    
*   **`[run_spacing](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/gridview/#flet.GridView.run_spacing) `**(`[Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number) `) –
    
    The number of logical pixels between each child along the cross axis.
    
*   **`[runs_count](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/gridview/#flet.GridView.runs_count) `**(`[int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int) `) –
    
    The number of children in the cross axis.
    
*   **`[semantic_child_count](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/gridview/#flet.GridView.semantic_child_count) `**(`[int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int)  | None`) –
    
    The number of children that will contribute semantic information.
    
*   **`[spacing](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/gridview/#flet.GridView.spacing) `**(`[Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number) `) –
    
    The number of logical pixels between each child along the main axis.
    

Examples[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/gridview/#examples "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[Live example](https://flet-controls-gallery.fly.dev/layout/gridview)

### Photo gallery[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/gridview/#photo-gallery "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/gridview/#__codelineno-0-1) import flet as ft [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/gridview/#__codelineno-0-2) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/gridview/#__codelineno-0-3)[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/gridview/#__codelineno-0-4) def main(page: ft.Page):     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/gridview/#__codelineno-0-5)     page.title = "GridView Example"    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/gridview/#__codelineno-0-6)     page.theme_mode = ft.ThemeMode.DARK    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/gridview/#__codelineno-0-7)     page.padding = 50 [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/gridview/#__codelineno-0-8)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/gridview/#__codelineno-0-9)     page.add(        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/gridview/#__codelineno-0-10)         ft.GridView(            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/gridview/#__codelineno-0-11)             expand=1,            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/gridview/#__codelineno-0-12)             runs_count=5,            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/gridview/#__codelineno-0-13)             max_extent=150,            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/gridview/#__codelineno-0-14)             child_aspect_ratio=1.0,            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/gridview/#__codelineno-0-15)             spacing=5,            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/gridview/#__codelineno-0-16)             run_spacing=5,            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/gridview/#__codelineno-0-17)             controls=[                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/gridview/#__codelineno-0-18)                 ft.Image(                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/gridview/#__codelineno-0-19)                     src=f"https://picsum.photos/150/150?{i}",                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/gridview/#__codelineno-0-20)                     fit=ft.BoxFit.NONE,                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/gridview/#__codelineno-0-21)                     repeat=ft.ImageRepeat.NO_REPEAT,                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/gridview/#__codelineno-0-22)                     border_radius=ft.BorderRadius.all(10),                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/gridview/#__codelineno-0-23)                 )                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/gridview/#__codelineno-0-24)                 for i in range(0, 60)            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/gridview/#__codelineno-0-25)             ],        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/gridview/#__codelineno-0-26)         )    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/gridview/#__codelineno-0-27)     ) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/gridview/#__codelineno-0-28) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/gridview/#__codelineno-0-29)[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/gridview/#__codelineno-0-30) ft.run(main)`

Properties[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/gridview/#flet.GridView-properties "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### build\_controls\_on\_demand `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/gridview/#flet.GridView.build_controls_on_demand "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/gridview/#__codelineno-0-1) build_controls_on_demand: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)  = True`

TBD

### cache\_extent `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/gridview/#flet.GridView.cache_extent "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/gridview/#__codelineno-0-1) cache_extent: [Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None = None`

Items that fall in the cache area (area before or after the visible area that are about to become visible when the user scrolls) are laid out even though they are not (yet) visible on screen.

The cacheExtent describes how many pixels the cache area extends before the leading edge and after the trailing edge of the viewport.

The total extent, which the viewport will try to cover with `controls`, is `cache_extent` before the leading edge + extent of the main axis + `cache_extent` after the trailing edge.

### child\_aspect\_ratio `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/gridview/#flet.GridView.child_aspect_ratio "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/gridview/#__codelineno-0-1) child_aspect_ratio: [Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  = 1.0`

The ratio of the cross-axis to the main-axis extent of each child.

### clip\_behavior `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/gridview/#flet.GridView.clip_behavior "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/gridview/#__codelineno-0-1) clip_behavior: [ClipBehavior](https://docs.flet.dev/types/clipbehavior/#flet.ClipBehavior)  | None = None`

The content will be clipped (or not) according to this option.

### controls `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/gridview/#flet.GridView.controls "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/gridview/#__codelineno-0-1) controls: [list](https://docs.python.org/3/glossary.html#term-list) [[Control](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control) ] = [field](https://docs.python.org/3/library/dataclasses.html#dataclasses.field "<code>dataclasses.field</code>") (default_factory=[list](https://docs.python.org/3/glossary.html#term-list) )`

A list of controls to display inside grid.

### horizontal `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/gridview/#flet.GridView.horizontal "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/gridview/#__codelineno-0-1) horizontal: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)  = False`

Whether to layout the grid items horizontally.

### max\_extent `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/gridview/#flet.GridView.max_extent "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/gridview/#__codelineno-0-1) max_extent: [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int)  | None = None`

The maximum width or height of the grid item.

### padding `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/gridview/#flet.GridView.padding "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/gridview/#__codelineno-0-1) padding: [PaddingValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.PaddingValue)  | None = None`

The amount of space by which to inset the children.

### reverse `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/gridview/#flet.GridView.reverse "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/gridview/#__codelineno-0-1) reverse: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)  = False`

Whether the scroll view scrolls in the reading direction.

For example, if the reading direction is left-to-right and `horizontal` is `True`, then the scroll view scrolls from left to right when `reverse` is `False` and from right to left when `reverse` is `True`.

Similarly, if `horizontal` is `False`, then the scroll view scrolls from top to bottom when `reverse` is `False` and from bottom to top when `reverse` is `True`.

### run\_spacing `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/gridview/#flet.GridView.run_spacing "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/gridview/#__codelineno-0-1) run_spacing: [Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  = 10`

The number of logical pixels between each child along the cross axis.

### runs\_count `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/gridview/#flet.GridView.runs_count "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/gridview/#__codelineno-0-1) runs_count: [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int)  = 1`

The number of children in the cross axis.

### semantic\_child\_count `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/gridview/#flet.GridView.semantic_child_count "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/gridview/#__codelineno-0-1) semantic_child_count: [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int)  | None = None`

The number of children that will contribute semantic information.

### spacing `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/gridview/#flet.GridView.spacing "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/gridview/#__codelineno-0-1) spacing: [Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  = 10`

The number of logical pixels between each child along the main axis.

# NavigationRailDestination - Flet

 

[Skip to content](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/navigationraildestination/#flet.NavigationRailDestination)

NavigationRailDestination
=========================

Inherits: `[Control](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control) `

Represents a destination in a `NavigationRail`.

Properties

*   **`[icon](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/navigationraildestination/#flet.NavigationRailDestination.icon) `**(`[IconDataOrControl](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.IconDataOrControl)  | None`) –
    
    The [name of the icon](https://docs.flet.dev/types/icons)
     or `Control` of the destination.
    
*   **`[indicator_color](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/navigationraildestination/#flet.NavigationRailDestination.indicator_color) `**(`[ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None`) –
    
    The color of the [`indicator_shape`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/navigationraildestination/#flet.NavigationRailDestination.indicator_shape)
     when this destination is selected.
    
*   **`[indicator_shape](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/navigationraildestination/#flet.NavigationRailDestination.indicator_shape) `**(`[OutlinedBorder](https://docs.flet.dev/types/outlinedborder/#flet.OutlinedBorder)  | None`) –
    
    The shape of the selection indicator.
    
*   **`[label](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/navigationraildestination/#flet.NavigationRailDestination.label) `**(`[StrOrControl](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.StrOrControl)  | None`) –
    
    A string or Control representing the destination's label.
    
*   **`[padding](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/navigationraildestination/#flet.NavigationRailDestination.padding) `**(`[PaddingValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.PaddingValue)  | None`) –
    
    The amount of space to inset the destination item.
    
*   **`[selected_icon](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/navigationraildestination/#flet.NavigationRailDestination.selected_icon) `**(`[IconDataOrControl](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.IconDataOrControl)  | None`) –
    
    The [name](https://docs.flet.dev/types/icons)
     of alternative icon or `Control` displayed when this destination is selected.
    

Properties[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/navigationraildestination/#flet.NavigationRailDestination-properties "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### icon `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/navigationraildestination/#flet.NavigationRailDestination.icon "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/navigationraildestination/#__codelineno-0-1) icon: [IconDataOrControl](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.IconDataOrControl)  | None = None`

The [name of the icon](https://docs.flet.dev/types/icons)
 or `Control` of the destination.

If `selected_icon` is provided, this will only be displayed when the destination is not selected.

To make the NavigationRail more accessible, consider choosing an icon with a stroked and filled version, such as `Icons.CLOUD` and `Icons.CLOUD_QUEUE`. The icon should be set to the stroked version and `selected_icon` to the filled version.

### indicator\_color `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/navigationraildestination/#flet.NavigationRailDestination.indicator_color "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/navigationraildestination/#__codelineno-0-1) indicator_color: [ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None = None`

The color of the [`indicator_shape`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/navigationraildestination/#flet.NavigationRailDestination.indicator_shape)
 when this destination is selected.

### indicator\_shape `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/navigationraildestination/#flet.NavigationRailDestination.indicator_shape "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/navigationraildestination/#__codelineno-0-1) indicator_shape: [OutlinedBorder](https://docs.flet.dev/types/outlinedborder/#flet.OutlinedBorder)  | None = None`

The shape of the selection indicator.

### label `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/navigationraildestination/#flet.NavigationRailDestination.label "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/navigationraildestination/#__codelineno-0-1) label: [StrOrControl](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.StrOrControl)  | None = None`

A string or Control representing the destination's label.

### padding `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/navigationraildestination/#flet.NavigationRailDestination.padding "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/navigationraildestination/#__codelineno-0-1) padding: [PaddingValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.PaddingValue)  | None = None`

The amount of space to inset the destination item.

### selected\_icon `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/navigationraildestination/#flet.NavigationRailDestination.selected_icon "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/navigationraildestination/#__codelineno-0-1) selected_icon: [IconDataOrControl](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.IconDataOrControl)  | None = None`

The [name](https://docs.flet.dev/types/icons)
 of alternative icon or `Control` displayed when this destination is selected.

If this icon is not provided, the NavigationRail will display `icon` in either state.

Back to top

# AlertDialog - Flet

 

[Skip to content](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#flet.AlertDialog)

AlertDialog
===========

Can be used to inform the user about situations that require acknowledgement.

It has an optional [`title`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#flet.AlertDialog.title)
 and an optional list of [`actions`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#flet.AlertDialog.actions)
 . The `title` is displayed above the [`content`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#flet.AlertDialog.content)
 and the `actions` are displayed below the `content`.

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#__codelineno-0-1) ft.AlertDialog(     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#__codelineno-0-2)     title=ft.Text("Session expired"),    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#__codelineno-0-3)     content=ft.Text("Please sign in again to continue."),    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#__codelineno-0-4)     actions=[ft.TextButton("Dismiss")],    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#__codelineno-0-5)     open=True, [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#__codelineno-0-6) )`

[![AlertDialog](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/material/golden/macos/alert_dialog/image_for_docs.png)](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/material/golden/macos/alert_dialog/image_for_docs.png)

Basic AlertDialog

Inherits: `[DialogControl](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/dialogcontrol/#flet.DialogControl) `

Properties

*   **`[action_button_padding](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#flet.AlertDialog.action_button_padding) `**(`[PaddingValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.PaddingValue)  | None`) –
    
    The padding that surrounds each button in [`actions`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#flet.AlertDialog.actions)
    .
    
*   **`[actions](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#flet.AlertDialog.actions) `**(`[list](https://docs.python.org/3/glossary.html#term-list) [[Control](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control) ]`) –
    
    A set of actions that are displayed at the bottom of this dialog.
    
*   **`[actions_alignment](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#flet.AlertDialog.actions_alignment) `**(`[MainAxisAlignment](https://docs.flet.dev/types/mainaxisalignment/#flet.MainAxisAlignment)  | None`) –
    
    Defines the horizontal layout of the actions.
    
*   **`[actions_overflow_button_spacing](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#flet.AlertDialog.actions_overflow_button_spacing) `**(`[Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None`) –
    
    The spacing between [`actions`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#flet.AlertDialog.actions)
     when the `OverflowBar` switches to a column layout because the actions don't fit horizontally.
    
*   **`[actions_padding](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#flet.AlertDialog.actions_padding) `**(`[PaddingValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.PaddingValue)  | None`) –
    
    Padding around the set of [`actions`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#flet.AlertDialog.actions)
     at the bottom of this dialog.
    
*   **`[alignment](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#flet.AlertDialog.alignment) `**(`[Alignment](https://docs.flet.dev/types/alignment/#flet.Alignment)  | None`) –
    
    How to align this dialog.
    
*   **`[barrier_color](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#flet.AlertDialog.barrier_color) `**(`[ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None`) –
    
    The color of the modal barrier below this dialog.
    
*   **`[bgcolor](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#flet.AlertDialog.bgcolor) `**(`[ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None`) –
    
    The background color of this dialog's surface.
    
*   **`[clip_behavior](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#flet.AlertDialog.clip_behavior) `**(`[ClipBehavior](https://docs.flet.dev/types/clipbehavior/#flet.ClipBehavior) `) –
    
    Defines how the contents of this dialog are clipped (or not) to the given [`shape`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#flet.AlertDialog.shape)
    .
    
*   **`[content](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#flet.AlertDialog.content) `**(`[Control](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control)  | None`) –
    
    The content of this dialog is displayed in the center of this dialog in a lighter font.
    
*   **`[content_padding](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#flet.AlertDialog.content_padding) `**(`[PaddingValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.PaddingValue)  | None`) –
    
    Padding around the [`content`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#flet.AlertDialog.content)
    .
    
*   **`[content_text_style](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#flet.AlertDialog.content_text_style) `**(`[TextStyle](https://docs.flet.dev/types/textstyle/#flet.TextStyle)  | None`) –
    
    The style for the text in the [`content`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#flet.AlertDialog.content)
     of this dialog.
    
*   **`[elevation](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#flet.AlertDialog.elevation) `**(`[Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None`) –
    
    Defines the elevation (z-coordinate) at which this dialog should appear.
    
*   **`[icon](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#flet.AlertDialog.icon) `**(`[Control](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control)  | None`) –
    
    A control that is displayed at the top of this dialog.
    
*   **`[icon_color](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#flet.AlertDialog.icon_color) `**(`[ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None`) –
    
    The color for the Icon in the [`icon`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#flet.AlertDialog.icon)
     of this dialog.
    
*   **`[icon_padding](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#flet.AlertDialog.icon_padding) `**(`[PaddingValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.PaddingValue)  | None`) –
    
    Padding around the [`icon`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#flet.AlertDialog.icon)
    .
    
*   **`[inset_padding](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#flet.AlertDialog.inset_padding) `**(`[PaddingValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.PaddingValue)  | None`) –
    
    Padding around this dialog itself.
    
*   **`[modal](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#flet.AlertDialog.modal) `**(`[bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool) `) –
    
    Whether dialog can be dismissed/closed by clicking the area outside of it.
    
*   **`[scrollable](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#flet.AlertDialog.scrollable) `**(`[bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool) `) –
    
    Determines whether the [`title`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#flet.AlertDialog.title)
     and [`content`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#flet.AlertDialog.content)
     controls are wrapped in a scrollable.
    
*   **`[semantics_label](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#flet.AlertDialog.semantics_label) `**(`[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)  | None`) –
    
    The semantic label of this dialog used by accessibility frameworks to announce screen transitions when this dialog is opened and closed.
    
*   **`[shadow_color](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#flet.AlertDialog.shadow_color) `**(`[ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None`) –
    
    The color used to paint a drop shadow under this dialog, which reflects this dialog's [`elevation`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#flet.AlertDialog.elevation)
    .
    
*   **`[shape](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#flet.AlertDialog.shape) `**(`[OutlinedBorder](https://docs.flet.dev/types/outlinedborder/#flet.OutlinedBorder)  | None`) –
    
    The shape of this dialog.
    
*   **`[title](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#flet.AlertDialog.title) `**(`[StrOrControl](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.StrOrControl)  | None`) –
    
    The title of this dialog is displayed in a large font at its top.
    
*   **`[title_padding](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#flet.AlertDialog.title_padding) `**(`[PaddingValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.PaddingValue)  | None`) –
    
    Padding around the [`title`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#flet.AlertDialog.title)
    .

### Modal and non-modal dialogs[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#modal-and-non-modal-dialogs "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#__codelineno-0-1) import flet as ft [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#__codelineno-0-2) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#__codelineno-0-3)[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#__codelineno-0-4) def main(page: ft.Page):     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#__codelineno-0-5)     page.title = "AlertDialog examples" [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#__codelineno-0-6)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#__codelineno-0-7)     dialog = ft.AlertDialog(        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#__codelineno-0-8)         title=ft.Text("Hello"),        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#__codelineno-0-9)         content=ft.Text("You are notified!"),        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#__codelineno-0-10)         alignment=ft.Alignment.CENTER,        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#__codelineno-0-11)         on_dismiss=lambda e: print("Dialog dismissed!"),        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#__codelineno-0-12)         title_padding=ft.Padding.all(25),    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#__codelineno-0-13)     ) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#__codelineno-0-14)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#__codelineno-0-15)     modal_dialog = ft.AlertDialog(        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#__codelineno-0-16)         modal=True,        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#__codelineno-0-17)         title=ft.Text("Please confirm"),        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#__codelineno-0-18)         content=ft.Text("Do you really want to delete all those files?"),        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#__codelineno-0-19)         actions=[            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#__codelineno-0-20)             ft.TextButton("Yes", on_click=lambda e: page.pop_dialog()),            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#__codelineno-0-21)             ft.TextButton("No", on_click=lambda e: page.pop_dialog()),        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#__codelineno-0-22)         ],        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#__codelineno-0-23)         actions_alignment=ft.MainAxisAlignment.END,        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#__codelineno-0-24)         on_dismiss=lambda e: print("Modal dialog dismissed!"),    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#__codelineno-0-25)     ) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#__codelineno-0-26)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#__codelineno-0-27)     page.add(        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#__codelineno-0-28)         ft.Button(            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#__codelineno-0-29)             content="Open dialog",            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#__codelineno-0-30)             on_click=lambda e: page.show_dialog(dialog),        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#__codelineno-0-31)         ),        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#__codelineno-0-32)         ft.Button(            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#__codelineno-0-33)             content="Open modal dialog",            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#__codelineno-0-34)             on_click=lambda e: page.show_dialog(modal_dialog),        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#__codelineno-0-35)         ),    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#__codelineno-0-36)     ) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#__codelineno-0-37) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#__codelineno-0-38)[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#__codelineno-0-39) if Modal and non-modal dialogs

Properties[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#flet.AlertDialog-properties "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### action\_button\_padding `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#flet.AlertDialog.action_button_padding "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#__codelineno-0-1) action_button_padding: [PaddingValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.PaddingValue)  | None = None`

The padding that surrounds each button in [`actions`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#flet.AlertDialog.actions)
.

### actions `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#flet.AlertDialog.actions "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#__codelineno-0-1) actions: [list](https://docs.python.org/3/glossary.html#term-list) [[Control](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control) ] = [field](https://docs.python.org/3/library/dataclasses.html#dataclasses.field "<code>dataclasses.field</code>") (default_factory=[list](https://docs.python.org/3/glossary.html#term-list) )`

A set of actions that are displayed at the bottom of this dialog.

Typically this is a list of [`TextButton`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/textbutton/#flet.TextButton)
 controls.

Raises:

*   `[ValueError](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ValueError) ` –
    
    If none of [`title`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#flet.AlertDialog.title)
    , [`content`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#flet.AlertDialog.content)
    , or [`actions`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#flet.AlertDialog.actions)
     are provided, as the dialog would have nothing to display.
    

### actions\_alignment `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#flet.AlertDialog.actions_alignment "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#__codelineno-0-1) actions_alignment: [MainAxisAlignment](https://docs.flet.dev/types/mainaxisalignment/#flet.MainAxisAlignment)  | None = None`

Defines the horizontal layout of the actions.

Internally defaults to [`MainAxisAlignment.END`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/mainaxisalignment/#flet.MainAxisAlignment.END)
.

### actions\_overflow\_button\_spacing `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#flet.AlertDialog.actions_overflow_button_spacing "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#__codelineno-0-1) actions_overflow_button_spacing: [Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None = None`

The spacing between [`actions`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#flet.AlertDialog.actions)
 when the `OverflowBar` switches to a column layout because the actions don't fit horizontally.

If the controls in `actions` do not fit into a single row, they are arranged into a column. This parameter provides additional vertical space between buttons when it does overflow.

### actions\_padding `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#flet.AlertDialog.actions_padding "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#__codelineno-0-1) actions_padding: [PaddingValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.PaddingValue)  | None = None`

Padding around the set of [`actions`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#flet.AlertDialog.actions)
 at the bottom of this dialog.

Typically used to provide padding to the button bar between the button bar and the edges of this dialog.

If are no actions, then no padding will be included. The padding around the button bar defaults to zero.

### alignment `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#flet.AlertDialog.alignment "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#__codelineno-0-1) alignment: [Alignment](https://docs.flet.dev/types/alignment/#flet.Alignment)  | None = None`

How to align this dialog.

If `None`, then [`DialogTheme.alignment`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/dialogtheme/#flet.DialogTheme.alignment)
 is used. If that is also `None`, the default is [`Alignment.CENTER`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/alignment/#flet.Alignment.CENTER)
.

### barrier\_color `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#flet.AlertDialog.barrier_color "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#__codelineno-0-1) barrier_color: [ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None = None`

The color of the modal barrier below this dialog.

If `None`, then [`DialogTheme.barrier_color`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/dialogtheme/#flet.DialogTheme.barrier_color)
 is used. If that is also `None`, the default is `Colors.BLACK_54`.

### bgcolor `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#flet.AlertDialog.bgcolor "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#__codelineno-0-1) bgcolor: [ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None = None`

The background color of this dialog's surface.

### clip\_behavior `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#flet.AlertDialog.clip_behavior "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#__codelineno-0-1) clip_behavior: [ClipBehavior](https://docs.flet.dev/types/clipbehavior/#flet.ClipBehavior)  = [NONE](https://docs.flet.dev/types/clipbehavior/#flet.ClipBehavior.NONE)`

Defines how the contents of this dialog are clipped (or not) to the given [`shape`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#flet.AlertDialog.shape)
.

### content `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#flet.AlertDialog.content "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#__codelineno-0-1) content: [Control](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control)  | None = None`

The content of this dialog is displayed in the center of this dialog in a lighter font.

Typically this is a [`Column`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#flet.Column)
 that contains this dialog's [`Text`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/text/#flet.Text)
 message.

### content\_padding `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#flet.AlertDialog.content_padding "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#__codelineno-0-1) content_padding: [PaddingValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.PaddingValue)  | None = None`

Padding around the [`content`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#flet.AlertDialog.content)
.

If there is no `content`, no padding will be provided. Otherwise, padding of `20` pixels is provided above the content to separate the [`content`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#flet.AlertDialog.content)
 from the [`title`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#flet.AlertDialog.title)
, and padding of `24` pixels is provided on the left, right, and bottom to separate the [`content`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#flet.AlertDialog.content)
 from the other edges of this dialog.

### content\_text\_style `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#flet.AlertDialog.content_text_style "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#__codelineno-0-1) content_text_style: [TextStyle](https://docs.flet.dev/types/textstyle/#flet.TextStyle)  | None = None`

The style for the text in the [`content`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#flet.AlertDialog.content)
 of this dialog.

If `None`, [`DialogTheme.content_text_style`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/dialogtheme/#flet.DialogTheme.content_text_style)
 is used. If that's is also `None`, defaults to [`TextTheme.body_medium`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/texttheme/#flet.TextTheme.body_medium)
 (if [`Theme.use_material3`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/theme/#flet.Theme.use_material3)
 is `True`; [`TextTheme.title_medium`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/texttheme/#flet.TextTheme.title_medium)
 otherwise) of [Theme.text\_theme](https://docs.flet.dev/types/theme/#flet.Theme.text_theme)
.

### elevation `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#flet.AlertDialog.elevation "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#__codelineno-0-1) elevation: [Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None = None`

Defines the elevation (z-coordinate) at which this dialog should appear.

### icon `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#flet.AlertDialog.icon "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#__codelineno-0-1) icon: [Control](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control)  | None = None`

A control that is displayed at the top of this dialog.

Typically a [`Icon`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/icon/#flet.Icon)
 control.

### icon\_color `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#flet.AlertDialog.icon_color "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#__codelineno-0-1) icon_color: [ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None = None`

The color for the Icon in the [`icon`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#flet.AlertDialog.icon)
 of this dialog.

If `None`, [`DialogTheme.icon_color`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/dialogtheme/#flet.DialogTheme.icon_color)
 is used. If that is null, defaults to color scheme's [`ColorScheme.secondary`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/colorscheme/#flet.ColorScheme.secondary)
 if [`Theme.use_material3`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/theme/#flet.Theme.use_material3)
 is `True`, `Colors.BLACK` otherwise.

### icon\_padding `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#flet.AlertDialog.icon_padding "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#__codelineno-0-1) icon_padding: [PaddingValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.PaddingValue)  | None = None`

Padding around the [`icon`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#flet.AlertDialog.icon)
.

### inset\_padding `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#flet.AlertDialog.inset_padding "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#__codelineno-0-1) inset_padding: [PaddingValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.PaddingValue)  | None = None`

Padding around this dialog itself.

Defaults to `Padding.symmetric(vertical=40, horizontal=24)` - `40` pixels horizontally and `24` pixels vertically outside of this dialog box.

### modal `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#flet.AlertDialog.modal "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#__codelineno-0-1) modal: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)  = False`

Whether dialog can be dismissed/closed by clicking the area outside of it.

### scrollable `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#flet.AlertDialog.scrollable "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#__codelineno-0-1) scrollable: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)  = False`

Determines whether the [`title`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#flet.AlertDialog.title)
 and [`content`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#flet.AlertDialog.content)
 controls are wrapped in a scrollable.

This configuration is used when the `title` and `content` are expected to overflow. Both `title` and `content` are wrapped in a scroll view, allowing all overflowed content to be visible while still showing the button bar.

### semantics\_label `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#flet.AlertDialog.semantics_label "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#__codelineno-0-1) semantics_label: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)  | None = None`

The semantic label of this dialog used by accessibility frameworks to announce screen transitions when this dialog is opened and closed.

On iOS, if this label is not provided, a semantic label will be inferred from the [`title`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#flet.AlertDialog.title)
 if it is not `None`.

### shadow\_color `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#flet.AlertDialog.shadow_color "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#__codelineno-0-1) shadow_color: [ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None = None`

The color used to paint a drop shadow under this dialog, which reflects this dialog's [`elevation`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#flet.AlertDialog.elevation)
.

### shape `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#flet.AlertDialog.shape "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#__codelineno-0-1) shape: [OutlinedBorder](https://docs.flet.dev/types/outlinedborder/#flet.OutlinedBorder)  | None = None`

The shape of this dialog.

If `None`, defaults to [`DialogTheme.shape`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/dialogtheme/#flet.DialogTheme.shape)
. If it is also `None`, it defaults to `RoundedRectangleBorder(radius=4.0)`.

### title `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#flet.AlertDialog.title "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#__codelineno-0-1) title: [StrOrControl](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.StrOrControl)  | None = None`

The title of this dialog is displayed in a large font at its top.

Typically a [`Text`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/text/#flet.Text)
 control.

### title\_padding `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#flet.AlertDialog.title_padding "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#__codelineno-0-1) title_padding: [PaddingValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.PaddingValue)  | None = None`

Padding around the [`title`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#flet.AlertDialog.title)
.

If there is no title, no padding will be provided. Otherwise, this padding is used.

Defaults to `24` pixels on the top, left, and right of the [`title`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#flet.AlertDialog.title)
. If the [`content`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#flet.AlertDialog.content)
 is not `None`, then no bottom padding is provided (see [`content_padding`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#flet.AlertDialog.content_padding)
). If it is not set, then an extra `20` pixels of bottom padding is added to separate the [`title`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#flet.AlertDialog.title)
 from the [`actions`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#flet.AlertDialog.actions)
.

### title\_text\_style `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#flet.AlertDialog.title_text_style "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/alertdialog/#__codelineno-0-1) title_text_style: [TextStyle](https://docs.flet.dev/types/textstyle/#flet.TextStyle)  | None = None`

TBD
