# ReorderableDragHandle - Flet

 

[Skip to content](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderabledraghandle/#flet.ReorderableDragHandle)

ReorderableDragHandle
=====================

Used to drag an item in a [`ReorderableListView`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#flet.ReorderableListView)
.

It creates a listener for a drag immediately following a pointer down event over the given [`content`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderabledraghandle/#flet.ReorderableDragHandle.content)
 control.

Example:

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderabledraghandle/#__codelineno-0-1) ft.ReorderableListView(     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderabledraghandle/#__codelineno-0-2)     show_default_drag_handles=False,    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderabledraghandle/#__codelineno-0-3)     controls=[        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderabledraghandle/#__codelineno-0-4)         ft.ListTile(            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderabledraghandle/#__codelineno-0-5)             title=ft.Text(f"Draggable Item {i}", color=ft.Colors.BLACK),            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderabledraghandle/#__codelineno-0-6)             leading=ft.ReorderableDragHandle(                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderabledraghandle/#__codelineno-0-7)                 content=ft.Icon(ft.Icons.DRAG_INDICATOR, color=ft.Colors.RED),                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderabledraghandle/#__codelineno-0-8)                 mouse_cursor=ft.MouseCursor.GRAB,            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderabledraghandle/#__codelineno-0-9)             ),        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderabledraghandle/#__codelineno-0-10)         )        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderabledraghandle/#__codelineno-0-11)         for i in range(10)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderabledraghandle/#__codelineno-0-12)     ], [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderabledraghandle/#__codelineno-0-13) )`

Inherits: `[LayoutControl](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/layoutcontrol/#flet.LayoutControl) `, `[AdaptiveControl](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/adaptivecontrol/#flet.AdaptiveControl) `

Properties

*   **`[content](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderabledraghandle/#flet.ReorderableDragHandle.content) `**(`[Control](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control) `) –
    
    The control for which the application would like to respond to a tap and drag gesture by starting a reordering drag on a reorderable list.
    
*   **`[mouse_cursor](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderabledraghandle/#flet.ReorderableDragHandle.mouse_cursor) `**(`[MouseCursor](https://docs.flet.dev/types/mousecursor/#flet.MouseCursor)  | None`) –
    
    The mouse cursor for mouse pointers that are hovering over the control.
    

Examples[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderabledraghandle/#examples "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Basic Example[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderabledraghandle/#basic-example "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderabledraghandle/#__codelineno-0-1) import flet as ft [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderabledraghandle/#__codelineno-0-2) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderabledraghandle/#__codelineno-0-3)[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderabledraghandle/#__codelineno-0-4) def main(page: ft.Page):     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderabledraghandle/#__codelineno-0-5)     def on_reorder(e: ft.OnReorderEvent):        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderabledraghandle/#__codelineno-0-6)         # Reorder controls list to match the UI change        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderabledraghandle/#__codelineno-0-7)         e.control.controls.insert(e.new_index, e.control.controls.pop(e.old_index)) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderabledraghandle/#__codelineno-0-8)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderabledraghandle/#__codelineno-0-9)     page.add(        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderabledraghandle/#__codelineno-0-10)         ft.ReorderableListView(            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderabledraghandle/#__codelineno-0-11)             expand=True,            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderabledraghandle/#__codelineno-0-12)             show_default_drag_handles=False,            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderabledraghandle/#__codelineno-0-13)             on_reorder=on_reorder,            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderabledraghandle/#__codelineno-0-14)             controls=[                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderabledraghandle/#__codelineno-0-15)                 ft.ListTile(                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderabledraghandle/#__codelineno-0-16)                     title=ft.Text(f"Draggable Item {i}", color=ft.Colors.BLACK),                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderabledraghandle/#__codelineno-0-17)                     leading=ft.ReorderableDragHandle(                        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderabledraghandle/#__codelineno-0-18)                         content=ft.Icon(ft.Icons.DRAG_INDICATOR, color=ft.Colors.RED),                        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderabledraghandle/#__codelineno-0-19)                         mouse_cursor=ft.MouseCursor.GRAB,                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderabledraghandle/#__codelineno-0-20)                     ),                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderabledraghandle/#__codelineno-0-21)                     bgcolor=ft.Colors.ERROR                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderabledraghandle/#__codelineno-0-22)                     if i % 2 == 0                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderabledraghandle/#__codelineno-0-23)                     else ft.Colors.ON_ERROR_CONTAINER,                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderabledraghandle/#__codelineno-0-24)                 )                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderabledraghandle/#__codelineno-0-25)                 for i in range(10)            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderabledraghandle/#__codelineno-0-26)             ],        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderabledraghandle/#__codelineno-0-27)         )    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderabledraghandle/#__codelineno-0-28)     ) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderabledraghandle/#__codelineno-0-29) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderabledraghandle/#__codelineno-0-30)[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderabledraghandle/#__codelineno-0-31) ft.run(main)`

Properties[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderabledraghandle/#flet.ReorderableDragHandle-properties "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### content `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderabledraghandle/#flet.ReorderableDragHandle.content "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderabledraghandle/#__codelineno-0-1) content: [Control](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control)`

The control for which the application would like to respond to a tap and drag gesture by starting a reordering drag on a reorderable list.

Raises:

*   `[ValueError](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ValueError) ` –
    
    If it is not visible.
    

### mouse\_cursor `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderabledraghandle/#flet.ReorderableDragHandle.mouse_cursor "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderabledraghandle/#__codelineno-0-1) mouse_cursor: [MouseCursor](https://docs.flet.dev/types/mousecursor/#flet.MouseCursor)  | None = None`

The mouse cursor for mouse pointers that are hovering over the control.

Back to top

# Icon - Flet

 

[Skip to content](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/icon/#flet.Icon)

Icon
====

A control that displays an icon from a built-in or custom icon set.

Icons can be customized in color, size, and visual style using various parameters such as stroke weight, fill level, and shadows.

Example:

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/icon/#__codelineno-0-1) ft.Icon(ft.Icons.FAVORITE, color=ft.Colors.PRIMARY, size=40)`

[![Icon](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/core/golden/macos/icon/image_for_docs.png)](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/core/golden/macos/icon/image_for_docs.png)

Basic Icon

Inherits: `[LayoutControl](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/layoutcontrol/#flet.LayoutControl) `

Properties

*   **`[apply_text_scaling](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/icon/#flet.Icon.apply_text_scaling) `**(`[bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)  | None`) –
    
    Whether to scale the icon based on the system or user's preferred text size.
    
*   **`[blend_mode](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/icon/#flet.Icon.blend_mode) `**(`[BlendMode](https://docs.flet.dev/types/blendmode/#flet.BlendMode)  | None`) –
    
    The blend mode used when rendering the icon.
    
*   **`[color](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/icon/#flet.Icon.color) `**(`[ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None`) –
    
    The color to use when drawing the icon.
    
*   **`[fill](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/icon/#flet.Icon.fill) `**(`[Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None`) –
    
    The fill amount of the icon, between `0.0` (outline) and `1.0` (solid).
    
*   **`[grade](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/icon/#flet.Icon.grade) `**(`[Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None`) –
    
    A fine-tuning adjustment for the stroke thickness of the icon.
    
*   **`[icon](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/icon/#flet.Icon.icon) `**(`[IconData](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.IconData) `) –
    
    The icon to display, selected from a predefined icon set.
    
*   **`[optical_size](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/icon/#flet.Icon.optical_size) `**(`[Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None`) –
    
    Adjusts the icon's visual style for different sizes to maintain clarity and balance.
    
*   **`[semantics_label](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/icon/#flet.Icon.semantics_label) `**(`[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)  | None`) –
    
    An accessibility label for the icon.
    
*   **`[shadows](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/icon/#flet.Icon.shadows) `**(`[BoxShadowValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.BoxShadowValue)  | None`) –
    
    A list of shadows to apply beneath the icon.
    
*   **`[size](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/icon/#flet.Icon.size) `**(`[Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None`) –
    
    The size (width and height) of the square area the icon will occupy.
    
*   **`[weight](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/icon/#flet.Icon.weight) `**(`[Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None`) –
    
    The stroke weight (thickness) of the icon's lines.
    

Examples[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/icon/#examples "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------

To browse and visualize all available icons, visit our [icons browser](https://examples.flet.dev/icons_browser/)

[Live example](https://flet-controls-gallery.fly.dev/displays/icon)

### Basic Example[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/icon/#basic-example "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/icon/#__codelineno-0-1) from typing import cast [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/icon/#__codelineno-0-2)[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/icon/#__codelineno-0-3) import flet as ft [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/icon/#__codelineno-0-4) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/icon/#__codelineno-0-5)[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/icon/#__codelineno-0-6) def main(page: ft.Page):     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/icon/#__codelineno-0-7)     page.add(        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/icon/#__codelineno-0-8)         # material        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/icon/#__codelineno-0-9)         ft.Row(            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/icon/#__codelineno-0-10)             controls=[                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/icon/#__codelineno-0-11)                 ft.Icon(ft.Icons.FAVORITE, color=ft.Colors.PINK),                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/icon/#__codelineno-0-12)                 ft.Icon(ft.Icons.AUDIOTRACK, color=ft.Colors.GREEN_400, size=30),                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/icon/#__codelineno-0-13)                 ft.Icon(ft.Icons.BEACH_ACCESS, color=ft.Colors.BLUE, size=50),                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/icon/#__codelineno-0-14)                 ft.Icon(ft.Icons.SETTINGS, color="#c1c1c1"),            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/icon/#__codelineno-0-15)             ]        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/icon/#__codelineno-0-16)         ),        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/icon/#__codelineno-0-17)         # cupertino        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/icon/#__codelineno-0-18)         ft.Row(            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/icon/#__codelineno-0-19)             controls=[                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/icon/#__codelineno-0-20)                 ft.Icon(ft.CupertinoIcons.PROFILE_CIRCLED, color=ft.Colors.PINK),                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/icon/#__codelineno-0-21)                 ft.Icon(                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/icon/#__codelineno-0-22)                     icon=cast(ft.CupertinoIcons, ft.CupertinoIcons.random()),                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/icon/#__codelineno-0-23)                     color=ft.Colors.GREEN_400,                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/icon/#__codelineno-0-24)                     size=30,                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/icon/#__codelineno-0-25)                 ),                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/icon/#__codelineno-0-26)                 ft.Icon(                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/icon/#__codelineno-0-27)                     icon=cast(ft.CupertinoIcons, ft.CupertinoIcons.random()),                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/icon/#__codelineno-0-28)                     color=ft.Colors.BLUE,                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/icon/#__codelineno-0-29)                     size=50,                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/icon/#__codelineno-0-30)                 ),                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/icon/#__codelineno-0-31)                 ft.Icon(                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/icon/#__codelineno-0-32)                     icon=cast(ft.CupertinoIcons, ft.CupertinoIcons.random()),                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/icon/#__codelineno-0-33)                     color="#c1c1c1",                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/icon/#__codelineno-0-34)                 ),            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/icon/#__codelineno-0-35)             ]        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/icon/#__codelineno-0-36)         ),    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/icon/#__codelineno-0-37)     ) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/icon/#__codelineno-0-38) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/icon/#__codelineno-0-39)[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/icon/#__codelineno-0-40) if __name__ == "__main__":     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/icon/#__codelineno-0-41)     ft.run(main)`

[![basic](https://docs.flet.dev/examples/controls/icon/media/basic.png)](https://docs.flet.dev/examples/controls/icon/media/basic.png)

Properties[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/icon/#flet.Icon-properties "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### apply\_text\_scaling `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/icon/#flet.Icon.apply_text_scaling "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/icon/#__codelineno-0-1) apply_text_scaling: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)  | None = None`

Whether to scale the icon based on the system or user's preferred text size.

Useful when placing icons alongside text, ensuring both scale consistently for better readability and accessibility.

### blend\_mode `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/icon/#flet.Icon.blend_mode "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/icon/#__codelineno-0-1) blend_mode: [BlendMode](https://docs.flet.dev/types/blendmode/#flet.BlendMode)  | None = [SRC_OVER](https://docs.flet.dev/types/blendmode/#flet.BlendMode.SRC_OVER)`

The blend mode used when rendering the icon.

Blend modes control how the icon's color interacts with the background.

### color `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/icon/#flet.Icon.color "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/icon/#__codelineno-0-1) color: [ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None = None`

The color to use when drawing the icon.

### fill `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/icon/#flet.Icon.fill "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/icon/#__codelineno-0-1) fill: [Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None = None`

The fill amount of the icon, between `0.0` (outline) and `1.0` (solid).

This feature requires the icon's font to support fill variation. It can be used to indicate state transitions or selection visually.

Raises:

*   `[ValueError](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ValueError) ` –
    
    If it is not between `0.0` and `1.0`, inclusive.
    

### grade `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/icon/#flet.Icon.grade "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/icon/#__codelineno-0-1) grade: [Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None = None`

A fine-tuning adjustment for the stroke thickness of the icon.

This requires support from the icon's font. Grade values can be negative or positive. It allows precise visual adjustments without changing icon size.

### icon `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/icon/#flet.Icon.icon "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/icon/#__codelineno-0-1) icon: [IconData](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.IconData)`

The icon to display, selected from a predefined icon set.

You can explore available icons using the [Flet Icons Browser](https://examples.flet.dev/icons_browser/)
.

### optical\_size `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/icon/#flet.Icon.optical_size "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/icon/#__codelineno-0-1) optical_size: [Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None = None`

Adjusts the icon's visual style for different sizes to maintain clarity and balance.

This requires the icon font to support optical sizing.

Raises:

*   `[ValueError](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ValueError) ` –
    
    If it is not strictly greater than `0.0`.
    

### semantics\_label `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/icon/#flet.Icon.semantics_label "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/icon/#__codelineno-0-1) semantics_label: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)  | None = None`

An accessibility label for the icon.

This text is not displayed visually but may be announced by screen readers or other assistive technologies.

### shadows `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/icon/#flet.Icon.shadows "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/icon/#__codelineno-0-1) shadows: [BoxShadowValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.BoxShadowValue)  | None = None`

A list of shadows to apply beneath the icon.

Use multiple shadows to simulate complex lighting effects. The order of shadows matters for how transparency is blended.

### size `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/icon/#flet.Icon.size "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/icon/#__codelineno-0-1) size: [Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None = None`

The size (width and height) of the square area the icon will occupy.

If not set, a default size will be used. When placing this icon inside other controls (such as buttons), those controls may also affect sizing.

### weight `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/icon/#flet.Icon.weight "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/icon/#__codelineno-0-1) weight: [Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None = None`

The stroke weight (thickness) of the icon's lines.

This requires the icon font to support weight variation.

Raises:

*   `[ValueError](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ValueError) ` –
    
    If it is not strictly greater than `0.0`.
    

Back to top

# Screenshot - Flet

 

[Skip to content](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/screenshot/#flet.Screenshot)

Screenshot
==========

Takes a screenshot of containing control.

Inherits: `[Control](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control) `

Properties

*   **`[content](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/screenshot/#flet.Screenshot.content) `**(`[Control](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control) `) –
    
    The control to be captured.
    

Methods

*   **`[capture](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/screenshot/#flet.Screenshot.capture) `** –
    
    Captures a screenshot of the enclosed content control.
    

Examples[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/screenshot/#examples "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Taking control screenshot[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/screenshot/#taking-control-screenshot "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/screenshot/#__codelineno-0-1) from pathlib import Path [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/screenshot/#__codelineno-0-2)[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/screenshot/#__codelineno-0-3) import flet as ft [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/screenshot/#__codelineno-0-4) from flet.utils.files import get_current_script_dir [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/screenshot/#__codelineno-0-5) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/screenshot/#__codelineno-0-6)[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/screenshot/#__codelineno-0-7) def main(page: ft.Page):     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/screenshot/#__codelineno-0-8)     async def take_screenshot():        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/screenshot/#__codelineno-0-9)         image = await scr.capture()        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/screenshot/#__codelineno-0-10)         with open(Path(get_current_script_dir(), "screenshot.png"), "wb") as f:            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/screenshot/#__codelineno-0-11)             f.write(image) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/screenshot/#__codelineno-0-12)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/screenshot/#__codelineno-0-13)     page.add(        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/screenshot/#__codelineno-0-14)         scr := ft.Screenshot(            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/screenshot/#__codelineno-0-15)             ft.Container(                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/screenshot/#__codelineno-0-16)                 ft.Button("Hello, world!", bgcolor=ft.Colors.BLUE, elevation=10),                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/screenshot/#__codelineno-0-17)                 padding=10,            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/screenshot/#__codelineno-0-18)             )        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/screenshot/#__codelineno-0-19)         ),        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/screenshot/#__codelineno-0-20)         ft.Button("Take screenshot", on_click=take_screenshot),    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/screenshot/#__codelineno-0-21)     ) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/screenshot/#__codelineno-0-22) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/screenshot/#__codelineno-0-23)[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/screenshot/#__codelineno-0-24) ft.run(main)`

Properties[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/screenshot/#flet.Screenshot-properties "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### content `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/screenshot/#flet.Screenshot.content "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/screenshot/#__codelineno-0-1) content: [Control](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control)`

The control to be captured.

Methods[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/screenshot/#flet.Screenshot-methods "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### capture `async` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/screenshot/#flet.Screenshot.capture "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/screenshot/#__codelineno-0-1) capture(     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/screenshot/#__codelineno-0-2)     pixel_ratio: [Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None = None,    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/screenshot/#__codelineno-0-3)     delay: [DurationValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.DurationValue)  | None = None, [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/screenshot/#__codelineno-0-4) ) -> [bytes](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#bytes)`

Captures a screenshot of the enclosed content control.

Parameters:

*   **`pixel_ratio`** (`[Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None`, default: `None` ) –
    
    A pixel ratio of the captured screenshot. If `None`, device-specific pixel ratio will be used.
    
*   **`delay`** (`[DurationValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.DurationValue)  | None`, default: `None` ) –
    
    A delay before taking a screenshot. The delay will be 20 milliseconds if not specified.
    

Returns:

*   `[bytes](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#bytes) ` –
    
    Screenshot in PNG format.

# AdaptiveControl - Flet

 

[Skip to content](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/adaptivecontrol/#flet.AdaptiveControl)

AdaptiveControl
===============

Inherits: `[Control](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control) `

Base class for controls that support adaptive behavior, which allows them to adjust their appearance and behavior based on the target platform (ex: Material design on Android/Windows/Linux, Cupertino design on iOS/macOS).

The [`adaptive`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/adaptivecontrol/#flet.AdaptiveControl.adaptive)
 property is applicable in two common scenarios:

1.  **Platform-adaptive controls**: These controls have a corresponding version on both Material and Cupertino platforms. When `adaptive` is set to `True`, the control renders the appropriate platform-specific implementation.
    
2.  **Container controls**: Controls that contain children (ex: [`Row`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/row/#flet.Row)
    , [`Column`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#flet.Column)
    ) can pass the `adaptive` value down to their children that do not explicitly define it themselves. This enables nested adaptive behavior in complex layouts.
    

Extension developers can use this base class to create their own adaptive controls by checking the `adaptive` flag at runtime and rendering accordingly.

Note

This class does not implement any platform-specific rendering itself. It is up to the control inheriting from it to interpret the [`adaptive`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/adaptivecontrol/#flet.AdaptiveControl.adaptive)
 flag and render accordingly.

Properties

*   **`[adaptive](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/adaptivecontrol/#flet.AdaptiveControl.adaptive) `**(`[bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)  | None`) –
    
    Enables platform-specific rendering or inheritance of adaptiveness from parent controls.
    

Properties[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/adaptivecontrol/#flet.AdaptiveControl-properties "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### adaptive `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/adaptivecontrol/#flet.AdaptiveControl.adaptive "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/adaptivecontrol/#__codelineno-0-1) adaptive: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)  | None = None`

Enables platform-specific rendering or inheritance of adaptiveness from parent controls.

# SlidePicker - Flet

 

[Skip to content](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/slidepicker/#slidepicker)

SlidePicker[#](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/slidepicker/#slidepicker "Permanent link")

=============================================================================================

A slider-based color picker that exposes RGB/HSV/HSL channels with optional labels and indicators.

[![SlidePicker](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/extensions/color_pickers/golden/macos/color_pickers/slide_picker.png)](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/extensions/color_pickers/golden/macos/color_pickers/slide_picker.png)

Basic SlidePicker

Inherits: `[LayoutControl](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/layoutcontrol/#flet.LayoutControl) `

Properties

*   **`[color](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/slidepicker/#flet_color_pickers.SlidePicker.color) `**(`[ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None`) –
    
    The currently selected color.
    
*   **`[color_model](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/slidepicker/#flet_color_pickers.SlidePicker.color_model) `**(`ColorModel | None`) –
    
    The color model used by the sliders.
    
*   **`[display_thumb_color](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/slidepicker/#flet_color_pickers.SlidePicker.display_thumb_color) `**(`[bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool) `) –
    
    Whether to display the thumb color in sliders.
    
*   **`[enable_alpha](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/slidepicker/#flet_color_pickers.SlidePicker.enable_alpha) `**(`[bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool) `) –
    
    Whether to enable alpha (opacity) slider.
    
*   **`[indicator_alignment_begin](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/slidepicker/#flet_color_pickers.SlidePicker.indicator_alignment_begin) `**(`[Alignment](https://docs.flet.dev/types/alignment/#flet.Alignment)  | None`) –
    
    Alignment for the indicator split begin.
    
*   **`[indicator_alignment_end](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/slidepicker/#flet_color_pickers.SlidePicker.indicator_alignment_end) `**(`[Alignment](https://docs.flet.dev/types/alignment/#flet.Alignment)  | None`) –
    
    Alignment for the indicator split end.
    
*   **`[indicator_border_radius](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/slidepicker/#flet_color_pickers.SlidePicker.indicator_border_radius) `**(`[BorderRadiusValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.BorderRadiusValue)  | None`) –
    
    Border radius for the indicator.
    
*   **`[indicator_size](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/slidepicker/#flet_color_pickers.SlidePicker.indicator_size) `**(`[Size](https://docs.flet.dev/types/size/#flet.Size)  | None`) –
    
    Size of the indicator.
    
*   **`[label_text_style](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/slidepicker/#flet_color_pickers.SlidePicker.label_text_style) `**(`[TextStyle](https://docs.flet.dev/types/textstyle/#flet.TextStyle)  | None`) –
    
    Text style for labels.
    
*   **`[label_types](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/slidepicker/#flet_color_pickers.SlidePicker.label_types) `**(`[list](https://docs.python.org/3/glossary.html#term-list) [ColorLabelType] | None`) –
    
    Color label types to display.
    
*   **`[show_indicator](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/slidepicker/#flet_color_pickers.SlidePicker.show_indicator) `**(`[bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool) `) –
    
    Whether to show the color indicator.
    
*   **`[show_label](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/slidepicker/#flet_color_pickers.SlidePicker.show_label) `**(`[bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool) `) –
    
    Whether to show labels.
    
*   **`[show_params](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/slidepicker/#flet_color_pickers.SlidePicker.show_params) `**(`[bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool) `) –
    
    Whether to show parameter values.
    
*   **`[show_slider_text](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/slidepicker/#flet_color_pickers.SlidePicker.show_slider_text) `**(`[bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool) `) –
    
    Whether to show slider text.
    
*   **`[slider_size](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/slidepicker/#flet_color_pickers.SlidePicker.slider_size) `**(`[Size](https://docs.flet.dev/types/size/#flet.Size)  | None`) –
    
    Size of the sliders.
    
*   **`[slider_text_style](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/slidepicker/#flet_color_pickers.SlidePicker.slider_text_style) `**(`[TextStyle](https://docs.flet.dev/types/textstyle/#flet.TextStyle)  | None`) –
    
    Text style for slider text.
    

Events

*   **`[on_color_change](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/slidepicker/#flet_color_pickers.SlidePicker.on_color_change) `**(`[ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[SlidePicker](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/slidepicker/#flet_color_pickers.SlidePicker) ] | None`) –
    
    Called when the picker color is changed.
    

Example[#](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/slidepicker/#example "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/slidepicker/#__codelineno-0-1) import flet as ft [](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/slidepicker/#__codelineno-0-2) from flet_color_pickers import ColorModel, SlidePicker [](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/slidepicker/#__codelineno-0-3) [](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/slidepicker/#__codelineno-0-4)[](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/slidepicker/#__codelineno-0-5) def main(page: ft.Page):     [](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/slidepicker/#__codelineno-0-6)     page.title = "SlidePicker"    [](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/slidepicker/#__codelineno-0-7)     page.padding = 20 [](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/slidepicker/#__codelineno-0-8)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/slidepicker/#__codelineno-0-9)     def on_color_change(e: ft.ControlEvent):        [](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/slidepicker/#__codelineno-0-10)         print(f"color: {e.data}") [](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/slidepicker/#__codelineno-0-11)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/slidepicker/#__codelineno-0-12)     picker = SlidePicker(        [](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/slidepicker/#__codelineno-0-13)         color="#0000ff",        [](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/slidepicker/#__codelineno-0-14)         color_model=ColorModel.RGB,        [](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/slidepicker/#__codelineno-0-15)         indicator_border_radius=ft.BorderRadius.all(5),        [](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/slidepicker/#__codelineno-0-16)         on_color_change=on_color_change,    [](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/slidepicker/#__codelineno-0-17)     ) [](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/slidepicker/#__codelineno-0-18)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/slidepicker/#__codelineno-0-19)     page.add(picker) [](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/slidepicker/#__codelineno-0-20) [](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/slidepicker/#__codelineno-0-21)[](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/slidepicker/#__codelineno-0-22) ft.run(main)`

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/slidepicker/#__codelineno-0-1) indicator_border_radius: [BorderRadiusValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.BorderRadiusValue)  | None = None`

Border radius for the indicator.

### indicator\_size `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/slidepicker/#flet_color_pickers.SlidePicker.indicator_size "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/slidepicker/#__codelineno-0-1) indicator_size: [Size](https://docs.flet.dev/types/size/#flet.Size)  | None = None`

Size of the indicator.

### label\_text\_style `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/slidepicker/#flet_color_pickers.SlidePicker.label_text_style "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/slidepicker/#__codelineno-0-1) label_text_style: [TextStyle](https://docs.flet.dev/types/textstyle/#flet.TextStyle)  | None = None`

Text style for labels.

### label\_types `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/slidepicker/#flet_color_pickers.SlidePicker.label_types "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/slidepicker/#__codelineno-0-1) label_types: [list](https://docs.python.org/3/glossary.html#term-list) [ColorLabelType] | None = None`

Color label types to display.

### show\_indicator `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/slidepicker/#flet_color_pickers.SlidePicker.show_indicator "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/slidepicker/#__codelineno-0-1) show_indicator: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)  = True`

Whether to show the color indicator.

### show\_label `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/slidepicker/#flet_color_pickers.SlidePicker.show_label "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/slidepicker/#__codelineno-0-1) show_label: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)  = True`

Whether to show labels.

### show\_params `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/slidepicker/#flet_color_pickers.SlidePicker.show_params "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/slidepicker/#__codelineno-0-1) show_params: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)  = True`

Whether to show parameter values.

### show\_slider\_text `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/slidepicker/#flet_color_pickers.SlidePicker.show_slider_text "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/slidepicker/#__codelineno-0-1) show_slider_text: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)  = True`

Whether to show slider text.

### slider\_size `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/slidepicker/#flet_color_pickers.SlidePicker.slider_size "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/slidepicker/#__codelineno-0-1) slider_size: [Size](https://docs.flet.dev/types/size/#flet.Size)  | None = None`

Size of the sliders.

### slider\_text\_style `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/slidepicker/#flet_color_pickers.SlidePicker.slider_text_style "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/slidepicker/#__codelineno-0-1) slider_text_style: [TextStyle](https://docs.flet.dev/types/textstyle/#flet.TextStyle)  | None = None`

Text style for slider text.

Events[#](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/slidepicker/#flet_color_pickers.SlidePicker-events "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### on\_color\_change `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/slidepicker/#flet_color_pickers.SlidePicker.on_color_change "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/slidepicker/#__codelineno-0-1) on_color_change: [ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[SlidePicker](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/slidepicker/#flet_color_pickers.SlidePicker) ] | None = (     [](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/slidepicker/#__codelineno-0-2)     None [](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/slidepicker/#__codelineno-0-3) )`

Called when the picker color is changed.

The [`data`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/event/#flet.Event.data)
 property of the event handler argument contains the color value as a hex string.

# ColorPicker - Flet

 

[Skip to content](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/colorpicker/#colorpicker)

ColorPicker[#](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/colorpicker/#colorpicker "Permanent link")

=============================================================================================

A full-featured color picker with palette, sliders, labels, and optional history.

[![ColorPicker](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/extensions/color_pickers/golden/macos/color_pickers/image_for_docs.png)](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/extensions/color_pickers/golden/macos/color_pickers/image_for_docs.png)

Basic ColorPicker

Inherits: `[LayoutControl](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/layoutcontrol/#flet.LayoutControl) `

Properties

*   **`[color](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/colorpicker/#flet_color_pickers.ColorPicker.color) `**(`[ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None`) –
    
    The currently selected color.
    
*   **`[color_history](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/colorpicker/#flet_color_pickers.ColorPicker.color_history) `**(`[list](https://docs.python.org/3/glossary.html#term-list) [[ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue) ] | None`) –
    
    A list of colors to display as a history palette.
    
*   **`[color_picker_width](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/colorpicker/#flet_color_pickers.ColorPicker.color_picker_width) `**(`[Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None`) –
    
    Width of the color picker in virtual pixels.
    
*   **`[display_thumb_color](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/colorpicker/#flet_color_pickers.ColorPicker.display_thumb_color) `**(`[bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool) `) –
    
    Whether to display the thumb color in slider.
    
*   **`[enable_alpha](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/colorpicker/#flet_color_pickers.ColorPicker.enable_alpha) `**(`[bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool) `) –
    
    Whether to enable alpha (opacity) slider.
    
*   **`[hex_input_bar](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/colorpicker/#flet_color_pickers.ColorPicker.hex_input_bar) `**(`[bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool) `) –
    
    Whether to show the hex input bar.
    
*   **`[hsv_color](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/colorpicker/#flet_color_pickers.ColorPicker.hsv_color) `**(`HsvColor | None`) –
    
    The currently selected HSV color.
    
*   **`[label_text_style](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/colorpicker/#flet_color_pickers.ColorPicker.label_text_style) `**(`[TextStyle](https://docs.flet.dev/types/textstyle/#flet.TextStyle)  | None`) –
    
    Text style for labels.
    
*   **`[label_types](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/colorpicker/#flet_color_pickers.ColorPicker.label_types) `**(`[list](https://docs.python.org/3/glossary.html#term-list) [ColorLabelType] | None`) –
    
    Color label types to display.
    
*   **`[palette_type](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/colorpicker/#flet_color_pickers.ColorPicker.palette_type) `**(`PaletteType | None`) –
    
    Palette type for the picker area.
    
*   **`[picker_area_border_radius](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/colorpicker/#flet_color_pickers.ColorPicker.picker_area_border_radius) `**(`[BorderRadiusValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.BorderRadiusValue)  | None`) –
    
    Border radius for the picker area.
    
*   **`[picker_area_height_percent](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/colorpicker/#flet_color_pickers.ColorPicker.picker_area_height_percent) `**(`[Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None`) –
    
    Height of the picker area as a percentage of the picker width.
    

Events

*   **`[on_color_change](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/colorpicker/#flet_color_pickers.ColorPicker.on_color_change) `**(`[ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[ColorPicker](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/colorpicker/#flet_color_pickers.ColorPicker) ] | None`) –
    
    Called when the picker color is changed.
    
*   **`[on_history_change](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/colorpicker/#flet_color_pickers.ColorPicker.on_history_change) `**(`[ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[ColorPicker](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/colorpicker/#flet_color_pickers.ColorPicker) ] | None`) –
    
    Called when the history palette is changed.
    
*   **`[on_hsv_color_change](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/colorpicker/#flet_color_pickers.ColorPicker.on_hsv_color_change) `**(`[ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[ColorPicker](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/colorpicker/#flet_color_pickers.ColorPicker) ] | None`) –
    
    Called when the picker HSV color is changed.
    

Example[#](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/colorpicker/#example "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/colorpicker/#__codelineno-0-1) import flet as ft [](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/colorpicker/#__codelineno-0-2) from flet_color_pickers import ColorLabelType, ColorPicker, PaletteType [](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/colorpicker/#__codelineno-0-3) [](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/colorpicker/#__codelineno-0-4)[](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/colorpicker/#__codelineno-0-5) def main(page: ft.Page):     [](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/colorpicker/#__codelineno-0-6)     page.title = "ColorPicker"    [](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/colorpicker/#__codelineno-0-7)     page.padding = 20 [](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/colorpicker/#__codelineno-0-8)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/colorpicker/#__codelineno-0-9)     def on_color_change(e: ft.ControlEvent):        [](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/colorpicker/#__codelineno-0-10)         print(f"color: {e.data}") [](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/colorpicker/#__codelineno-0-11)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/colorpicker/#__codelineno-0-12)     def on_history_change(e: ft.ControlEvent):        [](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/colorpicker/#__codelineno-0-13)         # e.data is a list of hex strings        [](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/colorpicker/#__codelineno-0-14)         print(f"history: {e.data}") [](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/colorpicker/#__codelineno-0-15)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/colorpicker/#__codelineno-0-16)     def on_hsv_color_change(e: ft.ControlEvent):        [](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/colorpicker/#__codelineno-0-17)         print("hsv: ", e.control.hsv_color) [](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/colorpicker/#__codelineno-0-18)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/colorpicker/#__codelineno-0-19)     picker = ColorPicker(        [](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/colorpicker/#__codelineno-0-20)         color="#ff0000",        [](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/colorpicker/#__codelineno-0-21)         # hsv_color=HsvColor(alpha=1, hue=0, saturation=1, value=1),        [](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/colorpicker/#__codelineno-0-22)         color_history=[            [](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/colorpicker/#__codelineno-0-23)             "#ff0000",            [](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/colorpicker/#__codelineno-0-24)             "#00ff00",            [](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/colorpicker/#__codelineno-0-25)             "#0000ff",            [](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/colorpicker/#__codelineno-0-26)             "#ffff00",            [](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/colorpicker/#__codelineno-0-27)             "#00ffff",            [](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/colorpicker/#__codelineno-0-28)             "#ff00ff",        [](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/colorpicker/#__codelineno-0-29)         ],        [](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/colorpicker/#__codelineno-0-30)         on_color_change=on_color_change,        [](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/colorpicker/#__codelineno-0-31)         palette_type=PaletteType.RGB_WITH_GREEN,        [](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/colorpicker/#__codelineno-0-32)         on_history_change=on_history_change,        [](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/colorpicker/#__codelineno-0-33)         on_hsv_color_change=on_hsv_color_change,        [](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/colorpicker/#__codelineno-0-34)         label_types=[            [](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/colorpicker/#__codelineno-0-35)             ColorLabelType.HEX,            [](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/colorpicker/#__codelineno-0-36)             ColorLabelType.RGB,        [](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/colorpicker/#__codelineno-0-37)         ],        [](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/colorpicker/#__codelineno-0-38)         picker_area_border_radius=ft.BorderRadius.all(20),    [](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/colorpicker/#__codelineno-0-39)     ) [](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/colorpicker/#__codelineno-0-40)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/colorpicker/#__codelineno-0-41)     page.add(picker) [](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/colorpicker/#__codelineno-0-42) [](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/colorpicker/#__codelineno-0-43)[](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/colorpicker/#__codelineno-0-44) ft.run(main)`

Properties[#](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/colorpicker/#flet_color_pickers.ColorPicker-properties "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### color `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/colorpicker/#flet_color_pickers.ColorPicker.color "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/colorpicker/#__codelineno-0-1) color: [ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None = None`

The currently selected color.

### color\_history `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/colorpicker/#flet_color_pickers.ColorPicker.color_history "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/colorpicker/#__codelineno-0-1) color_history: [list](https://docs.python.org/3/glossary.html#term-list) [[ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue) ] | None = None`

A list of colors to display as a history palette.

To be notified when the palette changes, set `on_history_change`.

### color\_picker\_width `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/colorpicker/#flet_color_pickers.ColorPicker.color_picker_width "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/colorpicker/#__codelineno-0-1) color_picker_width: [Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None = None`

Width of the color picker in virtual pixels.

### display\_thumb\_color `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/colorpicker/#flet_color_pickers.ColorPicker.display_thumb_color "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/colorpicker/#__codelineno-0-1) display_thumb_color: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)  = True`

Whether to display the thumb color in slider.

### enable\_alpha `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/colorpicker/#flet_color_pickers.ColorPicker.enable_alpha "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/colorpicker/#__codelineno-0-1) enable_alpha: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)  = True`

Whether to enable alpha (opacity) slider.

### hex\_input\_bar `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/colorpicker/#flet_color_pickers.ColorPicker.hex_input_bar "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/colorpicker/#__codelineno-0-1) hex_input_bar: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)  = True`

Whether to show the hex input bar.

### hsv\_color `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/colorpicker/#flet_color_pickers.ColorPicker.hsv_color "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/colorpicker/#__codelineno-0-1) hsv_color: HsvColor | None = None`

The currently selected HSV color.

Provide an `HsvColor` instance with fields: `alpha`, `hue`, `saturation`, `value`.

### label\_text\_style `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/colorpicker/#flet_color_pickers.ColorPicker.label_text_style "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/colorpicker/#__codelineno-0-1) label_text_style: [TextStyle](https://docs.flet.dev/types/textstyle/#flet.TextStyle)  | None = None`

Text style for labels.

### label\_types `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/colorpicker/#flet_color_pickers.ColorPicker.label_types "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/colorpicker/#__codelineno-0-1) label_types: [list](https://docs.python.org/3/glossary.html#term-list) [ColorLabelType] | None = None`

Color label types to display.

### palette\_type `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/colorpicker/#flet_color_pickers.ColorPicker.palette_type "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/colorpicker/#__codelineno-0-1) palette_type: PaletteType | None = None`

Palette type for the picker area.

### picker\_area\_border\_radius `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/colorpicker/#flet_color_pickers.ColorPicker.picker_area_border_radius "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/colorpicker/#__codelineno-0-1) picker_area_border_radius: [BorderRadiusValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.BorderRadiusValue)  | None = None`

Border radius for the picker area.

### picker\_area\_height\_percent `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/colorpicker/#flet_color_pickers.ColorPicker.picker_area_height_percent "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/colorpicker/#__codelineno-0-1) picker_area_height_percent: [Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None = None`

Height of the picker area as a percentage of the picker width.

Events[#](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/colorpicker/#flet_color_pickers.ColorPicker-events "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### on\_color\_change `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/colorpicker/#flet_color_pickers.ColorPicker.on_color_change "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/colorpicker/#__codelineno-0-1) on_color_change: [ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[ColorPicker](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/colorpicker/#flet_color_pickers.ColorPicker) ] | None = (     [](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/colorpicker/#__codelineno-0-2)     None [](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/colorpicker/#__codelineno-0-3) )`

Called when the picker color is changed.

The [`data`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/event/#flet.Event.data)
 property of the event handler argument contains the color value as a hex string.

### on\_history\_change `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/colorpicker/#flet_color_pickers.ColorPicker.on_history_change "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/colorpicker/#__codelineno-0-1) on_history_change: (     [](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/colorpicker/#__codelineno-0-2)     [ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[ColorPicker](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/colorpicker/#flet_color_pickers.ColorPicker) ] | None [](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/colorpicker/#__codelineno-0-3) ) = None`

Called when the history palette is changed.

The [`data`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/event/#flet.Event.data)
 property of the event handler argument contains the list of color values as hex strings.

### on\_hsv\_color\_change `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/colorpicker/#flet_color_pickers.ColorPicker.on_hsv_color_change "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/colorpicker/#__codelineno-0-1) on_hsv_color_change: (     [](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/colorpicker/#__codelineno-0-2)     [ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[ColorPicker](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/colorpicker/#flet_color_pickers.ColorPicker) ] | None [](https://docs.flet.dev/colorpickers/colorpicker/#__codelineno-0-3) ) = None`

Called when the picker HSV color is changed.

The [`data`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/event/#flet.Event.data)
 property of the event handler argument contains the HSV values as a dict with keys: `alpha`, `hue`, `saturation`, `value`.

# ProgressBar - Flet

 

[Skip to content](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/progressbar/#flet.ProgressBar)

ProgressBar
===========

A material design linear progress indicator, also known as a progress bar.

A control that shows progress along a line.

Example:

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/progressbar/#__codelineno-0-1) ft.ProgressBar(width=400, value=0.8)`

[![ProgressBar](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/material/golden/macos/progress_bar/image_for_docs.png)](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/material/golden/macos/progress_bar/image_for_docs.png)

Fixed progress bar

Inherits: `[LayoutControl](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/layoutcontrol/#flet.LayoutControl) `

Properties

*   **`[bar_height](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/progressbar/#flet.ProgressBar.bar_height) `**(`[Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None`) –
    
    The minimum height of the line used to draw the linear indicator.
    
*   **`[bgcolor](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/progressbar/#flet.ProgressBar.bgcolor) `**(`[ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None`) –
    
    Color of the track being filled by the linear indicator.
    
*   **`[border_radius](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/progressbar/#flet.ProgressBar.border_radius) `**(`[BorderRadiusValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.BorderRadiusValue)  | None`) –
    
    The border radius of both the indicator and the track.
    
*   **`[color](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/progressbar/#flet.ProgressBar.color) `**(`[ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None`) –
    
    The progress indicator's color.
    
*   **`[semantics_label](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/progressbar/#flet.ProgressBar.semantics_label) `**(`[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)  | None`) –
    
    The semantics label for this progress indicator.
    
*   **`[semantics_value](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/progressbar/#flet.ProgressBar.semantics_value) `**(`[Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None`) –
    
    The semantics label for this progress indicator.
    
*   **`[stop_indicator_color](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/progressbar/#flet.ProgressBar.stop_indicator_color) `**(`[ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None`) –
    
    The color of the stop indicator.
    
*   **`[stop_indicator_radius](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/progressbar/#flet.ProgressBar.stop_indicator_radius) `**(`[Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None`) –
    
    The radius of the stop indicator.
    
*   **`[track_gap](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/progressbar/#flet.ProgressBar.track_gap) `**(`[Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None`) –
    
    The gap between the indicator and the track.
    
*   **`[value](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/progressbar/#flet.ProgressBar.value) `**(`[Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None`) –
    
    The value of this progress indicator.
    
*   **`[year_2023](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/progressbar/#flet.ProgressBar.year_2023) `**(`[bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)  | None`) –
    
    If this is set to `False`, the ProgressBar will use the latest Material Design 3 appearance, which was introduced in December 2023.
    

Examples[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/progressbar/#examples "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[Live example](https://flet-controls-gallery.fly.dev/displays/progressbar)

### Determinate and indeterminate progress bars[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/progressbar/#determinate-and-indeterminate-progress-bars "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/progressbar/#__codelineno-0-1) import asyncio [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/progressbar/#__codelineno-0-2)[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/progressbar/#__codelineno-0-3) import flet as ft [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/progressbar/#__codelineno-0-4) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/progressbar/#__codelineno-0-5)[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/progressbar/#__codelineno-0-6) async def main(page: ft.Page):     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/progressbar/#__codelineno-0-7)     determinate_bar = ft.ProgressBar(width=400)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/progressbar/#__codelineno-0-8)     determinate_message = ft.Text("Doing something...") [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/progressbar/#__codelineno-0-9)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/progressbar/#__codelineno-0-10)     page.add(        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/progressbar/#__codelineno-0-11)         ft.Text(            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/progressbar/#__codelineno-0-12)             value="Linear progress indicator",            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/progressbar/#__codelineno-0-13)             theme_style=ft.TextThemeStyle.HEADLINE_SMALL,        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/progressbar/#__codelineno-0-14)         ),        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/progressbar/#__codelineno-0-15)         ft.Column(controls=[determinate_message, determinate_bar]),        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/progressbar/#__codelineno-0-16)         ft.Text(            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/progressbar/#__codelineno-0-17)             value="Indeterminate progress bar",            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/progressbar/#__codelineno-0-18)             theme_style=ft.TextThemeStyle.HEADLINE_SMALL,        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/progressbar/#__codelineno-0-19)         ),        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/progressbar/#__codelineno-0-20)         ft.ProgressBar(width=400, color=ft.Colors.AMBER),    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/progressbar/#__codelineno-0-21)     ) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/progressbar/#__codelineno-0-22)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/progressbar/#__codelineno-0-23)     for i in range(0, 101):        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/progressbar/#__codelineno-0-24)         determinate_bar.value = i * 0.01        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/progressbar/#__codelineno-0-25)         await asyncio.sleep(0.1)        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/progressbar/#__codelineno-0-26)         if i == 100:            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/progressbar/#__codelineno-0-27)             determinate_message.value = "Finished!"        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/progressbar/#__codelineno-0-28)         page.update() [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/progressbar/#__codelineno-0-29) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/progressbar/#__codelineno-0-30)[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/progressbar/#__codelineno-0-31) if __name__ == "__main__":     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/progressbar/#__codelineno-0-32)     ft.run(main)`

[![determinate-and-indeterminate](https://docs.flet.dev/examples/controls/progress_bar/media/determinate_and_indeterminate.gif)](https://docs.flet.dev/examples/controls/progress_bar/media/determinate_and_indeterminate.gif)

Properties[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/progressbar/#flet.ProgressBar-properties "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### bar\_height `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/progressbar/#flet.ProgressBar.bar_height "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/progressbar/#__codelineno-0-1) bar_height: [Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None = None`

The minimum height of the line used to draw the linear indicator.

Raises:

*   `[ValueError](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ValueError) ` –
    
    If it is not greater than or equal to `0`.
    

### bgcolor `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/progressbar/#flet.ProgressBar.bgcolor "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/progressbar/#__codelineno-0-1) bgcolor: [ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None = None`

Color of the track being filled by the linear indicator.

### border\_radius `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/progressbar/#flet.ProgressBar.border_radius "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/progressbar/#__codelineno-0-1) border_radius: [BorderRadiusValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.BorderRadiusValue)  | None = None`

The border radius of both the indicator and the track.

Defaults to `BorderRadius.all(0)` - rectangular shape.

### color `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/progressbar/#flet.ProgressBar.color "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/progressbar/#__codelineno-0-1) color: [ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None = None`

The progress indicator's color.

### semantics\_label `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/progressbar/#flet.ProgressBar.semantics_label "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/progressbar/#__codelineno-0-1) semantics_label: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)  | None = None`

The semantics label for this progress indicator.

### semantics\_value `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/progressbar/#flet.ProgressBar.semantics_value "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/progressbar/#__codelineno-0-1) semantics_value: [Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None = None`

The semantics label for this progress indicator.

Raises:

*   `[ValueError](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ValueError) ` –
    
    If it is not greater than or equal to `0`.
    

### stop\_indicator\_color `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/progressbar/#flet.ProgressBar.stop_indicator_color "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/progressbar/#__codelineno-0-1) stop_indicator_color: [ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None = None`

The color of the stop indicator.

If [`ProgressBar.year_2023`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/progressbar/#flet.ProgressBar.year_2023)
 is `True` or [`Theme.use_material3`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/theme/#flet.Theme.use_material3)
 is `False`, then no stop indicator will be drawn.

If not set, then the [`ProgressIndicatorTheme.stop_indicator_color`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/progressindicatortheme/#flet.ProgressIndicatorTheme.stop_indicator_color)
 will be used. If that is not set, then the [`ColorScheme.primary`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/colorscheme/#flet.ColorScheme.primary)
 will be used.

### stop\_indicator\_radius `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/progressbar/#flet.ProgressBar.stop_indicator_radius "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/progressbar/#__codelineno-0-1) stop_indicator_radius: [Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None = None`

The radius of the stop indicator.

If [`ProgressBar.year_2023`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/progressbar/#flet.ProgressBar.year_2023)
 is `True` or [`Theme.use_material3`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/theme/#flet.Theme.use_material3)
 is `False`, then no stop indicator will be drawn.

Set `stop_indicator_radius` to `0` to hide the stop indicator.

If not set, then the [`ProgressIndicatorTheme.stop_indicator_radius`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/progressindicatortheme/#flet.ProgressIndicatorTheme.stop_indicator_radius)
 will be used. If that is not set, then defaults to `2`.

### track\_gap `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/progressbar/#flet.ProgressBar.track_gap "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/progressbar/#__codelineno-0-1) track_gap: [Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None = None`

The gap between the indicator and the track.

If [`ProgressBar.year_2023`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/progressbar/#flet.ProgressBar.year_2023)
 is `True` or [`Theme.use_material3`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/theme/#flet.Theme.use_material3)
 is `False`, then no track gap will be drawn.

If not set, then the [`ProgressIndicatorTheme.track_gap`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/progressindicatortheme/#flet.ProgressIndicatorTheme.track_gap)
 will be used. If that is not set, then defaults to `4`.

Tip

Set `track_gap` to `0` to hide the track gap.

### value `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/progressbar/#flet.ProgressBar.value "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/progressbar/#__codelineno-0-1) value: [Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None = None`

The value of this progress indicator.

A value of `0.0` means no progress and `1.0` means that progress is complete. The value will be clamped to be in the range `0.0` - `1.0`.

Defaults to `None`, meaning that this progress indicator is indeterminate - displays a predetermined animation that does not indicate how much actual progress is being made.

Raises:

*   `[ValueError](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ValueError) ` –
    
    If it is not greater than or equal to `0`.
    

### year\_2023 `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/progressbar/#flet.ProgressBar.year_2023 "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/progressbar/#__codelineno-0-1) year_2023: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)  | None = None`

If this is set to `False`, the ProgressBar will use the latest Material Design 3 appearance, which was introduced in December 2023.

When `True`, the ProgressBar will use the 2023 Material Design 3 appearance.

If not set, then the [`ProgressIndicatorTheme.year_2023`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/progressindicatortheme/#flet.ProgressIndicatorTheme.year_2023)
 will be used, which is `False` by default.

If [`Theme.use_material3`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/theme/#flet.Theme.use_material3)
 is `False`, then this property is ignored.

Back to top

---

# Draggable - Flet

 

[Skip to content](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/draggable/#flet.Draggable)

Draggable
=========

A control that can be dragged from to a [`DragTarget`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/dragtarget/#flet.DragTarget)
.

When a draggable control recognizes the start of a drag gesture, it displays the [`content_feedback`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/draggable/#flet.Draggable.content_feedback)
 control that tracks the user's finger across the screen. If the user lifts their finger while on top of a `DragTarget`, this target is given the opportunity to complete drag-and-drop flow.

Inherits: `[Control](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control) `

Properties

*   **`[affinity](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/draggable/#flet.Draggable.affinity) `**(`[Axis](https://docs.flet.dev/types/axis/#flet.Axis)  | None`) –
    
    Specifies the axis along which this control competes with other gestures to initiate a drag.
    
*   **`[axis](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/draggable/#flet.Draggable.axis) `**(`[Axis](https://docs.flet.dev/types/axis/#flet.Axis)  | None`) –
    
    Restricts the draggable's movement to a specific axis.
    
*   **`[content](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/draggable/#flet.Draggable.content) `**(`[Control](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control) `) –
    
    The control to display when the draggable is not being dragged.
    
*   **`[content_feedback](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/draggable/#flet.Draggable.content_feedback) `**(`[Control](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control)  | None`) –
    
    The control to show under the pointer when a drag is under way.
    
*   **`[content_when_dragging](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/draggable/#flet.Draggable.content_when_dragging) `**(`[Control](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control)  | None`) –
    
    The control to display instead of [`content`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/draggable/#flet.Draggable.content)
     when this draggable is being dragged.
    
*   **`[group](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/draggable/#flet.Draggable.group) `**(`[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str) `) –
    
    The group this draggable belongs to.
    
*   **`[max_simultaneous_drags](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/draggable/#flet.Draggable.max_simultaneous_drags) `**(`[int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int)  | None`) –
    
    Specifies how many simultaneous drag operations are allowed for this draggable.
    

Events

*   **`[on_drag_complete](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/draggable/#flet.Draggable.on_drag_complete) `**(`[ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[Draggable](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/draggable/#flet.Draggable) ] | None`) –
    
    Called when this draggable is dropped and accepted by a [`DragTarget`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/dragtarget/#flet.DragTarget)
    .
    
*   **`[on_drag_start](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/draggable/#flet.Draggable.on_drag_start) `**(`[ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[Draggable](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/draggable/#flet.Draggable) ] | None`) –
    
    Called when this draggable starts being dragged.
    

Examples[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/draggable/#examples "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[Live example](https://flet-controls-gallery.fly.dev/utility/draggable)

### Drag and drop Containers[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/draggable/#drag-and-drop-containers "Permanent link")

[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/draggable/#__codelineno-1-7) @ft.observable [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/draggable/#__codelineno-1-8) class TargetState: bool = False [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/draggable/#__codelineno-1-11)
