[![drag-and-drop-containers](https://docs.flet.dev/examples/controls/drag_target_and_draggable/media/drag_and_drop_containers.gif)](https://docs.flet.dev/examples/controls/drag_target_and_draggable/media/drag_and_drop_containers.gif)

Properties[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/draggable/#flet.Draggable-properties "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### affinity `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/draggable/#flet.Draggable.affinity "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/draggable/#__codelineno-0-1) affinity: [Axis](https://docs.flet.dev/types/axis/#flet.Axis)  | None = None`

Specifies the axis along which this control competes with other gestures to initiate a drag.

*   If `None`, the drag starts as soon as a tap down gesture is recognized, regardless of direction.
*   If set to `Axis.HORIZONTAL` or `Axis.VERTICAL`, the control will only initiate a drag when the gesture matches the specified axis, allowing it to compete with other gestures in that direction.

### axis `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/draggable/#flet.Draggable.axis "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/draggable/#__codelineno-0-1) axis: [Axis](https://docs.flet.dev/types/axis/#flet.Axis)  | None = None`

Restricts the draggable's movement to a specific axis.

*   `Axis.HORIZONTAL`: Only allows horizontal dragging.
*   `Axis.VERTICAL`: Only allows vertical dragging.
*   `None`: Allows dragging in any direction.

### content `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/draggable/#flet.Draggable.content "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/draggable/#__codelineno-0-1) content: [Control](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control)`

The control to display when the draggable is not being dragged.

If the draggable is being dragged, the [`content_when_dragging`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/draggable/#flet.Draggable.content_when_dragging)
 is displayed instead.

Raises:

*   `[ValueError](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ValueError) ` –
    
    If it is not visible.
    

### content\_feedback `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/draggable/#flet.Draggable.content_feedback "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/draggable/#__codelineno-0-1) content_feedback: [Control](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control)  | None = None`

The control to show under the pointer when a drag is under way.

### content\_when\_dragging `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/draggable/#flet.Draggable.content_when_dragging "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/draggable/#__codelineno-0-1) content_when_dragging: [Control](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control)  | None = None`

The control to display instead of [`content`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/draggable/#flet.Draggable.content)
 when this draggable is being dragged.

If set, this control visually replaces `content` during an active drag operation, allowing you to show a different appearance or an "empty" placeholder. If `None`, the original `content` remains visible while dragging.

### group `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/draggable/#flet.Draggable.group "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/draggable/#__codelineno-0-1) group: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)  = 'default'`

The group this draggable belongs to.

Note

For a [`DragTarget`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/dragtarget/#flet.DragTarget)
 to accept an incoming drop from a `Draggable`, they must both be in the same `group`.

### max\_simultaneous\_drags `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/draggable/#flet.Draggable.max_simultaneous_drags "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/draggable/#__codelineno-0-1) max_simultaneous_drags: [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int)  | None = None`

Specifies how many simultaneous drag operations are allowed for this draggable.

*   `0` - disables dragging entirely.
*   `1` - allows only one drag at a time. For a better user experience, you may want to provide an "empty" widget for [`content_when_dragging`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/draggable/#flet.Draggable.content_when_dragging)
     to visually indicate the item is being moved.
*   any other positive integer - allows that many concurrent drags.
*   `None` - no limit on the number of simultaneous drags.

Raises:

*   `[ValueError](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ValueError) ` –
    
    If it is not greater than or equal to `0`.
    

Events[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/draggable/#flet.Draggable-events "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### on\_drag\_complete `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/draggable/#flet.Draggable.on_drag_complete "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/draggable/#__codelineno-0-1) on_drag_complete: [ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[Draggable](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/draggable/#flet.Draggable) ] | None = (     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/draggable/#__codelineno-0-2)     None [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/draggable/#__codelineno-0-3) )`

Called when this draggable is dropped and accepted by a [`DragTarget`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/dragtarget/#flet.DragTarget)
.

### on\_drag\_start `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/draggable/#flet.Draggable.on_drag_start "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/draggable/#__codelineno-0-1) on_drag_start: [ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[Draggable](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/draggable/#flet.Draggable) ] | None = None`

Called when this draggable starts being dragged.

Back to top

# DatePicker - Flet

 

[Skip to content](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#flet.DatePicker)

DatePicker
==========

A Material-style date picker dialog.

It can be opened by calling [`Page.show_dialog()`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#flet.BasePage.show_dialog)
 method.

Depending on the [`entry_mode`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#flet.DatePicker.entry_mode)
, it will show either a Calendar or an Input (TextField) for picking a date.

Inherits: `[DialogControl](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/dialogcontrol/#flet.DialogControl) `

Properties

*   **`[barrier_color](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#flet.DatePicker.barrier_color) `**(`[ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None`) –
    
    The color of the modal barrier that darkens everything below this picker's dialog.
    
*   **`[cancel_text](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#flet.DatePicker.cancel_text) `**(`[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)  | None`) –
    
    The text that is displayed on the cancel button.
    
*   **`[confirm_text](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#flet.DatePicker.confirm_text) `**(`[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)  | None`) –
    
    The text that is displayed on the confirm button.
    
*   **`[current_date](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#flet.DatePicker.current_date) `**(`[DateTimeValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.DateTimeValue) `) –
    
    The date representing today. It will be highlighted in the day grid.
    
*   **`[date_picker_mode](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#flet.DatePicker.date_picker_mode) `**(`[DatePickerMode](https://docs.flet.dev/types/datepickermode/#flet.DatePickerMode) `) –
    
    Initial display mode of this picker.
    
*   **`[entry_mode](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#flet.DatePicker.entry_mode) `**(`[DatePickerEntryMode](https://docs.flet.dev/types/datepickerentrymode/#flet.DatePickerEntryMode) `) –
    
    The initial mode of date entry method for the date picker dialog.
    
*   **`[error_format_text](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#flet.DatePicker.error_format_text) `**(`[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)  | None`) –
    
    The error message displayed below the text field if the entered date is not in the correct format.
    
*   **`[error_invalid_text](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#flet.DatePicker.error_invalid_text) `**(`[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)  | None`) –
    
    The error message displayed below the text field if the date is earlier than [`first_date`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#flet.DatePicker.first_date)
     or later than [`last_date`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#flet.DatePicker.last_date)
    .
    
*   **`[field_hint_text](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#flet.DatePicker.field_hint_text) `**(`[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)  | None`) –
    
    The hint text displayed in the text field.
    
*   **`[field_label_text](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#flet.DatePicker.field_label_text) `**(`[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)  | None`) –
    
    The label text displayed in the `TextField`.
    
*   **`[first_date](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#flet.DatePicker.first_date) `**(`[DateTimeValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.DateTimeValue) `) –
    
    The earliest allowable date that the user can select.
    
*   **`[help_text](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#flet.DatePicker.help_text) `**(`[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)  | None`) –
    
    The text that is displayed at the top of the header.
    
*   **`[inset_padding](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#flet.DatePicker.inset_padding) `**(`[PaddingValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.PaddingValue) `) –
    
    The amount of padding added to [`view_insets`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/pagemediadata/#flet.PageMediaData.view_insets)
     of the [`Page.media`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#flet.BasePage.media)
     on the outside of this picker's dialog.
    
*   **`[keyboard_type](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#flet.DatePicker.keyboard_type) `**(`[KeyboardType](https://docs.flet.dev/types/keyboardtype/#flet.KeyboardType) `) –
    
    The type of keyboard to use for editing the text.
    
*   **`[last_date](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#flet.DatePicker.last_date) `**(`[DateTimeValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.DateTimeValue) `) –
    
    The latest allowable date that the user can select.
    
*   **`[locale](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#flet.DatePicker.locale) `**(`[Locale](https://docs.flet.dev/types/locale/#flet.Locale)  | None`) –
    
    The locale for this date picker dialog. It is intended for (rare) cases where this dialog should be localized differently from the rest of the page.
    
*   **`[modal](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#flet.DatePicker.modal) `**(`[bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool) `) –
    
    Whether this date picker cannot be dismissed by clicking the area outside of it.
    
*   **`[switch_to_calendar_icon](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#flet.DatePicker.switch_to_calendar_icon) `**(`[IconData](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.IconData)  | None`) –
    
    The icon displayed in the corner of this picker's dialog when [`entry_mode`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#flet.DatePicker.entry_mode)
     is [`DatePickerEntryMode.INPUT`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/datepickerentrymode/#flet.DatePickerEntryMode.INPUT)
    .
    
*   **`[switch_to_input_icon](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#flet.DatePicker.switch_to_input_icon) `**(`[IconData](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.IconData)  | None`) –
    
    The icon displayed in the corner of this picker's dialog when [`entry_mode`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#flet.DatePicker.entry_mode)
     is [`DatePickerEntryMode.CALENDAR`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/datepickerentrymode/#flet.DatePickerEntryMode.CALENDAR)
    .
    
*   **`[value](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#flet.DatePicker.value) `**(`[DateTimeValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.DateTimeValue)  | None`) –
    
    The selected date that the picker should display.
    

Events

*   **`[on_change](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#flet.DatePicker.on_change) `**(`[ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[DatePicker](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#flet.DatePicker) ] | None`) –
    
    Called when user clicks confirm button.
    
*   **`[on_entry_mode_change](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#flet.DatePicker.on_entry_mode_change) `**(`[EventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.EventHandler) [[DatePickerEntryModeChangeEvent](https://docs.flet.dev/types/datepickerentrymodechangeevent/#flet.DatePickerEntryModeChangeEvent) ] | None`) –
    
    Called when the [`entry_mode`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#flet.DatePicker.entry_mode)
     is changed from the user interface.
    

Examples[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#examples "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[Live example](https://flet-controls-gallery.fly.dev/dialogs/datepicker)

### Basic Example[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#basic-example "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#__codelineno-0-1) import datetime [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#__codelineno-0-2)[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#__codelineno-0-3) import flet as ft [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#__codelineno-0-4) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#__codelineno-0-5)[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#__codelineno-0-6) def main(page: ft.Page):     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#__codelineno-0-7)     page.horizontal_alignment = ft.CrossAxisAlignment.CENTER [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#__codelineno-0-8)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#__codelineno-0-9)     def handle_change(e: ft.Event[ft.DatePicker]):        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#__codelineno-0-10)         page.add(ft.Text(f"Date changed: {e.control.value.strftime('%m/%d/%Y')}")) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#__codelineno-0-11)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#__codelineno-0-12)     def handle_dismissal(e: ft.Event[ft.DialogControl]):        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#__codelineno-0-13)         page.add(ft.Text("DatePicker dismissed")) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#__codelineno-0-14)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#__codelineno-0-15)     today = datetime.datetime.now() [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#__codelineno-0-16)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#__codelineno-0-17)     d = ft.DatePicker(        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#__codelineno-0-18)         first_date=datetime.datetime(year=today.year - 1, month=1, day=1),        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#__codelineno-0-19)         last_date=datetime.datetime(year=today.year + 1, month=today.month, day=20),        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#__codelineno-0-20)         on_change=handle_change,        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#__codelineno-0-21)         on_dismiss=handle_dismissal,    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#__codelineno-0-22)     ) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#__codelineno-0-23)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#__codelineno-0-24)     page.add(        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#__codelineno-0-25)         ft.Button(            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#__codelineno-0-26)             content="Pick date",            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#__codelineno-0-27)             icon=ft.Icons.CALENDAR_MONTH,            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#__codelineno-0-28)             on_click=lambda e: page.show_dialog(d),        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#__codelineno-0-29)         )    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#__codelineno-0-30)     ) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#__codelineno-0-31) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#__codelineno-0-32)[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#__codelineno-0-33) ft.run(main)`

[![basic](https://docs.flet.dev/examples/controls/date_picker/media/basic.png)](https://docs.flet.dev/examples/controls/date_picker/media/basic.png)

Properties[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#flet.DatePicker-properties "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### barrier\_color `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#flet.DatePicker.barrier_color "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#__codelineno-0-1) barrier_color: [ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None = None`

The color of the modal barrier that darkens everything below this picker's dialog.

If `None`, the [`DialogTheme.barrier_color`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/dialogtheme/#flet.DialogTheme.barrier_color)
 is used. If it is also `None`, then [`Colors.BLACK_54`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/colors/#flet.Colors.BLACK_54)
 is used.

### cancel\_text `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#flet.DatePicker.cancel_text "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#__codelineno-0-1) cancel_text: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)  | None = None`

The text that is displayed on the cancel button.

Defaults to `"Cancel"`.

### confirm\_text `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#flet.DatePicker.confirm_text "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#__codelineno-0-1) confirm_text: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)  | None = None`

The text that is displayed on the confirm button.

Defaults to `"OK"`.

### current\_date `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#flet.DatePicker.current_date "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#__codelineno-0-1) current_date: [DateTimeValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.DateTimeValue)  = [field](https://docs.python.org/3/library/dataclasses.html#dataclasses.field "<code>dataclasses.field</code>") (     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#__codelineno-0-2)     default_factory=lambda: [now](https://docs.python.org/3/library/datetime.html#datetime.datetime.now "<code>datetime.datetime.now</code>") () [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#__codelineno-0-3) )`

The date representing today. It will be highlighted in the day grid.

### date\_picker\_mode `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#flet.DatePicker.date_picker_mode "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#__codelineno-0-1) date_picker_mode: [DatePickerMode](https://docs.flet.dev/types/datepickermode/#flet.DatePickerMode)  = [DAY](https://docs.flet.dev/types/datepickermode/#flet.DatePickerMode.DAY)`

Initial display mode of this picker.

### entry\_mode `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#flet.DatePicker.entry_mode "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#__codelineno-0-1) entry_mode: [DatePickerEntryMode](https://docs.flet.dev/types/datepickerentrymode/#flet.DatePickerEntryMode)  = [CALENDAR](https://docs.flet.dev/types/datepickerentrymode/#flet.DatePickerEntryMode.CALENDAR)`

The initial mode of date entry method for the date picker dialog.

### error\_format\_text `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#flet.DatePicker.error_format_text "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#__codelineno-0-1) error_format_text: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)  | None = None`

The error message displayed below the text field if the entered date is not in the correct format.

Defaults to `"Invalid format"`.

### error\_invalid\_text `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#flet.DatePicker.error_invalid_text "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#__codelineno-0-1) error_invalid_text: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)  | None = None`

The error message displayed below the text field if the date is earlier than [`first_date`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#flet.DatePicker.first_date)
 or later than [`last_date`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#flet.DatePicker.last_date)
.

Defaults to `"Out of range"`.

### field\_hint\_text `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#flet.DatePicker.field_hint_text "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#__codelineno-0-1) field_hint_text: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)  | None = None`

The hint text displayed in the text field.

The default value is the date format string that depends on your locale. For example, `'mm/dd/yyyy'` for en\_US.

### field\_label\_text `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#flet.DatePicker.field_label_text "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#__codelineno-0-1) field_label_text: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)  | None = None`

The label text displayed in the `TextField`.

If `None`, defaults to the words representing the date format string. For example, `'Month, Day, Year'` for en\_US.

Defaults to `"Enter Date"`.

### first\_date `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#flet.DatePicker.first_date "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#__codelineno-0-1) first_date: [DateTimeValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.DateTimeValue)  = [field](https://docs.python.org/3/library/dataclasses.html#dataclasses.field "<code>dataclasses.field</code>") (     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#__codelineno-0-2)     default_factory=lambda: [datetime](https://docs.python.org/3/library/datetime.html#datetime.datetime "<code>datetime.datetime</code>") (        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#__codelineno-0-3)         year=1900, month=1, day=1    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#__codelineno-0-4)     ) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#__codelineno-0-5) )`

The earliest allowable date that the user can select.

Defaults to `January 1, 1900`.

### help\_text `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#flet.DatePicker.help_text "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#__codelineno-0-1) help_text: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)  | None = None`

The text that is displayed at the top of the header.

This is used to indicate to the user what they are selecting a date for.

Defaults to `"Select date"`.

### inset\_padding `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#flet.DatePicker.inset_padding "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#__codelineno-0-1) inset_padding: [PaddingValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.PaddingValue)  = [field](https://docs.python.org/3/library/dataclasses.html#dataclasses.field "<code>dataclasses.field</code>") (     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#__codelineno-0-2)     default_factory=lambda: [symmetric](https://docs.flet.dev/types/padding/#flet.Padding.symmetric) (        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#__codelineno-0-3)         horizontal=16.0, vertical=24.0    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#__codelineno-0-4)     ) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#__codelineno-0-5) )`

The amount of padding added to [`view_insets`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/pagemediadata/#flet.PageMediaData.view_insets)
 of the [`Page.media`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#flet.BasePage.media)
 on the outside of this picker's dialog.

This defines the minimum space between the screen's edges and the dialog.

### keyboard\_type `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#flet.DatePicker.keyboard_type "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#__codelineno-0-1) keyboard_type: [KeyboardType](https://docs.flet.dev/types/keyboardtype/#flet.KeyboardType)  = [DATETIME](https://docs.flet.dev/types/keyboardtype/#flet.KeyboardType.DATETIME)`

The type of keyboard to use for editing the text.

### last\_date `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#flet.DatePicker.last_date "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#__codelineno-0-1) last_date: [DateTimeValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.DateTimeValue)  = [field](https://docs.python.org/3/library/dataclasses.html#dataclasses.field "<code>dataclasses.field</code>") (     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#__codelineno-0-2)     default_factory=lambda: [datetime](https://docs.python.org/3/library/datetime.html#datetime.datetime "<code>datetime.datetime</code>") (        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#__codelineno-0-3)         year=2050, month=1, day=1    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#__codelineno-0-4)     ) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#__codelineno-0-5) )`

The latest allowable date that the user can select.

Defaults to `January 1, 2050`.

### locale `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#flet.DatePicker.locale "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#__codelineno-0-1) locale: [Locale](https://docs.flet.dev/types/locale/#flet.Locale)  | None = None`

The locale for this date picker dialog. It is intended for (rare) cases where this dialog should be localized differently from the rest of the page.

It overrides the locale used by the page (see [`Page.locale_configuration`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#flet.BasePage.locale_configuration)
), but does not participate in page-level locale resolution.

If set to `None` (the default) or an inexistent/unsupported locale, the [`current_locale`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/localeconfiguration/#flet.LocaleConfiguration.current_locale)
 of the [`Page.locale_configuration`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#flet.BasePage.locale_configuration)
 is used as fallback.

### modal `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#flet.DatePicker.modal "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#__codelineno-0-1) modal: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)  = False`

Whether this date picker cannot be dismissed by clicking the area outside of it.

### switch\_to\_calendar\_icon `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#flet.DatePicker.switch_to_calendar_icon "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#__codelineno-0-1) switch_to_calendar_icon: [IconData](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.IconData)  | None = None`

The icon displayed in the corner of this picker's dialog when [`entry_mode`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#flet.DatePicker.entry_mode)
 is [`DatePickerEntryMode.INPUT`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/datepickerentrymode/#flet.DatePickerEntryMode.INPUT)
.

Clicking on this icon changes the [`entry_mode`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#flet.DatePicker.entry_mode)
 to [`DatePickerEntryMode.CALENDAR`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/datepickerentrymode/#flet.DatePickerEntryMode.CALENDAR)
.

If `None`, defaults to `Icons.CALENDAR_TODAY`.

### switch\_to\_input\_icon `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#flet.DatePicker.switch_to_input_icon "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#__codelineno-0-1) switch_to_input_icon: [IconData](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.IconData)  | None = None`

The icon displayed in the corner of this picker's dialog when [`entry_mode`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#flet.DatePicker.entry_mode)
 is [`DatePickerEntryMode.CALENDAR`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/datepickerentrymode/#flet.DatePickerEntryMode.CALENDAR)
.

Clicking on icon changes the [`entry_mode`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#flet.DatePicker.entry_mode)
 to [`DatePickerEntryMode.INPUT`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/datepickerentrymode/#flet.DatePickerEntryMode.INPUT)
.

If `None`, defaults to `Icons.EDIT_OUTLINED`.

### value `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#flet.DatePicker.value "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#__codelineno-0-1) value: [DateTimeValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.DateTimeValue)  | None = None`

The selected date that the picker should display.

Defaults to [`current_date`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#flet.DatePicker.current_date)
.

Events[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#flet.DatePicker-events "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### on\_change `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#flet.DatePicker.on_change "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#__codelineno-0-1) on_change: [ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[DatePicker](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#flet.DatePicker) ] | None = None`

Called when user clicks confirm button. [`value`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#flet.DatePicker.value)
 is updated with selected date.

The [`data`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/event/#flet.Event.data)
 property of the event handler argument contains the selected date.

### on\_entry\_mode\_change `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#flet.DatePicker.on_entry_mode_change "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#__codelineno-0-1) on_entry_mode_change: (     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#__codelineno-0-2)     [EventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.EventHandler) [[DatePickerEntryModeChangeEvent](https://docs.flet.dev/types/datepickerentrymodechangeevent/#flet.DatePickerEntryModeChangeEvent) ] | None [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#__codelineno-0-3) ) = None`

Called when the [`entry_mode`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/datepicker/#flet.DatePicker.entry_mode)
 is changed from the user interface.

# DateRangePicker - Flet

 

[Skip to content](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#flet.DateRangePicker)

DateRangePicker
===============

A Material-style date range picker dialog.

It can be opened by calling [`Page.show_dialog()`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#flet.BasePage.show_dialog)
 method.

Depending on the [`entry_mode`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#flet.DateRangePicker.entry_mode)
, it will show either a Calendar or an Input (text field) for picking a date range.

Inherits: `[DialogControl](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/dialogcontrol/#flet.DialogControl) `

Properties

*   **`[barrier_color](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#flet.DateRangePicker.barrier_color) `**(`[ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None`) –
    
    The color of the modal barrier that darkens everything below the date picker.
    
*   **`[cancel_text](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#flet.DateRangePicker.cancel_text) `**(`[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)  | None`) –
    
    The text that is displayed on the cancel button.
    
*   **`[confirm_text](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#flet.DateRangePicker.confirm_text) `**(`[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)  | None`) –
    
    The text that is displayed on the confirm button.
    
*   **`[current_date](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#flet.DateRangePicker.current_date) `**(`[DateTimeValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.DateTimeValue) `) –
    
    The date representing today. It will be highlighted in the day grid.
    
*   **`[end_value](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#flet.DateRangePicker.end_value) `**(`[DateTimeValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.DateTimeValue)  | None`) –
    
    The selected end date that the picker should display.
    
*   **`[entry_mode](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#flet.DateRangePicker.entry_mode) `**(`[DatePickerEntryMode](https://docs.flet.dev/types/datepickerentrymode/#flet.DatePickerEntryMode) `) –
    
    The initial mode of date entry method for the date picker dialog.
    
*   **`[error_format_text](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#flet.DateRangePicker.error_format_text) `**(`[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)  | None`) –
    
    The error message displayed below the TextField if the entered date is not in the correct format.
    
*   **`[error_invalid_range_text](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#flet.DateRangePicker.error_invalid_range_text) `**(`[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)  | None`) –
    
    The message used when the date range is invalid (e.g. start date is after end date).
    
*   **`[error_invalid_text](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#flet.DateRangePicker.error_invalid_text) `**(`[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)  | None`) –
    
    The error message displayed below the TextField if the date is earlier than [`first_date`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#flet.DateRangePicker.first_date)
     or later than [`last_date`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#flet.DateRangePicker.last_date)
    .
    
*   **`[field_end_hint_text](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#flet.DateRangePicker.field_end_hint_text) `**(`[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)  | None`) –
    
    The text used to prompt the user when no text has been entered in the end field.
    
*   **`[field_end_label_text](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#flet.DateRangePicker.field_end_label_text) `**(`[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)  | None`) –
    
    The label for the end date text input field.
    
*   **`[field_start_hint_text](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#flet.DateRangePicker.field_start_hint_text) `**(`[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)  | None`) –
    
    The text used to prompt the user when no text has been entered in the start field.
    
*   **`[field_start_label_text](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#flet.DateRangePicker.field_start_label_text) `**(`[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)  | None`) –
    
    The label for the start date text input field.
    
*   **`[first_date](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#flet.DateRangePicker.first_date) `**(`[DateTimeValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.DateTimeValue) `) –
    
    The earliest allowable date on the date range.
    
*   **`[help_text](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#flet.DateRangePicker.help_text) `**(`[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)  | None`) –
    
    The text that is displayed at the top of the header.
    
*   **`[keyboard_type](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#flet.DateRangePicker.keyboard_type) `**(`[KeyboardType](https://docs.flet.dev/types/keyboardtype/#flet.KeyboardType) `) –
    
    The type of keyboard to use for editing the text.
    
*   **`[last_date](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#flet.DateRangePicker.last_date) `**(`[DateTimeValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.DateTimeValue) `) –
    
    The latest allowable date on the date range.
    
*   **`[locale](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#flet.DateRangePicker.locale) `**(`[Locale](https://docs.flet.dev/types/locale/#flet.Locale)  | None`) –
    
    The locale for this date picker dialog. It is intended for (rare) cases where this dialog should be localized differently from the rest of the page.
    
*   **`[modal](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#flet.DateRangePicker.modal) `**(`[bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool) `) –
    
    Whether this date picker cannot be dismissed by clicking the area outside of it.
    
*   **`[save_text](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#flet.DateRangePicker.save_text) `**(`[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)  | None`) –
    
    The label on the save button for the fullscreen calendar mode.
    
*   **`[start_value](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#flet.DateRangePicker.start_value) `**(`[DateTimeValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.DateTimeValue)  | None`) –
    
    The selected start date that the picker should display.
    
*   **`[switch_to_calendar_icon](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#flet.DateRangePicker.switch_to_calendar_icon) `**(`[IconData](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.IconData)  | None`) –
    
    The name of the icon displayed in the corner of the dialog when [`entry_mode`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#flet.DateRangePicker.entry_mode)
     is [`DatePickerEntryMode.INPUT`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/datepickerentrymode/#flet.DatePickerEntryMode.INPUT)
    .
    
*   **`[switch_to_input_icon](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#flet.DateRangePicker.switch_to_input_icon) `**(`[IconData](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.IconData)  | None`) –
    
    The name of the icon displayed in the corner of the dialog when [`entry_mode`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#flet.DateRangePicker.entry_mode)
     is [`DatePickerEntryMode.CALENDAR`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/datepickerentrymode/#flet.DatePickerEntryMode.CALENDAR)
    .
    

Events

*   **`[on_change](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#flet.DateRangePicker.on_change) `**(`[ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[DateRangePicker](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#flet.DateRangePicker) ] | None`) –
    
    Called when user clicks confirm button.

### Basic Example[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#basic-example "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#__codelineno-0-1) import datetime [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#__codelineno-0-2)[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#__codelineno-0-3) import flet as ft [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#__codelineno-0-4) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#__codelineno-0-5)[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#__codelineno-0-6) def main(page: ft.Page):     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#__codelineno-0-7)     page.horizontal_alignment = ft.CrossAxisAlignment.CENTER [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#__codelineno-0-8)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#__codelineno-0-9)     def handle_change(e: ft.Event[ft.DateRangePicker]):        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#__codelineno-0-10)         page.add(            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#__codelineno-0-11)             ft.Text(                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#__codelineno-0-12)                 f"Start Date changed: {e.control.start_value.strftime('%m/%d/%Y')}"            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#__codelineno-0-13)             ),            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#__codelineno-0-14)             ft.Text(f"End Date changed: {e.control.end_value.strftime('%m/%d/%Y')}"),        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#__codelineno-0-15)         ) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#__codelineno-0-16)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#__codelineno-0-17)     def handle_dismissal(e: ft.Event[ft.DialogControl]):        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#__codelineno-0-18)         page.add(ft.Text("DatePicker dismissed")) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#__codelineno-0-19)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#__codelineno-0-20)     today = datetime.datetime.now() [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#__codelineno-0-21)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#__codelineno-0-22)     drp = ft.DateRangePicker(        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#__codelineno-0-23)         start_value=datetime.datetime(year=today.year, month=today.month, day=1),        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#__codelineno-0-24)         end_value=datetime.datetime(year=today.year, month=today.month, day=15),        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#__codelineno-0-25)         on_change=handle_change,        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#__codelineno-0-26)         on_dismiss=handle_dismissal,    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#__codelineno-0-27)     ) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#__codelineno-0-28)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#__codelineno-0-29)     page.add(        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#__codelineno-0-30)         ft.Button(            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#__codelineno-0-31)             content=ft.Text("Pick date"),            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#__codelineno-0-32)             icon=ft.Icons.PHONE,            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#__codelineno-0-33)             on_click=lambda e: page.show_dialog(drp),        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#__codelineno-0-34)         )    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#__codelineno-0-35)     ) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#__codelineno-0-36) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#__codelineno-0-37)[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#__codelineno-0-38) ft.run(main)`

[![basic](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/controls/material/golden/macos/date_range_picker/basic.png)](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/controls/material/golden/macos/date_range_picker/basic.png)

Properties[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#flet.DateRangePicker-properties "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### barrier\_color `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#flet.DateRangePicker.barrier_color "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#__codelineno-0-1) barrier_color: [ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None = None`

The color of the modal barrier that darkens everything below the date picker.

If `None`, the [`DialogTheme.barrier_color`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/dialogtheme/#flet.DialogTheme.barrier_color)
 is used. If it is also `None`, then `Colors.BLACK_54` is used.

### cancel\_text `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#flet.DateRangePicker.cancel_text "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#__codelineno-0-1) cancel_text: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)  | None = None`

The text that is displayed on the cancel button.

### confirm\_text `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#flet.DateRangePicker.confirm_text "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#__codelineno-0-1) confirm_text: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)  | None = None`

The text that is displayed on the confirm button.

### current\_date `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#flet.DateRangePicker.current_date "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#__codelineno-0-1) current_date: [DateTimeValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.DateTimeValue)  = [field](https://docs.python.org/3/library/dataclasses.html#dataclasses.field "<code>dataclasses.field</code>") (     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#__codelineno-0-2)     default_factory=lambda: [now](https://docs.python.org/3/library/datetime.html#datetime.datetime.now "<code>datetime.datetime.now</code>") () [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#__codelineno-0-3) )`

The date representing today. It will be highlighted in the day grid.

### end\_value `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#flet.DateRangePicker.end_value "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#__codelineno-0-1) end_value: [DateTimeValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.DateTimeValue)  | None = None`

The selected end date that the picker should display.

Defaults to [`current_date`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#flet.DateRangePicker.current_date)
.

### entry\_mode `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#flet.DateRangePicker.entry_mode "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#__codelineno-0-1) entry_mode: [DatePickerEntryMode](https://docs.flet.dev/types/datepickerentrymode/#flet.DatePickerEntryMode)  = [CALENDAR](https://docs.flet.dev/types/datepickerentrymode/#flet.DatePickerEntryMode.CALENDAR)`

The initial mode of date entry method for the date picker dialog.

### error\_format\_text `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#flet.DateRangePicker.error_format_text "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#__codelineno-0-1) error_format_text: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)  | None = None`

The error message displayed below the TextField if the entered date is not in the correct format.

### error\_invalid\_range\_text `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#flet.DateRangePicker.error_invalid_range_text "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#__codelineno-0-1) error_invalid_range_text: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)  | None = None`

The message used when the date range is invalid (e.g. start date is after end date).

### error\_invalid\_text `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#flet.DateRangePicker.error_invalid_text "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#__codelineno-0-1) error_invalid_text: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)  | None = None`

The error message displayed below the TextField if the date is earlier than [`first_date`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#flet.DateRangePicker.first_date)
 or later than [`last_date`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#flet.DateRangePicker.last_date)
.

### field\_end\_hint\_text `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#flet.DateRangePicker.field_end_hint_text "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#__codelineno-0-1) field_end_hint_text: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)  | None = None`

The text used to prompt the user when no text has been entered in the end field.

### field\_end\_label\_text `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#flet.DateRangePicker.field_end_label_text "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#__codelineno-0-1) field_end_label_text: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)  | None = None`

The label for the end date text input field.

### field\_start\_hint\_text `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#flet.DateRangePicker.field_start_hint_text "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#__codelineno-0-1) field_start_hint_text: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)  | None = None`

The text used to prompt the user when no text has been entered in the start field.

### field\_start\_label\_text `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#flet.DateRangePicker.field_start_label_text "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#__codelineno-0-1) field_start_label_text: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)  | None = None`

The label for the start date text input field.

### first\_date `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#flet.DateRangePicker.first_date "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#__codelineno-0-1) first_date: [DateTimeValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.DateTimeValue)  = [field](https://docs.python.org/3/library/dataclasses.html#dataclasses.field "<code>dataclasses.field</code>") (     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#__codelineno-0-2)     default_factory=lambda: [datetime](https://docs.python.org/3/library/datetime.html#datetime.datetime "<code>datetime.datetime</code>") (        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#__codelineno-0-3)         year=1900, month=1, day=1    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#__codelineno-0-4)     ) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#__codelineno-0-5) )`

The earliest allowable date on the date range.

### help\_text `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#flet.DateRangePicker.help_text "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#__codelineno-0-1) help_text: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)  | None = None`

The text that is displayed at the top of the header.

This is used to indicate to the user what they are selecting a date for.

### keyboard\_type `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#flet.DateRangePicker.keyboard_type "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#__codelineno-0-1) keyboard_type: [KeyboardType](https://docs.flet.dev/types/keyboardtype/#flet.KeyboardType)  = [DATETIME](https://docs.flet.dev/types/keyboardtype/#flet.KeyboardType.DATETIME)`

The type of keyboard to use for editing the text.

### last\_date `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#flet.DateRangePicker.last_date "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#__codelineno-0-1) last_date: [DateTimeValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.DateTimeValue)  = [field](https://docs.python.org/3/library/dataclasses.html#dataclasses.field "<code>dataclasses.field</code>") (     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#__codelineno-0-2)     default_factory=lambda: [datetime](https://docs.python.org/3/library/datetime.html#datetime.datetime "<code>datetime.datetime</code>") (        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#__codelineno-0-3)         year=2050, month=1, day=1    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#__codelineno-0-4)     ) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#__codelineno-0-5) )`

The latest allowable date on the date range.

### locale `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#flet.DateRangePicker.locale "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#__codelineno-0-1) locale: [Locale](https://docs.flet.dev/types/locale/#flet.Locale)  | None = None`

The locale for this date picker dialog. It is intended for (rare) cases where this dialog should be localized differently from the rest of the page.

It overrides the locale used by the page (see [`Page.locale_configuration`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#flet.BasePage.locale_configuration)
), but does not participate in page-level locale resolution.

If set to `None` (the default) or an inexistent/unsupported locale, the [`current_locale`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/localeconfiguration/#flet.LocaleConfiguration.current_locale)
 of the [`Page.locale_configuration`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#flet.BasePage.locale_configuration)
 is used as fallback.

### modal `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#flet.DateRangePicker.modal "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#__codelineno-0-1) modal: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)  = False`

Whether this date picker cannot be dismissed by clicking the area outside of it.

### save\_text `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#flet.DateRangePicker.save_text "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#__codelineno-0-1) save_text: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)  | None = None`

The label on the save button for the fullscreen calendar mode.

### start\_value `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#flet.DateRangePicker.start_value "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#__codelineno-0-1) start_value: [DateTimeValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.DateTimeValue)  | None = None`

The selected start date that the picker should display.

Defaults to [`current_date`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#flet.DateRangePicker.current_date)
.

### switch\_to\_calendar\_icon `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#flet.DateRangePicker.switch_to_calendar_icon "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#__codelineno-0-1) switch_to_calendar_icon: [IconData](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.IconData)  | None = None`

The name of the icon displayed in the corner of the dialog when [`entry_mode`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#flet.DateRangePicker.entry_mode)
 is [`DatePickerEntryMode.INPUT`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/datepickerentrymode/#flet.DatePickerEntryMode.INPUT)
.

Clicking on this icon changes the `entry_mode` to [`DatePickerEntryMode.CALENDAR`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/datepickerentrymode/#flet.DatePickerEntryMode.CALENDAR)
.

If `None`, `Icons.CALENDAR_TODAY` is used.

### switch\_to\_input\_icon `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#flet.DateRangePicker.switch_to_input_icon "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#__codelineno-0-1) switch_to_input_icon: [IconData](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.IconData)  | None = None`

The name of the icon displayed in the corner of the dialog when [`entry_mode`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#flet.DateRangePicker.entry_mode)
 is [`DatePickerEntryMode.CALENDAR`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/datepickerentrymode/#flet.DatePickerEntryMode.CALENDAR)
.

Clicking on this icon changes the `entry_mode` to [`DatePickerEntryMode.INPUT`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/datepickerentrymode/#flet.DatePickerEntryMode.INPUT)
.

If `None`, `Icons.EDIT_OUTLINED` is used.

Events[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#flet.DateRangePicker-events "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### on\_change `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#flet.DateRangePicker.on_change "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#__codelineno-0-1) on_change: [ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[DateRangePicker](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#flet.DateRangePicker) ] | None = (     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#__codelineno-0-2)     None [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#__codelineno-0-3) )`

Called when user clicks confirm button.

[`start_value`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#flet.DateRangePicker.start_value)
 and [`end_value`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/daterangepicker/#flet.DateRangePicker.end_value)
 are updated with selected dates.

The [`data`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/event/#flet.Event.data)
 property of the event handler argument contains the selected dates.

Back to top

# SafeArea - Flet

 

[Skip to content](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/safearea/#flet.SafeArea)

SafeArea
========

A control that insets its [`content`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/safearea/#flet.SafeArea.content)
 by sufficient padding to avoid intrusions by the operating system.

For example, this will indent the `content` by enough to avoid the status bar at the top of the screen.

It will also indent the `content` by the amount necessary to avoid the Notch on the iPhone X, or other similar creative physical features of the display.

When a [`minimum_padding`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/safearea/#flet.SafeArea.minimum_padding)
 is specified, the greater of the minimum padding or the safe area padding will be applied.

Inherits: `[LayoutControl](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/layoutcontrol/#flet.LayoutControl) `, `[AdaptiveControl](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/adaptivecontrol/#flet.AdaptiveControl) `

Properties

*   **`[avoid_intrusions_bottom](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/safearea/#flet.SafeArea.avoid_intrusions_bottom) `**(`[bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool) `) –
    
    Whether to avoid system intrusions on the bottom side of the screen.
    
*   **`[avoid_intrusions_left](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/safearea/#flet.SafeArea.avoid_intrusions_left) `**(`[bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool) `) –
    
    Whether to avoid system intrusions on the left.
    
*   **`[avoid_intrusions_right](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/safearea/#flet.SafeArea.avoid_intrusions_right) `**(`[bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool) `) –
    
    Whether to avoid system intrusions on the right.
    
*   **`[avoid_intrusions_top](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/safearea/#flet.SafeArea.avoid_intrusions_top) `**(`[bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool) `) –
    
    Whether to avoid system intrusions at the top of the screen, typically the system status bar.
    
*   **`[content](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/safearea/#flet.SafeArea.content) `**(`[Control](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control) `) –
    
    The control to display.
    
*   **`[maintain_bottom_view_padding](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/safearea/#flet.SafeArea.maintain_bottom_view_padding) `**(`[bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool) `) –
    
    Specifies whether the `SafeArea` should maintain the bottom `MediaQueryData.viewPadding` instead of the bottom `MediaQueryData.padding`.
    
*   **`[minimum_padding](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/safearea/#flet.SafeArea.minimum_padding) `**(`[PaddingValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.PaddingValue) `) –
    
    The minimum padding to apply.
    

Example[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/safearea/#example "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[Live example](https://flet-controls-gallery.fly.dev/layout/safearea)

### Basic Example[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/safearea/#basic-example "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/safearea/#__codelineno-0-1) import flet as ft [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/safearea/#__codelineno-0-2) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/safearea/#__codelineno-0-3)[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/safearea/#__codelineno-0-4) class State:     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/safearea/#__codelineno-0-5)     counter = 0 [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/safearea/#__codelineno-0-6) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/safearea/#__codelineno-0-7)[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/safearea/#__codelineno-0-8) def main(page: ft.Page): Properties[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/safearea/#flet.SafeArea-properties "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### avoid\_intrusions\_bottom `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/safearea/#flet.SafeArea.avoid_intrusions_bottom "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/safearea/#__codelineno-0-1) avoid_intrusions_bottom: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)  = True`

Whether to avoid system intrusions on the bottom side of the screen.

### avoid\_intrusions\_left `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/safearea/#flet.SafeArea.avoid_intrusions_left "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/safearea/#__codelineno-0-1) avoid_intrusions_left: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)  = True`

Whether to avoid system intrusions on the left.

### avoid\_intrusions\_right `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/safearea/#flet.SafeArea.avoid_intrusions_right "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/safearea/#__codelineno-0-1) avoid_intrusions_right: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)  = True`

Whether to avoid system intrusions on the right.

### avoid\_intrusions\_top `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/safearea/#flet.SafeArea.avoid_intrusions_top "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/safearea/#__codelineno-0-1) avoid_intrusions_top: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)  = True`

Whether to avoid system intrusions at the top of the screen, typically the system status bar.

### content `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/safearea/#flet.SafeArea.content "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/safearea/#__codelineno-0-1) content: [Control](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control)`

The control to display.

Raises:

*   `[ValueError](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ValueError) ` –
    
    If it is not visible.
    

### maintain\_bottom\_view\_padding `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/safearea/#flet.SafeArea.maintain_bottom_view_padding "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/safearea/#__codelineno-0-1) maintain_bottom_view_padding: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)  = False`

Specifies whether the `SafeArea` should maintain the bottom `MediaQueryData.viewPadding` instead of the bottom `MediaQueryData.padding`.

This avoids layout shifts caused by keyboard overlays, useful when flexible controls are used.

### minimum\_padding `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/safearea/#flet.SafeArea.minimum_padding "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/safearea/#__codelineno-0-1) minimum_padding: [PaddingValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.PaddingValue)  = 0`

The minimum padding to apply.

The greater of the minimum insets and the media padding will be applied.

# RotatedBox - Flet

 

[Skip to content](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rotatedbox/#flet.RotatedBox)

RotatedBox
==========

Rotates its [`content`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rotatedbox/#flet.RotatedBox.content)
 by an integral number of quarter turns.

Unlike [`LayoutControl.rotate`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/layoutcontrol/#flet.LayoutControl.rotate)
 (which uses `Transform.rotate` and applies the rotation only at paint time), `RotatedBox` applies the rotation before layout. This means the control's rotated dimensions participate in layout and can affect surrounding controls.

Inherits: `[LayoutControl](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/layoutcontrol/#flet.LayoutControl) `

Properties

*   **`[content](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rotatedbox/#flet.RotatedBox.content) `**(`[Control](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control)  | None`) –
    
    The `Control` to rotate.
    
*   **`[quarter_turns](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rotatedbox/#flet.RotatedBox.quarter_turns) `**(`[int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int) `) –
    
    The number of clockwise quarter turns.
    

Example[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rotatedbox/#example "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rotatedbox/#__codelineno-0-1) import flet as ft [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rotatedbox/#__codelineno-0-2) ### quarter\_turns `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rotatedbox/#flet.RotatedBox.quarter_turns "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rotatedbox/#__codelineno-0-1) quarter_turns: [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int)  = 0`

The number of clockwise quarter turns.

For example, `1` rotates by 90 degrees, `2` by 180 degrees.

# TransparentPointer - Flet

 

[Skip to content](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/transparentpointer/#flet.TransparentPointer)

TransparentPointer
==================

TransparentPointer is the solution to ["How to pass through all gestures between two widgets in Stack"](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/65269190/pass-trough-all-gestures-between-two-widgets-in-stack)
 problem.

For example, if there is an [`Button`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#flet.Button)
 inside [`Container`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/container/#flet.Container)
 with [`GestureDetector`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/gesturedetector/#flet.GestureDetector)
 then tapping on a button won't be "visible" to a gesture detector behind it. With `TransparentPointer` a tapping event doesn't stop on a button, but goes up to the parent, similar to event bubbling in HTML/JS.

Inherits: `[LayoutControl](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/layoutcontrol/#flet.LayoutControl) `

Properties

*   **`[content](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/transparentpointer/#flet.TransparentPointer.content) `**(`[Control](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control)  | None`) –
    
    The `Control` that should be displayed.
    

Example[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/transparentpointer/#example "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Basic Example[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/transparentpointer/#basic-example "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/transparentpointer/#__codelineno-0-1) import flet as ft [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/transparentpointer/#__codelineno-0-2) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/transparentpointer/#__codelineno-0-3)[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/transparentpointer/#__codelineno-0-4) def main(page: ft.Page):     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/transparentpointer/#__codelineno-0-5)     def button_clicked(e):        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/transparentpointer/#__codelineno-0-6)         print("transparent pointer button clicked") [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/transparentpointer/#__codelineno-0-7)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/transparentpointer/#__codelineno-0-8)     page.add(        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/transparentpointer/#__codelineno-0-9)         ft.Stack(            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/transparentpointer/#__codelineno-0-10)             expand=True,            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/transparentpointer/#__codelineno-0-11)             controls=[                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/transparentpointer/#__codelineno-0-12)                 ft.GestureDetector(                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/transparentpointer/#__codelineno-0-13)                     on_tap=lambda _: print("TAP!"),                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/transparentpointer/#__codelineno-0-14)                     multi_tap_touches=3,                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/transparentpointer/#__codelineno-0-15)                     on_multi_tap=lambda e: print("MULTI TAP:", e.global_position),                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/transparentpointer/#__codelineno-0-16)                     on_multi_long_press=lambda _: print("Multi tap long press"),                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/transparentpointer/#__codelineno-0-17)                 ),                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/transparentpointer/#__codelineno-0-18)                 ft.TransparentPointer(                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/transparentpointer/#__codelineno-0-19)                     content=ft.Container(                        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/transparentpointer/#__codelineno-0-20)                         content=ft.Button("Test button", on_click=button_clicked),                        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/transparentpointer/#__codelineno-0-21)                         padding=50,                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/transparentpointer/#__codelineno-0-22)                     )                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/transparentpointer/#__codelineno-0-23)                 ),            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/transparentpointer/#__codelineno-0-24)             ],        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/transparentpointer/#__codelineno-0-25)         )    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/transparentpointer/#__codelineno-0-26)     ) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/transparentpointer/#__codelineno-0-27) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/transparentpointer/#__codelineno-0-28)[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/transparentpointer/#__codelineno-0-29) ft.run(main)`

Properties[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/transparentpointer/#flet.TransparentPointer-properties "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### content `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/transparentpointer/#flet.TransparentPointer.content "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/transparentpointer/#__codelineno-0-1) content: [Control](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control)  | None = None`

The `Control` that should be displayed.

# Button - Flet

 

[Skip to content](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#flet.Button)

Button
======

A material button.

It supports various styles, colors, event handlers for user interaction, and can be used to display text, icons, etc.

Example:

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#__codelineno-0-1) ft.Button(content="Enabled button") [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#__codelineno-0-2) ft.Button(content="Disabled button", disabled=True)`

[![Button](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/material/golden/macos/button/image_for_docs.png)](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/material/golden/macos/button/image_for_docs.png)

Enabled and disabled buttons

Inherits: `[LayoutControl](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/layoutcontrol/#flet.LayoutControl) `, `[AdaptiveControl](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/adaptivecontrol/#flet.AdaptiveControl) `

Properties

*   **`[autofocus](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#flet.Button.autofocus) `**(`[bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)  | None`) –
    
    Whether this button should be focused initially.
    
*   **`[bgcolor](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#flet.Button.bgcolor) `**(`[ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None`) –
    
    The button's background color.
    
*   **`[clip_behavior](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#flet.Button.clip_behavior) `**(`[ClipBehavior](https://docs.flet.dev/types/clipbehavior/#flet.ClipBehavior)  | None`) –
    
    The button's clip behavior.
    
*   **`[color](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#flet.Button.color) `**(`[ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None`) –
    
    The button's foreground color.
    
*   **`[content](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#flet.Button.content) `**(`[StrOrControl](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.StrOrControl)  | None`) –
    
    The button's label.
    
*   **`[elevation](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#flet.Button.elevation) `**(`[Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number) `) –
    
    The button's elevation.
    
*   **`[icon](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#flet.Button.icon) `**(`[IconDataOrControl](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.IconDataOrControl)  | None`) –
    
    The icon to display inside the button.
    
*   **`[icon_color](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#flet.Button.icon_color) `**(`[ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None`) –
    
    The color of the icon.
    
*   **`[style](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#flet.Button.style) `**(`[ButtonStyle](https://docs.flet.dev/types/buttonstyle/#flet.ButtonStyle)  | None`) –
    
    The button's style.
    
*   **`[url](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#flet.Button.url) `**(`[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)  | [Url](https://docs.flet.dev/types/url/#flet.Url)  | None`) –
    
    The URL to open when the button is clicked.
    

Events

*   **`[on_blur](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#flet.Button.on_blur) `**(`[ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[Button](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#flet.Button) ] | None`) –
    
    Called when the button loses focus.
    
*   **`[on_click](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#flet.Button.on_click) `**(`[ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[Button](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#flet.Button) ] | None`) –
    
    Called when the button is clicked.
    
*   **`[on_focus](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#flet.Button.on_focus) `**(`[ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[Button](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#flet.Button) ] | None`) –
    
    Called when the button is focused.
    
*   **`[on_hover](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#flet.Button.on_hover) `**(`[ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[Button](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#flet.Button) ] | None`) –
    
    Called when the button is hovered.
    
*   **`[on_long_press](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#flet.Button.on_long_press) `**(`[ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[Button](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#flet.Button) ] | None`) –
    
    Called when the button is long-pressed.
    

Methods

*   **`[focus](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#flet.Button.focus) `** –
    
    Requests focus for this control.
    

Examples[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#examples "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[Live example](https://flet-controls-gallery.fly.dev/buttons/elevatedbutton)

### Button[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#button "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#__codelineno-0-1) import flet as ft [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#__codelineno-0-2) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#__codelineno-0-3)[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#__codelineno-0-4) def main(page: ft.Page):     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#__codelineno-0-5)     page.add(        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#__codelineno-0-6)         ft.Button(content="Enabled button"),        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#__codelineno-0-7)         ft.Button(content="Disabled button", disabled=True),    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#__codelineno-0-8)     ) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#__codelineno-0-9) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#__codelineno-0-10)[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#__codelineno-0-11) if __name__ == "__main__":     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#__codelineno-0-12)     ft.run(main)`

[![Basic button](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/material/golden/macos/button/basic.png)](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/material/golden/macos/button/basic.png)
