### Handling clicks[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#handling-clicks "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#__codelineno-2-1) import flet as ft [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#__codelineno-2-2) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#__codelineno-2-3)[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#__codelineno-2-4) def main(page: ft.Page):     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#__codelineno-2-5)     def button_clicked(e: ft.Event[ft.Button]):        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#__codelineno-2-6)         button.data += 1        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#__codelineno-2-7)         message.value = f"Button clicked {button.data} time(s)"        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#__codelineno-2-8)         page.update() [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#__codelineno-2-9)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#__codelineno-2-10)     page.add(        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#__codelineno-2-11)         button := ft.Button(            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#__codelineno-2-12)             content="Button with 'click' event",            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#__codelineno-2-13)             data=0,            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#__codelineno-2-14)             on_click=button_clicked,        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#__codelineno-2-15)         ),        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#__codelineno-2-16)         message := ft.Text(),    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#__codelineno-2-17)     ) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#__codelineno-2-18) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#__codelineno-2-19)[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#__codelineno-2-20) if __name__ == "__main__":     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#__codelineno-2-21)     ft.run(main)`

[![Handling clicks](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/material/golden/macos/button/handling_clicks.png)](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/material/golden/macos/button/handling_clicks.png)

### Animate on hover[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#animate-on-hover "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#__codelineno-6-1) import flet as ft [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#__codelineno-6-2) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#__codelineno-6-3)[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#__codelineno-6-4) def main(page: ft.Page):     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#__codelineno-6-5)     def animate(e: ft.Event[ft.Button]):        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#__codelineno-6-6)         e.control.rotate = 0.1 if e.data else 0        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#__codelineno-6-7)         page.update() [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#__codelineno-6-8)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#__codelineno-6-9)     page.add(        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#__codelineno-6-10)         ft.Button(            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#__codelineno-6-11)             content="Hover over me, I'm animated!",            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#__codelineno-6-12)             rotate=0,            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#__codelineno-6-13)             animate_rotation=100,            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#__codelineno-6-14)             on_hover=animate,            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#__codelineno-6-15)             on_click=lambda e: page.add(ft.Text("Clicked! Try a long press!")),            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#__codelineno-6-16)             on_long_press=lambda e: page.add(ft.Text("I knew you could do it!")),        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#__codelineno-6-17)         )    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#__codelineno-6-18)     ) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#__codelineno-6-19) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#__codelineno-6-20)[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#__codelineno-6-21) if __name__ == "__main__":     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#__codelineno-6-22)     ft.run(main)`

[![Unhovered button](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/material/golden/macos/button/animate_on_hover_initial.png)](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/material/golden/macos/button/animate_on_hover_initial.png)

Normal button

[![Hovered button](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/material/golden/macos/button/animate_on_hover_hovered.png)](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/material/golden/macos/button/animate_on_hover_hovered.png)

Hovered button

Properties[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#flet.Button-properties "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### autofocus `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#flet.Button.autofocus "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#__codelineno-0-1) autofocus: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)  | None = None`

Whether this button should be focused initially.

### bgcolor `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#flet.Button.bgcolor "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#__codelineno-0-1) bgcolor: [ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None = [field](https://docs.python.org/3/library/dataclasses.html#dataclasses.field "<code>dataclasses.field</code>") (     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#__codelineno-0-2)     default=None, metadata={"skip": True} [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#__codelineno-0-3) )`

The button's background color. If not specified, defaults to the theme's primary color.

### clip\_behavior `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#flet.Button.clip_behavior "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#__codelineno-0-1) clip_behavior: [ClipBehavior](https://docs.flet.dev/types/clipbehavior/#flet.ClipBehavior)  | None = None`

The button's clip behavior.

### color `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#flet.Button.color "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#__codelineno-0-1) color: [ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None = [field](https://docs.python.org/3/library/dataclasses.html#dataclasses.field "<code>dataclasses.field</code>") (     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#__codelineno-0-2)     default=None, metadata={"skip": True} [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#__codelineno-0-3) )`

The button's foreground color. If not specified, defaults to the theme's primary color.

### content `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#flet.Button.content "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#__codelineno-0-1) content: [StrOrControl](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.StrOrControl)  | None = None`

The button's label. Typically a [`Text`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/text/#flet.Text)
 control or a string. If a string is provided, it will be wrapped in a [`Text`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/text/#flet.Text)
 control.

Raises:

*   `[ValueError](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ValueError) ` –
    
    If neither `content` nor [`icon`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#flet.Button.icon)
     (string or visible control) is provided.
    

### elevation `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#flet.Button.elevation "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#__codelineno-0-1) elevation: [Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  = [field](https://docs.python.org/3/library/dataclasses.html#dataclasses.field "<code>dataclasses.field</code>") (     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#__codelineno-0-2)     default=1, metadata={"skip": True} [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#__codelineno-0-3) )`

The button's elevation. If not specified, defaults to `1`.

### icon `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#flet.Button.icon "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#__codelineno-0-1) icon: [IconDataOrControl](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.IconDataOrControl)  | None = None`

The icon to display inside the button. Typically an [`Icon`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/icon/#flet.Icon)
 control or an `IconData`. If an `IconData` is provided, it will be wrapped in an [`Icon`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/icon/#flet.Icon)
 control.

Raises:

*   `[ValueError](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ValueError) ` –
    
    If neither `icon` nor [`content`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#flet.Button.content)
     (string or visible control) is provided.
    

### icon\_color `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#flet.Button.icon_color "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#__codelineno-0-1) icon_color: [ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None = None`

The color of the icon. If not specified, defaults to the current foreground color.

### style `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#flet.Button.style "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#__codelineno-0-1) style: [ButtonStyle](https://docs.flet.dev/types/buttonstyle/#flet.ButtonStyle)  | None = [field](https://docs.python.org/3/library/dataclasses.html#dataclasses.field "<code>dataclasses.field</code>") (     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#__codelineno-0-2)     default=None, metadata={"skip": True} [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#__codelineno-0-3) )`

The button's style.

### url `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#flet.Button.url "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#__codelineno-0-1) url: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)  | [Url](https://docs.flet.dev/types/url/#flet.Url)  | None = None`

The URL to open when the button is clicked.

Events[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#flet.Button-events "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### on\_blur `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#flet.Button.on_blur "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#__codelineno-0-1) on_blur: [ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[Button](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#flet.Button) ] | None = None`

Called when the button loses focus.

### on\_click `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#flet.Button.on_click "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#__codelineno-0-1) on_click: [ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[Button](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#flet.Button) ] | None = None`

Called when the button is clicked.

### on\_focus `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#flet.Button.on_focus "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#__codelineno-0-1) on_focus: [ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[Button](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#flet.Button) ] | None = None`

Called when the button is focused.

### on\_hover `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#flet.Button.on_hover "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#__codelineno-0-1) on_hover: [ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[Button](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#flet.Button) ] | None = None`

Called when the button is hovered.

### on\_long\_press `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#flet.Button.on_long_press "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#__codelineno-0-1) on_long_press: [ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[Button](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/button/#flet.Button) ] | None = None`

Called when the button is long-pressed.

# Chip - Flet

 

[Skip to content](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip)

Chip
====

Chips are compact elements that represent an attribute, text, entity, or action.

Example:

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#__codelineno-0-1) ft.Chip(     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#__codelineno-0-2)     label="Explore topics",    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#__codelineno-0-3)     leading=ft.Icon(ft.Icons.EXPLORE_OUTLINED), [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#__codelineno-0-4) )`

[![Chip](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/material/golden/macos/chip/image_for_docs.png)](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/material/golden/macos/chip/image_for_docs.png)

Basic Chip

Inherits: `[LayoutControl](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/layoutcontrol/#flet.LayoutControl) `

Properties

*   **`[autofocus](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip.autofocus) `**(`[bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool) `) –
    
    Whether this chip will be selected as the initial focus.
    
*   **`[bgcolor](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip.bgcolor) `**(`[ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None`) –
    
    Color to be used for the unselected, enabled chip's background.
    
*   **`[border_side](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip.border_side) `**(`[BorderSide](https://docs.flet.dev/types/borderside/#flet.BorderSide)  | None`) –
    
    Defines the color and weight of this chip's outline.
    
*   **`[check_color](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip.check_color) `**(`[ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None`) –
    
    The color of this chip's check mark when a check mark is visible.
    
*   **`[clip_behavior](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip.clip_behavior) `**(`[ClipBehavior](https://docs.flet.dev/types/clipbehavior/#flet.ClipBehavior) `) –
    
    The content will be clipped (or not) according to this option.
    
*   **`[color](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip.color) `**(`[ControlStateValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlStateValue) [[ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue) ] | None`) –
    
    The color that fills this chip in various [`ControlState`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/controlstate/#flet.ControlState)
    .
    
*   **`[delete_drawer_animation_style](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip.delete_drawer_animation_style) `**(`[AnimationStyle](https://docs.flet.dev/types/animationstyle/#flet.AnimationStyle)  | None`) –
    
    The animation style for the [`delete_icon`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip.delete_icon)
    's animations.
    
*   **`[delete_icon](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip.delete_icon) `**(`[Control](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control)  | None`) –
    
    A `Control` to display to the right of this chip's [`label`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip.label)
    
*   **`[delete_icon_color](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip.delete_icon_color) `**(`[ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None`) –
    
    The color of the [`delete_icon`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip.delete_icon)
    .
    
*   **`[delete_icon_size_constraints](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip.delete_icon_size_constraints) `**(`[BoxConstraints](https://docs.flet.dev/types/boxconstraints/#flet.BoxConstraints)  | None`) –
    
    The size constraints for the [`delete_icon`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip.delete_icon)
     control.
    
*   **`[delete_icon_tooltip](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip.delete_icon_tooltip) `**(`[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)  | None`) –
    
    The text to be used for this chip's `delete_icon` tooltip. If not provided or provided with an empty string, the tooltip of the delete icon will not be displayed.
    
*   **`[disabled_color](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip.disabled_color) `**(`[ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None`) –
    
    The color used for this chip's background if it is disabled.
    
*   **`[elevation](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip.elevation) `**(`[Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None`) –
    
    A non-negative value which defines the size of the shadow below this chip.
    
*   **`[elevation_on_click](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip.elevation_on_click) `**(`[Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None`) –
    
    The elevation to be applied on this chip relative to its parent during the press motion. This controls the size of the shadow below this chip.
    
*   **`[enable_animation_style](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip.enable_animation_style) `**(`[AnimationStyle](https://docs.flet.dev/types/animationstyle/#flet.AnimationStyle)  | None`) –
    
    The animation style for the enable and disable animations.
    
*   **`[label](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip.label) `**(`[StrOrControl](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.StrOrControl) `) –
    
    The primary content of this chip.
    
*   **`[label_padding](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip.label_padding) `**(`[PaddingValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.PaddingValue)  | None`) –
    
    The padding around the [`label`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip.label)
    .
    
*   **`[label_text_style](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip.label_text_style) `**(`[TextStyle](https://docs.flet.dev/types/textstyle/#flet.TextStyle)  | None`) –
    
    The style to be applied to this chip's [`label`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip.label)
    .
    
*   **`[leading](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip.leading) `**(`[Control](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control)  | None`) –
    
    A `Control` to display to the left of this chip's [`label`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip.label)
    .
    
*   **`[leading_drawer_animation_style](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip.leading_drawer_animation_style) `**(`[AnimationStyle](https://docs.flet.dev/types/animationstyle/#flet.AnimationStyle)  | None`) –
    
    The animation style for the [`leading`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip.leading)
     control's animations.
    
*   **`[leading_size_constraints](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip.leading_size_constraints) `**(`[BoxConstraints](https://docs.flet.dev/types/boxconstraints/#flet.BoxConstraints)  | None`) –
    
    The size constraints for the [`leading`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip.leading)
     control.
    
*   **`[padding](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip.padding) `**(`[PaddingValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.PaddingValue)  | None`) –
    
    The padding between the [`label`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip.label)
     and the outside shape.
    
*   **`[select_animation_style](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip.select_animation_style) `**(`[AnimationStyle](https://docs.flet.dev/types/animationstyle/#flet.AnimationStyle)  | None`) –
    
    The animation style for the select and unselect animations.
    
*   **`[selected](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip.selected) `**(`[bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool) `) –
    
    If [`on_select`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip.on_select)
     event is specified, `selected` property is used to determine whether this chip is selected or not.
    
*   **`[selected_color](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip.selected_color) `**(`[ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None`) –
    
    The color used for this chip's background when it is selected.
    
*   **`[selected_shadow_color](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip.selected_shadow_color) `**(`[ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None`) –
    
    The color used for this chip's background when the elevation is greater than `0` and this chip is selected.
    
*   **`[shadow_color](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip.shadow_color) `**(`[ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None`) –
    
    The color used for this chip's background when the elevation is greater than `0` and this chip is not selected.
    
*   **`[shape](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip.shape) `**(`[OutlinedBorder](https://docs.flet.dev/types/outlinedborder/#flet.OutlinedBorder)  | None`) –
    
    The shape of the border around this chip.
    
*   **`[show_checkmark](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip.show_checkmark) `**(`[bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool) `) –
    
    If [`on_select`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip.on_select)
     event is specified and chip is selected, `show_checkmark` is used to determine whether or not to show a checkmark.
    
*   **`[visual_density](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip.visual_density) `**(`[VisualDensity](https://docs.flet.dev/types/visualdensity/#flet.VisualDensity)  | None`) –
    
    TBD
    

Events

*   **`[on_blur](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip.on_blur) `**(`[ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[Chip](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip) ] | None`) –
    
    Called when this chip has lost focus.
    
*   **`[on_click](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip.on_click) `**(`[ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[Chip](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip) ] | None`) –
    
    Called when the user clicks on this chip.
    
*   **`[on_delete](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip.on_delete) `**(`[ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[Chip](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip) ] | None`) –
    
    Called when the user clicks on the [`delete_icon`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip.delete_icon)
    .
    
*   **`[on_focus](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip.on_focus) `**(`[ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[Chip](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip) ] | None`) –
    
    Called when this chip has received focus.
    
*   **`[on_select](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip.on_select) `**(`[ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[Chip](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip) ] | None`) –
    
    Called when the user clicks on this chip.
    

Examples[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#examples "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------

[Live example](https://flet-controls-gallery.fly.dev/input/chip)

### Assist chips[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#assist-chips "Permanent link")

Assist chips are chips with [`leading`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip.leading)
 icon and [`on_click`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip.on_click)
 event specified.

They represent smart or automated actions that appear dynamically and contextually in a UI.

An alternative to assist chips are buttons, which should appear persistently and consistently.

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#__codelineno-0-1) import flet as ft [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#__codelineno-0-2) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#__codelineno-0-3)[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#__codelineno-0-4) def main(page: ft.Page):     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#__codelineno-0-5)     def handle_chip1_click(e: ft.Event[ft.Chip]):        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#__codelineno-0-6)         e.control.label.value = "Saved to favorites"        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#__codelineno-0-7)         e.control.leading = ft.Icon(ft.Icons.FAVORITE_OUTLINED)        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#__codelineno-0-8)         e.control.disabled = True        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#__codelineno-0-9)         page.update() [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#__codelineno-0-10)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#__codelineno-0-11)     async def handle_chip2_click(e: ft.Event[ft.Chip]):        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#__codelineno-0-12)         await page.launch_url("https://maps.google.com")        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#__codelineno-0-13)         page.update() [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#__codelineno-0-14)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#__codelineno-0-15)     page.add(        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#__codelineno-0-16)         ft.Row(            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#__codelineno-0-17)             controls=[                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#__codelineno-0-18)                 ft.Chip(                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#__codelineno-0-19)                     label=ft.Text("Save to favourites"),                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#__codelineno-0-20)                     leading=ft.Icon(ft.Icons.FAVORITE_BORDER_OUTLINED),                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#__codelineno-0-21)                     bgcolor=ft.Colors.GREEN_200,                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#__codelineno-0-22)                     disabled_color=ft.Colors.GREEN_100,                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#__codelineno-0-23)                     autofocus=True,                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#__codelineno-0-24)                     on_click=handle_chip1_click,                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#__codelineno-0-25)                 ),                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#__codelineno-0-26)                 ft.Chip(                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#__codelineno-0-27)                     label=ft.Text("9 min walk"),                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#__codelineno-0-28)                     leading=ft.Icon(ft.Icons.MAP_SHARP),                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#__codelineno-0-29)                     bgcolor=ft.Colors.GREEN_200,                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#__codelineno-0-30)                     on_click=handle_chip2_click,                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#__codelineno-0-31)                 ),            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#__codelineno-0-32)             ]        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#__codelineno-0-33)         )    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#__codelineno-0-34)     ) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#__codelineno-0-35) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#__codelineno-0-36)[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#__codelineno-0-37) if __name__ == "__main__":     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#__codelineno-0-38)     ft.run(main)`

[![assist-chips](https://docs.flet.dev/examples/controls/chip/media/assist_chips.png)](https://docs.flet.dev/examples/controls/chip/media/assist_chips.png)

### Filter chips[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#filter-chips "Permanent link")

Filter chips are chips with [`on_select`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip.on_select)
 event specified.

They use tags or descriptive words provided in the [`label`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip.label)
 to filter content. They can be a good alternative to switches or checkboxes.

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#__codelineno-1-1) import flet as ft [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#__codelineno-1-2) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#__codelineno-1-3)[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#__codelineno-1-4) def main(page: ft.Page):     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#__codelineno-1-5)     def handle_amenity_selection(e: ft.Event[ft.Chip]):        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#__codelineno-1-6)         page.update() [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#__codelineno-1-7)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#__codelineno-1-8)     amenities = ["Washer / Dryer", "Ramp access", "Dogs OK", "Cats OK", "Smoke-free"] [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#__codelineno-1-9)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#__codelineno-1-10)     page.add(        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#__codelineno-1-11)         ft.Row(            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#__codelineno-1-12)             controls=[                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#__codelineno-1-13)                 ft.Icon(ft.Icons.HOTEL_CLASS),                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#__codelineno-1-14)                 ft.Text("Amenities"),            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#__codelineno-1-15)             ]        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#__codelineno-1-16)         ),        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#__codelineno-1-17)         ft.Row(            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#__codelineno-1-18)             controls=[                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#__codelineno-1-19)                 ft.Chip(                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#__codelineno-1-20)                     label=ft.Text(amenity),                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#__codelineno-1-21)                     bgcolor=ft.Colors.GREEN_200,                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#__codelineno-1-22)                     disabled_color=ft.Colors.GREEN_100,                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#__codelineno-1-23)                     autofocus=True,                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#__codelineno-1-24)                     on_select=handle_amenity_selection,                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#__codelineno-1-25)                 )                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#__codelineno-1-26)                 for amenity in amenities            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#__codelineno-1-27)             ]        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#__codelineno-1-28)         ),    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#__codelineno-1-29)     ) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#__codelineno-1-30) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#__codelineno-1-31)[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#__codelineno-1-32) if __name__ == "__main__":     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#__codelineno-1-33)     ft.run(main)`

[![filter-chips](https://docs.flet.dev/examples/controls/chip/media/filter_chips.png)](https://docs.flet.dev/examples/controls/chip/media/filter_chips.png)

Properties[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip-properties "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### autofocus `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip.autofocus "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#__codelineno-0-1) autofocus: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)  = False`

Whether this chip will be selected as the initial focus.

If there is more than one control on a page with autofocus set, then the first one added to the page will get focus.

### bgcolor `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip.bgcolor "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#__codelineno-0-1) bgcolor: [ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None = None`

Color to be used for the unselected, enabled chip's background.

### border\_side `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip.border_side "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#__codelineno-0-1) border_side: [BorderSide](https://docs.flet.dev/types/borderside/#flet.BorderSide)  | None = None`

Defines the color and weight of this chip's outline.

### check\_color `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip.check_color "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#__codelineno-0-1) check_color: [ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None = None`

The color of this chip's check mark when a check mark is visible.

### clip\_behavior `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip.clip_behavior "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#__codelineno-0-1) clip_behavior: [ClipBehavior](https://docs.flet.dev/types/clipbehavior/#flet.ClipBehavior)  = [NONE](https://docs.flet.dev/types/clipbehavior/#flet.ClipBehavior.NONE)`

The content will be clipped (or not) according to this option.

### color `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip.color "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#__codelineno-0-1) color: [ControlStateValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlStateValue) [[ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue) ] | None = None`

The color that fills this chip in various [`ControlState`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/controlstate/#flet.ControlState)
.

### delete\_drawer\_animation\_style `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip.delete_drawer_animation_style "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#__codelineno-0-1) delete_drawer_animation_style: [AnimationStyle](https://docs.flet.dev/types/animationstyle/#flet.AnimationStyle)  | None = None`

The animation style for the [`delete_icon`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip.delete_icon)
's animations.

### delete\_icon `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip.delete_icon "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#__codelineno-0-1) delete_icon: [Control](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control)  | None = None`

A `Control` to display to the right of this chip's [`label`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip.label)
 in case [`on_delete`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip.on_delete)
 event is specified.

### delete\_icon\_color `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip.delete_icon_color "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#__codelineno-0-1) delete_icon_color: [ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None = None`

The color of the [`delete_icon`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip.delete_icon)
.

### delete\_icon\_size\_constraints `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip.delete_icon_size_constraints "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#__codelineno-0-1) delete_icon_size_constraints: [BoxConstraints](https://docs.flet.dev/types/boxconstraints/#flet.BoxConstraints)  | None = None`

The size constraints for the [`delete_icon`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip.delete_icon)
 control.

If `None`, it defaults to a minimum size of chip height or label height (whichever is greater) and a padding of 8.0 pixels on all sides.

### delete\_icon\_tooltip `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip.delete_icon_tooltip "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#__codelineno-0-1) delete_icon_tooltip: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)  | None = None`

The text to be used for this chip's `delete_icon` tooltip. If not provided or provided with an empty string, the tooltip of the delete icon will not be displayed.

### disabled\_color `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip.disabled_color "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#__codelineno-0-1) disabled_color: [ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None = None`

The color used for this chip's background if it is disabled.

### elevation `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip.elevation "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#__codelineno-0-1) elevation: [Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None = None`

A non-negative value which defines the size of the shadow below this chip.

Defaults to `0`.

Raises:

*   `[ValueError](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ValueError) ` –
    
    If [`elevation`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip.elevation)
     is negative.
    

### elevation\_on\_click `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip.elevation_on_click "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#__codelineno-0-1) elevation_on_click: [Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None = None`

The elevation to be applied on this chip relative to its parent during the press motion. This controls the size of the shadow below this chip.

Defaults to `8.0`.

Raises:

*   `[ValueError](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ValueError) ` –
    
    If it is not greater than or equal to `0.0`.
    

### enable\_animation\_style `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip.enable_animation_style "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#__codelineno-0-1) enable_animation_style: [AnimationStyle](https://docs.flet.dev/types/animationstyle/#flet.AnimationStyle)  | None = None`

The animation style for the enable and disable animations.

### label `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip.label "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#__codelineno-0-1) label: [StrOrControl](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.StrOrControl)`

The primary content of this chip.

Typically a [`Text`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/text/#flet.Text)
 control.

### label\_padding `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip.label_padding "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#__codelineno-0-1) label_padding: [PaddingValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.PaddingValue)  | None = None`

The padding around the [`label`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip.label)
.

By default, this is `4` logical pixels at the beginning and the end of the [`label`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip.label)
, and zero on `top` and `bottom`.

### label\_text\_style `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip.label_text_style "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#__codelineno-0-1) label_text_style: [TextStyle](https://docs.flet.dev/types/textstyle/#flet.TextStyle)  | None = None`

The style to be applied to this chip's [`label`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip.label)
.

### leading `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip.leading "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#__codelineno-0-1) leading: [Control](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control)  | None = None`

A `Control` to display to the left of this chip's [`label`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip.label)
.

Typically the leading control is an [`Icon`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/icon/#flet.Icon)
 or a [`CircleAvatar`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/circleavatar/#flet.CircleAvatar)
.

### leading\_drawer\_animation\_style `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip.leading_drawer_animation_style "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#__codelineno-0-1) leading_drawer_animation_style: [AnimationStyle](https://docs.flet.dev/types/animationstyle/#flet.AnimationStyle)  | None = None`

The animation style for the [`leading`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip.leading)
 control's animations.

### leading\_size\_constraints `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip.leading_size_constraints "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#__codelineno-0-1) leading_size_constraints: [BoxConstraints](https://docs.flet.dev/types/boxconstraints/#flet.BoxConstraints)  | None = None`

The size constraints for the [`leading`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip.leading)
 control.

If `None`, it defaults to a minimum size of chip height or label height (whichever is greater) and a padding of `8.0` pixels on all sides.

### padding `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip.padding "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#__codelineno-0-1) padding: [PaddingValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.PaddingValue)  | None = None`

The padding between the [`label`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip.label)
 and the outside shape.

Defaults to `8` logical pixels on all sides.

### select\_animation\_style `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip.select_animation_style "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#__codelineno-0-1) select_animation_style: [AnimationStyle](https://docs.flet.dev/types/animationstyle/#flet.AnimationStyle)  | None = None`

The animation style for the select and unselect animations.

### selected `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip.selected "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#__codelineno-0-1) selected: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)  = False`

If [`on_select`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip.on_select)
 event is specified, `selected` property is used to determine whether this chip is selected or not.

### selected\_color `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip.selected_color "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#__codelineno-0-1) selected_color: [ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None = None`

The color used for this chip's background when it is selected.

### selected\_shadow\_color `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip.selected_shadow_color "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#__codelineno-0-1) selected_shadow_color: [ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None = None`

The color used for this chip's background when the elevation is greater than `0` and this chip is selected.

### shadow\_color `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip.shadow_color "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#__codelineno-0-1) shadow_color: [ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None = None`

The color used for this chip's background when the elevation is greater than `0` and this chip is not selected.

### shape `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip.shape "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#__codelineno-0-1) shape: [OutlinedBorder](https://docs.flet.dev/types/outlinedborder/#flet.OutlinedBorder)  | None = None`

The shape of the border around this chip.

Defaults to [`ChipTheme.shape`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/chiptheme/#flet.ChipTheme.shape)
, or if that is resolves to `None`, falls back to `RoundedRectangleBorder(radius=8)`.

### show\_checkmark `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip.show_checkmark "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#__codelineno-0-1) show_checkmark: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)  = True`

If [`on_select`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip.on_select)
 event is specified and chip is selected, `show_checkmark` is used to determine whether or not to show a checkmark.

### visual\_density `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip.visual_density "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#__codelineno-0-1) visual_density: [VisualDensity](https://docs.flet.dev/types/visualdensity/#flet.VisualDensity)  | None = None`

TBD

Events[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip-events "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### on\_blur `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip.on_blur "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#__codelineno-0-1) on_blur: [ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[Chip](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip) ] | None = None`

Called when this chip has lost focus.

### on\_click `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip.on_click "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#__codelineno-0-1) on_click: [ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[Chip](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip) ] | None = None`

Called when the user clicks on this chip.

Raises:

*   `[ValueError](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ValueError) ` –
    
    If specified together with [`on_select`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip.on_select)
    .
    

### on\_delete `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip.on_delete "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#__codelineno-0-1) on_delete: [ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[Chip](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip) ] | None = None`

Called when the user clicks on the [`delete_icon`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip.delete_icon)
.

### on\_focus `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip.on_focus "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#__codelineno-0-1) on_focus: [ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[Chip](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip) ] | None = None`

Called when this chip has received focus.

### on\_select `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip.on_select "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#__codelineno-0-1) on_select: [ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[Chip](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip) ] | None = None`

Called when the user clicks on this chip.

It internally changes [`selected`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip.selected)
 property to the opposite value.

Raises:

*   `[ValueError](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ValueError) ` –
    
    If specified together with [`on_click`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/chip/#flet.Chip.on_click)
    .
    

Back to top

# AppBar - Flet

 

[Skip to content](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#flet.AppBar)

AppBar
======

A material design app bar.

Example:

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#__codelineno-0-1) ft.AppBar(     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#__codelineno-0-2)     leading=ft.Icon(ft.Icons.MENU),    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#__codelineno-0-3)     title=ft.Text("Dashboard"),    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#__codelineno-0-4)     bgcolor=ft.Colors.SURFACE_CONTAINER,    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#__codelineno-0-5)     actions=[        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#__codelineno-0-6)         ft.IconButton(ft.Icons.SEARCH),        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#__codelineno-0-7)         ft.IconButton(ft.Icons.MORE_VERT),    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#__codelineno-0-8)     ], [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#__codelineno-0-9) )`

[![AppBar](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/material/golden/macos/app_bar/image_for_docs.png)](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/material/golden/macos/app_bar/image_for_docs.png)

Basic AppBar

Inherits: `[AdaptiveControl](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/adaptivecontrol/#flet.AdaptiveControl) `

Properties

*   **`[actions](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#flet.AppBar.actions) `**(`[list](https://docs.python.org/3/glossary.html#term-list) [[Control](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control) ] | None`) –
    
    A list of `Control`s to display in a row after the title control.
    
*   **`[actions_padding](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#flet.AppBar.actions_padding) `**(`[PaddingValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.PaddingValue)  | None`) –
    
    The padding between the [`actions`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#flet.AppBar.actions)
     and the end of this app bar.
    
*   **`[automatically_imply_leading](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#flet.AppBar.automatically_imply_leading) `**(`[bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool) `) –
    
    Whether we should try to imply the [`leading`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#flet.AppBar.leading)
     control if it is `None`.
    
*   **`[bgcolor](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#flet.AppBar.bgcolor) `**(`[ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None`) –
    
    The fill color to use for this app bar.
    
*   **`[center_title](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#flet.AppBar.center_title) `**(`[bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)  | None`) –
    
    Whether the [`title`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#flet.AppBar.title)
     should be centered.
    
*   **`[clip_behavior](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#flet.AppBar.clip_behavior) `**(`[ClipBehavior](https://docs.flet.dev/types/clipbehavior/#flet.ClipBehavior)  | None`) –
    
    The content will be clipped (or not) according to this option.
    
*   **`[color](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#flet.AppBar.color) `**(`[ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None`) –
    
    The default color for [`Text`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/text/#flet.Text)
    
*   **`[elevation](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#flet.AppBar.elevation) `**(`[Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None`) –
    
    The app bar's elevation.
    
*   **`[elevation_on_scroll](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#flet.AppBar.elevation_on_scroll) `**(`[Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None`) –
    
    The elevation to be used if this app bar has something scrolled underneath it.
    
*   **`[exclude_header_semantics](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#flet.AppBar.exclude_header_semantics) `**(`[bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool) `) –
    
    Whether the [`title`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#flet.AppBar.title)
     should be wrapped with header [`Semantics`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/semantics/#flet.Semantics)
    .
    
*   **`[force_material_transparency](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#flet.AppBar.force_material_transparency) `**(`[bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool) `) –
    
    Forces this app bar to be transparent (instead of Material's default type).
    
*   **`[leading](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#flet.AppBar.leading) `**(`[Control](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control)  | None`) –
    
    A control to display before the toolbar's [`title`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#flet.AppBar.title)
    .
    
*   **`[leading_width](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#flet.AppBar.leading_width) `**(`[Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None`) –
    
    Defines the width of the [`leading`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#flet.AppBar.leading)
     control.
    
*   **`[secondary](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#flet.AppBar.secondary) `**(`[bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool) `) –
    
    Whether this app bar is not being displayed at the top of the screen.
    
*   **`[shadow_color](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#flet.AppBar.shadow_color) `**(`[ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None`) –
    
    The color of the shadow below this app bar.
    
*   **`[shape](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#flet.AppBar.shape) `**(`[OutlinedBorder](https://docs.flet.dev/types/outlinedborder/#flet.OutlinedBorder)  | None`) –
    
    The shape of this app bar's Material as well as its shadow.
    
*   **`[title](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#flet.AppBar.title) `**(`[StrOrControl](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.StrOrControl)  | None`) –
    
    The primary Control displayed in this app bar.
    
*   **`[title_spacing](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#flet.AppBar.title_spacing) `**(`[Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None`) –
    
    The spacing around [`title`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#flet.AppBar.title)
     on the horizontal axis.
    
*   **`[title_text_style](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#flet.AppBar.title_text_style) `**(`[TextStyle](https://docs.flet.dev/types/textstyle/#flet.TextStyle)  | None`) –
    
    The style to be used for the [`Text`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/text/#flet.Text)
     controls in the [`title`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#flet.AppBar.title)
    .
    
*   **`[toolbar_height](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#flet.AppBar.toolbar_height) `**(`[Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None`) –
    
    Defines the height of the toolbar component of this app bar.
    
*   **`[toolbar_opacity](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#flet.AppBar.toolbar_opacity) `**(`[Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number) `) –
    
    The opacity of the toolbar.
    
*   **`[toolbar_text_style](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#flet.AppBar.toolbar_text_style) `**(`[TextStyle](https://docs.flet.dev/types/textstyle/#flet.TextStyle)  | None`) –
    
    The style to be used for the [`Text`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/text/#flet.Text)
     controls in the app bar's [`leading`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#flet.AppBar.leading)
     and [`actions`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#flet.AppBar.actions)
    .
    

Examples[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#examples "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[Live example](https://flet-controls-gallery.fly.dev/navigation/appbar)

### Actions and Popup Menu[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#actions-and-popup-menu "Permanent link")

e.control.checked = not e.control.checked        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#__codelineno-0-8)         page.update() [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#__codelineno-0-9)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#__codelineno-0-10)     page.appbar = ft.AppBar(        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#__codelineno-0-11)         leading=ft.Icon(ft.Icons.PALETTE),        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#__codelineno-0-12)         leading_width=40,        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#__codelineno-0-13)         title=ft.Text("AppBar bgcolor=ft.Colors.BLUE_GREY_400,        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#__codelineno-0-16)         actions=[            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#__codelineno-0-17) Properties[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#flet.AppBar-properties "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### actions `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#flet.AppBar.actions "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#__codelineno-0-1) actions: [list](https://docs.python.org/3/glossary.html#term-list) [[Control](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control) ] | None = None`

A list of `Control`s to display in a row after the title control.

Typically, these controls are [`IconButton`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#flet.IconButton)
s representing common operations. For less common operations, consider using a [`PopupMenuButton`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/popupmenubutton/#flet.PopupMenuButton)
 as the last action.

Info

If [`AppBar.adaptive`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/adaptivecontrol/#flet.AdaptiveControl.adaptive)
 is `True` and this app is opened on an iOS or macOS device, these `actions` will be automatically placed in a [`Row`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/row/#flet.Row)
. This is because [`CupertinoAppBar.trailing`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinoappbar/#flet.CupertinoAppBar.trailing)
 (which is the counterpart property of `actions`) takes only a single `Control`.

### actions\_padding `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#flet.AppBar.actions_padding "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#__codelineno-0-1) actions_padding: [PaddingValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.PaddingValue)  | None = None`

The padding between the [`actions`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#flet.AppBar.actions)
 and the end of this app bar.

### automatically\_imply\_leading `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#flet.AppBar.automatically_imply_leading "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#__codelineno-0-1) automatically_imply_leading: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)  = True`

Whether we should try to imply the [`leading`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#flet.AppBar.leading)
 control if it is `None`.

*   If `True` and `leading` is `None`, this app bar will automatically determine an appropriate leading control.
*   If `False` and `leading` is `None`, the space is allocated to the [`title`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#flet.AppBar.title)
    .
*   If a `leading` control is provided, this parameter has no effect.

### bgcolor `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#flet.AppBar.bgcolor "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#__codelineno-0-1) bgcolor: [ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None = None`

The fill color to use for this app bar.

Default color is defined by [`AppBarTheme.bgcolor`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/appbartheme/#flet.AppBarTheme.bgcolor)

### center\_title `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#flet.AppBar.center_title "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#__codelineno-0-1) center_title: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)  | None = None`

Whether the [`title`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#flet.AppBar.title)
 should be centered.

Default value is defined by [`AppBarTheme.center_title`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/appbartheme/#flet.AppBarTheme.center_title)

### clip\_behavior `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#flet.AppBar.clip_behavior "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#__codelineno-0-1) clip_behavior: [ClipBehavior](https://docs.flet.dev/types/clipbehavior/#flet.ClipBehavior)  | None = None`

The content will be clipped (or not) according to this option.

### color `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#flet.AppBar.color "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#__codelineno-0-1) color: [ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None = None`

The default color for [`Text`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/text/#flet.Text)
 and [`Icon`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/icon/#flet.Icon)
 controls within this app bar.

Default color is defined by [`AppBarTheme.color`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/appbartheme/#flet.AppBarTheme.color)

### elevation `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#flet.AppBar.elevation "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#__codelineno-0-1) elevation: [Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None = None`

The app bar's elevation.

Note

This effect is only visible when using the Material 2 design (when [`Theme.use_material3`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/theme/#flet.Theme.use_material3)
 is `False`).

Raises:

*   `[ValueError](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ValueError) ` –
    
    If it is not greater than or equal to `0`.
    

### elevation\_on\_scroll `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#flet.AppBar.elevation_on_scroll "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#__codelineno-0-1) elevation_on_scroll: [Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None = None`

The elevation to be used if this app bar has something scrolled underneath it.

Raises:

*   `[ValueError](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ValueError) ` –
    
    If it is not greater than or equal to `0`.
    

### exclude\_header\_semantics `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#flet.AppBar.exclude_header_semantics "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#__codelineno-0-1) exclude_header_semantics: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)  = False`

Whether the [`title`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#flet.AppBar.title)
 should be wrapped with header [`Semantics`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/semantics/#flet.Semantics)
.

### force\_material\_transparency `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#flet.AppBar.force_material_transparency "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#__codelineno-0-1) force_material_transparency: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)  = False`

Forces this app bar to be transparent (instead of Material's default type).

This will also remove the visual display of [`bgcolor`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#flet.AppBar.bgcolor)
 and [`elevation`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#flet.AppBar.elevation)
, and affect other characteristics of this app bar.

### leading `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#flet.AppBar.leading "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#__codelineno-0-1) leading: [Control](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control)  | None = None`

A control to display before the toolbar's [`title`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#flet.AppBar.title)
.

Typically an [`Icon`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/icon/#flet.Icon)
 or [`IconButton`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#flet.IconButton)
 control.

### leading\_width `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#flet.AppBar.leading_width "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#__codelineno-0-1) leading_width: [Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None = None`

Defines the width of the [`leading`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#flet.AppBar.leading)
 control.

### secondary `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#flet.AppBar.secondary "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#__codelineno-0-1) secondary: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)  = False`

Whether this app bar is not being displayed at the top of the screen.

### shadow\_color `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#flet.AppBar.shadow_color "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#__codelineno-0-1) shadow_color: [ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None = None`

The color of the shadow below this app bar.

A shadow is only visible and displayed if the [`elevation`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#flet.AppBar.elevation)
 is greater than zero.

### shape `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#flet.AppBar.shape "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#__codelineno-0-1) shape: [OutlinedBorder](https://docs.flet.dev/types/outlinedborder/#flet.OutlinedBorder)  | None = None`

The shape of this app bar's Material as well as its shadow.

### title `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#flet.AppBar.title "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#__codelineno-0-1) title: [StrOrControl](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.StrOrControl)  | None = None`

The primary Control displayed in this app bar.

Typically a [`Text`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/text/#flet.Text)
 control that contains a description of the current contents of this app.

Note

If [`AppBar.adaptive=True`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/adaptivecontrol/#flet.AdaptiveControl.adaptive)
 and this app is opened on an iOS or macOS device, this [`title`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#flet.AppBar.title)
 control will be automatically centered, independent of the value of [`center_title`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#flet.AppBar.center_title)
.

### title\_spacing `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#flet.AppBar.title_spacing "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#__codelineno-0-1) title_spacing: [Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None = None`

The spacing around [`title`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#flet.AppBar.title)
 on the horizontal axis. It is applied even if there are no [`leading`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#flet.AppBar.leading)
 or [`actions`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#flet.AppBar.actions)
 controls.

Tip

If you want [`title`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#flet.AppBar.title)
 to take all the space available, set `title_spacing` to `0.0`.

### title\_text\_style `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#flet.AppBar.title_text_style "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#__codelineno-0-1) title_text_style: [TextStyle](https://docs.flet.dev/types/textstyle/#flet.TextStyle)  | None = None`

The style to be used for the [`Text`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/text/#flet.Text)
 controls in the [`title`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#flet.AppBar.title)
.

### toolbar\_height `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#flet.AppBar.toolbar_height "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#__codelineno-0-1) toolbar_height: [Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None = None`

Defines the height of the toolbar component of this app bar.

### toolbar\_opacity `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#flet.AppBar.toolbar_opacity "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#__codelineno-0-1) toolbar_opacity: [Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  = 1.0`

The opacity of the toolbar.

*   `0.0`: transparent
*   `1.0`: fully opaque

Raises:

*   `[ValueError](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ValueError) ` –
    
    If it is not between `0.0` and `1.0`, inclusive.
    

### toolbar\_text\_style `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#flet.AppBar.toolbar_text_style "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#__codelineno-0-1) toolbar_text_style: [TextStyle](https://docs.flet.dev/types/textstyle/#flet.TextStyle)  | None = None`

The style to be used for the [`Text`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/text/#flet.Text)
 controls in the app bar's [`leading`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#flet.AppBar.leading)
 and [`actions`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#flet.AppBar.actions)
.

# BaseControl - Flet

 

[Skip to content](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basecontrol/#flet.BaseControl)

BaseControl
===========

Base class for all Flet controls and services.

Properties

*   **`[data](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basecontrol/#flet.BaseControl.data) `**(`[Any](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "<code>typing.Any</code>") `) –
    
    Arbitrary data of any type.
    
*   **`[key](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basecontrol/#flet.BaseControl.key) `**(`[KeyValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.KeyValue)  | None`) –
    
*   **`[page](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basecontrol/#flet.BaseControl.page) `**(`[Page](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/page/#flet.Page)  | [BasePage](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#flet.BasePage) `) –
    
    The page to which this control belongs to.
    
*   **`[parent](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basecontrol/#flet.BaseControl.parent) `**(`[BaseControl](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basecontrol/#flet.BaseControl)  | None`) –
    
    The direct ancestor(parent) of this control.
    

Methods

*   **`[before_event](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basecontrol/#flet.BaseControl.before_event) `** –
    
    Intercept an event before its handler is executed.
    
*   **`[is_isolated](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basecontrol/#flet.BaseControl.is_isolated) `** –
    
    Return whether this control is marked as isolated.
    
*   **`[update](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basecontrol/#flet.BaseControl.update) `** –
    
    Request a UI update for this control.
    

Properties[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basecontrol/#flet.BaseControl-properties "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### data `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basecontrol/#flet.BaseControl.data "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basecontrol/#__codelineno-0-1) data: [Any](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Any "<code>typing.Any</code>")  = skip_field()`

Arbitrary data of any type.

### key `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basecontrol/#flet.BaseControl.key "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basecontrol/#__codelineno-0-1) key: [KeyValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.KeyValue)  | None = None`

### page `property` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basecontrol/#flet.BaseControl.page "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basecontrol/#__codelineno-0-1) page: [Page](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/page/#flet.Page)  | [BasePage](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#flet.BasePage)`

The page to which this control belongs to.

### parent `property` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basecontrol/#flet.BaseControl.parent "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basecontrol/#__codelineno-0-1) parent: [BaseControl](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basecontrol/#flet.BaseControl)  | None`

The direct ancestor(parent) of this control.

It defaults to `None` and will only have a value when this control is mounted (added to the page tree).

The `Page` control (which is the root of the tree) is an exception - it always has `parent=None`.

Methods[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basecontrol/#flet.BaseControl-methods "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### before\_event [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basecontrol/#flet.BaseControl.before_event "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basecontrol/#__codelineno-0-1) before_event(e: [ControlEvent](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEvent) )`

Intercept an event before its handler is executed.

Return `False` to cancel dispatch. Return `True` or `None` to continue normal event processing.

### is\_isolated [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basecontrol/#flet.BaseControl.is_isolated "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basecontrol/#__codelineno-0-1) is_isolated()`

Return whether this control is marked as isolated.

Isolated controls are excluded from parent-driven update traversal and are expected to manage their own update boundaries.

### update [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basecontrol/#flet.BaseControl.update "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basecontrol/#__codelineno-0-1) update() -> None`

Request a UI update for this control.

Call after changing control state or properties. The control must be attached to a page and not marked as frozen.

# Segment - Flet

 

[Skip to content](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/segment/#flet.Segment)

Segment
=======

Inherits: `[Control](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control) `

A segment for a [`SegmentedButton`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/segmentedbutton/#flet.SegmentedButton)
.

Properties

*   **`[icon](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/segment/#flet.Segment.icon) `**(`[IconDataOrControl](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.IconDataOrControl)  | None`) –
    
    The icon to be displayed in the segment.
    
*   **`[label](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/segment/#flet.Segment.label) `**(`[StrOrControl](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.StrOrControl)  | None`) –
    
    The label (usually a [`Text`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/text/#flet.Text)
    ) to be displayed in the segment.
    
*   **`[value](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/segment/#flet.Segment.value) `**(`[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str) `) –
    
    Used to identify this segment.
    

Properties[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/segment/#flet.Segment-properties "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### icon `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/segment/#flet.Segment.icon "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/segment/#__codelineno-0-1) icon: [IconDataOrControl](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.IconDataOrControl)  | None = None`

The icon to be displayed in the segment.

Typically an [`Icon`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/icon/#flet.Icon)
.

Raises:

     is set and visible.
    

### label `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/segment/#flet.Segment.label "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/segment/#__codelineno-0-1) label: [StrOrControl](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.StrOrControl)  | None = None`

The label (usually a [`Text`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/text/#flet.Text)
) to be displayed in the segment.

Raises:

*   `[ValueError](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ValueError) ` –
    
    If neither it nor [`icon`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/segment/#flet.Segment.icon)
     is set and visible.
    

### value `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/segment/#flet.Segment.value "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/segment/#__codelineno-0-1) value: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)`

Used to identify this segment.
