# Tab - Flet

 

[Skip to content](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/tab/#flet.Tab)

Tab
===

Inherits: `[AdaptiveControl](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/adaptivecontrol/#flet.AdaptiveControl) `

A Material Design [`TabBar`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/tabbar/#flet.TabBar)
 tab.

Properties

*   **`[height](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/tab/#flet.Tab.height) `**(`[Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None`) –
    
    The height of the tab.
    
*   **`[icon](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/tab/#flet.Tab.icon) `**(`IconDataOrControl | None`) –
    
    An icon to display on the left of Tab text.
    
*   **`[icon_margin](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/tab/#flet.Tab.icon_margin) `**(`[MarginValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.MarginValue)  | None`) –
    
    The margin added around the tab's icon.
    
*   **`[label](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/tab/#flet.Tab.label) `**(`[StrOrControl](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.StrOrControl)  | None`) –
    
    The tab's name. Can be either a string or a control.
    

Properties[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/tab/#flet.Tab-properties "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### height `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/tab/#flet.Tab.height "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/tab/#__codelineno-0-1) height: [Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None = None`

The height of the tab.

If `None`, it will be calculated based on the content of the Tab. When [`icon`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/tab/#flet.Tab.icon)
 is not `None` along with [`label`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/tab/#flet.Tab.label)
, the default `height` is `72.0` pixels. Without an `icon`, the `height` is 46.0 pixels.

Currently, the provided tab height cannot be lower than the default height.

### icon `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/tab/#flet.Tab.icon "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/tab/#__codelineno-0-1) icon: IconDataOrControl | None = None`

An icon to display on the left of Tab text.

### icon\_margin `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/tab/#flet.Tab.icon_margin "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/tab/#__codelineno-0-1) icon_margin: [MarginValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.MarginValue)  | None = None`

The margin added around the tab's icon.

Only useful when used in combination with [`icon`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/tab/#flet.Tab.icon)
, and [`label`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/tab/#flet.Tab.label)
 is not `None`.

Defaults to `2` pixels of bottom margin. If [`Theme.use_material3`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/theme/#flet.Theme.use_material3)
 is `False`, then defaults to `10` pixels of bottom margin.

### label `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/tab/#flet.Tab.label "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/tab/#__codelineno-0-1) label: [StrOrControl](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.StrOrControl)  | None = None`

The tab's name. Can be either a string or a control.

Raises:

*   `[ValueError](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ValueError) ` –
    
    If both [`label`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/tab/#flet.Tab.label)
     and [`icon`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/tab/#flet.Tab.icon)
     are not set.
    

Back to top

---

# TabBarView - Flet

 

[Skip to content](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/tabbarview/#flet.TabBarView)

TabBarView
==========

Inherits: `[LayoutControl](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/layoutcontrol/#flet.LayoutControl) `, `[AdaptiveControl](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/adaptivecontrol/#flet.AdaptiveControl) `

A page view with one child per tab.

Note

The length of [`controls`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/tabbarview/#flet.TabBarView.controls)
 must be the same as the [`length`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/tabs/#flet.Tabs.length)
 property of the ancestor [`Tabs`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/tabs/#flet.Tabs)
.

Properties

*   **`[clip_behavior](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/tabbarview/#flet.TabBarView.clip_behavior) `**(`[ClipBehavior](https://docs.flet.dev/types/clipbehavior/#flet.ClipBehavior) `) –
    
    Defines how the [`controls`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/tabbarview/#flet.TabBarView.controls)
     will be clipped.
    
*   **`[controls](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/tabbarview/#flet.TabBarView.controls) `**(`[list](https://docs.python.org/3/glossary.html#term-list) [[Control](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control) ]`) –
    
    A list of controls, where each control represents the content of a corresponding tab. So, a control at index `i` in this list is displayed when the [`Tab`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/tab/#flet.Tab)
     at index `i` is selected.
    
*   **`[viewport_fraction](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/tabbarview/#flet.TabBarView.viewport_fraction) `**(`[Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number) `) –
    
    The fraction of the viewport that each page should occupy.
    

Properties[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/tabbarview/#flet.TabBarView-properties "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### clip\_behavior `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/tabbarview/#flet.TabBarView.clip_behavior "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/tabbarview/#__codelineno-0-1) clip_behavior: [ClipBehavior](https://docs.flet.dev/types/clipbehavior/#flet.ClipBehavior)  = [HARD_EDGE](https://docs.flet.dev/types/clipbehavior/#flet.ClipBehavior.HARD_EDGE)`

Defines how the [`controls`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/tabbarview/#flet.TabBarView.controls)
 will be clipped.

### controls `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/tabbarview/#flet.TabBarView.controls "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/tabbarview/#__codelineno-0-1) controls: [list](https://docs.python.org/3/glossary.html#term-list) [[Control](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control) ]`

A list of controls, where each control represents the content of a corresponding tab. So, a control at index `i` in this list is displayed when the [`Tab`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/tab/#flet.Tab)
 at index `i` is selected.

Note

The length of this list must be equal to the number of tabs specified in an ancestor [`Tabs`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/tabs/#flet.Tabs)
 control.

### viewport\_fraction `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/tabbarview/#flet.TabBarView.viewport_fraction "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/tabbarview/#__codelineno-0-1) viewport_fraction: [Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  = 1.0`

The fraction of the viewport that each page should occupy.

For example, `1.0` (the default), means each page fills the viewport in the scrolling direction.

Back to top

---

# SelectionArea - Flet

 

[Skip to content](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/selectionarea/#flet.SelectionArea)

SelectionArea
=============

Flet controls are not selectable by default. SelectionArea is used to enable selection for its child control.

[![SelectionArea](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/material/golden/macos/selection_area/image_for_docs.png)](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/material/golden/macos/selection_area/image_for_docs.png)

Selectable and unselectable text

Inherits: `[Control](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control) `

Properties

*   **`[content](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/selectionarea/#flet.SelectionArea.content) `**(`[Control](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control) `) –
    
    The child control this selection area applies to.
    

Events

*   **`[on_change](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/selectionarea/#flet.SelectionArea.on_change) `**(`[ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[SelectionArea](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/selectionarea/#flet.SelectionArea) ] | None`) –
    
    Called when the selected content changes.
    

Examples[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/selectionarea/#examples "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Basic Example[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/selectionarea/#basic-example "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/selectionarea/#__codelineno-0-1) import flet as ft [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/selectionarea/#__codelineno-0-2)[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/selectionarea/#__codelineno-0-3) TEXT_STYLE = ft.TextStyle(     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/selectionarea/#__codelineno-0-4)     size=22,    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/selectionarea/#__codelineno-0-5)     weight=ft.FontWeight.W_600,    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/selectionarea/#__codelineno-0-6)     decoration=ft.TextDecoration(        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/selectionarea/#__codelineno-0-7)         ft.TextDecoration.UNDERLINE | ft.TextDecoration.OVERLINE    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/selectionarea/#__codelineno-0-8)     ),    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/selectionarea/#__codelineno-0-9)     decoration_style=ft.TextDecorationStyle.WAVY, [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/selectionarea/#__codelineno-0-10) ) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/selectionarea/#__codelineno-0-11) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/selectionarea/#__codelineno-0-12)[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/selectionarea/#__codelineno-0-13) def main(page: ft.Page):     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/selectionarea/#__codelineno-0-14)     page.add(        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/selectionarea/#__codelineno-0-15)         ft.SelectionArea(            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/selectionarea/#__codelineno-0-16)             content=ft.Column(                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/selectionarea/#__codelineno-0-17)                 controls=[                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/selectionarea/#__codelineno-0-18)                     ft.Text(                        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/selectionarea/#__codelineno-0-19)                         "Selectable text",                        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/selectionarea/#__codelineno-0-20)                         color=ft.Colors.GREEN,                        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/selectionarea/#__codelineno-0-21)                         style=TEXT_STYLE,                        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/selectionarea/#__codelineno-0-22)                         key="selectable",                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/selectionarea/#__codelineno-0-23)                     ),                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/selectionarea/#__codelineno-0-24)                     ft.Text("Also selectable", color=ft.Colors.GREEN, style=TEXT_STYLE),                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/selectionarea/#__codelineno-0-25)                 ]            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/selectionarea/#__codelineno-0-26)             )        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/selectionarea/#__codelineno-0-27)         )    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/selectionarea/#__codelineno-0-28)     ) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/selectionarea/#__codelineno-0-29)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/selectionarea/#__codelineno-0-30)     page.add(        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/selectionarea/#__codelineno-0-31)         ft.Text(            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/selectionarea/#__codelineno-0-32)             "Not selectable",            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/selectionarea/#__codelineno-0-33)             color=ft.Colors.RED,            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/selectionarea/#__codelineno-0-34)             style=TEXT_STYLE,            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/selectionarea/#__codelineno-0-35)             key="non-selectable",        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/selectionarea/#__codelineno-0-36)         )    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/selectionarea/#__codelineno-0-37)     ) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/selectionarea/#__codelineno-0-38) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/selectionarea/#__codelineno-0-39)[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/selectionarea/#__codelineno-0-40) if __name__ == "__main__":     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/selectionarea/#__codelineno-0-41)     ft.run(main)`

[![basic](https://docs.flet.dev/examples/controls/selection_area/media/basic.gif)](https://docs.flet.dev/examples/controls/selection_area/media/basic.gif)

Properties[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/selectionarea/#flet.SelectionArea-properties "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### content `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/selectionarea/#flet.SelectionArea.content "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/selectionarea/#__codelineno-0-1) content: [Control](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control)`

The child control this selection area applies to.

If you need to have multiple selectable controls, use container-like controls like [`Row`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/row/#flet.Row)
 or [`Column`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#flet.Column)
, which have a `controls` property for this purpose.

Raises:

*   `[ValueError](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ValueError) ` –
    
    If [`content`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/selectionarea/#flet.SelectionArea.content)
     is not visible.
    

Events[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/selectionarea/#flet.SelectionArea-events "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### on\_change `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/selectionarea/#flet.SelectionArea.on_change "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/selectionarea/#__codelineno-0-1) on_change: [ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[SelectionArea](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/selectionarea/#flet.SelectionArea) ] | None = None`

Called when the selected content changes.

Back to top

---

# Pagelet - Flet

 

[Skip to content](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pagelet/#flet.Pagelet)

Pagelet
=======

Implements the basic Material Design visual layout structure.

Use it for projects that require a "page within a page" layouts with its own [`AppBar`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#flet.AppBar)
, [`BottomAppBar`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/bottomappbar/#flet.BottomAppBar)
, [`NavigationDrawer`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/navigationdrawer/#flet.NavigationDrawer)
, such as demos and galleries.

[![Pagelet](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/core/golden/macos/pagelet/image_for_docs.png)](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/core/golden/macos/pagelet/image_for_docs.png)

Basic Pagelet

Inherits: `[LayoutControl](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/layoutcontrol/#flet.LayoutControl) `, `[AdaptiveControl](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/adaptivecontrol/#flet.AdaptiveControl) `

Properties

*   **`[appbar](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pagelet/#flet.Pagelet.appbar) `**(`[AppBar](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#flet.AppBar)  | [CupertinoAppBar](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinoappbar/#flet.CupertinoAppBar)  | None`) –
    
    An [`AppBar`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#flet.AppBar)
     control to display at the top of the Pagelet.
    
*   **`[bgcolor](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pagelet/#flet.Pagelet.bgcolor) `**(`[ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None`) –
    
    Background color of this Pagelet.
    
*   **`[bottom_appbar](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pagelet/#flet.Pagelet.bottom_appbar) `**(`[BottomAppBar](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/bottomappbar/#flet.BottomAppBar)  | None`) –
    
    A [`BottomAppBar`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/bottomappbar/#flet.BottomAppBar)
     control to display at the bottom of this Pagelet.
    
*   **`[bottom_sheet](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pagelet/#flet.Pagelet.bottom_sheet) `**(`[Control](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control)  | None`) –
    
    The persistent bottom sheet to show information that supplements the primary content of this Pagelet.
    
*   **`[content](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pagelet/#flet.Pagelet.content) `**(`[Control](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control) `) –
    
    A child Control contained by this Pagelet.
    
*   **`[drawer](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pagelet/#flet.Pagelet.drawer) `**(`[NavigationDrawer](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/navigationdrawer/#flet.NavigationDrawer)  | None`) –
    
    A [`NavigationDrawer`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/navigationdrawer/#flet.NavigationDrawer)
     control to display as a panel sliding from the start edge of the page.
    
*   **`[end_drawer](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pagelet/#flet.Pagelet.end_drawer) `**(`[NavigationDrawer](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/navigationdrawer/#flet.NavigationDrawer)  | None`) –
    
    A [`NavigationDrawer`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/navigationdrawer/#flet.NavigationDrawer)
     control to display as a panel sliding from the end edge of the page.
    
*   **`[floating_action_button](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pagelet/#flet.Pagelet.floating_action_button) `**(`[Control](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control)  | None`) –
    
    A [`FloatingActionButton`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#flet.FloatingActionButton)
    
*   **`[floating_action_button_location](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pagelet/#flet.Pagelet.floating_action_button_location) `**(`[FloatingActionButtonLocation](https://docs.flet.dev/types/floatingactionbuttonlocation/#flet.FloatingActionButtonLocation)  | [OffsetValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.OffsetValue)  | None`) –
    
    Defines the position of the [`floating_action_button`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pagelet/#flet.Pagelet.floating_action_button)
    .
    
*   **`[navigation_bar](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pagelet/#flet.Pagelet.navigation_bar) `**(`[NavigationBar](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/navigationbar/#flet.NavigationBar)  | [CupertinoNavigationBar](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinonavigationbar/#flet.CupertinoNavigationBar)  | None`) –
    
    A navigation bar ([`NavigationBar`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/navigationbar/#flet.NavigationBar)
     or [`CupertinoNavigationBar`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinonavigationbar/#flet.CupertinoNavigationBar)
    ) control to display at the bottom of the `Pagelet`.
    

Methods

*   **`[close_drawer](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pagelet/#flet.Pagelet.close_drawer) `** –
    
    Close the drawer.
    
*   **`[close_end_drawer](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pagelet/#flet.Pagelet.close_end_drawer) `** –
    
    Close the end drawer.
    
*   **`[show_drawer](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pagelet/#flet.Pagelet.show_drawer) `** –
    
    Show the drawer.
    
*   **`[show_end_drawer](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pagelet/#flet.Pagelet.show_end_drawer) `** –
    
    Show the end drawer.
    

Examples[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pagelet/#examples "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[Live example](https://flet-controls-gallery.fly.dev/layout/pagelet)

[![basic.png](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/core/golden/macos/pagelet/basic.png)](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/core/golden/macos/pagelet/basic.png)

Properties[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pagelet/#flet.Pagelet-properties "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### appbar `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pagelet/#flet.Pagelet.appbar "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pagelet/#__codelineno-0-1) appbar: [AppBar](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#flet.AppBar)  | [CupertinoAppBar](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinoappbar/#flet.CupertinoAppBar)  | None = None`

An [`AppBar`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#flet.AppBar)
 control to display at the top of the Pagelet.

### bgcolor `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pagelet/#flet.Pagelet.bgcolor "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pagelet/#__codelineno-0-1) bgcolor: [ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None = None`

Background color of this Pagelet.

### bottom\_appbar `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pagelet/#flet.Pagelet.bottom_appbar "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pagelet/#__codelineno-0-1) bottom_appbar: [BottomAppBar](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/bottomappbar/#flet.BottomAppBar)  | None = None`

A [`BottomAppBar`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/bottomappbar/#flet.BottomAppBar)
 control to display at the bottom of this Pagelet.

Note

If both the `bottom_appbar` and [`navigation_bar`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pagelet/#flet.Pagelet.navigation_bar)
 properties are specified, `bottom_appbar` takes precedence and will be displayed.

### bottom\_sheet `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pagelet/#flet.Pagelet.bottom_sheet "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pagelet/#__codelineno-0-1) bottom_sheet: [Control](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control)  | None = None`

The persistent bottom sheet to show information that supplements the primary content of this Pagelet.

### content `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pagelet/#flet.Pagelet.content "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pagelet/#__codelineno-0-1) content: [Control](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control)`

A child Control contained by this Pagelet.

The control in the content of the Pagelet is positioned at the top-left of the available space between the app bar and the bottom of the Pagelet.

Raises:

*   `[ValueError](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ValueError) ` –
    
    If it is not visible.
    

### drawer `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pagelet/#flet.Pagelet.drawer "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pagelet/#__codelineno-0-1) drawer: [NavigationDrawer](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/navigationdrawer/#flet.NavigationDrawer)  | None = None`

A [`NavigationDrawer`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/navigationdrawer/#flet.NavigationDrawer)
 control to display as a panel sliding from the start edge of the page.

### end\_drawer `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pagelet/#flet.Pagelet.end_drawer "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pagelet/#__codelineno-0-1) end_drawer: [NavigationDrawer](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/navigationdrawer/#flet.NavigationDrawer)  | None = None`

A [`NavigationDrawer`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/navigationdrawer/#flet.NavigationDrawer)
 control to display as a panel sliding from the end edge of the page.

### floating\_action\_button `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pagelet/#flet.Pagelet.floating_action_button "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pagelet/#__codelineno-0-1) floating_action_button: [Control](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control)  | None = None`

A [`FloatingActionButton`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#flet.FloatingActionButton)
 control to display on top of this Pagelet's content.

### floating\_action\_button\_location `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pagelet/#flet.Pagelet.floating_action_button_location "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pagelet/#__codelineno-0-1) floating_action_button_location: (     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pagelet/#__codelineno-0-2)     [FloatingActionButtonLocation](https://docs.flet.dev/types/floatingactionbuttonlocation/#flet.FloatingActionButtonLocation)  | [OffsetValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.OffsetValue)  | None [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pagelet/#__codelineno-0-3) ) = [END_FLOAT](https://docs.flet.dev/types/floatingactionbuttonlocation/#flet.FloatingActionButtonLocation.END_FLOAT)`

Defines the position of the [`floating_action_button`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pagelet/#flet.Pagelet.floating_action_button)
.

### navigation\_bar `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pagelet/#flet.Pagelet.navigation_bar "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pagelet/#__codelineno-0-1) navigation_bar: (     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pagelet/#__codelineno-0-2)     [NavigationBar](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/navigationbar/#flet.NavigationBar)  | [CupertinoNavigationBar](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinonavigationbar/#flet.CupertinoNavigationBar)  | None [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pagelet/#__codelineno-0-3) ) = None`

A navigation bar ([`NavigationBar`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/navigationbar/#flet.NavigationBar)
 or [`CupertinoNavigationBar`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinonavigationbar/#flet.CupertinoNavigationBar)
) control to display at the bottom of the `Pagelet`.

Note

If both the `navigation_bar` and [`bottom_appbar`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pagelet/#flet.Pagelet.bottom_appbar)
 properties are specified, `navigation_bar` takes precedence and will be displayed.

Methods[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pagelet/#flet.Pagelet-methods "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### close\_drawer `async` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pagelet/#flet.Pagelet.close_drawer "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pagelet/#__codelineno-0-1) close_drawer()`

Close the drawer.

### close\_end\_drawer `async` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pagelet/#flet.Pagelet.close_end_drawer "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pagelet/#__codelineno-0-1) close_end_drawer()`

Close the end drawer.

### show\_drawer `async` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pagelet/#flet.Pagelet.show_drawer "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pagelet/#__codelineno-0-1) show_drawer()`

Show the drawer.

Raises:

*   `[ValueError](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ValueError) ` –
    
    If no [`drawer`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pagelet/#flet.Pagelet.drawer)
     is defined.
    

### show\_end\_drawer `async` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pagelet/#flet.Pagelet.show_end_drawer "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pagelet/#__codelineno-0-1) show_end_drawer()`

Show the end drawer.

Raises:

*   `[ValueError](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ValueError) ` –
    
    If no [`end_drawer`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pagelet/#flet.Pagelet.end_drawer)
     is defined.

# FloatingActionButton - Flet

 

[Skip to content](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#flet.FloatingActionButton)

FloatingActionButton
====================

A floating action button is a circular icon button that hovers over content to promote a primary action in the application. Floating action button is usually set to `page.floating_action_button`, but can also be added as a regular control at any place on a page.

Example:

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#__codelineno-0-1) ft.FloatingActionButton(icon=ft.Icons.ADD)`

[![FloatingActionButton](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/material/golden/macos/floating_action_button/image_for_docs.png)](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/material/golden/macos/floating_action_button/image_for_docs.png)

Basic FloatingActionButton

Inherits: `[LayoutControl](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/layoutcontrol/#flet.LayoutControl) `

Properties

*   **`[autofocus](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#flet.FloatingActionButton.autofocus) `**(`[bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool) `) –
    
    True if the control will be selected as the initial focus. If there is more than one control on a page with autofocus set, then the first one added to the page will get focus.
    
*   **`[bgcolor](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#flet.FloatingActionButton.bgcolor) `**(`[ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None`) –
    
    Button background color.
    
*   **`[clip_behavior](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#flet.FloatingActionButton.clip_behavior) `**(`[ClipBehavior](https://docs.flet.dev/types/clipbehavior/#flet.ClipBehavior) `) –
    
    Defines how the [`content`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#flet.FloatingActionButton.content)
     is clipped.
    
*   **`[content](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#flet.FloatingActionButton.content) `**(`[StrOrControl](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.StrOrControl)  | None`) –
    
    The content of this button.
    
*   **`[disabled_elevation](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#flet.FloatingActionButton.disabled_elevation) `**(`[Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None`) –
    
    The elevation of this button when disabled.
    
*   **`[elevation](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#flet.FloatingActionButton.elevation) `**(`[Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None`) –
    
    The elevation of this button.
    
*   **`[enable_feedback](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#flet.FloatingActionButton.enable_feedback) `**(`[bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)  | None`) –
    
    Whether detected gestures should provide acoustic and/or haptic feedback. On Android, for example, setting this to `True` will produce a click sound and a long-press will produce a short vibration.
    
*   **`[focus_color](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#flet.FloatingActionButton.focus_color) `**(`[ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None`) –
    
    The color to use for filling this button when it has input focus.
    
*   **`[focus_elevation](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#flet.FloatingActionButton.focus_elevation) `**(`[Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None`) –
    
    The elevation of this button when it has input focus.
    
*   **`[foreground_color](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#flet.FloatingActionButton.foreground_color) `**(`[ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None`) –
    
    The default foreground color for icons and text within this button.
    
*   **`[highlight_elevation](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#flet.FloatingActionButton.highlight_elevation) `**(`[Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None`) –
    
    The elevation of this button when it is highlighted.
    
*   **`[hover_color](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#flet.FloatingActionButton.hover_color) `**(`[ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None`) –
    
    The color to use for filling this button when hovered.
    
*   **`[hover_elevation](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#flet.FloatingActionButton.hover_elevation) `**(`[Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None`) –
    
    The elevation of this button it is enabled and being hovered over.
    
*   **`[icon](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#flet.FloatingActionButton.icon) `**(`[IconDataOrControl](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.IconDataOrControl)  | None`) –
    
    Icon shown in this button.
    
*   **`[mini](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#flet.FloatingActionButton.mini) `**(`[bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool) `) –
    
    Controls the size of this button.
    
*   **`[mouse_cursor](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#flet.FloatingActionButton.mouse_cursor) `**(`[MouseCursor](https://docs.flet.dev/types/mousecursor/#flet.MouseCursor)  | None`) –
    
    The cursor to be displayed when a mouse pointer enters or is hovering over this control.
    
*   **`[shape](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#flet.FloatingActionButton.shape) `**(`[OutlinedBorder](https://docs.flet.dev/types/outlinedborder/#flet.OutlinedBorder)  | None`) –
    
    The shape of the FAB's border.
    
*   **`[splash_color](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#flet.FloatingActionButton.splash_color) `**(`[ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None`) –
    
    The color to use for the ink splash.
    
*   **`[url](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#flet.FloatingActionButton.url) `**(`[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)  | [Url](https://docs.flet.dev/types/url/#flet.Url)  | None`) –
    
    The URL to open when this button is clicked.
    

Events

*   **`[on_click](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#flet.FloatingActionButton.on_click) `**(`[ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[FloatingActionButton](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#flet.FloatingActionButton) ] | None`) –
    
    Called when a user clicks this button.
    

Examples[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#examples "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[Live example](https://flet-controls-gallery.fly.dev/buttons/floatingactionbutton)

### Handling clicks[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#handling-clicks "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#__codelineno-0-1) import flet as ft [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#__codelineno-0-2) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#__codelineno-0-3)[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#__codelineno-0-4) def main(page: ft.Page):     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#__codelineno-0-5)     page.title = "Floating Action Button"    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#__codelineno-0-6)     page.theme_mode = ft.ThemeMode.LIGHT    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#__codelineno-0-7)     page.horizontal_alignment = ft.CrossAxisAlignment.CENTER    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#__codelineno-0-8)     page.padding = 0    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#__codelineno-0-9)     page.scroll = ft.ScrollMode.HIDDEN [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#__codelineno-0-10)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#__codelineno-0-11)     # keeps track of the number of tiles already added    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#__codelineno-0-12)     count = 1 [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#__codelineno-0-13)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#__codelineno-0-14)     def handle_fab_click(e: ft.Event[ft.FloatingActionButton]):        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#__codelineno-0-15)         nonlocal count  # to modify the count variable found in the outer scope        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#__codelineno-0-16)         page.add(            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#__codelineno-0-17)             ft.ListTile(                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#__codelineno-0-18)                 title=ft.Text(f"Tile {count}"),                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#__codelineno-0-19)                 bgcolor=ft.Colors.TEAL_300,                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#__codelineno-0-20)                 leading=ft.Icon(                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#__codelineno-0-21)                     ft.Icons.CIRCLE_OUTLINED, color=ft.Colors.DEEP_ORANGE_300                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#__codelineno-0-22)                 ),                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#__codelineno-0-23)                 on_click=lambda x: print(x.control.title.value + " was clicked!"),            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#__codelineno-0-24)             )        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#__codelineno-0-25)         )        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#__codelineno-0-26)         page.show_dialog(ft.SnackBar(ft.Text("Tile was added successfully!")))        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#__codelineno-0-27)         count += 1 [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#__codelineno-0-28)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#__codelineno-0-29)     page.floating_action_button = ft.FloatingActionButton(        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#__codelineno-0-30)         icon=ft.Icons.ADD,        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#__codelineno-0-31)         on_click=handle_fab_click,        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#__codelineno-0-32)         bgcolor=ft.Colors.LIME_300,    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#__codelineno-0-33)     ) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#__codelineno-0-34)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#__codelineno-0-35)     page.add(        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#__codelineno-0-36)         ft.Container(            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#__codelineno-0-37)             bgcolor=ft.Colors.BLUE,            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#__codelineno-0-38)             padding=ft.Padding.all(20),            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#__codelineno-0-39)             content=ft.Row(                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#__codelineno-0-40)                 alignment=ft.MainAxisAlignment.CENTER,                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#__codelineno-0-41)                 controls=[                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#__codelineno-0-42)                     ft.Text(                        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#__codelineno-0-43)                         value="Floating Action Button Example",                        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#__codelineno-0-44)                         style=ft.TextStyle(size=20, weight=ft.FontWeight.W_500),      [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#__codelineno-0-49)         ft.Text("Press the FAB to add a tile!"), [![handling-clicks](https://docs.flet.dev/examples/controls/floating_action_button/media/handling_clicks.gif)](https://docs.flet.dev/examples/controls/floating_action_button/media/handling_clicks.gif)

Properties[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#flet.FloatingActionButton-properties "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### autofocus `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#flet.FloatingActionButton.autofocus "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#__codelineno-0-1) autofocus: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)  = False`

True if the control will be selected as the initial focus. If there is more than one control on a page with autofocus set, then the first one added to the page will get focus.

### bgcolor `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#flet.FloatingActionButton.bgcolor "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#__codelineno-0-1) bgcolor: [ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None = None`

Button background color.

### clip\_behavior `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#flet.FloatingActionButton.clip_behavior "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#__codelineno-0-1) clip_behavior: [ClipBehavior](https://docs.flet.dev/types/clipbehavior/#flet.ClipBehavior)  = [NONE](https://docs.flet.dev/types/clipbehavior/#flet.ClipBehavior.NONE)`

Defines how the [`content`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#flet.FloatingActionButton.content)
 is clipped.

### content `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#flet.FloatingActionButton.content "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#__codelineno-0-1) content: [StrOrControl](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.StrOrControl)  | None = None`

The content of this button.

Raises:

*   `[ValueError](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ValueError) ` –
    
    If neither [`icon`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#flet.FloatingActionButton.icon)
     nor a valid `content` (string or visible Control) is provided.
    

### disabled\_elevation `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#flet.FloatingActionButton.disabled_elevation "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#__codelineno-0-1) disabled_elevation: [Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None = None`

The elevation of this button when disabled.

Defaults to [`elevation`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#flet.FloatingActionButton.elevation)
.

Raises:

*   `[ValueError](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ValueError) ` –
    
    If it is not greater than or equal to `0`.
    

### elevation `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#flet.FloatingActionButton.elevation "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#__codelineno-0-1) elevation: [Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None = None`

The elevation of this button.

Defaults to `6`.

Raises:

*   `[ValueError](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ValueError) ` –
    
    If it is not greater than or equal to `0`.
    

### enable\_feedback `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#flet.FloatingActionButton.enable_feedback "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#__codelineno-0-1) enable_feedback: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)  | None = None`

Whether detected gestures should provide acoustic and/or haptic feedback. On Android, for example, setting this to `True` will produce a click sound and a long-press will produce a short vibration.

### focus\_color `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#flet.FloatingActionButton.focus_color "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#__codelineno-0-1) focus_color: [ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None = None`

The color to use for filling this button when it has input focus.

### focus\_elevation `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#flet.FloatingActionButton.focus_elevation "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#__codelineno-0-1) focus_elevation: [Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None = None`

The elevation of this button when it has input focus.

Defaults to `8`.

Raises:

*   `[ValueError](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ValueError) ` –
    
    If it is not greater than or equal to `0`.
    

### foreground\_color `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#flet.FloatingActionButton.foreground_color "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#__codelineno-0-1) foreground_color: [ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None = None`

The default foreground color for icons and text within this button.

### highlight\_elevation `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#flet.FloatingActionButton.highlight_elevation "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#__codelineno-0-1) highlight_elevation: [Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None = None`

The elevation of this button when it is highlighted.

Defaults to `12`.

Raises:

*   `[ValueError](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ValueError) ` –
    
    If it is not greater than or equal to `0`.
    

### hover\_color `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#flet.FloatingActionButton.hover_color "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#__codelineno-0-1) hover_color: [ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None = None`

The color to use for filling this button when hovered.

### hover\_elevation `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#flet.FloatingActionButton.hover_elevation "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#__codelineno-0-1) hover_elevation: [Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None = None`

The elevation of this button it is enabled and being hovered over.

Defaults to `8`.

Raises:

*   `[ValueError](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ValueError) ` –
    
    If it is not greater than or equal to `0`.
    

### icon `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#flet.FloatingActionButton.icon "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#__codelineno-0-1) icon: [IconDataOrControl](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.IconDataOrControl)  | None = None`

Icon shown in this button.

### mini `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#flet.FloatingActionButton.mini "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#__codelineno-0-1) mini: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)  = False`

Controls the size of this button.

By default, floating action buttons are non-mini and have a height and width of `56.0` logical pixels. Mini floating action buttons have a height and width of `40.0` logical pixels with a layout width and height of `48.0` logical pixels.

### mouse\_cursor `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#flet.FloatingActionButton.mouse_cursor "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#__codelineno-0-1) mouse_cursor: [MouseCursor](https://docs.flet.dev/types/mousecursor/#flet.MouseCursor)  | None = None`

The cursor to be displayed when a mouse pointer enters or is hovering over this control.

### shape `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#flet.FloatingActionButton.shape "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#__codelineno-0-1) shape: [OutlinedBorder](https://docs.flet.dev/types/outlinedborder/#flet.OutlinedBorder)  | None = None`

The shape of the FAB's border.

### splash\_color `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#flet.FloatingActionButton.splash_color "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#__codelineno-0-1) splash_color: [ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None = None`

The color to use for the ink splash.

### url `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#flet.FloatingActionButton.url "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#__codelineno-0-1) url: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)  | [Url](https://docs.flet.dev/types/url/#flet.Url)  | None = None`

The URL to open when this button is clicked.

Additionally, if [`on_click`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#flet.FloatingActionButton.on_click)
 event callback is provided, it is fired after that.

Events[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#flet.FloatingActionButton-events "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### on\_click `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#flet.FloatingActionButton.on_click "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#__codelineno-0-1) on_click: (     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#__codelineno-0-2)     [ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[FloatingActionButton](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#flet.FloatingActionButton) ] | None [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#__codelineno-0-3) ) = None`

Called when a user clicks this button.

# ListView - Flet

 

[Skip to content](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listview/#flet.ListView)

ListView
========

A scrollable list of controls arranged linearly.

ListView is the most commonly used scrolling control. It displays its children one after another in the scroll direction. In the cross axis, the children are required to fill the ListView.

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listview/#__codelineno-0-1) ft.ListView(     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listview/#__codelineno-0-2)     controls=[ft.Text(f"Item {i}") for i in range(1, 6)], [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listview/#__codelineno-0-3) )`

[![ListView](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/core/golden/macos/list_view/image_for_docs.png)](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/core/golden/macos/list_view/image_for_docs.png)

Basic list view

Inherits: `[LayoutControl](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/layoutcontrol/#flet.LayoutControl) `, `[ScrollableControl](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/scrollablecontrol/#flet.ScrollableControl) `, `[AdaptiveControl](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/adaptivecontrol/#flet.AdaptiveControl) `

Properties

*   **`[build_controls_on_demand](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listview/#flet.ListView.build_controls_on_demand) `**(`[bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool) `) –
    
    Whether the [`controls`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listview/#flet.ListView.controls)
     should be built lazily/on-demand, i.e. only when they are about to become visible.
    
*   **`[cache_extent](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listview/#flet.ListView.cache_extent) `**(`[Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None`) –
    
    The viewport has an area before and after the visible area to cache items that are about to become visible when the user scrolls.
    
*   **`[clip_behavior](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listview/#flet.ListView.clip_behavior) `**(`[ClipBehavior](https://docs.flet.dev/types/clipbehavior/#flet.ClipBehavior) `) –
    
    Defines how to clip the [`controls`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listview/#flet.ListView.controls)
    .
    
*   **`[controls](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listview/#flet.ListView.controls) `**(`[list](https://docs.python.org/3/glossary.html#term-list) [[Control](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control) ]`) –
    
    A list of `Control`s to display inside ListView.
    
*   **`[divider_thickness](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listview/#flet.ListView.divider_thickness) `**(`[Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number) `) –
    
    If greater than `0` then `Divider` is used as a spacing between list view items.
    
*   **`[first_item_prototype](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listview/#flet.ListView.first_item_prototype) `**(`[bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool) `) –
    
    Whether the dimensions of the first item should be used as a "prototype" for all other items.
    
*   **`[horizontal](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listview/#flet.ListView.horizontal) `**(`[bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool) `) –
    
    Whether the [`controls`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listview/#flet.ListView.controls)
     should be laid out horizontally.
    
*   **`[item_extent](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listview/#flet.ListView.item_extent) `**(`[Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None`) –
    
    A fixed height or width (when [`horizontal`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listview/#flet.ListView.horizontal)
     is `True`) of an item to optimize rendering.
    
*   **`[padding](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listview/#flet.ListView.padding) `**(`[PaddingValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.PaddingValue)  | None`) –
    
    The amount of space by which to inset the [`controls`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listview/#flet.ListView.controls)
    .
    
*   **`[prototype_item](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listview/#flet.ListView.prototype_item) `**(`[Control](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control)  | None`) –
    
    A control to be used as a "prototype" for all items, i.e. their `height` or `width` will be the same as the `prototype_item`.
    
*   **`[reverse](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listview/#flet.ListView.reverse) `**(`[bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool) `) –
    
    Whether the scroll view scrolls in the reading direction.
    
*   **`[semantic_child_count](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listview/#flet.ListView.semantic_child_count) `**(`[int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int)  | None`) –
    
    The number of children that will contribute semantic information.
    
*   **`[spacing](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listview/#flet.ListView.spacing) `**(`[Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number) `) –
    
    The height of divider between the [`controls`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listview/#flet.ListView.controls)
    .
    

Examples[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listview/#examples "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[Live example](https://flet-controls-gallery.fly.dev/layout/listview)

[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listview/#__codelineno-0-27)                     content=lv,                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listview/#__codelineno-0-28)                     bgcolor=ft.Colors.GREY_500,                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listview/#__codelineno-0-29)                 ), thumb_icon=ft.Icons.LIST_OUTLINED,                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listview/#__codelineno-0-32)                     value=True,                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listview/#__codelineno-0-33)                     label="Auto-scroll",                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listview/#__codelineno-0-34)                     label_position=ft.LabelPosition.RIGHT,                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listview/#__codelineno-0-35)                     on_change=handle_switch_change, asyncio.sleep(1)        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listview/#__codelineno-0-44)         lv.controls.append(ft.Text(f"Line {i}", color=ft.Colors.ON_SECONDARY)) [![autoscroll-and-dynamic-items](https://docs.flet.dev/examples/controls/list_view/media/autoscroll_and_dynamic_items.gif)](https://docs.flet.dev/examples/controls/list_view/media/autoscroll_and_dynamic_items.gif)

Properties[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listview/#flet.ListView-properties "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### build\_controls\_on\_demand `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listview/#flet.ListView.build_controls_on_demand "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listview/#__codelineno-0-1) build_controls_on_demand: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)  = True`

Whether the [`controls`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listview/#flet.ListView.controls)
 should be built lazily/on-demand, i.e. only when they are about to become visible.

This is particularly useful when dealing with a large number of controls.

### cache\_extent `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listview/#flet.ListView.cache_extent "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listview/#__codelineno-0-1) cache_extent: [Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None = None`

The viewport has an area before and after the visible area to cache items that are about to become visible when the user scrolls.

Items that fall in this cache area are laid out even though they are not (yet) visible on screen. The `cache_extent` describes how many pixels the cache area extends before the leading edge and after the trailing edge of the viewport.

The total extent, which the viewport will try to cover with children, is `cache_extent` before the leading edge + extent of the main axis + `cache_extent` after the trailing edge.

The cache area is also used to implement implicit accessibility scrolling on iOS: When the accessibility focus moves from an item in the visible viewport to an invisible item in the cache area, the framework will bring that item into view with an (implicit) scroll action.

### clip\_behavior `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listview/#flet.ListView.clip_behavior "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listview/#__codelineno-0-1) clip_behavior: [ClipBehavior](https://docs.flet.dev/types/clipbehavior/#flet.ClipBehavior)  = [HARD_EDGE](https://docs.flet.dev/types/clipbehavior/#flet.ClipBehavior.HARD_EDGE)`

Defines how to clip the [`controls`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listview/#flet.ListView.controls)
.

### controls `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listview/#flet.ListView.controls "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listview/#__codelineno-0-1) controls: [list](https://docs.python.org/3/glossary.html#term-list) [[Control](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control) ] = [field](https://docs.python.org/3/library/dataclasses.html#dataclasses.field "<code>dataclasses.field</code>") (default_factory=[list](https://docs.python.org/3/glossary.html#term-list) )`

A list of `Control`s to display inside ListView.

### divider\_thickness `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listview/#flet.ListView.divider_thickness "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listview/#__codelineno-0-1) divider_thickness: [Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  = 0`

If greater than `0` then `Divider` is used as a spacing between list view items.

### first\_item\_prototype `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listview/#flet.ListView.first_item_prototype "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listview/#__codelineno-0-1) first_item_prototype: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)  = False`

Whether the dimensions of the first item should be used as a "prototype" for all other items.

If `True`, their `height` or `width` will be the same as the first item.

### horizontal `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listview/#flet.ListView.horizontal "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listview/#__codelineno-0-1) horizontal: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)  = False`

Whether the [`controls`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listview/#flet.ListView.controls)
 should be laid out horizontally.

### item\_extent `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listview/#flet.ListView.item_extent "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listview/#__codelineno-0-1) item_extent: [Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None = None`

A fixed height or width (when [`horizontal`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listview/#flet.ListView.horizontal)
 is `True`) of an item to optimize rendering.

Note

This property has effect only when [`build_controls_on_demand`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listview/#flet.ListView.build_controls_on_demand)
 is `True` or [`spacing`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listview/#flet.ListView.spacing)
 is `0`.

### padding `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listview/#flet.ListView.padding "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listview/#__codelineno-0-1) padding: [PaddingValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.PaddingValue)  | None = None`

The amount of space by which to inset the [`controls`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listview/#flet.ListView.controls)
.

### prototype\_item `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listview/#flet.ListView.prototype_item "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listview/#__codelineno-0-1) prototype_item: [Control](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control)  | None = None`

A control to be used as a "prototype" for all items, i.e. their `height` or `width` will be the same as the `prototype_item`.

Note

This property has effect only when [`build_controls_on_demand`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listview/#flet.ListView.build_controls_on_demand)
 is `True` or [`spacing`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listview/#flet.ListView.spacing)
 is `0`.

### reverse `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listview/#flet.ListView.reverse "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listview/#__codelineno-0-1) reverse: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)  = False`

Whether the scroll view scrolls in the reading direction.

For example, if the reading direction is left-to-right and [`horizontal`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listview/#flet.ListView.horizontal)
 is `True`, then the scroll view scrolls from left to right when `reverse` is `False` and from right to left when `reverse` is `True`.

Similarly, if `horizontal` is `False`, then the scroll view scrolls from top to bottom when `reverse` is `False` and from bottom to top when `reverse` is `True`.

### semantic\_child\_count `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listview/#flet.ListView.semantic_child_count "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listview/#__codelineno-0-1) semantic_child_count: [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int)  | None = None`

The number of children that will contribute semantic information.

### spacing `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listview/#flet.ListView.spacing "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listview/#__codelineno-0-1) spacing: [Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  = 0`

The height of divider between the [`controls`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listview/#flet.ListView.controls)
.

# WindowDragArea - Flet

 

[Skip to content](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/windowdragarea/#flet.WindowDragArea)

WindowDragArea
==============

It mimics the behavior (drag, move, maximize, restore) of a native OS window title bar on the [`content`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/windowdragarea/#flet.WindowDragArea.content)
 control.

[![WindowDragArea](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/core/golden/macos/window_drag_area/image_for_docs.png)](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/core/golden/macos/window_drag_area/image_for_docs.png)

Simple Window Drag Area

Inherits: `[LayoutControl](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/layoutcontrol/#flet.LayoutControl) `

Properties

*   **`[content](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/windowdragarea/#flet.WindowDragArea.content) `**(`[Control](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control) `) –
    
    The content of this drag area.
    
*   **`[maximizable](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/windowdragarea/#flet.WindowDragArea.maximizable) `**(`[bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool) `) –
    
    Whether double-clicking on the `WindowDragArea` should maximize/maximize the app's window.
    

Events

*   **`[on_double_tap](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/windowdragarea/#flet.WindowDragArea.on_double_tap) `**(`[EventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.EventHandler) [[WindowEvent](https://docs.flet.dev/types/windowevent/#flet.WindowEvent) [[WindowDragArea](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/windowdragarea/#flet.WindowDragArea) ]] | None`) –
    
    Called when the `WindowDragArea` is double-tapped and `maximizable=True`.
    
*   **`[on_drag_end](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/windowdragarea/#flet.WindowDragArea.on_drag_end) `**(`[EventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.EventHandler) [[DragEndEvent](https://docs.flet.dev/types/dragendevent/#flet.DragEndEvent) [[WindowDragArea](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/windowdragarea/#flet.WindowDragArea) ]] | None`) –
    
    Called when a pointer that was previously in contact with the screen and moving/dragging is no longer in contact with the screen.
    
*   **`[on_drag_start](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/windowdragarea/#flet.WindowDragArea.on_drag_start) `**(`[EventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.EventHandler) [[DragStartEvent](https://docs.flet.dev/types/dragstartevent/#flet.DragStartEvent) [[WindowDragArea](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/windowdragarea/#flet.WindowDragArea) ]] | None`) –
    
    Called when a pointer has contacted the screen and has begun to move/drag.
    

Examples[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/windowdragarea/#examples "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### No frame window[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/windowdragarea/#no-frame-window "Permanent link")

page.window.title_bar_buttons_hidden = True [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/windowdragarea/#__codelineno-0-7)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/windowdragarea/#__codelineno-0-8)     async def                     content=ft.Text(                            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/windowdragarea/#__codelineno-0-20)                             "Drag this area to move, maximize and " [![no-frame-window](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/core/golden/macos/window_drag_area/no_frame_window.png)](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/core/golden/macos/window_drag_area/no_frame_window.png)

Properties[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/windowdragarea/#flet.WindowDragArea-properties "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### content `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/windowdragarea/#flet.WindowDragArea.content "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/windowdragarea/#__codelineno-0-1) content: [Control](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control)`

The content of this drag area.

Raises:

*   `[ValueError](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ValueError) ` –
    
    If it is not visible.
    

### maximizable `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/windowdragarea/#flet.WindowDragArea.maximizable "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/windowdragarea/#__codelineno-0-1) maximizable: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)  = True`

Whether double-clicking on the `WindowDragArea` should maximize/maximize the app's window.

Events[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/windowdragarea/#flet.WindowDragArea-events "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### on\_double\_tap `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/windowdragarea/#flet.WindowDragArea.on_double_tap "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/windowdragarea/#__codelineno-0-1) on_double_tap: (     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/windowdragarea/#__codelineno-0-2)     [EventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.EventHandler) [[WindowEvent](https://docs.flet.dev/types/windowevent/#flet.WindowEvent) [[WindowDragArea](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/windowdragarea/#flet.WindowDragArea) ]] | None [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/windowdragarea/#__codelineno-0-3) ) = None`

Called when the `WindowDragArea` is double-tapped and `maximizable=True`.

Info

When a double-tap event is fired, the [`type`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/windowevent/#flet.WindowEvent.type)
 property of the event handler argument can only be one of the following: `WindowEventType.MAXIMIZE`, `WindowEventType.UNMAXIMIZE`.

### on\_drag\_end `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/windowdragarea/#flet.WindowDragArea.on_drag_end "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/windowdragarea/#__codelineno-0-1) on_drag_end: (     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/windowdragarea/#__codelineno-0-2)     [EventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.EventHandler) [[DragEndEvent](https://docs.flet.dev/types/dragendevent/#flet.DragEndEvent) [[WindowDragArea](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/windowdragarea/#flet.WindowDragArea) ]] | None [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/windowdragarea/#__codelineno-0-3) ) = None`

Called when a pointer that was previously in contact with the screen and moving/dragging is no longer in contact with the screen.

### on\_drag\_start `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/windowdragarea/#flet.WindowDragArea.on_drag_start "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/windowdragarea/#__codelineno-0-1) on_drag_start: (     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/windowdragarea/#__codelineno-0-2)     [EventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.EventHandler) [[DragStartEvent](https://docs.flet.dev/types/dragstartevent/#flet.DragStartEvent) [[WindowDragArea](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/windowdragarea/#flet.WindowDragArea) ]] | None [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/windowdragarea/#__codelineno-0-3) ) = None`

Called when a pointer has contacted the screen and has begun to move/drag.
