# Control - Flet

 

[Skip to content](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control)

Control
=======

Inherits: `[BaseControl](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basecontrol/#flet.BaseControl) `

Base class for controls.

Not meant to be used directly.

Properties

*   **`[badge](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control.badge) `**(`[BadgeValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.BadgeValue)  | None`) –
    
    A badge to show on top of this control.
    
*   **`[col](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control.col) `**(`[ResponsiveNumber](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ResponsiveNumber) `) –
    
    If a parent of this control is a [`ResponsiveRow`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/responsiverow/#flet.ResponsiveRow)
    , this property is used to determine how many virtual columns of a screen this control will span.
    
*   **`[disabled](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control.disabled) `**(`[bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool) `) –
    
    Every control has `disabled` property which is `False` by default - control and all its children are enabled.
    
*   **`[expand](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control.expand) `**(`[bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)  | [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int)  | None`) –
    
    Specifies whether/how this control should expand to fill available space in its parent layout.
    
*   **`[expand_loose](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control.expand_loose) `**(`[bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool) `) –
    
    Allows the control to expand along the main axis if space is available, but does not require it to fill all available space.
    
*   **`[opacity](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control.opacity) `**(`[Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number) `) –
    
    Defines the transparency of the control.
    
*   **`[rtl](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control.rtl) `**(`[bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool) `) –
    
    Whether the text direction of the control should be right-to-left (RTL).
    
*   **`[tooltip](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control.tooltip) `**(`[TooltipValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.TooltipValue)  | None`) –
    
    The tooltip to show when this control is hovered over.
    
*   **`[visible](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control.visible) `**(`[bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool) `) –
    
    Every control has `visible` property which is `True` by default - control is rendered on the page. Setting `visible` to `False` completely prevents control (and all its children if any) from rendering on a page canvas. Hidden controls cannot be focused or selected with a keyboard or mouse and they do not emit any events.
    

Properties[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control-properties "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### badge `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control.badge "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#__codelineno-0-1) badge: [BadgeValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.BadgeValue)  | None = None`

A badge to show on top of this control.

### col `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control.col "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#__codelineno-0-1) col: [ResponsiveNumber](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ResponsiveNumber)  = 12`

If a parent of this control is a [`ResponsiveRow`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/responsiverow/#flet.ResponsiveRow)
, this property is used to determine how many virtual columns of a screen this control will span.

Can be a number or a dictionary configured to have a different value for specific breakpoints, for example `col={"sm": 6}`.

This control spans the 12 virtual columns by default.

Dimensions

| Breakpoint | Dimension |
| --- | --- |
| xs  | <576px |
| sm  | ≥576px |
| md  | ≥768px |
| lg  | ≥992px |
| xl  | ≥1200px |
| xxl | ≥1400px |

### disabled `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control.disabled "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#__codelineno-0-1) disabled: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)  = False`

Every control has `disabled` property which is `False` by default - control and all its children are enabled.

Note

The value of this property will be propagated down to all children controls recursively.

Example

For example, if you have a form with multiple entry controls you can disable them all together by disabling container:

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#__codelineno-0-1) ft.Column(     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#__codelineno-0-2)     disabled = True,    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#__codelineno-0-3)     controls=[        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#__codelineno-0-4)         ft.TextField(),        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#__codelineno-0-5)         ft.TextField()    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#__codelineno-0-6)     ] [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#__codelineno-0-7) )`

### expand `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control.expand "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#__codelineno-0-1) expand: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)  | [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int)  | None = None`

Specifies whether/how this control should expand to fill available space in its parent layout.

More information [here](https://docs.flet.dev/cookbook/expanding-controls/#expand)
.

Note

Has effect only if the direct parent of this control is one of the following controls, or their subclasses: [`Column`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#flet.Column)
, [`Row`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/row/#flet.Row)
, [`View`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/view/#flet.View)
, [`Page`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/page/#flet.Page)
.

Raises:

*   `[ValueError](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ValueError) ` –
    
    If it is not of type `bool` or `int`.
    

### expand\_loose `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control.expand_loose "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#__codelineno-0-1) expand_loose: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)  = False`

Allows the control to expand along the main axis if space is available, but does not require it to fill all available space.

More information [here](https://docs.flet.dev/cookbook/expanding-controls/#expand_loose)
.

Note

If `expand_loose` is `True`, it will have effect only if:

*   `expand` is not `None` and
*   the direct parent of this control is one of the following controls, or their subclasses: [`Column`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#flet.Column)
    , [`Row`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/row/#flet.Row)
    , [`View`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/view/#flet.View)
    , [`Page`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/page/#flet.Page)
    .

### opacity `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control.opacity "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#__codelineno-0-1) opacity: [Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  = 1.0`

Defines the transparency of the control.

Value ranges from `0.0` (completely transparent) to `1.0` (completely opaque without any transparency).

Raises:

*   `[ValueError](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ValueError) ` –
    
    If it is not between `0.0` and `1.0`, inclusive.
    

### rtl `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control.rtl "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#__codelineno-0-1) rtl: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)  = False`

Whether the text direction of the control should be right-to-left (RTL).

### tooltip `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control.tooltip "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#__codelineno-0-1) tooltip: [TooltipValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.TooltipValue)  | None = None`

The tooltip to show when this control is hovered over.

### visible `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control.visible "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#__codelineno-0-1) visible: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)  = True`

Every control has `visible` property which is `True` by default - control is rendered on the page. Setting `visible` to `False` completely prevents control (and all its children if any) from rendering on a page canvas. Hidden controls cannot be focused or selected with a keyboard or mouse and they do not emit any events.

Back to top

---

# Column - Flet

 

[Skip to content](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#flet.Column)

Column
======

Arranges child controls vertically, optionally aligning and spacing them within the available space.

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-0-1) ft.Column(     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-0-2)     width=220,    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-0-3)     height=120,    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-0-4)     spacing=12,    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-0-5)     controls=[        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-0-6)         ft.Text("Daily planning", size=20, weight=ft.FontWeight.W_600),        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-0-7)         ft.Text("Review pull requests"),        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-0-8)         ft.Text("Ship release"),    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-0-9)     ], [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-0-10) )`

[![Column](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/core/golden/macos/column/image_for_docs.png)](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/core/golden/macos/column/image_for_docs.png)

Basic Column with Text controls

Inherits: `[LayoutControl](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/layoutcontrol/#flet.LayoutControl) `, `[ScrollableControl](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/scrollablecontrol/#flet.ScrollableControl) `, `[AdaptiveControl](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/adaptivecontrol/#flet.AdaptiveControl) `

Properties

*   **`[alignment](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#flet.Column.alignment) `**(`[MainAxisAlignment](https://docs.flet.dev/types/mainaxisalignment/#flet.MainAxisAlignment) `) –
    
    How the child Controls should be placed vertically.
    
*   **`[controls](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#flet.Column.controls) `**(`[list](https://docs.python.org/3/glossary.html#term-list) [[Control](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control) ]`) –
    
    A list of controls to display.
    
*   **`[horizontal_alignment](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#flet.Column.horizontal_alignment) `**(`[CrossAxisAlignment](https://docs.flet.dev/types/crossaxisalignment/#flet.CrossAxisAlignment) `) –
    
    Defines how the [`controls`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#flet.Column.controls)
     should be placed horizontally.
    
*   **`[intrinsic_width](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#flet.Column.intrinsic_width) `**(`[bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool) `) –
    
    If `True`, the Column will be as wide as the widest child control.
    
*   **`[run_alignment](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#flet.Column.run_alignment) `**(`[MainAxisAlignment](https://docs.flet.dev/types/mainaxisalignment/#flet.MainAxisAlignment) `) –
    
    How the runs should be placed in the cross-axis when [`wrap`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#flet.Column.wrap)
    
*   **`[run_spacing](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#flet.Column.run_spacing) `**(`[Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number) `) –
    
    The spacing between runs when [`wrap`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#flet.Column.wrap)
     is `True`.
    
*   **`[spacing](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#flet.Column.spacing) `**(`[Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number) `) –
    
    Spacing between the `controls`.
    
*   **`[tight](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#flet.Column.tight) `**(`[bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool) `) –
    
    Determines how vertical space is allocated.
    
*   **`[wrap](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#flet.Column.wrap) `**(`[bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool) `) –
    
    Whether the [`controls`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#flet.Column.controls)
     should wrap into additional columns (runs) when they don't fit in a single vertical column.
    

Examples[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#examples "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[Live example](https://flet-controls-gallery.fly.dev/layout/column)

### Column `spacing`[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#column-spacing "Permanent link")

label="{value}",                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-0-34)                     width=500, [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-2-45)         )    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-2-46)     ) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-2-47) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-2-48)[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-2-49) ft.run(main)``

[![alignment](https://docs.flet.dev/examples/controls/column/media/alignment.png)](https://docs.flet.dev/examples/controls/column/media/alignment.png)

### Column horizontal alignments[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#column-horizontal-alignments "Permanent link")

``[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-3-1) import flet as ft [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-3-2) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-3-3)[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-3-4) class ColumnFromHorizontalAlignment(ft.Column):     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-3-5)     def __init__(self, alignment: ft.CrossAxisAlignment):        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-3-6)         super().__init__()        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-3-7)         self.controls = [            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-3-8)             ft.Text(str(alignment), size=16),            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-3-9)             ft.Container(                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-3-10)                 bgcolor=ft.Colors.AMBER_100,                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-3-11)                 width=100,                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-3-12)                 content=ft.Column(                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-3-13)                     controls=self.generate_items(3),                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-3-14)                     alignment=ft.MainAxisAlignment.START,                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-3-15)                     horizontal_alignment=alignment,                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-3-16)                 ),            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-3-17)             ),        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-3-18)         ] [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-3-19)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-3-20)     @staticmethod    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-3-21)     def generate_items(count: int):        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-3-22)         """Generates a list of custom Containers with length `count`."""        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-3-23)         return [            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-3-24)             ft.Container(                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-3-25)                 content=ft.Text(value=str(i)),                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-3-26)                 alignment=ft.Alignment.CENTER,                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-3-27)                 width=50,                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-3-28)                 height=50,                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-3-29)                 bgcolor=ft.Colors.AMBER_500,            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-3-30)             )            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-3-31)             for i in range(1, count + 1)        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-3-32)         ] [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-3-33) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-3-34)[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-3-35) def main(page: ft.Page):     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-3-36)     page.add(        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-3-37)         ft.Row(            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-3-38)             spacing=30,            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-3-39)             alignment=ft.MainAxisAlignment.START,            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-3-40)             controls=[                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-3-41)                 ColumnFromHorizontalAlignment(ft.CrossAxisAlignment.START),                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-3-42)                 ColumnFromHorizontalAlignment(ft.CrossAxisAlignment.CENTER),                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-3-43)                 ColumnFromHorizontalAlignment(ft.CrossAxisAlignment.END),            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-3-44)             ],        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-3-45)         )    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-3-46)     ) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-3-47) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-3-48)[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-3-49) ft.run(main)``

[![horizontal-alignment](https://docs.flet.dev/examples/controls/column/media/horizontal_alignment.png)](https://docs.flet.dev/examples/controls/column/media/horizontal_alignment.png)

### Infinite scrolling[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#infinite-scrolling "Permanent link")

This example demonstrates adding of list items on-the-fly, as user scroll to the bottom, creating the illusion of infinite list:

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-4-1) import threading [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-4-2)[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-4-3) import flet as ft [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-4-4) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-4-5)[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-4-6) class State:     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-4-7)     i = 0 [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-4-8) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-4-9)[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-4-10) s = State() [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-4-11) sem = threading.Semaphore() [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-4-12) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-4-13)[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-4-14) def main(page: ft.Page):     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-4-15)     def on_scroll(e: ft.OnScrollEvent):        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-4-16)         if e.pixels >= e.max_scroll_extent - 100 and sem.acquire(blocking=False):            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-4-17)             try:                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-4-18)                 for _i in range(0, 10):                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-4-19)                     cl.controls.append(ft.Text(f"Text line {s.i}", key=str(s.i)))                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-4-20)                     s.i += 1                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-4-21)                 cl.update()            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-4-22)             finally:                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-4-23)                 sem.release() [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-4-24)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-4-25)     cl = ft.Column(        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-4-26)         spacing=10,        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-4-27)         height=200,        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-4-28)         width=200,        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-4-29)         scroll=ft.ScrollMode.ALWAYS,        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-4-30)         scroll_interval=0,        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-4-31)         on_scroll=on_scroll,    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-4-32)     )    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-4-33)     for _i in range(0, 50):        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-4-34)         cl.controls.append(ft.Text(f"Text line {s.i}", key=str(s.i)))        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-4-35)         s.i += 1 [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-4-36)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-4-37)     page.add(ft.Container(cl, border=ft.Border.all(1))) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-4-38) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-4-39)[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-4-40) ft.run(main)`

### Scrolling programmatically[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#scrolling-programmatically "Permanent link")

This example shows how to use [`scroll_to()`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/scrollablecontrol/#flet.ScrollableControl.scroll_to)
 to programmatically scroll a column:

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-5-1) import flet as ft [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-5-2) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-5-3)[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-5-4) def main(page: ft.Page):     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-5-5)     column = ft.Column(        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-5-6)         spacing=10,        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-5-7)         height=200,        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-5-8)         width=float("inf"),        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-5-9)         scroll=ft.ScrollMode.ALWAYS,        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-5-10)         controls=[            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-5-11)             ft.Text(f"Text line {i}", key=ft.ScrollKey(i)) for i in range(0, 100)        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-5-12)         ],    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-5-13)     ) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-5-14)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-5-15)     async def scroll_to_offset(e):        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-5-16)         await column.scroll_to(offset=500, duration=1000) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-5-17)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-5-18)     async def scroll_to_start(e):        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-5-19)         await column.scroll_to(offset=0, duration=1000) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-5-20)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-5-21)     async def scroll_to_end(e):        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-5-22)         await column.scroll_to(            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-5-23)             offset=-1, duration=2000, curve=ft.AnimationCurve.EASE_IN_OUT        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-5-24)         ) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-5-25)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-5-26)     async def scroll_to_key(e):        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-5-27)         await column.scroll_to(scroll_key="20", duration=1000) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-5-28)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-5-29)     async def scroll_to_delta(e):        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-5-30)         await column.scroll_to(delta=100, duration=200) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-5-31)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-5-32)     async def scroll_to_minus_delta(e):        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-5-33)         await column.scroll_to(delta=-100, duration=200) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-5-34)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-5-35)     page.add(        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-5-36)         ft.Container(content=column, border=ft.Border.all(1)),        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-5-37)         ft.Button("Scroll to offset 500", on_click=scroll_to_offset),        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-5-38)         ft.Row(            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-5-39)             controls=[                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-5-40)                 ft.Button("Scroll -100", on_click=scroll_to_minus_delta),                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-5-41)                 ft.Button("Scroll +100", on_click=scroll_to_delta),            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-5-42)             ]        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-5-43)         ),        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-5-44)         ft.Button("Scroll to key '20'", on_click=scroll_to_key),        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-5-45)         ft.Row(            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-5-46)             controls=[                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-5-47)                 ft.Button("Scroll to start", on_click=scroll_to_start),                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-5-48)                 ft.Button("Scroll to end", on_click=scroll_to_end),            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-5-49)             ]        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-5-50)         ),    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-5-51)     ) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-5-52) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-5-53)[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-5-54) ft.run(main)`

[![programmatic-scroll](https://docs.flet.dev/examples/controls/column/media/programmatic_scroll.png)](https://docs.flet.dev/examples/controls/column/media/programmatic_scroll.png)

Properties[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#flet.Column-properties "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### alignment `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#flet.Column.alignment "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-0-1) alignment: [MainAxisAlignment](https://docs.flet.dev/types/mainaxisalignment/#flet.MainAxisAlignment)  = [START](https://docs.flet.dev/types/mainaxisalignment/#flet.MainAxisAlignment.START)`

How the child Controls should be placed vertically.

### controls `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#flet.Column.controls "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-0-1) controls: [list](https://docs.python.org/3/glossary.html#term-list) [[Control](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control) ] = [field](https://docs.python.org/3/library/dataclasses.html#dataclasses.field "<code>dataclasses.field</code>") (default_factory=[list](https://docs.python.org/3/glossary.html#term-list) )`

A list of controls to display.

### horizontal\_alignment `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#flet.Column.horizontal_alignment "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-0-1) horizontal_alignment: [CrossAxisAlignment](https://docs.flet.dev/types/crossaxisalignment/#flet.CrossAxisAlignment)  = [START](https://docs.flet.dev/types/crossaxisalignment/#flet.CrossAxisAlignment.START)`

Defines how the [`controls`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#flet.Column.controls)
 should be placed horizontally.

### intrinsic\_width `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#flet.Column.intrinsic_width "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-0-1) intrinsic_width: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)  = False`

If `True`, the Column will be as wide as the widest child control.

### run\_alignment `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#flet.Column.run_alignment "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-0-1) run_alignment: [MainAxisAlignment](https://docs.flet.dev/types/mainaxisalignment/#flet.MainAxisAlignment)  = [START](https://docs.flet.dev/types/mainaxisalignment/#flet.MainAxisAlignment.START)`

How the runs should be placed in the cross-axis when [`wrap`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#flet.Column.wrap)
 is `True`.

### run\_spacing `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#flet.Column.run_spacing "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-0-1) run_spacing: [Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  = 10`

The spacing between runs when [`wrap`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#flet.Column.wrap)
 is `True`.

### spacing `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#flet.Column.spacing "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-0-1) spacing: [Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  = 10`

Spacing between the `controls`.

It is applied only when [`alignment`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#flet.Column.alignment)
 is `MainAxisAlignment.START`, `MainAxisAlignment.END` or `MainAxisAlignment.CENTER`.

### tight `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#flet.Column.tight "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-0-1) tight: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)  = False`

Determines how vertical space is allocated.

If `False` (default), children expand to fill the available vertical space. If `True`, only the minimum vertical space required by the children is used.

### wrap `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#flet.Column.wrap "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#__codelineno-0-1) wrap: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)  = False`

Whether the [`controls`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#flet.Column.controls)
 should wrap into additional columns (runs) when they don't fit in a single vertical column.

Back to top

---

# OutlinedButton - Flet

 

[Skip to content](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/outlinedbutton/#flet.OutlinedButton)

OutlinedButton
==============

Outlined buttons are medium-emphasis buttons. They contain actions that are important, but aren't the primary action in an app. Outlined buttons pair well with filled buttons to indicate an alternative, secondary action.

Example:

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/outlinedbutton/#__codelineno-0-1) ft.OutlinedButton(content="Outlined button")`

[![OutlinedButton](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/material/golden/macos/outlined_button/image_for_docs.png)](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/material/golden/macos/outlined_button/image_for_docs.png)

Simple Outlined Button

Inherits: `[LayoutControl](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/layoutcontrol/#flet.LayoutControl) `, `[AdaptiveControl](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/adaptivecontrol/#flet.AdaptiveControl) `

Properties

*   **`[autofocus](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/outlinedbutton/#flet.OutlinedButton.autofocus) `**(`[bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool) `) –
    
    True if the control will be selected as the initial focus.
    
*   **`[clip_behavior](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/outlinedbutton/#flet.OutlinedButton.clip_behavior) `**(`[ClipBehavior](https://docs.flet.dev/types/clipbehavior/#flet.ClipBehavior) `) –
    
    The content will be clipped (or not) according to this option.
    
*   **`[content](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/outlinedbutton/#flet.OutlinedButton.content) `**(`[StrOrControl](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.StrOrControl)  | None`) –
    
    A Control representing custom button content.
    
*   **`[icon](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/outlinedbutton/#flet.OutlinedButton.icon) `**(`[IconDataOrControl](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.IconDataOrControl)  | None`) –
    
    An icon to display in this button.
    
*   **`[icon_color](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/outlinedbutton/#flet.OutlinedButton.icon_color) `**(`[ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None`) –
    
    Icon color.
    
*   **`[style](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/outlinedbutton/#flet.OutlinedButton.style) `**(`[ButtonStyle](https://docs.flet.dev/types/buttonstyle/#flet.ButtonStyle)  | None`) –
    
*   **`[url](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/outlinedbutton/#flet.OutlinedButton.url) `**(`[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)  | [Url](https://docs.flet.dev/types/url/#flet.Url)  | None`) –
    
    The URL to open when this button is clicked.
    

Events

*   **`[on_blur](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/outlinedbutton/#flet.OutlinedButton.on_blur) `**(`[ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[OutlinedButton](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/outlinedbutton/#flet.OutlinedButton) ] | None`) –
    
    Called when this button has lost focus.
    
*   **`[on_click](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/outlinedbutton/#flet.OutlinedButton.on_click) `**(`[ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[OutlinedButton](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/outlinedbutton/#flet.OutlinedButton) ] | None`) –
    
    Called when a user clicks this button.
    
*   **`[on_focus](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/outlinedbutton/#flet.OutlinedButton.on_focus) `**(`[ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[OutlinedButton](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/outlinedbutton/#flet.OutlinedButton) ] | None`) –
    
    Called when this button has received focus.
    
*   **`[on_hover](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/outlinedbutton/#flet.OutlinedButton.on_hover) `**(`[ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[OutlinedButton](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/outlinedbutton/#flet.OutlinedButton) ] | None`) –
    
    Called when a mouse pointer enters or exists this button's response area.
    
*   **`[on_long_press](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/outlinedbutton/#flet.OutlinedButton.on_long_press) `**(`[ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[OutlinedButton](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/outlinedbutton/#flet.OutlinedButton) ] | None`) –
    
    Called when this button is long-pressed.
    

Methods

*   **`[focus](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/outlinedbutton/#flet.OutlinedButton.focus) `** –
    
    Requests focus for this control.
    

Examples[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/outlinedbutton/#examples "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[Live example](https://flet-controls-gallery.fly.dev/buttons/outlinedbutton)

### Basic example[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/outlinedbutton/#basic-example "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/outlinedbutton/#__codelineno-0-1) import flet as ft [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/outlinedbutton/#__codelineno-0-2) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/outlinedbutton/#__codelineno-0-3)[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/outlinedbutton/#__codelineno-0-4) def main(page: ft.Page):     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/outlinedbutton/#__codelineno-0-5)     page.title = "OutlinedButton Example" [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/outlinedbutton/#__codelineno-0-6)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/outlinedbutton/#__codelineno-0-7)     page.add(        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/outlinedbutton/#__codelineno-0-8)         ft.OutlinedButton(content="Outlined button"),        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/outlinedbutton/#__codelineno-0-9)         ft.OutlinedButton(content="Disabled button", disabled=True),    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/outlinedbutton/#__codelineno-0-10)     ) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/outlinedbutton/#__codelineno-0-11) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/outlinedbutton/#__codelineno-0-12)[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/outlinedbutton/#__codelineno-0-13) if __name__ == "__main__":     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/outlinedbutton/#__codelineno-0-14)     ft.run(main)`

[![basic](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/material/golden/macos/outlined_button/basic.png)](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/material/golden/macos/outlined_button/basic.png)

### Handling clicks[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/outlinedbutton/#handling-clicks "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/outlinedbutton/#__codelineno-1-1) import flet as ft [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/outlinedbutton/#__codelineno-1-2) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/outlinedbutton/#__codelineno-1-3)[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/outlinedbutton/#__codelineno-1-4) def main(page: ft.Page):     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/outlinedbutton/#__codelineno-1-5)     page.title = "OutlinedButton Example"    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/outlinedbutton/#__codelineno-1-6)     page.theme_mode = ft.ThemeMode.LIGHT [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/outlinedbutton/#__codelineno-1-7)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/outlinedbutton/#__codelineno-1-8)     def handle_button_click(e: ft.Event[ft.OutlinedButton]):        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/outlinedbutton/#__codelineno-1-9)         button.data += 1        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/outlinedbutton/#__codelineno-1-10)         message.value = f"Button clicked {button.data} time(s)"        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/outlinedbutton/#__codelineno-1-11)         page.update() [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/outlinedbutton/#__codelineno-1-12)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/outlinedbutton/#__codelineno-1-13)     button = ft.OutlinedButton(        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/outlinedbutton/#__codelineno-1-14)         content="Button with 'click' event",        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/outlinedbutton/#__codelineno-1-15)         data=0,        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/outlinedbutton/#__codelineno-1-16)         on_click=handle_button_click,    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/outlinedbutton/#__codelineno-1-17)     )    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/outlinedbutton/#__codelineno-1-18)     message = ft.Text() [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/outlinedbutton/#__codelineno-1-19)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/outlinedbutton/#__codelineno-1-20)     page.add(        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/outlinedbutton/#__codelineno-1-21)         ft.Column(            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/outlinedbutton/#__codelineno-1-22)             controls=[button, message],            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/outlinedbutton/#__codelineno-1-23)             alignment=ft.MainAxisAlignment.CENTER,            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/outlinedbutton/#__codelineno-1-24)             horizontal_alignment=ft.CrossAxisAlignment.CENTER,            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/outlinedbutton/#__codelineno-1-25)             expand=True,        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/outlinedbutton/#__codelineno-1-26)         )    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/outlinedbutton/#__codelineno-1-27)     ) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/outlinedbutton/#__codelineno-1-28) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/outlinedbutton/#__codelineno-1-29)[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/outlinedbutton/#__codelineno-1-30) if __name__ == "__main__":     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/outlinedbutton/#__codelineno-1-31)     ft.run(main)`

Properties[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/outlinedbutton/#flet.OutlinedButton-properties "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### autofocus `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/outlinedbutton/#flet.OutlinedButton.autofocus "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/outlinedbutton/#__codelineno-0-1) autofocus: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)  = False`

True if the control will be selected as the initial focus.

If there is more than one control on a page with autofocus set, then the first one added to the page will get focus.

### clip\_behavior `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/outlinedbutton/#flet.OutlinedButton.clip_behavior "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/outlinedbutton/#__codelineno-0-1) clip_behavior: [ClipBehavior](https://docs.flet.dev/types/clipbehavior/#flet.ClipBehavior)  = [NONE](https://docs.flet.dev/types/clipbehavior/#flet.ClipBehavior.NONE)`

The content will be clipped (or not) according to this option.

### content `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/outlinedbutton/#flet.OutlinedButton.content "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/outlinedbutton/#__codelineno-0-1) content: [StrOrControl](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.StrOrControl)  | None = None`

A Control representing custom button content.

Raises:

*   `[ValueError](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ValueError) ` –
    
    If neither [`icon`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/outlinedbutton/#flet.OutlinedButton.icon)
     nor `content` is provided.
    

### icon `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/outlinedbutton/#flet.OutlinedButton.icon "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/outlinedbutton/#__codelineno-0-1) icon: [IconDataOrControl](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.IconDataOrControl)  | None = None`

An icon to display in this button.

### icon\_color `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/outlinedbutton/#flet.OutlinedButton.icon_color "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/outlinedbutton/#__codelineno-0-1) icon_color: [ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None = None`

Icon color.

### style `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/outlinedbutton/#flet.OutlinedButton.style "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/outlinedbutton/#__codelineno-0-1) style: [ButtonStyle](https://docs.flet.dev/types/buttonstyle/#flet.ButtonStyle)  | None = None`

### url `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/outlinedbutton/#flet.OutlinedButton.url "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/outlinedbutton/#__codelineno-0-1) url: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)  | [Url](https://docs.flet.dev/types/url/#flet.Url)  | None = None`

The URL to open when this button is clicked.

Additionally, if [`on_click`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/outlinedbutton/#flet.OutlinedButton.on_click)
 event callback is provided, it is fired after that.

Events[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/outlinedbutton/#flet.OutlinedButton-events "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### on\_blur `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/outlinedbutton/#flet.OutlinedButton.on_blur "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/outlinedbutton/#__codelineno-0-1) on_blur: [ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[OutlinedButton](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/outlinedbutton/#flet.OutlinedButton) ] | None = None`

Called when this button has lost focus.

### on\_click `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/outlinedbutton/#flet.OutlinedButton.on_click "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/outlinedbutton/#__codelineno-0-1) on_click: [ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[OutlinedButton](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/outlinedbutton/#flet.OutlinedButton) ] | None = None`

Called when a user clicks this button.

### on\_focus `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/outlinedbutton/#flet.OutlinedButton.on_focus "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/outlinedbutton/#__codelineno-0-1) on_focus: [ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[OutlinedButton](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/outlinedbutton/#flet.OutlinedButton) ] | None = None`

Called when this button has received focus.

### on\_hover `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/outlinedbutton/#flet.OutlinedButton.on_hover "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/outlinedbutton/#__codelineno-0-1) on_hover: [ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[OutlinedButton](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/outlinedbutton/#flet.OutlinedButton) ] | None = None`

Called when a mouse pointer enters or exists this button's response area.

`data` property of event object contains `true` (string) when cursor enters and `false` when it exits.

### on\_long\_press `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/outlinedbutton/#flet.OutlinedButton.on_long_press "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/outlinedbutton/#__codelineno-0-1) on_long_press: (     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/outlinedbutton/#__codelineno-0-2)     [ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[OutlinedButton](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/outlinedbutton/#flet.OutlinedButton) ] | None [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/outlinedbutton/#__codelineno-0-3) ) = None`

Called when this button is long-pressed.

Methods[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/outlinedbutton/#flet.OutlinedButton-methods "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### focus `async` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/outlinedbutton/#flet.OutlinedButton.focus "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/outlinedbutton/#__codelineno-0-1) focus()`

Requests focus for this control.

# RangeSlider - Flet

 

[Skip to content](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#flet.RangeSlider)

RangeSlider
===========

A Material Design range slider. Used to select a range from a range of values.

A range slider can be used to select from either a continuous or a discrete set of values. The default is to use a continuous range of values from [`min`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#flet.RangeSlider.min)
 to [`max`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#flet.RangeSlider.max)
.

Example:

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#__codelineno-0-1) ft.RangeSlider(     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#__codelineno-0-2)     min=0,    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#__codelineno-0-3)     max=10,    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#__codelineno-0-4)     start_value=2,    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#__codelineno-0-5)     divisions=10,    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#__codelineno-0-6)     end_value=7, [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#__codelineno-0-7) )`

[![RangeSlider](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/material/golden/macos/range_slider/image_for_docs.png)](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/material/golden/macos/range_slider/image_for_docs.png)

Range Slider

Inherits: `[LayoutControl](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/layoutcontrol/#flet.LayoutControl) `

Properties

*   **`[active_color](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#flet.RangeSlider.active_color) `**(`[ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None`) –
    
    The color to use for the portion of the slider track that is active.
    
*   **`[divisions](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#flet.RangeSlider.divisions) `**(`[int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int)  | None`) –
    
    The number of discrete divisions.
    
*   **`[end_value](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#flet.RangeSlider.end_value) `**(`[Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number) `) –
    
    The currently selected end value of this slider.
    
*   **`[inactive_color](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#flet.RangeSlider.inactive_color) `**(`[ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None`) –
    
    The color for the inactive portions of the slider track.
    
*   **`[label](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#flet.RangeSlider.label) `**(`[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)  | None`) –
    
    A label to show above the slider thumbs when the slider is active.
    
*   **`[max](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#flet.RangeSlider.max) `**(`[Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number) `) –
    
    The maximum value the user can select.
    
*   **`[min](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#flet.RangeSlider.min) `**(`[Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number) `) –
    
    The minimum value the user can select.
    
*   **`[mouse_cursor](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#flet.RangeSlider.mouse_cursor) `**(`[ControlStateValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlStateValue) [[MouseCursor](https://docs.flet.dev/types/mousecursor/#flet.MouseCursor) ] | None`) –
    
    The cursor for a mouse pointer entering or hovering over this control.
    
*   **`[overlay_color](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#flet.RangeSlider.overlay_color) `**(`[ControlStateValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlStateValue) [[ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue) ] | None`) –
    
    The highlight color that's typically used to indicate that the range slider thumb is in [`ControlState.HOVERED`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/controlstate/#flet.ControlState.HOVERED)
     or [`ControlState.DRAGGED`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/controlstate/#flet.ControlState.DRAGGED)
     state.
    
*   **`[round](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#flet.RangeSlider.round) `**(`[int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int) `) –
    
    The number of decimals displayed on the [`label`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#flet.RangeSlider.label)
     containing `{value}`.
    
*   **`[start_value](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#flet.RangeSlider.start_value) `**(`[Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number) `) –
    
    The currently selected start value for this slider.
    

Events

*   **`[on_change](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#flet.RangeSlider.on_change) `**(`[ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[RangeSlider](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#flet.RangeSlider) ] | None`) –
    
    Called when the state of this slider is changed.
    
*   **`[on_change_end](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#flet.RangeSlider.on_change_end) `**(`[ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[RangeSlider](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#flet.RangeSlider) ] | None`) –
    
    Called when the user is done selecting a new value for this slider.
    
*   **`[on_change_start](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#flet.RangeSlider.on_change_start) `**(`[ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[RangeSlider](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#flet.RangeSlider) ] | None`) –
    
    Called when the user starts selecting a new value for this slider.
    

Examples[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#examples "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[Live example](https://flet-controls-gallery.fly.dev/input/rangeslider)

### Basic Example[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#basic-example "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#__codelineno-0-1) import flet as ft [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#__codelineno-0-2) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#__codelineno-0-3)[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#__codelineno-0-4) def main(page: ft.Page):     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#__codelineno-0-5)     page.add(        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#__codelineno-0-6)         ft.Column(            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#__codelineno-0-7)             horizontal_alignment=ft.CrossAxisAlignment.CENTER,            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#__codelineno-0-8)             controls=[                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#__codelineno-0-9)                 ft.Text(                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#__codelineno-0-10)                     value="Range slider with divisions and labels",                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#__codelineno-0-11)                     size=20,                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#__codelineno-0-12)                     weight=ft.FontWeight.BOLD,                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#__codelineno-0-13)                 ),                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#__codelineno-0-14)                 ft.Container(height=30),                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#__codelineno-0-15)                 ft.RangeSlider(                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#__codelineno-0-16)                     min=0,                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#__codelineno-0-17)                     max=50,                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#__codelineno-0-18)                     start_value=10,                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#__codelineno-0-19)                     divisions=10,                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#__codelineno-0-20)                     end_value=20,                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#__codelineno-0-21)                     inactive_color=ft.Colors.GREEN_300,                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#__codelineno-0-22)                     active_color=ft.Colors.GREEN_700,                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#__codelineno-0-23)                     overlay_color=ft.Colors.GREEN_100,                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#__codelineno-0-24)                     label="{value}",                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#__codelineno-0-25)                 ),            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#__codelineno-0-26)             ],        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#__codelineno-0-27)         )    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#__codelineno-0-28)     ) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#__codelineno-0-29) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#__codelineno-0-30)[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#__codelineno-0-31) if __name__ == "__main__":     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#__codelineno-0-32)     ft.run(main)`

### RangeSlider with events[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#rangeslider-with-events "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#__codelineno-1-1) import flet as ft [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#__codelineno-1-2) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#__codelineno-1-3)[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#__codelineno-1-4) def main(page: ft.Page):     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#__codelineno-1-5)     page.scroll = ft.ScrollMode.AUTO [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#__codelineno-1-6)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#__codelineno-1-7)     def handle_slider_change_start(e: ft.Event[ft.RangeSlider]):        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#__codelineno-1-8)         print(f"on_change_start: {e.control.start_value}, {e.control.end_value}") [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#__codelineno-1-9)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#__codelineno-1-10)     def handle_slider_change(e: ft.Event[ft.RangeSlider]):        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#__codelineno-1-11)         print(f"on_change: {e.control.start_value}, {e.control.end_value}") [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#__codelineno-1-12)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#__codelineno-1-13)     def handle_slider_change_end(e: ft.Event[ft.RangeSlider]):        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#__codelineno-1-14)         print(f"on_change_end: {e.control.start_value}, {e.control.end_value}")        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#__codelineno-1-15)         message.value = f"on_change_end: {e.control.start_value}, {e.control.end_value}"        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#__codelineno-1-16)         page.update() [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#__codelineno-1-17)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#__codelineno-1-18)     page.add(        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#__codelineno-1-19)         ft.Column(            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#__codelineno-1-20)             horizontal_alignment=ft.CrossAxisAlignment.CENTER,            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#__codelineno-1-21)             controls=[                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#__codelineno-1-22)                 ft.Text(                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#__codelineno-1-23)                     value="Range slider with events",                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#__codelineno-1-24)                     size=20,                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#__codelineno-1-25)                     weight=ft.FontWeight.BOLD,                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#__codelineno-1-26)                 ),                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#__codelineno-1-27)                 ft.Container(height=30),                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#__codelineno-1-28)                 ft.RangeSlider(                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#__codelineno-1-29)                     divisions=100,                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#__codelineno-1-30)                     min=0,                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#__codelineno-1-31)                     max=100,                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#__codelineno-1-32)                     start_value=10,                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#__codelineno-1-33)                     end_value=20,                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#__codelineno-1-34)                     on_change_start=handle_slider_change_start,                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#__codelineno-1-35)                     on_change=handle_slider_change,                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#__codelineno-1-36)                     on_change_end=handle_slider_change_end,                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#__codelineno-1-37)                     label="{value}%",                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#__codelineno-1-38)                 ),                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#__codelineno-1-39)                 message := ft.Text(),            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#__codelineno-1-40)             ],        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#__codelineno-1-41)         )    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#__codelineno-1-42)     ) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#__codelineno-1-43) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#__codelineno-1-44)[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#__codelineno-1-45) if __name__ == "__main__":     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#__codelineno-1-46)     ft.run(main)`

[![handling_events](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/material/golden/macos/range_slider/handling_events.gif)](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/material/golden/macos/range_slider/handling_events.gif)

Properties[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#flet.RangeSlider-properties "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### active\_color `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#flet.RangeSlider.active_color "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#__codelineno-0-1) active_color: [ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None = None`

The color to use for the portion of the slider track that is active.

The "active" segment of the range slider is the span between the thumbs.

### divisions `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#flet.RangeSlider.divisions "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#__codelineno-0-1) divisions: [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int)  | None = None`

The number of discrete divisions.

Typically used with [`label`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#flet.RangeSlider.label)
 to show the current discrete values.

If not set, this slider is continuous and [`label`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#flet.RangeSlider.label)
 is not displayed.

Raises:

*   `[ValueError](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ValueError) ` –
    
    If it is not strictly greater than `0`.
    

### end\_value `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#flet.RangeSlider.end_value "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#__codelineno-0-1) end_value: [Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)`

The currently selected end value of this slider.

The right thumb of this slider is drawn at a position that corresponds to this value. Use [`label`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#flet.RangeSlider.label)
 to change the label displayed on the thumb.

Raises:

*   `[ValueError](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ValueError) ` –
    
    If it is not less than or equal to [`max`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#flet.RangeSlider.max)
    .
    
*   `[ValueError](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ValueError) ` –
    
    If it is not greater than or equal to [`start_value`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#flet.RangeSlider.start_value)
    .
    

### inactive\_color `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#flet.RangeSlider.inactive_color "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#__codelineno-0-1) inactive_color: [ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None = None`

The color for the inactive portions of the slider track.

The "inactive" segments of the slider are the span of tracks between the min and the start thumb, and the end thumb and the max.

### label `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#flet.RangeSlider.label "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#__codelineno-0-1) label: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)  | None = None`

A label to show above the slider thumbs when the slider is active.

It may contain `{value}` which will be replaced with realtime values of [`start_value`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#flet.RangeSlider.start_value)
 and [`end_value`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#flet.RangeSlider.end_value)
, in the corresponding slider thumbs.

If not set, then the labels will not be displayed. If [`divisions`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#flet.RangeSlider.divisions)
 is not set, this slider is continuous and labels are not displayed.

### max `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#flet.RangeSlider.max "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#__codelineno-0-1) max: [Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  = 1.0`

The maximum value the user can select.

If the [`max`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#flet.RangeSlider.max)
 is equal to the [`min`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#flet.RangeSlider.min)
, then the slider is disabled.

Raises:

*   `[ValueError](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ValueError) ` –
    
    If it is not greater than or equal to [`end_value`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#flet.RangeSlider.end_value)
    .
    
*   `[ValueError](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ValueError) ` –
    
    If it is not greater than or equal to [`min`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#flet.RangeSlider.min)
    .
    

### min `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#flet.RangeSlider.min "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#__codelineno-0-1) min: [Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  = 0.0`

The minimum value the user can select.

If the [`max`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#flet.RangeSlider.max)
 is equal to the `min`, then the slider is disabled.

Raises:

*   `[ValueError](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ValueError) ` –
    
    If it is not less than or equal to [`start_value`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#flet.RangeSlider.start_value)
    .
    
*   `[ValueError](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ValueError) ` –
    
    If it is not less than or equal to [`max`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#flet.RangeSlider.max)
    .
    

### mouse\_cursor `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#flet.RangeSlider.mouse_cursor "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#__codelineno-0-1) mouse_cursor: [ControlStateValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlStateValue) [[MouseCursor](https://docs.flet.dev/types/mousecursor/#flet.MouseCursor) ] | None = None`

The cursor for a mouse pointer entering or hovering over this control.

### overlay\_color `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#flet.RangeSlider.overlay_color "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#__codelineno-0-1) overlay_color: [ControlStateValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlStateValue) [[ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue) ] | None = None`

The highlight color that's typically used to indicate that the range slider thumb is in [`ControlState.HOVERED`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/controlstate/#flet.ControlState.HOVERED)
 or [`ControlState.DRAGGED`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/controlstate/#flet.ControlState.DRAGGED)
 state.

### round `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#flet.RangeSlider.round "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#__codelineno-0-1) round: [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int)  = 0`

The number of decimals displayed on the [`label`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#flet.RangeSlider.label)
 containing `{value}`.

Defaults to `0` - value rounded to the nearest integer.

Raises:

*   `[ValueError](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ValueError) ` –
    
    If it is not between `0` and `20`, inclusive.
    

### start\_value `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#flet.RangeSlider.start_value "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#__codelineno-0-1) start_value: [Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)`

The currently selected start value for this slider.

The left thumb of this slider is drawn at a position that corresponds to this value. Use [`label`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#flet.RangeSlider.label)
 to change the label displayed on the thumb.

Raises:

*   `[ValueError](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ValueError) ` –
    
    If it is not greater than or equal to [`min`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#flet.RangeSlider.min)
    .
    
*   `[ValueError](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ValueError) ` –
    
    If it is not less than or equal to [`end_value`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#flet.RangeSlider.end_value)
    .
    

Events[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#flet.RangeSlider-events "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### on\_change `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#flet.RangeSlider.on_change "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#__codelineno-0-1) on_change: [ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[RangeSlider](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#flet.RangeSlider) ] | None = None`

Called when the state of this slider is changed.

### on\_change\_end `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#flet.RangeSlider.on_change_end "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#__codelineno-0-1) on_change_end: [ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[RangeSlider](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#flet.RangeSlider) ] | None = (     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#__codelineno-0-2)     None [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#__codelineno-0-3) )`

Called when the user is done selecting a new value for this slider.

### on\_change\_start `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#flet.RangeSlider.on_change_start "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#__codelineno-0-1) on_change_start: [ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[RangeSlider](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#flet.RangeSlider) ] | None = (     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#__codelineno-0-2)     None [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/rangeslider/#__codelineno-0-3) )`

Called when the user starts selecting a new value for this slider.
