# ReorderableListView - Flet

 

[Skip to content](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#flet.ReorderableListView)

ReorderableListView
===================

A scrollable list of controls that can be reordered.

Tip

By default, each child control (from [`controls`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#flet.ReorderableListView.controls)
) is draggable using an automatically created drag handle (see [`show_default_drag_handles`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#flet.ReorderableListView.show_default_drag_handles)
). To customize the draggable area, use the [`ReorderableDragHandle`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderabledraghandle/#flet.ReorderableDragHandle)
 to define your own drag handle or region.

Example:

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#__codelineno-0-1) ft.ReorderableListView(     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#__codelineno-0-2)     controls=[        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#__codelineno-0-3)         ft.ListTile(            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#__codelineno-0-4)             title=ft.Text(f"Item {i}"),            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#__codelineno-0-5)             bgcolor=ft.Colors.BLUE_GREY_300,        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#__codelineno-0-6)         )        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#__codelineno-0-7)         for i in range(1, 6)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#__codelineno-0-8)     ], [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#__codelineno-0-9) )`

[![ReorderableListView](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/material/golden/macos/reorderable_list_view/image_for_docs.png)](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/material/golden/macos/reorderable_list_view/image_for_docs.png)

Reorderable list view

Inherits: `[ListView](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listview/#flet.ListView) `

Properties

*   **`[anchor](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#flet.ReorderableListView.anchor) `**(`[Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number) `) –
    
    The relative position of the zero scroll offset.
    
*   **`[auto_scroller_velocity_scalar](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#flet.ReorderableListView.auto_scroller_velocity_scalar) `**(`[Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None`) –
    
    The velocity scalar per pixel over scroll.
    
*   **`[controls](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#flet.ReorderableListView.controls) `**(`[list](https://docs.python.org/3/glossary.html#term-list) [[Control](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control) ]`) –
    
    The controls displayed by this [`ReorderableListView`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#flet.ReorderableListView)
    .
    
*   **`[footer](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#flet.ReorderableListView.footer) `**(`[Control](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control)  | None`) –
    
    A non-reorderable footer item to show after the [`controls`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#flet.ReorderableListView.controls)
    .
    
*   **`[header](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#flet.ReorderableListView.header) `**(`[Control](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control)  | None`) –
    
    A non-reorderable header item to show before the [`controls`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#flet.ReorderableListView.controls)
    .
    
*   **`[mouse_cursor](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#flet.ReorderableListView.mouse_cursor) `**(`[MouseCursor](https://docs.flet.dev/types/mousecursor/#flet.MouseCursor)  | None`) –
    
    The cursor for a mouse pointer when it enters or is hovering over the drag handle.
    
*   **`[show_default_drag_handles](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#flet.ReorderableListView.show_default_drag_handles) `**(`[bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool) `) –
    
    Whether to show default drag handles for each [`controls`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#flet.ReorderableListView.controls)
     item.
    

Events

*   **`[on_reorder](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#flet.ReorderableListView.on_reorder) `**(`[EventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.EventHandler) [[OnReorderEvent](https://docs.flet.dev/types/onreorderevent/#flet.OnReorderEvent) ] | None`) –
    
    Called when a [`controls`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#flet.ReorderableListView.controls)
     item has been dragged to a new location/position.
    
*   **`[on_reorder_end](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#flet.ReorderableListView.on_reorder_end) `**(`[EventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.EventHandler) [[OnReorderEvent](https://docs.flet.dev/types/onreorderevent/#flet.OnReorderEvent) ] | None`) –
    
    Called when the dragged [`controls`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#flet.ReorderableListView.controls)
     item is dropped.
    
*   **`[on_reorder_start](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#flet.ReorderableListView.on_reorder_start) `**(`[EventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.EventHandler) [[OnReorderEvent](https://docs.flet.dev/types/onreorderevent/#flet.OnReorderEvent) ] | None`) –
    
    Called when a [`controls`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#flet.ReorderableListView.controls)
     item drag has started.
    

Examples[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#examples "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[Live example](https://flet-controls-gallery.fly.dev/input/autofillgroup)

### Horizontal and Vertical[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#horizontal-and-vertical "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#__codelineno-0-1) import flet as ft [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#__codelineno-0-2) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#__codelineno-0-3)[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#__codelineno-0-4) def main(page: ft.Page):     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#__codelineno-0-5)     # the primary color is the color of the reorder handle    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#__codelineno-0-6)     page.theme = page.dark_theme = ft.Theme(        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#__codelineno-0-7)         color_scheme=ft.ColorScheme(primary=ft.Colors.BLUE)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#__codelineno-0-8)     ) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#__codelineno-0-9)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#__codelineno-0-10)     def handle_reorder(e: ft.OnReorderEvent):        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#__codelineno-0-11)         # Reorder controls list to match the UI change        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#__codelineno-0-12)         e.control.controls.insert(e.new_index, e.control.controls.pop(e.old_index)) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#__codelineno-0-13)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#__codelineno-0-14)     def get_color(i):        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#__codelineno-0-15)         return ft.Colors.ERROR if i % 2 == 0 else ft.Colors.ON_ERROR_CONTAINER [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#__codelineno-0-16)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#__codelineno-0-17)     page.add(        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#__codelineno-0-18)         # horizontal        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#__codelineno-0-19)         ft.ReorderableListView(            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#__codelineno-0-20)             expand=True,            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#__codelineno-0-21)             horizontal=True,            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#__codelineno-0-22)             on_reorder=handle_reorder,            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#__codelineno-0-23)             controls=[                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#__codelineno-0-24)                 ft.Container(                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#__codelineno-0-25)                     content=ft.Text(f"Item {i}", color=ft.Colors.BLACK),                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#__codelineno-0-26)                     bgcolor=get_color(i),                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#__codelineno-0-27)                     margin=ft.Margin.symmetric(horizontal=5, vertical=10),                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#__codelineno-0-28)                     width=100,                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#__codelineno-0-29)                     alignment=ft.Alignment.CENTER,                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#__codelineno-0-30)                 )                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#__codelineno-0-31)                 for i in range(10)            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#__codelineno-0-32)             ],        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#__codelineno-0-33)         ),        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#__codelineno-0-34)         # vertical        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#__codelineno-0-35)         ft.ReorderableListView(            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#__codelineno-0-36)             expand=True,            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#__codelineno-0-37)             on_reorder=handle_reorder,            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#__codelineno-0-38)             controls=[                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#__codelineno-0-39)                 ft.ListTile(                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#__codelineno-0-40)                     title=ft.Text(f"Item {i}", color=ft.Colors.BLACK),                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#__codelineno-0-41)                     leading=ft.Icon(ft.Icons.CHECK, color=ft.Colors.RED),                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#__codelineno-0-42)                     bgcolor=get_color(i),                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#__codelineno-0-43)                 )                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#__codelineno-0-44)                 for i in range(10)            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#__codelineno-0-45)             ],        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#__codelineno-0-46)         ),    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#__codelineno-0-47)     ) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#__codelineno-0-48) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#__codelineno-0-49)[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#__codelineno-0-50) if __name__ == "__main__":     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#__codelineno-0-51)     ft.run(main)`

[![horizontal-and-vertical](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/material/golden/macos/reorderable_list_view/horizontal_and_vertical.png)](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/material/golden/macos/reorderable_list_view/horizontal_and_vertical.png)

### Custom drag handle[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#custom-drag-handle "Permanent link")

See [this](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderabledraghandle/#examples)
.

Properties[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#flet.ReorderableListView-properties "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### anchor `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#flet.ReorderableListView.anchor "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#__codelineno-0-1) anchor: [Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  = 0.0`

The relative position of the zero scroll offset.

### auto\_scroller\_velocity\_scalar `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#flet.ReorderableListView.auto_scroller_velocity_scalar "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#__codelineno-0-1) auto_scroller_velocity_scalar: [Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None = None`

The velocity scalar per pixel over scroll.

It represents how the velocity scale with the over scroll distance. The auto-scroll velocity = (distance of overscroll) \* velocity scalar.

### controls `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#flet.ReorderableListView.controls "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#__codelineno-0-1) controls: [list](https://docs.python.org/3/glossary.html#term-list) [[Control](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control) ] = [field](https://docs.python.org/3/library/dataclasses.html#dataclasses.field "<code>dataclasses.field</code>") (default_factory=[list](https://docs.python.org/3/glossary.html#term-list) )`

The controls displayed by this [`ReorderableListView`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#flet.ReorderableListView)
.

Note

When an item of this list gets reordered, [`on_reorder`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#flet.ReorderableListView.on_reorder)
 event gets fired, but it doesn't reorder the `controls` list automatically. So, to keep `controls` list in sync with the UI, reorder this list inside your [`on_reorder`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#flet.ReorderableListView.on_reorder)
 event handler. ([example](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#flet.ReorderableListView.on_reorder)
)

### footer `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#flet.ReorderableListView.footer "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#__codelineno-0-1) footer: [Control](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control)  | None = None`

A non-reorderable footer item to show after the [`controls`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#flet.ReorderableListView.controls)
.

### header `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#flet.ReorderableListView.header "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#__codelineno-0-1) header: [Control](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control)  | None = None`

A non-reorderable header item to show before the [`controls`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#flet.ReorderableListView.controls)
.

### mouse\_cursor `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#flet.ReorderableListView.mouse_cursor "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#__codelineno-0-1) mouse_cursor: [MouseCursor](https://docs.flet.dev/types/mousecursor/#flet.MouseCursor)  | None = None`

The cursor for a mouse pointer when it enters or is hovering over the drag handle.

### show\_default\_drag\_handles `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#flet.ReorderableListView.show_default_drag_handles "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#__codelineno-0-1) show_default_drag_handles: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)  = True`

Whether to show default drag handles for each [`controls`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#flet.ReorderableListView.controls)
 item.

If `True`: on desktop platforms, a drag handle is stacked over the center of each item's trailing edge; on mobile platforms, a long press anywhere on the item starts a drag.

The default desktop drag handle is just an `Icons.DRAG_HANDLE` wrapped by a [`ReorderableDragHandle`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderabledraghandle/#flet.ReorderableDragHandle)
. On mobile platforms, the entire item is wrapped with a [`ReorderableDragHandle`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderabledraghandle/#flet.ReorderableDragHandle)
.

To customize the appearance or layout of drag handles, wrap each [`controls`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#flet.ReorderableListView.controls)
 item, or a control within each of them, with a [`ReorderableDragHandle`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderabledraghandle/#flet.ReorderableDragHandle)
. For full control over the drag handles, you might want to set `show_default_drag_handles` to `False`.

Example

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#__codelineno-0-1) ft.ReorderableListView(     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#__codelineno-0-2)     show_default_drag_handles=False,    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#__codelineno-0-3)     controls=[        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#__codelineno-0-4)         ft.ListTile(            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#__codelineno-0-5)             title=ft.Text(f"Draggable Item {i}", color=ft.Colors.BLACK),            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#__codelineno-0-6)             leading=ft.ReorderableDragHandle(                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#__codelineno-0-7)                 content=ft.Icon(ft.Icons.DRAG_INDICATOR, color=ft.Colors.RED),                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#__codelineno-0-8)                 mouse_cursor=ft.MouseCursor.GRAB,            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#__codelineno-0-9)             ),        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#__codelineno-0-10)         )        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#__codelineno-0-11)         for i in range(10)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#__codelineno-0-12)     ], [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#__codelineno-0-13) )`

Events[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#flet.ReorderableListView-events "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### on\_reorder `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#flet.ReorderableListView.on_reorder "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#__codelineno-0-1) on_reorder: [EventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.EventHandler) [[OnReorderEvent](https://docs.flet.dev/types/onreorderevent/#flet.OnReorderEvent) ] | None = None`

Called when a [`controls`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#flet.ReorderableListView.controls)
 item has been dragged to a new location/position.

Note

This event does not reorder [`controls`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#flet.ReorderableListView.controls)
 automatically. So, to keep [`controls`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#flet.ReorderableListView.controls)
 list in sync with the UI, reorder it accordingly inside your `on_reorder` event handler.

Example

    `[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#__codelineno-0-1)     def handle_reorder(e: ft.OnReorderEvent):        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#__codelineno-0-2)         rlv = e.control        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#__codelineno-0-3)         moved_item = rlv.controls.pop(e.old_index)  # Remove the reordered item from its old position        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#__codelineno-0-4)         rlv.controls.insert(e.new_index, moved_item)  # Insert the reordered item into its new position        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#__codelineno-0-5)         rlv.update()`

### on\_reorder\_end `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#flet.ReorderableListView.on_reorder_end "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#__codelineno-0-1) on_reorder_end: [EventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.EventHandler) [[OnReorderEvent](https://docs.flet.dev/types/onreorderevent/#flet.OnReorderEvent) ] | None = None`

Called when the dragged [`controls`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#flet.ReorderableListView.controls)
 item is dropped.

### on\_reorder\_start `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#flet.ReorderableListView.on_reorder_start "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#__codelineno-0-1) on_reorder_start: [EventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.EventHandler) [[OnReorderEvent](https://docs.flet.dev/types/onreorderevent/#flet.OnReorderEvent) ] | None = None`

Called when a [`controls`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/reorderablelistview/#flet.ReorderableListView.controls)
 item drag has started.

Back to top

---

# IconButton - Flet

 

[Skip to content](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#flet.IconButton)

IconButton
==========

An icon button is a round button with an icon in the middle that reacts to touches by filling with color (ink).

Icon buttons are commonly used in the toolbars, but they can be used in many other places as well.

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#__codelineno-0-1) ft.IconButton(icon=ft.Icons.FAVORITE, icon_color=ft.Colors.PRIMARY)`

[![IconButton](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/material/golden/macos/icon_button/image_for_docs.png)](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/material/golden/macos/icon_button/image_for_docs.png)

Basic IconButton

Inherits: `[LayoutControl](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/layoutcontrol/#flet.LayoutControl) `, `[AdaptiveControl](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/adaptivecontrol/#flet.AdaptiveControl) `

Properties

*   **`[alignment](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#flet.IconButton.alignment) `**(`[Alignment](https://docs.flet.dev/types/alignment/#flet.Alignment)  | None`) –
    
    Defines how the icon is positioned within this button.
    
*   **`[autofocus](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#flet.IconButton.autofocus) `**(`[bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool) `) –
    
    Whether this control will be provided initial focus. If there is more than one control on a page with autofocus set, then the first one added to the page will get focus.
    
*   **`[bgcolor](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#flet.IconButton.bgcolor) `**(`[ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None`) –
    
    The background color of this button.
    
*   **`[disabled_color](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#flet.IconButton.disabled_color) `**(`[ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None`) –
    
    The color to use for the icon inside the button when disabled.
    
*   **`[enable_feedback](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#flet.IconButton.enable_feedback) `**(`[bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)  | None`) –
    
    Whether detected gestures should provide acoustic and/or haptic feedback.
    
*   **`[focus_color](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#flet.IconButton.focus_color) `**(`[ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None`) –
    
    The color of this button when in focus.
    
*   **`[highlight_color](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#flet.IconButton.highlight_color) `**(`[ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None`) –
    
    The color of this button when it is pressed.
    
*   **`[hover_color](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#flet.IconButton.hover_color) `**(`[ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None`) –
    
    The color of this button when hovered.
    
*   **`[icon](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#flet.IconButton.icon) `**(`[IconDataOrControl](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.IconDataOrControl)  | None`) –
    
    An icon to be shown in this button.
    
*   **`[icon_color](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#flet.IconButton.icon_color) `**(`[ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None`) –
    
    The foreground color of the [`icon`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#flet.IconButton.icon)
    .
    
*   **`[icon_size](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#flet.IconButton.icon_size) `**(`[Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None`) –
    
    The [`icon`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#flet.IconButton.icon)
    's size in virtual pixels.
    
*   **`[mouse_cursor](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#flet.IconButton.mouse_cursor) `**(`[MouseCursor](https://docs.flet.dev/types/mousecursor/#flet.MouseCursor)  | None`) –
    
    The cursor to be displayed when a mouse pointer enters or is hovering over this control.
    
*   **`[padding](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#flet.IconButton.padding) `**(`[PaddingValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.PaddingValue)  | None`) –
    
    Defines the padding around this button. The entire padded icon will react to input gestures.
    
*   **`[selected](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#flet.IconButton.selected) `**(`[bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)  | None`) –
    
    The optional selection state of this button.
    
*   **`[selected_icon](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#flet.IconButton.selected_icon) `**(`[IconDataOrControl](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.IconDataOrControl)  | None`) –
    
    The icon to be shown in this button for the 'selected' state.
    
*   **`[selected_icon_color](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#flet.IconButton.selected_icon_color) `**(`[ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None`) –
    
    The icon color for the 'selected' state of this button.
    
*   **`[size_constraints](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#flet.IconButton.size_constraints) `**(`[BoxConstraints](https://docs.flet.dev/types/boxconstraints/#flet.BoxConstraints)  | None`) –
    
    Size constraints for this button.
    
*   **`[splash_color](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#flet.IconButton.splash_color) `**(`[ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None`) –
    
    The primary color of this button when it is in the pressed state.
    
*   **`[splash_radius](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#flet.IconButton.splash_radius) `**(`[Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None`) –
    
    The splash radius.
    
*   **`[style](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#flet.IconButton.style) `**(`[ButtonStyle](https://docs.flet.dev/types/buttonstyle/#flet.ButtonStyle)  | None`) –
    
    Customizes this button's appearance.
    
*   **`[url](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#flet.IconButton.url) `**(`[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)  | [Url](https://docs.flet.dev/types/url/#flet.Url)  | None`) –
    
    The URL to open when this button is clicked.
    
*   **`[visual_density](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#flet.IconButton.visual_density) `**(`[VisualDensity](https://docs.flet.dev/types/visualdensity/#flet.VisualDensity)  | None`) –
    
    Defines how compact this button's layout will be.
    

Events

*   **`[on_blur](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#flet.IconButton.on_blur) `**(`[ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[IconButton](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#flet.IconButton) ] | None`) –
    
    Called when this button has lost focus.
    
*   **`[on_click](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#flet.IconButton.on_click) `**(`[ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[IconButton](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#flet.IconButton) ] | None`) –
    
    Called when a user clicks this button.
    
*   **`[on_focus](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#flet.IconButton.on_focus) `**(`[ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[IconButton](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#flet.IconButton) ] | None`) –
    
    Called when this button has received focus.
    
*   **`[on_hover](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#flet.IconButton.on_hover) `**(`[ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[IconButton](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#flet.IconButton) ] | None`) –
    
    Called when the button is hovered.
    
*   **`[on_long_press](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#flet.IconButton.on_long_press) `**(`[ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[IconButton](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#flet.IconButton) ] | None`) –
    
    Called when the button is long-pressed.
    

Methods

*   **`[focus](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#flet.IconButton.focus) `** –
    
    Moves focus to this button.
    

Examples[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#examples "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[Live example](https://flet-controls-gallery.fly.dev/buttons/iconbutton)

### Handling clicks[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#handling-clicks "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#__codelineno-0-1) import flet as ft [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#__codelineno-0-2) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#__codelineno-0-3)[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#__codelineno-0-4) def main(page: ft.Page):     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#__codelineno-0-5)     page.title = "IconButton Example" [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#__codelineno-0-6)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#__codelineno-0-7)     def button_clicked(e: ft.Event[ft.IconButton]):        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#__codelineno-0-8)         button.data += 1        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#__codelineno-0-9)         message.value = f"Button clicked {button.data} time(s)"        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#__codelineno-0-10)         page.update() [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#__codelineno-0-11)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#__codelineno-0-12)     page.add(        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#__codelineno-0-13)         button := ft.IconButton(            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#__codelineno-0-14)             icon=ft.Icons.PLAY_CIRCLE_FILL_OUTLINED,            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#__codelineno-0-15)             data=0,            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#__codelineno-0-16)             on_click=button_clicked,        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#__codelineno-0-17)         ),        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#__codelineno-0-18)         message := ft.Text(),    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#__codelineno-0-19)     ) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#__codelineno-0-20) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#__codelineno-0-21)[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#__codelineno-0-22) ft.run(main)`

[![handling-clicks](https://docs.flet.dev/examples/controls/icon_button/media/handling_clicks.gif)](https://docs.flet.dev/examples/controls/icon_button/media/handling_clicks.gif)

selected_icon=ft.Icons.BATTERY_FULL,            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#__codelineno-1-18)             scale=5, Properties[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#flet.IconButton-properties "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### alignment `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#flet.IconButton.alignment "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#__codelineno-0-1) alignment: [Alignment](https://docs.flet.dev/types/alignment/#flet.Alignment)  | None = None`

Defines how the icon is positioned within this button.

Defaults to [`Alignment.CENTER`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/alignment/#flet.Alignment.CENTER)
.

### autofocus `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#flet.IconButton.autofocus "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#__codelineno-0-1) autofocus: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)  = False`

Whether this control will be provided initial focus. If there is more than one control on a page with autofocus set, then the first one added to the page will get focus.

### bgcolor `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#flet.IconButton.bgcolor "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#__codelineno-0-1) bgcolor: [ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None = [field](https://docs.python.org/3/library/dataclasses.html#dataclasses.field "<code>dataclasses.field</code>") (     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#__codelineno-0-2)     default=None, metadata={"skip": True} [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#__codelineno-0-3) )`

The background color of this button.

### disabled\_color `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#flet.IconButton.disabled_color "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#__codelineno-0-1) disabled_color: [ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None = None`

The color to use for the icon inside the button when disabled.

### enable\_feedback `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#flet.IconButton.enable_feedback "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#__codelineno-0-1) enable_feedback: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)  | None = None`

Whether detected gestures should provide acoustic and/or haptic feedback. On Android, for example, setting this to `True` produce a click sound and a long-press will produce a short vibration.

### focus\_color `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#flet.IconButton.focus_color "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#__codelineno-0-1) focus_color: [ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None = None`

The color of this button when in focus.

### highlight\_color `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#flet.IconButton.highlight_color "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#__codelineno-0-1) highlight_color: [ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None = None`

The color of this button when it is pressed. The highlight fades in quickly as this button is pressed/held down.

### hover\_color `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#flet.IconButton.hover_color "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#__codelineno-0-1) hover_color: [ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None = None`

The color of this button when hovered.

### icon `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#flet.IconButton.icon "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#__codelineno-0-1) icon: [IconDataOrControl](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.IconDataOrControl)  | None = None`

An icon to be shown in this button.

### icon\_color `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#flet.IconButton.icon_color "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#__codelineno-0-1) icon_color: [ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None = None`

The foreground color of the [`icon`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#flet.IconButton.icon)
.

### icon\_size `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#flet.IconButton.icon_size "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#__codelineno-0-1) icon_size: [Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None = None`

The [`icon`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#flet.IconButton.icon)
's size in virtual pixels.

Defaults to `24`.

### mouse\_cursor `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#flet.IconButton.mouse_cursor "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#__codelineno-0-1) mouse_cursor: [MouseCursor](https://docs.flet.dev/types/mousecursor/#flet.MouseCursor)  | None = [field](https://docs.python.org/3/library/dataclasses.html#dataclasses.field "<code>dataclasses.field</code>") (     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#__codelineno-0-2)     default=None, metadata={"skip": True} [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#__codelineno-0-3) )`

The cursor to be displayed when a mouse pointer enters or is hovering over this control.

### padding `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#flet.IconButton.padding "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#__codelineno-0-1) padding: [PaddingValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.PaddingValue)  | None = None`

Defines the padding around this button. The entire padded icon will react to input gestures.

Defaults to `Padding.all(8)`.

### selected `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#flet.IconButton.selected "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#__codelineno-0-1) selected: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)  | None = None`

The optional selection state of this button.

If this property is not set, the button will behave as a normal push button, otherwise, the button will toggle between showing [`icon`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#flet.IconButton.icon)
 (when `False`), and [`selected_icon`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#flet.IconButton.selected_icon)
 (when `True`).

### selected\_icon `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#flet.IconButton.selected_icon "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#__codelineno-0-1) selected_icon: [IconDataOrControl](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.IconDataOrControl)  | None = None`

The icon to be shown in this button for the 'selected' state.

### selected\_icon\_color `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#flet.IconButton.selected_icon_color "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#__codelineno-0-1) selected_icon_color: [ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None = None`

The icon color for the 'selected' state of this button.

### size\_constraints `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#flet.IconButton.size_constraints "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#__codelineno-0-1) size_constraints: [BoxConstraints](https://docs.flet.dev/types/boxconstraints/#flet.BoxConstraints)  | None = None`

Size constraints for this button.

### splash\_color `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#flet.IconButton.splash_color "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#__codelineno-0-1) splash_color: [ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None = None`

The primary color of this button when it is in the pressed state.

### splash\_radius `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#flet.IconButton.splash_radius "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#__codelineno-0-1) splash_radius: [Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None = None`

The splash radius.

Note

This value is honoured only when in Material 2 ([`Theme.use_material3`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/theme/#flet.Theme.use_material3)
 is `False`).

Raises:

*   `[ValueError](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ValueError) ` –
    
    If it is not strictly greater than `0`.
    

### style `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#flet.IconButton.style "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#__codelineno-0-1) style: [ButtonStyle](https://docs.flet.dev/types/buttonstyle/#flet.ButtonStyle)  | None = None`

Customizes this button's appearance.

Note

*   Only honoured in Material 3 design ([`Theme.use_material3`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/theme/#flet.Theme.use_material3)
     is `True`).
*   If [`Theme.use_material3`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/theme/#flet.Theme.use_material3)
     is `True`, any parameters defined in style will be overridden by the corresponding parameters in this button. For example, if icon button [`visual_density`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#flet.IconButton.visual_density)
     is set to [`VisualDensity.STANDARD`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/visualdensity/#flet.VisualDensity.STANDARD)
     and style's [`visual_density`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/buttonstyle/#flet.ButtonStyle.visual_density)
     is set to [`VisualDensity.COMPACT`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/visualdensity/#flet.VisualDensity.COMPACT)
    , [`VisualDensity.STANDARD`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/visualdensity/#flet.VisualDensity.STANDARD)
     will be used.

### url `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#flet.IconButton.url "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#__codelineno-0-1) url: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)  | [Url](https://docs.flet.dev/types/url/#flet.Url)  | None = None`

The URL to open when this button is clicked.

Additionally, if [`on_click`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#flet.IconButton.on_click)
 event callback is provided, it is fired after that.

### visual\_density `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#flet.IconButton.visual_density "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#__codelineno-0-1) visual_density: [VisualDensity](https://docs.flet.dev/types/visualdensity/#flet.VisualDensity)  | None = [field](https://docs.python.org/3/library/dataclasses.html#dataclasses.field "<code>dataclasses.field</code>") (     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#__codelineno-0-2)     default=None, metadata={"skip": True} [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#__codelineno-0-3) )`

Defines how compact this button's layout will be.

Events[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#flet.IconButton-events "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### on\_blur `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#flet.IconButton.on_blur "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#__codelineno-0-1) on_blur: [ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[IconButton](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#flet.IconButton) ] | None = None`

Called when this button has lost focus.

### on\_click `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#flet.IconButton.on_click "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#__codelineno-0-1) on_click: [ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[IconButton](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#flet.IconButton) ] | None = None`

Called when a user clicks this button.

### on\_focus `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#flet.IconButton.on_focus "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#__codelineno-0-1) on_focus: [ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[IconButton](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#flet.IconButton) ] | None = None`

Called when this button has received focus.

### on\_hover `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#flet.IconButton.on_hover "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#__codelineno-0-1) on_hover: [ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[IconButton](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#flet.IconButton) ] | None = None`

Called when the button is hovered.

### on\_long\_press `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#flet.IconButton.on_long_press "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#__codelineno-0-1) on_long_press: [ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[IconButton](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#flet.IconButton) ] | None = None`

Called when the button is long-pressed.

Methods[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#flet.IconButton-methods "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### focus `async` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#flet.IconButton.focus "Permanent link")

# Segmentedbutton - Flet

 

[Skip to content](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/segmentedbutton/#flet.SegmentedButton)

Segmentedbutton
===============

A segmented button control.

[![SegmentedButton](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/material/golden/macos/segmented_button/image_for_docs.png)](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/material/golden/macos/segmented_button/image_for_docs.png)

Basic segmented button

Inherits: `[LayoutControl](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/layoutcontrol/#flet.LayoutControl) `

Properties

*   **`[allow_empty_selection](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/segmentedbutton/#flet.SegmentedButton.allow_empty_selection) `**(`[bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool) `) –
    
    A boolean value that indicates if having no selected segments is allowed.
    
*   **`[allow_multiple_selection](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/segmentedbutton/#flet.SegmentedButton.allow_multiple_selection) `**(`[bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool) `) –
    
    A boolean value that indicates if multiple segments can be selected at one time.
    
*   **`[direction](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/segmentedbutton/#flet.SegmentedButton.direction) `**(`[Axis](https://docs.flet.dev/types/axis/#flet.Axis)  | None`) –
    
    The orientation of the button's `segments`.
    
*   **`[padding](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/segmentedbutton/#flet.SegmentedButton.padding) `**(`[PaddingValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.PaddingValue)  | None`) –
    
    Defines the button's size and padding. If specified, the button expands to fill its parent's space with this padding.
    
*   **`[segments](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/segmentedbutton/#flet.SegmentedButton.segments) `**(`[list](https://docs.python.org/3/glossary.html#term-list) [[Segment](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/segment/#flet.Segment) ]`) –
    
    The segments of this button.
    
*   **`[selected](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/segmentedbutton/#flet.SegmentedButton.selected) `**(`[list](https://docs.python.org/3/glossary.html#term-list) [[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str) ]`) –
    
    A set of `Segment.value`s that indicate which segments are selected. It is updated when the user (un)selects a segment.
    
*   **`[selected_icon](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/segmentedbutton/#flet.SegmentedButton.selected_icon) `**(`[IconDataOrControl](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.IconDataOrControl)  | None`) –
    
    An `Icon` control that is used to indicate a segment is selected.
    
*   **`[show_selected_icon](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/segmentedbutton/#flet.SegmentedButton.show_selected_icon) `**(`[bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool) `) –
    
    A boolean value that indicates if the `selected_icon` is displayed on the `selected` segments.
    
*   **`[style](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/segmentedbutton/#flet.SegmentedButton.style) `**(`[ButtonStyle](https://docs.flet.dev/types/buttonstyle/#flet.ButtonStyle)  | None`) –
    
    Customizes this button's appearance.
    

Events

*   **`[on_change](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/segmentedbutton/#flet.SegmentedButton.on_change) `**(`[ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[SegmentedButton](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/segmentedbutton/#flet.SegmentedButton) ] | None`) –
    
    Called when the selection changes.
    

Examples[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/segmentedbutton/#examples "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[Live example](https://flet-controls-gallery.fly.dev/buttons/segmentedbutton)

### Basic Example[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/segmentedbutton/#basic-example "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/segmentedbutton/#__codelineno-0-1) import flet as ft [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/segmentedbutton/#__codelineno-0-2) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/segmentedbutton/#__codelineno-0-3)[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/segmentedbutton/#__codelineno-0-4) def main(page: ft.Page):     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/segmentedbutton/#__codelineno-0-5)     def handle_selection_change(e: ft.Event[ft.SegmentedButton]):        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/segmentedbutton/#__codelineno-0-6)         print(e) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/segmentedbutton/#__codelineno-0-7)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/segmentedbutton/#__codelineno-0-8)     page.add(        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/segmentedbutton/#__codelineno-0-9)         ft.SegmentedButton(            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/segmentedbutton/#__codelineno-0-10)             on_change=handle_selection_change,            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/segmentedbutton/#__codelineno-0-11) [![single_multiple_selection.png](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/material/golden/macos/segmented_button/single_multiple_selection.png)](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/material/golden/macos/segmented_button/single_multiple_selection.png)

Properties[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/segmentedbutton/#flet.SegmentedButton-properties "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### allow\_empty\_selection `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/segmentedbutton/#flet.SegmentedButton.allow_empty_selection "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/segmentedbutton/#__codelineno-0-1) allow_empty_selection: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)  = False`

A boolean value that indicates if having no selected segments is allowed.

If `True`, then it is acceptable for none of the segments to be selected and also that `selected` can be empty.

If `False` (the default), there must be at least one segment selected. If the user taps on the only selected segment it will not be deselected, and `on_change` will not be called.

Raises:

*   `[ValueError](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ValueError) ` –
    
    If [`selected`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/segmentedbutton/#flet.SegmentedButton.selected)
     is empty while [`allow_empty_selection`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/segmentedbutton/#flet.SegmentedButton.allow_empty_selection)
     is `False`.
    

### allow\_multiple\_selection `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/segmentedbutton/#flet.SegmentedButton.allow_multiple_selection "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/segmentedbutton/#__codelineno-0-1) allow_multiple_selection: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)  = False`

A boolean value that indicates if multiple segments can be selected at one time.

If `True`, more than one segment can be selected. When selecting a segment, the other `selected` segments will stay selected. Selecting an already selected segment will unselect it.

If `False` (the default), only one segment may be selected at a time. When a segment is selected, any previously selected segment will be unselected.

Raises:

*   `[ValueError](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ValueError) ` –
    
    If [`selected`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/segmentedbutton/#flet.SegmentedButton.selected)
     has more than one item while [`allow_multiple_selection`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/segmentedbutton/#flet.SegmentedButton.allow_multiple_selection)
     is `False`.
    

### direction `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/segmentedbutton/#flet.SegmentedButton.direction "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/segmentedbutton/#__codelineno-0-1) direction: [Axis](https://docs.flet.dev/types/axis/#flet.Axis)  | None = None`

The orientation of the button's `segments`.

Defaults to `Axis.HORIZONTAL`.

### padding `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/segmentedbutton/#flet.SegmentedButton.padding "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/segmentedbutton/#__codelineno-0-1) padding: [PaddingValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.PaddingValue)  | None = None`

Defines the button's size and padding. If specified, the button expands to fill its parent's space with this padding.

When `None`, the button adopts its intrinsic content size.

### segments `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/segmentedbutton/#flet.SegmentedButton.segments "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/segmentedbutton/#__codelineno-0-1) segments: [list](https://docs.python.org/3/glossary.html#term-list) [[Segment](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/segment/#flet.Segment) ]`

The segments of this button.

Raises:

*   `[ValueError](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ValueError) ` –
    
    If it does not contain at least one visible `Segment`.
    

### selected `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/segmentedbutton/#flet.SegmentedButton.selected "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/segmentedbutton/#__codelineno-0-1) selected: [list](https://docs.python.org/3/glossary.html#term-list) [[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str) ] = [field](https://docs.python.org/3/library/dataclasses.html#dataclasses.field "<code>dataclasses.field</code>") (default_factory=[list](https://docs.python.org/3/glossary.html#term-list) )`

A set of `Segment.value`s that indicate which segments are selected. It is updated when the user (un)selects a segment.

Raises:

*   `[ValueError](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ValueError) ` –
    
    If [`selected`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/segmentedbutton/#flet.SegmentedButton.selected)
     violates the constraints defined by [`allow_empty_selection`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/segmentedbutton/#flet.SegmentedButton.allow_empty_selection)
     or [`allow_multiple_selection`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/segmentedbutton/#flet.SegmentedButton.allow_multiple_selection)
    .
    

### selected\_icon `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/segmentedbutton/#flet.SegmentedButton.selected_icon "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/segmentedbutton/#__codelineno-0-1) selected_icon: [IconDataOrControl](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.IconDataOrControl)  | None = None`

An `Icon` control that is used to indicate a segment is selected.

If `show_selected_icon` is `True` then for `selected` segments this icon will be shown before the `Segment.label`, replacing the `Segment.icon` if it is specified.

Defaults to an `Icon` with the `CHECK` icon.

### show\_selected\_icon `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/segmentedbutton/#flet.SegmentedButton.show_selected_icon "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/segmentedbutton/#__codelineno-0-1) show_selected_icon: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)  = True`

A boolean value that indicates if the `selected_icon` is displayed on the `selected` segments.

If `True`, the `selected_icon` will be displayed at the start of the `selected` segments.

If `False`, then the `selected_icon` is not used and will not be displayed on `selected` segments.

### style `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/segmentedbutton/#flet.SegmentedButton.style "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/segmentedbutton/#__codelineno-0-1) style: [ButtonStyle](https://docs.flet.dev/types/buttonstyle/#flet.ButtonStyle)  | None = None`

Customizes this button's appearance.

Events[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/segmentedbutton/#flet.SegmentedButton-events "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### on\_change `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/segmentedbutton/#flet.SegmentedButton.on_change "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/segmentedbutton/#__codelineno-0-1) on_change: [ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[SegmentedButton](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/segmentedbutton/#flet.SegmentedButton) ] | None = (     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/segmentedbutton/#__codelineno-0-2)     None [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/segmentedbutton/#__codelineno-0-3) )`

Called when the selection changes.

The [`data`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/event/#flet.Event.data)
 property of the event handler argument contains a list of strings identifying the selected segments.

Back to top

# PageView - Flet

 

[Skip to content](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pageview/#flet.PageView)

PageView
========

Displays its child [`controls`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pageview/#flet.PageView.controls)
 one page at a time and lets users swipe between them, similar to a carousel.

It is helpful for onboarding flows, photo carousels, or any scenario where content is divided into discrete full-width pages.

Inherits: `[LayoutControl](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/layoutcontrol/#flet.LayoutControl) `

Properties

*   **`[clip_behavior](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pageview/#flet.PageView.clip_behavior) `**(`[ClipBehavior](https://docs.flet.dev/types/clipbehavior/#flet.ClipBehavior) `) –
    
    Defines how pages are clipped if they overflow their bounds.
    
*   **`[controls](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pageview/#flet.PageView.controls) `**(`[list](https://docs.python.org/3/glossary.html#term-list) [[Control](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control) ]`) –
    
    A list of controls to display, one per page, in the order they should appear.
    
*   **`[horizontal](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pageview/#flet.PageView.horizontal) `**(`[bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool) `) –
    
    Whether the pages should be arranged and scrolled horizontally.
    
*   **`[implicit_scrolling](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pageview/#flet.PageView.implicit_scrolling) `**(`[bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool) `) –
    
    Whether to allow adjacent pages to render partially before the user scrolls to them,
    
*   **`[keep_page](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pageview/#flet.PageView.keep_page) `**(`[bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool) `) –
    
    Whether this page view should restore the most recently viewed page when rebuilt.
    
*   **`[pad_ends](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pageview/#flet.PageView.pad_ends) `**(`[bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool) `) –
    
    Whether to add padding to both ends of the list.
    
*   **`[reverse](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pageview/#flet.PageView.reverse) `**(`[bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool) `) –
    
    Whether to reverse the order in which pages are read and swiped.
    
*   **`[selected_index](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pageview/#flet.PageView.selected_index) `**(`[int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int) `) –
    
    The zero-based index of the currently visible page.
    
*   **`[snap](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pageview/#flet.PageView.snap) `**(`[bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool) `) –
    
    Whether the view should snap to exact page boundaries after a drag.
    
*   **`[viewport_fraction](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pageview/#flet.PageView.viewport_fraction) `**(`[Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number) `) –
    
    The fraction of the viewport that each page should occupy in the
    

Events

*   **`[on_change](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pageview/#flet.PageView.on_change) `**(`[ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[PageView](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pageview/#flet.PageView) ] | None`) –
    
    Fired when the visible page changes.
    

Methods

*   **`[go_to_page](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pageview/#flet.PageView.go_to_page) `** –
    
    Animates to the page at `index`.
    
*   **`[jump_to](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pageview/#flet.PageView.jump_to) `** –
    
    Immediately sets the scroll position to the given value, without animation
    
*   **`[jump_to_page](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pageview/#flet.PageView.jump_to_page) `** –
    
    Jumps immediately to the page at `index` without animation, moving the page
    
*   **`[next_page](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pageview/#flet.PageView.next_page) `** –
    
    Animates to the next page. Same as calling
    
*   **`[previous_page](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pageview/#flet.PageView.previous_page) `** –
    
    Animates to the previous page. Same as calling
    

Examples[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pageview/#examples "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pageview/#__codelineno-0-56)     ft.run(main)`

### Programmatic Swipes[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pageview/#programmatic-swipes "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pageview/#__codelineno-1-1) import flet as ft [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pageview/#__codelineno-1-2) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pageview/#__codelineno-1-3)[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pageview/#__codelineno-1-4) def main(page: ft.Page):     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pageview/#__codelineno-1-5)     page.padding = 0 [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pageview/#__codelineno-1-6)         await view.next_page(            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pageview/#__codelineno-1-15)             animation_curve=ft.AnimationCurve.BOUNCE_OUT, Properties[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pageview/#flet.PageView-properties "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### clip\_behavior `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pageview/#flet.PageView.clip_behavior "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pageview/#__codelineno-0-1) clip_behavior: [ClipBehavior](https://docs.flet.dev/types/clipbehavior/#flet.ClipBehavior)  = [HARD_EDGE](https://docs.flet.dev/types/clipbehavior/#flet.ClipBehavior.HARD_EDGE)`

Defines how pages are clipped if they overflow their bounds.

### controls `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pageview/#flet.PageView.controls "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pageview/#__codelineno-0-1) controls: [list](https://docs.python.org/3/glossary.html#term-list) [[Control](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control) ] = [field](https://docs.python.org/3/library/dataclasses.html#dataclasses.field "<code>dataclasses.field</code>") (default_factory=[list](https://docs.python.org/3/glossary.html#term-list) )`

A list of controls to display, one per page, in the order they should appear.

### horizontal `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pageview/#flet.PageView.horizontal "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pageview/#__codelineno-0-1) horizontal: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)  = True`

Whether the pages should be arranged and scrolled horizontally.

`False` implies vertical arrangement and scrolling.

### implicit\_scrolling `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pageview/#flet.PageView.implicit_scrolling "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pageview/#__codelineno-0-1) implicit_scrolling: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)  = False`

Whether to allow adjacent pages to render partially before the user scrolls to them, enabling smoother transitions and improved accessibility by allowing focus to move seamlessly between pages during navigation.

With this flag set to `False`, when accessibility focus reaches the end of the current page and the user attempts to move it to the next element, the focus will traverse to the next widget outside of the page view.

With this flag set to `True`, when accessibility focus reaches the end of the current page and user attempts to move it to the next element, focus will traverse to the next page in the page view.

### keep\_page `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pageview/#flet.PageView.keep_page "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pageview/#__codelineno-0-1) keep_page: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)  = True`

Whether this page view should restore the most recently viewed page when rebuilt.

### pad\_ends `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pageview/#flet.PageView.pad_ends "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pageview/#__codelineno-0-1) pad_ends: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)  = True`

Whether to add padding to both ends of the list.

If this is set to `True` and [`viewport_fraction`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pageview/#flet.PageView.viewport_fraction)
 < `1.0`, padding will be added before the first page and after the last page so they snap to the center of the viewport when scrolled all the way to the start or end.

Note

If [`viewport_fraction`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pageview/#flet.PageView.viewport_fraction)
 >= 1.0, this property has no effect.

### reverse `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pageview/#flet.PageView.reverse "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pageview/#__codelineno-0-1) reverse: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)  = False`

Whether to reverse the order in which pages are read and swiped.

For example, if the reading direction is left-to-right and [`horizontal`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pageview/#flet.PageView.horizontal)
 is `True`, then this page view scrolls from left to right when `reverse` is `False` and from right to left when `reverse` is `True`.

Similarly, if [`horizontal`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pageview/#flet.PageView.horizontal)
 is `False`, then this page view scrolls from top to bottom when `reverse` is `False` and from bottom to top when `reverse` is `True`.

### selected\_index `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pageview/#flet.PageView.selected_index "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pageview/#__codelineno-0-1) selected_index: [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int)  = 0`

The zero-based index of the currently visible page.

Changing it later on (followed by [`update()`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basecontrol/#flet.BaseControl.update)
) jumps to the specified page without animation.

Raises:

*   `[ValueError](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ValueError) ` –
    
    If it is not greater than or equal to `0`.
    

### snap `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pageview/#flet.PageView.snap "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pageview/#__codelineno-0-1) snap: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)  = True`

Whether the view should snap to exact page boundaries after a drag.

If the [`pad_ends`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pageview/#flet.PageView.pad_ends)
 is `False` and [`viewport_fraction`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pageview/#flet.PageView.viewport_fraction)
 < `1.0`, the page will snap to the beginning of the viewport; otherwise, the page will snap to the center of the viewport.

### viewport\_fraction `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pageview/#flet.PageView.viewport_fraction "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pageview/#__codelineno-0-1) viewport_fraction: [Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  = 1.0`

The fraction of the viewport that each page should occupy in the scrolling direction (see [`horizontal`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pageview/#flet.PageView.horizontal)
).

For example, `1.0` (default), means each page fills the viewport.

Raises:

*   `[ValueError](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ValueError) ` –
    
    If it is not strictly greater than `0`.
    

Events[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pageview/#flet.PageView-events "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### on\_change `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pageview/#flet.PageView.on_change "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pageview/#__codelineno-0-1) on_change: [ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[PageView](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pageview/#flet.PageView) ] | None = None`

Fired when the visible page changes.

The [`data`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/event/#flet.Event.data)
 property of the event argument contains the index of the new page.

Methods[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pageview/#flet.PageView-methods "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### go\_to\_page `async` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pageview/#flet.PageView.go_to_page "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pageview/#__codelineno-0-1) go_to_page(     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pageview/#__codelineno-0-2)     index: [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int) ,    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pageview/#__codelineno-0-3)     animation_duration: [DurationValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.DurationValue)  = DEFAULT_ANIMATION_DURATION,    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pageview/#__codelineno-0-4)     animation_curve: [AnimationCurve](https://docs.flet.dev/types/animationcurve/#flet.AnimationCurve)  = DEFAULT_ANIMATION_CURVE, [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pageview/#__codelineno-0-5) )`

Animates to the page at `index`.

Parameters:

*   **`index`** (`[int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int) `) –
    
    The index of the page to show.
    
*   **`animation_duration`** (`[DurationValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.DurationValue) `, default: `DEFAULT_ANIMATION_DURATION` ) –
    
    Length of the animation.
    
*   **`animation_curve`** (`[AnimationCurve](https://docs.flet.dev/types/animationcurve/#flet.AnimationCurve) `, default: `DEFAULT_ANIMATION_CURVE` ) –
    
    The easing curve of the animation.
    

Raises:

*   `[ValueError](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ValueError) ` –
    
    If `index` is negative.
    

### jump\_to `async` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pageview/#flet.PageView.jump_to "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pageview/#__codelineno-0-1) jump_to(value: [Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number) )`

Immediately sets the scroll position to the given value, without animation and without validating whether the value is within bounds.

Any active scrolling or animation is canceled.

If the scroll position changes, a start/update/end sequence of scroll notifications is dispatched. This method does not generate overscroll notifications.

After the jump, a ballistic activity is initiated if the value is outside the valid scroll range.

Parameters:

*   **`value`** (`[Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number) `) –
    
    The new scroll position.
    

### jump\_to\_page `async` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pageview/#flet.PageView.jump_to_page "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pageview/#__codelineno-0-1) jump_to_page(index: [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int) )`

Jumps immediately to the page at `index` without animation, moving the page position from its current value to the given value without animation nor range checking.

Parameters:

*   **`index`** (`[int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int) `) –
    
    The index of the page to show.
    

Raises:

*   `[ValueError](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ValueError) ` –
    
    If `index` is negative.
    

### next\_page `async` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pageview/#flet.PageView.next_page "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pageview/#__codelineno-0-1) next_page(     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pageview/#__codelineno-0-2)     animation_duration: [DurationValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.DurationValue)  = DEFAULT_ANIMATION_DURATION,    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pageview/#__codelineno-0-3)     animation_curve: [AnimationCurve](https://docs.flet.dev/types/animationcurve/#flet.AnimationCurve)  = DEFAULT_ANIMATION_CURVE, [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pageview/#__codelineno-0-4) )`

Animates to the next page. Same as calling [`go_to_page()`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pageview/#flet.PageView.go_to_page)
 with `selected_index + 1`.

Parameters:

*   **`animation_duration`** (`[DurationValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.DurationValue) `, default: `DEFAULT_ANIMATION_DURATION` ) –
    
    Length of the animation.
    
*   **`animation_curve`** (`[AnimationCurve](https://docs.flet.dev/types/animationcurve/#flet.AnimationCurve) `, default: `DEFAULT_ANIMATION_CURVE` ) –
    
    The easing curve of the animation.
    

### previous\_page `async` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pageview/#flet.PageView.previous_page "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pageview/#__codelineno-0-1) previous_page(     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pageview/#__codelineno-0-2)     animation_duration: [DurationValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.DurationValue)  = DEFAULT_ANIMATION_DURATION,    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pageview/#__codelineno-0-3)     animation_curve: [AnimationCurve](https://docs.flet.dev/types/animationcurve/#flet.AnimationCurve)  = DEFAULT_ANIMATION_CURVE, [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pageview/#__codelineno-0-4) )`

Animates to the previous page. Same as calling [`go_to_page()`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/pageview/#flet.PageView.go_to_page)
 with `selected_index - 1`.

Parameters:

*   **`animation_duration`** (`[DurationValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.DurationValue) `, default: `DEFAULT_ANIMATION_DURATION` ) –
    
    Length of the animation.
    
*   **`animation_curve`** (`[AnimationCurve](https://docs.flet.dev/types/animationcurve/#flet.AnimationCurve) `, default: `DEFAULT_ANIMATION_CURVE` ) –
    
    The easing curve of the animation.
