# InteractiveViewer - Flet

 

[Skip to content](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/interactiveviewer/#flet.InteractiveViewer)

InteractiveViewer
=================

Allows you to pan, zoom, and rotate its [`content`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/interactiveviewer/#flet.InteractiveViewer.content)
.

Inherits: `[LayoutControl](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/layoutcontrol/#flet.LayoutControl) `

Properties

*   **`[alignment](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/interactiveviewer/#flet.InteractiveViewer.alignment) `**(`[Alignment](https://docs.flet.dev/types/alignment/#flet.Alignment)  | None`) –
    
    The alignment of the [`content`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/interactiveviewer/#flet.InteractiveViewer.content)
     within this viewer.
    
*   **`[boundary_margin](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/interactiveviewer/#flet.InteractiveViewer.boundary_margin) `**(`[MarginValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.MarginValue) `) –
    
    A margin for the visible boundaries of the [`content`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/interactiveviewer/#flet.InteractiveViewer.content)
    .
    
*   **`[clip_behavior](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/interactiveviewer/#flet.InteractiveViewer.clip_behavior) `**(`[ClipBehavior](https://docs.flet.dev/types/clipbehavior/#flet.ClipBehavior) `) –
    
    Defines how to clip the [`content`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/interactiveviewer/#flet.InteractiveViewer.content)
    .
    
*   **`[constrained](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/interactiveviewer/#flet.InteractiveViewer.constrained) `**(`[bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool) `) –
    
    Whether the normal size constraints at this point in the control tree are applied to the [`content`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/interactiveviewer/#flet.InteractiveViewer.content)
    .
    
*   **`[content](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/interactiveviewer/#flet.InteractiveViewer.content) `**(`[Control](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control) `) –
    
    The `Control` to be transformed.
    
*   **`[interaction_end_friction_coefficient](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/interactiveviewer/#flet.InteractiveViewer.interaction_end_friction_coefficient) `**(`[Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number) `) –
    
    Changes the deceleration behavior after a gesture.
    
*   **`[interaction_update_interval](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/interactiveviewer/#flet.InteractiveViewer.interaction_update_interval) `**(`[int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int) `) –
    
    The interval (in milliseconds) at which the [`on_interaction_update`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/interactiveviewer/#flet.InteractiveViewer.on_interaction_update)
     event is fired.
    
*   **`[max_scale](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/interactiveviewer/#flet.InteractiveViewer.max_scale) `**(`[Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number) `) –
    
    The maximum allowed scale.
    
*   **`[min_scale](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/interactiveviewer/#flet.InteractiveViewer.min_scale) `**(`[Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number) `) –
    
    The minimum allowed scale.
    
*   **`[pan_enabled](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/interactiveviewer/#flet.InteractiveViewer.pan_enabled) `**(`[bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool) `) –
    
    Whether panning is enabled.
    
*   **`[scale_enabled](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/interactiveviewer/#flet.InteractiveViewer.scale_enabled) `**(`[bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool) `) –
    
    Whether scaling is enabled.
    
*   **`[scale_factor](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/interactiveviewer/#flet.InteractiveViewer.scale_factor) `**(`[Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number) `) –
    
    The amount of scale to be performed per pointer scroll.
    
*   **`[trackpad_scroll_causes_scale](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/interactiveviewer/#flet.InteractiveViewer.trackpad_scroll_causes_scale) `**(`[bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool) `) –
    
    Whether scrolling up/down on a trackpad should cause scaling instead of panning.
    

Events

*   **`[on_interaction_end](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/interactiveviewer/#flet.InteractiveViewer.on_interaction_end) `**(`[EventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.EventHandler) [[ScaleEndEvent](https://docs.flet.dev/types/scaleendevent/#flet.ScaleEndEvent) [[InteractiveViewer](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/interactiveviewer/#flet.InteractiveViewer) ]] | None`) –
    
    Called when the user ends a pan or scale gesture.
    
*   **`[on_interaction_start](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/interactiveviewer/#flet.InteractiveViewer.on_interaction_start) `**(`[EventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.EventHandler) [[ScaleStartEvent](https://docs.flet.dev/types/scalestartevent/#flet.ScaleStartEvent) [[InteractiveViewer](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/interactiveviewer/#flet.InteractiveViewer) ]] | None`) –
    
    Called when the user begins a pan or scale gesture.
    
*   **`[on_interaction_update](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/interactiveviewer/#flet.InteractiveViewer.on_interaction_update) `**(`[EventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.EventHandler) [[ScaleUpdateEvent](https://docs.flet.dev/types/scaleupdateevent/#flet.ScaleUpdateEvent) [[InteractiveViewer](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/interactiveviewer/#flet.InteractiveViewer) ]] | None`) –
    
    Called when the user updates a pan or scale gesture.
    

Methods

*   **`[pan](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/interactiveviewer/#flet.InteractiveViewer.pan) `** –
    
    Translates the current transform matrix.
    
*   **`[reset](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/interactiveviewer/#flet.InteractiveViewer.reset) `** –
    
    Resets the current transform matrix to identity.
    
*   **`[restore_state](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/interactiveviewer/#flet.InteractiveViewer.restore_state) `** –
    
    Restores the transform matrix previously captured by
    
*   **`[save_state](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/interactiveviewer/#flet.InteractiveViewer.save_state) `** –
    
    Saves a snapshot of the current transform matrix.
    
*   **`[zoom](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/interactiveviewer/#flet.InteractiveViewer.zoom) `** –
    
    Applies multiplicative zoom to the current transform.

Properties[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/interactiveviewer/#flet.InteractiveViewer-properties "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### alignment `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/interactiveviewer/#flet.InteractiveViewer.alignment "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/interactiveviewer/#__codelineno-0-1) alignment: [Alignment](https://docs.flet.dev/types/alignment/#flet.Alignment)  | None = None`

The alignment of the [`content`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/interactiveviewer/#flet.InteractiveViewer.content)
 within this viewer.

### boundary\_margin `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/interactiveviewer/#flet.InteractiveViewer.boundary_margin "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/interactiveviewer/#__codelineno-0-1) boundary_margin: [MarginValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.MarginValue)  = [field](https://docs.python.org/3/library/dataclasses.html#dataclasses.field "<code>dataclasses.field</code>") (     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/interactiveviewer/#__codelineno-0-2)     default_factory=lambda: [all](https://docs.flet.dev/types/margin/#flet.Margin.all) (0) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/interactiveviewer/#__codelineno-0-3) )`

A margin for the visible boundaries of the [`content`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/interactiveviewer/#flet.InteractiveViewer.content)
.

Any transformation that results in the viewport being able to view outside of the boundaries will be stopped at the boundary. The boundaries do not rotate with the rest of the scene, so they are always aligned with the viewport.

To produce no boundaries at all, pass an infinite value.

Defaults to `Margin.all(0)`, which results in boundaries that are the exact same size and position as the [`content`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/interactiveviewer/#flet.InteractiveViewer.content)
.

### clip\_behavior `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/interactiveviewer/#flet.InteractiveViewer.clip_behavior "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/interactiveviewer/#__codelineno-0-1) clip_behavior: [ClipBehavior](https://docs.flet.dev/types/clipbehavior/#flet.ClipBehavior)  = [HARD_EDGE](https://docs.flet.dev/types/clipbehavior/#flet.ClipBehavior.HARD_EDGE)`

Defines how to clip the [`content`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/interactiveviewer/#flet.InteractiveViewer.content)
.

If set to [`ClipBehavior.NONE`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/clipbehavior/#flet.ClipBehavior.NONE)
, the [`content`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/interactiveviewer/#flet.InteractiveViewer.content)
 can visually overflow the bounds of this `InteractiveViewer`, but gesture events (such as pan or zoom) will only be recognized within the viewer's area. Ensure this `InteractiveViewer` is sized appropriately when using [`ClipBehavior.NONE`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/clipbehavior/#flet.ClipBehavior.NONE)
.

### constrained `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/interactiveviewer/#flet.InteractiveViewer.constrained "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/interactiveviewer/#__codelineno-0-1) constrained: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)  = True`

Whether the normal size constraints at this point in the control tree are applied to the [`content`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/interactiveviewer/#flet.InteractiveViewer.content)
.

If set to `False`, then the content will be given infinite constraints. This is often useful when a content should be bigger than this `InteractiveViewer`.

For example, for a content which is bigger than the viewport but can be panned to reveal parts that were initially offscreen, `constrained` must be set to `False` to allow it to size itself properly. If `constrained` is `True` and the content can only size itself to the viewport, then areas initially outside of the viewport will not be able to receive user interaction events. If experiencing regions of the content that are not receptive to user gestures, make sure `constrained` is `False` and the content is sized properly.

### content `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/interactiveviewer/#flet.InteractiveViewer.content "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/interactiveviewer/#__codelineno-0-1) content: [Control](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control)`

The `Control` to be transformed.

Raises:

*   `[ValueError](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ValueError) ` –
    
    If it is not visible.
    

### interaction\_end\_friction\_coefficient `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/interactiveviewer/#flet.InteractiveViewer.interaction_end_friction_coefficient "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/interactiveviewer/#__codelineno-0-1) interaction_end_friction_coefficient: [Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  = 1.35e-05`

Changes the deceleration behavior after a gesture.

Raises:

*   `[ValueError](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ValueError) ` –
    
    If it is not strictly greater than `0`.
    

### interaction\_update\_interval `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/interactiveviewer/#flet.InteractiveViewer.interaction_update_interval "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/interactiveviewer/#__codelineno-0-1) interaction_update_interval: [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int)  = 200`

The interval (in milliseconds) at which the [`on_interaction_update`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/interactiveviewer/#flet.InteractiveViewer.on_interaction_update)
 event is fired.

### max\_scale `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/interactiveviewer/#flet.InteractiveViewer.max_scale "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/interactiveviewer/#__codelineno-0-1) max_scale: [Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  = 2.5`

The maximum allowed scale.

Raises:

*   `[ValueError](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ValueError) ` –
    
    If it is not strictly greater than `0`.
    
*   `[ValueError](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ValueError) ` –
    
    If it is not greater than or equal to [`min_scale`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/interactiveviewer/#flet.InteractiveViewer.min_scale)
    .
    

### min\_scale `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/interactiveviewer/#flet.InteractiveViewer.min_scale "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/interactiveviewer/#__codelineno-0-1) min_scale: [Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  = 0.8`

The minimum allowed scale.

The effective scale is limited by the value of [`boundary_margin`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/interactiveviewer/#flet.InteractiveViewer.boundary_margin)
. If scaling would cause the content to be displayed outside the defined boundary, it is prevented. By default, `boundary_margin` is set to `Margin.all(0)`, so scaling below `1.0` is typically not possible unless you increase the `boundary_margin` value.

Raises:

*   `[ValueError](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ValueError) ` –
    
    If it is not strictly greater than `0`.
    
*   `[ValueError](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ValueError) ` –
    
    If it is not less than or equal to [`max_scale`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/interactiveviewer/#flet.InteractiveViewer.max_scale)
    .
    

### pan\_enabled `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/interactiveviewer/#flet.InteractiveViewer.pan_enabled "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/interactiveviewer/#__codelineno-0-1) pan_enabled: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)  = True`

Whether panning is enabled.

### scale\_enabled `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/interactiveviewer/#flet.InteractiveViewer.scale_enabled "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/interactiveviewer/#__codelineno-0-1) scale_enabled: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)  = True`

Whether scaling is enabled.

### scale\_factor `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/interactiveviewer/#flet.InteractiveViewer.scale_factor "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/interactiveviewer/#__codelineno-0-1) scale_factor: [Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  = 200`

The amount of scale to be performed per pointer scroll.

Increasing this value above the default causes scaling to feel slower, while decreasing it causes scaling to feel faster.

Note

Has effect only on pointer device scrolling, not pinch to zoom.

### trackpad\_scroll\_causes\_scale `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/interactiveviewer/#flet.InteractiveViewer.trackpad_scroll_causes_scale "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/interactiveviewer/#__codelineno-0-1) trackpad_scroll_causes_scale: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)  = False`

Whether scrolling up/down on a trackpad should cause scaling instead of panning.

Events[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/interactiveviewer/#flet.InteractiveViewer-events "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### on\_interaction\_end `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/interactiveviewer/#flet.InteractiveViewer.on_interaction_end "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/interactiveviewer/#__codelineno-0-1) on_interaction_end: (     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/interactiveviewer/#__codelineno-0-2)     [EventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.EventHandler) [[ScaleEndEvent](https://docs.flet.dev/types/scaleendevent/#flet.ScaleEndEvent) [[InteractiveViewer](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/interactiveviewer/#flet.InteractiveViewer) ]] | None [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/interactiveviewer/#__codelineno-0-3) ) = None`

Called when the user ends a pan or scale gesture.

### on\_interaction\_start `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/interactiveviewer/#flet.InteractiveViewer.on_interaction_start "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/interactiveviewer/#__codelineno-0-1) on_interaction_start: (     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/interactiveviewer/#__codelineno-0-2)     [EventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.EventHandler) [[ScaleStartEvent](https://docs.flet.dev/types/scalestartevent/#flet.ScaleStartEvent) [[InteractiveViewer](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/interactiveviewer/#flet.InteractiveViewer) ]] | None [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/interactiveviewer/#__codelineno-0-3) ) = None`

Called when the user begins a pan or scale gesture.

### on\_interaction\_update `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/interactiveviewer/#flet.InteractiveViewer.on_interaction_update "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/interactiveviewer/#__codelineno-0-1) on_interaction_update: (     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/interactiveviewer/#__codelineno-0-2)     [EventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.EventHandler) [[ScaleUpdateEvent](https://docs.flet.dev/types/scaleupdateevent/#flet.ScaleUpdateEvent) [[InteractiveViewer](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/interactiveviewer/#flet.InteractiveViewer) ]] | None [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/interactiveviewer/#__codelineno-0-3) ) = None`

Called when the user updates a pan or scale gesture.

Methods[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/interactiveviewer/#flet.InteractiveViewer-methods "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### pan `async` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/interactiveviewer/#flet.InteractiveViewer.pan "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/interactiveviewer/#__codelineno-0-1) pan(dx: [Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number) , dy: [Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  = 0, dz: [Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  = 0)`

Translates the current transform matrix.

Parameters:

*   **`dx`** (`[Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number) `) –
    
    Horizontal translation delta in logical pixels.
    
*   **`dy`** (`[Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number) `, default: `0` ) –
    
    Vertical translation delta in logical pixels.
    
*   **`dz`** (`[Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number) `, default: `0` ) –
    
    Z-axis translation delta applied to the transform matrix.
    

Note

XY translation is clamped to the same interaction boundaries used for gesture-driven panning. `dz` is applied directly to the matrix and defaults to `0`.

### reset `async` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/interactiveviewer/#flet.InteractiveViewer.reset "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/interactiveviewer/#__codelineno-0-1) reset(animation_duration: [DurationValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.DurationValue)  | None = None)`

Resets the current transform matrix to identity.

Parameters:

*   **`animation_duration`** (`[DurationValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.DurationValue)  | None`, default: `None` ) –
    
    Optional animation duration for the reset transition. If `None`, the reset is applied immediately.
    

Notes

When `animation_duration` is provided, Flutter interpolates from the current transform to identity using a matrix tween.

### restore\_state `async` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/interactiveviewer/#flet.InteractiveViewer.restore_state "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/interactiveviewer/#__codelineno-0-1) restore_state()`

Restores the transform matrix previously captured by [`save_state()`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/interactiveviewer/#flet.InteractiveViewer.save_state)
.

If no state has been saved yet, this method has no effect.

### save\_state `async` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/interactiveviewer/#flet.InteractiveViewer.save_state "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/interactiveviewer/#__codelineno-0-1) save_state()`

Saves a snapshot of the current transform matrix.

The saved state can later be restored using [`restore_state()`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/interactiveviewer/#flet.InteractiveViewer.restore_state)
. Calling this method again overwrites the previously saved snapshot.

### zoom `async` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/interactiveviewer/#flet.InteractiveViewer.zoom "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/interactiveviewer/#__codelineno-0-1) zoom(factor: [Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number) )`

Applies multiplicative zoom to the current transform.

Parameters:

*   **`factor`** (`[Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number) `) –
    
    Scale multiplier relative to the current scale. Values greater than `1` zoom in, values between `0` and `1` zoom out.
    

Note

The resulting scale is clamped to [`min_scale`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/interactiveviewer/#flet.InteractiveViewer.min_scale)
 and [`max_scale`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/interactiveviewer/#flet.InteractiveViewer.max_scale)
. Additional boundary clamping is applied so the visible viewport remains within [`boundary_margin`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/interactiveviewer/#flet.InteractiveViewer.boundary_margin)
 limits.

Back to top

---

# CupertinoTextField - Flet

 

[Skip to content](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinotextfield/#flet.CupertinoTextField)

CupertinoTextField
==================

An iOS-style text field.

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinotextfield/#__codelineno-0-1) ft.CupertinoTextField(placeholder_text="Search")`

[![CupertinoTextField](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/cupertino/golden/macos/cupertino_textfield/image_for_docs.png)](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/cupertino/golden/macos/cupertino_textfield/image_for_docs.png)

Basic CupertinoTextField

Inherits: `[TextField](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/textfield/#flet.TextField) `

Properties

*   **`[blend_mode](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinotextfield/#flet.CupertinoTextField.blend_mode) `**(`[BlendMode](https://docs.flet.dev/types/blendmode/#flet.BlendMode)  | None`) –
    
    The blend mode applied to the [`bgcolor`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/formfieldcontrol/#flet.FormFieldControl.bgcolor)
    
*   **`[clear_button_semantics_label](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinotextfield/#flet.CupertinoTextField.clear_button_semantics_label) `**(`[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)  | None`) –
    
    The semantic label for the clear button used by screen readers.
    
*   **`[clear_button_visibility_mode](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinotextfield/#flet.CupertinoTextField.clear_button_visibility_mode) `**(`[OverlayVisibilityMode](https://docs.flet.dev/types/overlayvisibilitymode/#flet.OverlayVisibilityMode) `) –
    
    Defines the visibility of the clear button based on the state of text entry.
    
*   **`[gradient](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinotextfield/#flet.CupertinoTextField.gradient) `**(`[Gradient](https://docs.flet.dev/types/gradient/#flet.Gradient)  | None`) –
    
    Configures the gradient background.
    
*   **`[image](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinotextfield/#flet.CupertinoTextField.image) `**(`[DecorationImage](https://docs.flet.dev/types/decorationimage/#flet.DecorationImage)  | None`) –
    
    An image to paint above the [`bgcolor`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/formfieldcontrol/#flet.FormFieldControl.bgcolor)
    
*   **`[padding](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinotextfield/#flet.CupertinoTextField.padding) `**(`[PaddingValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.PaddingValue) `) –
    
    The padding around the text entry area between the [`prefix`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/formfieldcontrol/#flet.FormFieldControl.prefix)
    
*   **`[placeholder_style](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinotextfield/#flet.CupertinoTextField.placeholder_style) `**(`[TextStyle](https://docs.flet.dev/types/textstyle/#flet.TextStyle)  | None`) –
    
    The [`TextStyle`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/textstyle/#flet.TextStyle)
     to use for [`placeholder_text`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinotextfield/#flet.CupertinoTextField.placeholder_text)
    .
    
*   **`[placeholder_text](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinotextfield/#flet.CupertinoTextField.placeholder_text) `**(`[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)  | None`) –
    
    A lighter colored placeholder hint that appears on the first line of the text field when the text entry is empty.
    
*   **`[prefix_visibility_mode](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinotextfield/#flet.CupertinoTextField.prefix_visibility_mode) `**(`[OverlayVisibilityMode](https://docs.flet.dev/types/overlayvisibilitymode/#flet.OverlayVisibilityMode) `) –
    
    Defines the visibility of the [`prefix`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/formfieldcontrol/#flet.FormFieldControl.prefix)
    
*   **`[shadows](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinotextfield/#flet.CupertinoTextField.shadows) `**(`[BoxShadowValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.BoxShadowValue)  | None`) –
    
    A list of shadows behind this text field.
    
*   **`[suffix_visibility_mode](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinotextfield/#flet.CupertinoTextField.suffix_visibility_mode) `**(`[OverlayVisibilityMode](https://docs.flet.dev/types/overlayvisibilitymode/#flet.OverlayVisibilityMode) `) –
    
    Defines the visibility of the [`suffix`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/formfieldcontrol/#flet.FormFieldControl.suffix)
    

Examples[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinotextfield/#examples "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinotextfield/#__codelineno-0-1) import flet as ft [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinotextfield/#__codelineno-0-2) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinotextfield/#__codelineno-0-7)             label="Material text field",            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinotextfield/#__codelineno-0-8)             label_style=ft.TextStyle(color=ft.Colors.GREY_400),        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinotextfield/#__codelineno-0-9)         ),        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinotextfield/#__codelineno-0-10)         ft.CupertinoTextField(            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinotextfield/#__codelineno-0-11)             placeholder_text="Cupertino text field",            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinotextfield/#__codelineno-0-12)             placeholder_style=ft.TextStyle(color=ft.Colors.GREY_400), [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinotextfield/#__codelineno-0-15)             adaptive=True,            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinotextfield/#__codelineno-0-16)             label="Adaptive text field",            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinotextfield/#__codelineno-0-17)             label_style=ft.TextStyle(color=ft.Colors.GREY_400), ### Background image[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinotextfield/#background-image "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinotextfield/#__codelineno-2-1) import flet as ft [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinotextfield/#__codelineno-2-2) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinotextfield/#__codelineno-2-3)[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinotextfield/#__codelineno-2-4) async def main(page: ft.Page):     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinotextfield/#__codelineno-2-5)     page.theme_mode = ft.ThemeMode.LIGHT    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinotextfield/#__codelineno-2-6)     page.add(        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinotextfield/#__codelineno-2-7)         ctf := ft.CupertinoTextField(            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinotextfield/#__codelineno-2-8)             label="Textfield Label",            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinotextfield/#__codelineno-2-9)             label_style=ft.TextStyle(italic=True, weight=ft.FontWeight.BOLD),            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinotextfield/#__codelineno-2-10)             bgcolor=ft.Colors.BLUE_GREY,            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinotextfield/#__codelineno-2-11)             image=ft.DecorationImage(                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinotextfield/#__codelineno-2-12)                 src=""            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinotextfield/#__codelineno-2-13)             ),        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinotextfield/#__codelineno-2-14)         )    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinotextfield/#__codelineno-2-15)     ) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinotextfield/#__codelineno-2-16) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinotextfield/#__codelineno-2-17)[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinotextfield/#__codelineno-2-18) ft.run(main)`

Properties[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinotextfield/#flet.CupertinoTextField-properties "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### blend\_mode `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinotextfield/#flet.CupertinoTextField.blend_mode "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinotextfield/#__codelineno-0-1) blend_mode: [BlendMode](https://docs.flet.dev/types/blendmode/#flet.BlendMode)  | None = None`

The blend mode applied to the [`bgcolor`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/formfieldcontrol/#flet.FormFieldControl.bgcolor)
 or [`gradient`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinotextfield/#flet.CupertinoTextField.gradient)
 background.

### clear\_button\_semantics\_label `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinotextfield/#flet.CupertinoTextField.clear_button_semantics_label "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinotextfield/#__codelineno-0-1) clear_button_semantics_label: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)  | None = 'Clear'`

The semantic label for the clear button used by screen readers.

This will be used by screen reading software to identify the clear button widget.

### clear\_button\_visibility\_mode `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinotextfield/#flet.CupertinoTextField.clear_button_visibility_mode "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinotextfield/#__codelineno-0-1) clear_button_visibility_mode: [OverlayVisibilityMode](https://docs.flet.dev/types/overlayvisibilitymode/#flet.OverlayVisibilityMode)  = [NEVER](https://docs.flet.dev/types/overlayvisibilitymode/#flet.OverlayVisibilityMode.NEVER)`

Defines the visibility of the clear button based on the state of text entry.

Will appear only if no [`suffix`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/formfieldcontrol/#flet.FormFieldControl.suffix)
 is provided.

### gradient `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinotextfield/#flet.CupertinoTextField.gradient "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinotextfield/#__codelineno-0-1) gradient: [Gradient](https://docs.flet.dev/types/gradient/#flet.Gradient)  | None = None`

Configures the gradient background.

### image `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinotextfield/#flet.CupertinoTextField.image "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinotextfield/#__codelineno-0-1) image: [DecorationImage](https://docs.flet.dev/types/decorationimage/#flet.DecorationImage)  | None = None`

An image to paint above the [`bgcolor`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/formfieldcontrol/#flet.FormFieldControl.bgcolor)
 or [`gradient`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinotextfield/#flet.CupertinoTextField.gradient)
 background.

### padding `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinotextfield/#flet.CupertinoTextField.padding "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinotextfield/#__codelineno-0-1) padding: [PaddingValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.PaddingValue)  = [field](https://docs.python.org/3/library/dataclasses.html#dataclasses.field "<code>dataclasses.field</code>") (     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinotextfield/#__codelineno-0-2)     default_factory=lambda: [all](https://docs.flet.dev/types/padding/#flet.Padding.all) (7) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinotextfield/#__codelineno-0-3) )`

The padding around the text entry area between the [`prefix`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/formfieldcontrol/#flet.FormFieldControl.prefix)
 and [`suffix`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/formfieldcontrol/#flet.FormFieldControl.suffix)
 or the clear button when [`clear_button_visibility_mode`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinotextfield/#flet.CupertinoTextField.clear_button_visibility_mode)
 is not [`OverlayVisibilityMode.NEVER`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/overlayvisibilitymode/#flet.OverlayVisibilityMode.NEVER)
.

### placeholder\_style `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinotextfield/#flet.CupertinoTextField.placeholder_style "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinotextfield/#__codelineno-0-1) placeholder_style: [TextStyle](https://docs.flet.dev/types/textstyle/#flet.TextStyle)  | None = None`

The [`TextStyle`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/textstyle/#flet.TextStyle)
 to use for [`placeholder_text`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinotextfield/#flet.CupertinoTextField.placeholder_text)
.

### placeholder\_text `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinotextfield/#flet.CupertinoTextField.placeholder_text "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinotextfield/#__codelineno-0-1) placeholder_text: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)  | None = None`

A lighter colored placeholder hint that appears on the first line of the text field when the text entry is empty.

Defaults to an empty string.

### prefix\_visibility\_mode `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinotextfield/#flet.CupertinoTextField.prefix_visibility_mode "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinotextfield/#__codelineno-0-1) prefix_visibility_mode: [OverlayVisibilityMode](https://docs.flet.dev/types/overlayvisibilitymode/#flet.OverlayVisibilityMode)  = [ALWAYS](https://docs.flet.dev/types/overlayvisibilitymode/#flet.OverlayVisibilityMode.ALWAYS)`

Defines the visibility of the [`prefix`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/formfieldcontrol/#flet.FormFieldControl.prefix)
 control based on the state of text entry.

Note

Has no effect if [`prefix`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/formfieldcontrol/#flet.FormFieldControl.prefix)
 is not specified.

### shadows `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinotextfield/#flet.CupertinoTextField.shadows "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinotextfield/#__codelineno-0-1) shadows: [BoxShadowValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.BoxShadowValue)  | None = None`

A list of shadows behind this text field.

### suffix\_visibility\_mode `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinotextfield/#flet.CupertinoTextField.suffix_visibility_mode "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinotextfield/#__codelineno-0-1) suffix_visibility_mode: [OverlayVisibilityMode](https://docs.flet.dev/types/overlayvisibilitymode/#flet.OverlayVisibilityMode)  = [ALWAYS](https://docs.flet.dev/types/overlayvisibilitymode/#flet.OverlayVisibilityMode.ALWAYS)`

Defines the visibility of the [`suffix`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/formfieldcontrol/#flet.FormFieldControl.suffix)
 control based on the state of text entry.

Note

Has no effect if [`suffix`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/formfieldcontrol/#flet.FormFieldControl.suffix)
 is not specified.

# Hero - Flet

 

[Skip to content](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/hero/#flet.Hero)

Hero
====

Marks the [`content`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/hero/#flet.Hero.content)
 as a shared element for route transitions.

Place a `Hero` with the same [`tag`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/hero/#flet.Hero.tag)
 in both source and destination views to animate that control between routes.

Inherits: `[LayoutControl](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/layoutcontrol/#flet.LayoutControl) `

Properties

*   **`[content](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/hero/#flet.Hero.content) `**(`[Control](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control) `) –
    
    The control to display and animate.
    
*   **`[tag](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/hero/#flet.Hero.tag) `**(`[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str) `) –
    
    A unique identifier used to match source and destination Hero controls.
    
*   **`[transition_on_user_gestures](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/hero/#flet.Hero.transition_on_user_gestures) `**(`[bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool) `) –
    
    Whether to animate when the route is transitioned by a user gesture (for example, iOS back swipe).
    

Examples[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/hero/#examples "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------

Properties[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/hero/#flet.Hero-properties "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### content `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/hero/#flet.Hero.content "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/hero/#__codelineno-0-1) content: [Control](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control)`

The control to display and animate.

Raises:

*   `[ValueError](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ValueError) ` –
    
    If it is not visible.
    

### tag `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/hero/#flet.Hero.tag "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/hero/#__codelineno-0-1) tag: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)`

A unique identifier used to match source and destination Hero controls.

Raises:

*   `[ValueError](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ValueError) ` –
    
    If it is not of type `str`.
    

### transition\_on\_user\_gestures `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/hero/#flet.Hero.transition_on_user_gestures "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/hero/#__codelineno-0-1) transition_on_user_gestures: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)  = False`

Whether to animate when the route is transitioned by a user gesture (for example, iOS back swipe).

Back to top

---

# Row - Flet

 

[Skip to content](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/row/#flet.Row)

Row
===

Displays its children in a horizontal array.

To cause a child control to expand and fill the available horizontal space, set its [`expand`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control.expand)
 property.

Example:

    `[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/row/#__codelineno-0-1)     ft.Row(        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/row/#__codelineno-0-2)         controls=[            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/row/#__codelineno-0-3)             ft.Card(                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/row/#__codelineno-0-4)                 shape=ft.ContinuousRectangleBorder(radius=10),                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/row/#__codelineno-0-5)                 content=ft.Container(                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/row/#__codelineno-0-6)                     padding=5,                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/row/#__codelineno-0-7)                     border_radius=ft.BorderRadius.all(5),                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/row/#__codelineno-0-8)                     bgcolor=ft.Colors.AMBER_100,                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/row/#__codelineno-0-9)                     content=ft.Text(f"Control {i}"),                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/row/#__codelineno-0-10)                 ),            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/row/#__codelineno-0-11)             )            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/row/#__codelineno-0-12)             for i in range(1, 6)        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/row/#__codelineno-0-13)         ],    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/row/#__codelineno-0-14)     )`

[![Row](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/core/golden/macos/row/image_for_docs.png)](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/core/golden/macos/row/image_for_docs.png)

Basic row of controls

Inherits: `[LayoutControl](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/layoutcontrol/#flet.LayoutControl) `, `[ScrollableControl](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/scrollablecontrol/#flet.ScrollableControl) `, `[AdaptiveControl](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/adaptivecontrol/#flet.AdaptiveControl) `

Properties

*   **`[alignment](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/row/#flet.Row.alignment) `**(`[MainAxisAlignment](https://docs.flet.dev/types/mainaxisalignment/#flet.MainAxisAlignment) `) –
    
    Defines how the child [`controls`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/row/#flet.Row.controls)
     should be placed horizontally.
    
*   **`[controls](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/row/#flet.Row.controls) `**(`[list](https://docs.python.org/3/glossary.html#term-list) [[Control](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control) ]`) –
    
    A list of Controls to display.
    
*   **`[intrinsic_height](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/row/#flet.Row.intrinsic_height) `**(`[bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool) `) –
    
    Whether this row should be as tall as the tallest child control in [`controls`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/row/#flet.Row.controls)
    .
    
*   **`[run_alignment](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/row/#flet.Row.run_alignment) `**(`[MainAxisAlignment](https://docs.flet.dev/types/mainaxisalignment/#flet.MainAxisAlignment) `) –
    
    How the runs should be placed in the cross-axis when [`wrap`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/row/#flet.Row.wrap)
     is `True`.
    
*   **`[run_spacing](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/row/#flet.Row.run_spacing) `**(`[Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number) `) –
    
    The spacing between runs when [`wrap`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/row/#flet.Row.wrap)
     is `True`.
    
*   **`[spacing](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/row/#flet.Row.spacing) `**(`[Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number) `) –
    
    The spacing between the child [`controls`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/row/#flet.Row.controls)
    .
    
*   **`[tight](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/row/#flet.Row.tight) `**(`[bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool) `) –
    
    Whether this row should occupy all available horizontal space (`True`), or only as much as needed by its children [`controls`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/row/#flet.Row.controls)
     (`False`).
    
*   **`[vertical_alignment](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/row/#flet.Row.vertical_alignment) `**(`[CrossAxisAlignment](https://docs.flet.dev/types/crossaxisalignment/#flet.CrossAxisAlignment) `) –
    
    Defines how the child [`controls`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/row/#flet.Row.controls)
     should be placed vertically.
    
*   **`[wrap](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/row/#flet.Row.wrap) `**(`[bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool) `) –
    
    Whether this row should put child [`controls`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/row/#flet.Row.controls)
     into additional rows (runs) if they don't fit in a single row.
    

Examples[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/row/#examples "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------

[Live example](https://flet-controls-gallery.fly.dev/layout/row)

### Wrapping children[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/row/#wrapping-children "Permanent link")

### Setting vertical alignment[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/row/#setting-vertical-alignment "Permanent link")

[![vertical-alignment](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/core/golden/macos/row/vertical_alignment.png)](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/core/golden/macos/row/vertical_alignment.png)

Properties[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/row/#flet.Row-properties "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### alignment `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/row/#flet.Row.alignment "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/row/#__codelineno-0-1) alignment: [MainAxisAlignment](https://docs.flet.dev/types/mainaxisalignment/#flet.MainAxisAlignment)  = [START](https://docs.flet.dev/types/mainaxisalignment/#flet.MainAxisAlignment.START)`

Defines how the child [`controls`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/row/#flet.Row.controls)
 should be placed horizontally.

### controls `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/row/#flet.Row.controls "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/row/#__codelineno-0-1) controls: [list](https://docs.python.org/3/glossary.html#term-list) [[Control](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control) ] = [field](https://docs.python.org/3/library/dataclasses.html#dataclasses.field "<code>dataclasses.field</code>") (default_factory=[list](https://docs.python.org/3/glossary.html#term-list) )`

A list of Controls to display.

### intrinsic\_height `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/row/#flet.Row.intrinsic_height "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/row/#__codelineno-0-1) intrinsic_height: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)  = False`

Whether this row should be as tall as the tallest child control in [`controls`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/row/#flet.Row.controls)
.

### run\_alignment `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/row/#flet.Row.run_alignment "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/row/#__codelineno-0-1) run_alignment: [MainAxisAlignment](https://docs.flet.dev/types/mainaxisalignment/#flet.MainAxisAlignment)  = [START](https://docs.flet.dev/types/mainaxisalignment/#flet.MainAxisAlignment.START)`

How the runs should be placed in the cross-axis when [`wrap`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/row/#flet.Row.wrap)
 is `True`.

### run\_spacing `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/row/#flet.Row.run_spacing "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/row/#__codelineno-0-1) run_spacing: [Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  = 10`

The spacing between runs when [`wrap`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/row/#flet.Row.wrap)
 is `True`.

### spacing `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/row/#flet.Row.spacing "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/row/#__codelineno-0-1) spacing: [Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  = 10`

The spacing between the child [`controls`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/row/#flet.Row.controls)
.

Note

Has effect only when [`alignment`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/row/#flet.Row.alignment)
 is set to [`MainAxisAlignment.START`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/mainaxisalignment/#flet.MainAxisAlignment.START)
, [`MainAxisAlignment.END`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/mainaxisalignment/#flet.MainAxisAlignment.END)
, or [`MainAxisAlignment.CENTER`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/mainaxisalignment/#flet.MainAxisAlignment.CENTER)
.

### tight `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/row/#flet.Row.tight "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/row/#__codelineno-0-1) tight: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)  = False`

Whether this row should occupy all available horizontal space (`True`), or only as much as needed by its children [`controls`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/row/#flet.Row.controls)
 (`False`).

Note

Has effect only when [`wrap`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/row/#flet.Row.wrap)
 is `False`.

### vertical\_alignment `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/row/#flet.Row.vertical_alignment "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/row/#__codelineno-0-1) vertical_alignment: [CrossAxisAlignment](https://docs.flet.dev/types/crossaxisalignment/#flet.CrossAxisAlignment)  = [CENTER](https://docs.flet.dev/types/crossaxisalignment/#flet.CrossAxisAlignment.CENTER)`

Defines how the child [`controls`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/row/#flet.Row.controls)
 should be placed vertically.

Note

When [`wrap`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/row/#flet.Row.wrap)
 is `True`, this property doesn't support [`CrossAxisAlignment.STRETCH`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/crossaxisalignment/#flet.CrossAxisAlignment.STRETCH)
 or [`CrossAxisAlignment.BASELINE`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/crossaxisalignment/#flet.CrossAxisAlignment.BASELINE)
. If either is used, [`CrossAxisAlignment.CENTER`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/crossaxisalignment/#flet.CrossAxisAlignment.CENTER)
 will be applied instead.

### wrap `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/row/#flet.Row.wrap "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/row/#__codelineno-0-1) wrap: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)  = False`

Whether this row should put child [`controls`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/row/#flet.Row.controls)
 into additional rows (runs) if they don't fit in a single row.

Back to top

---

# ListTile - Flet

 

[Skip to content](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#flet.ListTile)

ListTile
========

A single fixed-height row that typically contains some text as well as a leading or trailing icon.

Example:

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#__codelineno-0-1) ft.ListTile(     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#__codelineno-0-2)     width=400,    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#__codelineno-0-3)     leading=ft.Icon(ft.Icons.ACCOUNT_CIRCLE),    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#__codelineno-0-4)     title="Jane Doe",    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#__codelineno-0-5)     subtitle="Product Manager",    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#__codelineno-0-6)     trailing=ft.Icon(ft.Icons.CHEVRON_RIGHT),    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#__codelineno-0-7)     bgcolor=ft.Colors.SURFACE_CONTAINER_LOW, [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#__codelineno-0-8) )`

[![ListTile](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/material/golden/macos/list_tile/image_for_docs.png)](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/material/golden/macos/list_tile/image_for_docs.png)

Basic ListTile

Inherits: `[LayoutControl](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/layoutcontrol/#flet.LayoutControl) `, `[AdaptiveControl](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/adaptivecontrol/#flet.AdaptiveControl) `

Properties

*   **`[autofocus](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#flet.ListTile.autofocus) `**(`[bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool) `) –
    
    `True` if the control will be selected as the initial focus. If there is more than one control on a page with autofocus set, then the first one added to the page will get focus.
    
*   **`[bgcolor](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#flet.ListTile.bgcolor) `**(`[ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None`) –
    
    The list tile's background color.
    
*   **`[content_padding](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#flet.ListTile.content_padding) `**(`[PaddingValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.PaddingValue)  | None`) –
    
    The tile's internal padding. It insets the contents of this tile.
    
*   **`[dense](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#flet.ListTile.dense) `**(`[bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)  | None`) –
    
    Whether this list tile is part of a vertically dense list.
    
*   **`[enable_feedback](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#flet.ListTile.enable_feedback) `**(`[bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)  | None`) –
    
    Whether detected gestures should provide acoustic and/or haptic feedback.
    
*   **`[horizontal_spacing](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#flet.ListTile.horizontal_spacing) `**(`[Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None`) –
    
    The horizontal gap between the `title` and the [`leading`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#flet.ListTile.leading)
     and [`trailing`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#flet.ListTile.trailing)
    
*   **`[hover_color](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#flet.ListTile.hover_color) `**(`[ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None`) –
    
    The tile's color when hovered. Only takes effect if [`toggle_inputs`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#flet.ListTile.toggle_inputs)
     is True or if [`on_click`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#flet.ListTile.on_click)
     is provided.
    
*   **`[icon_color](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#flet.ListTile.icon_color) `**(`[ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None`) –
    
    Defines the default color for the icons present in [`leading`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#flet.ListTile.leading)
     and [`trailing`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#flet.ListTile.trailing)
    .
    
*   **`[is_three_line](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#flet.ListTile.is_three_line) `**(`[bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)  | None`) –
    
    Whether this list tile is intended to display three lines of text.
    
*   **`[leading](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#flet.ListTile.leading) `**(`[IconDataOrControl](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.IconDataOrControl)  | None`) –
    
    A control to display before the [`title`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#flet.ListTile.title)
    .
    
*   **`[leading_and_trailing_text_style](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#flet.ListTile.leading_and_trailing_text_style) `**(`[TextStyle](https://docs.flet.dev/types/textstyle/#flet.TextStyle)  | None`) –
    
    The [`TextStyle`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/textstyle/#flet.TextStyle)
     for the `leading` and `trailing` controls.
    
*   **`[min_height](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#flet.ListTile.min_height) `**(`[Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None`) –
    
    The minimum height allocated for this control.
    
*   **`[min_leading_width](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#flet.ListTile.min_leading_width) `**(`[Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None`) –
    
    The minimum width allocated for the `leading` control.
    
*   **`[min_vertical_padding](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#flet.ListTile.min_vertical_padding) `**(`[Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None`) –
    
    The minimum padding on the top and bottom of the `title` and `subtitle` controls.
    
*   **`[mouse_cursor](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#flet.ListTile.mouse_cursor) `**(`[MouseCursor](https://docs.flet.dev/types/mousecursor/#flet.MouseCursor)  | None`) –
    
    The cursor to be displayed when a mouse pointer enters or is hovering over this control. The value is [`MouseCursor`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/mousecursor/#flet.MouseCursor)
    
*   **`[selected](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#flet.ListTile.selected) `**(`[bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool) `) –
    
    If this tile is also enabled then icons and text are rendered with the same color.
    
*   **`[selected_color](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#flet.ListTile.selected_color) `**(`[ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None`) –
    
    Defines the color used for icons and text when `selected=True`.
    
*   **`[selected_tile_color](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#flet.ListTile.selected_tile_color) `**(`[ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None`) –
    
    Defines the background color of ListTile when `selected=True`.
    
*   **`[shape](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#flet.ListTile.shape) `**(`[OutlinedBorder](https://docs.flet.dev/types/outlinedborder/#flet.OutlinedBorder)  | None`) –
    
    The tile's shape.
    
*   **`[splash_color](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#flet.ListTile.splash_color) `**(`[ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None`) –
    
    The list tile's splash color after the control has been tapped.
    
*   **`[style](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#flet.ListTile.style) `**(`[ListTileStyle](https://docs.flet.dev/types/listtilestyle/#flet.ListTileStyle)  | None`) –
    
    Defines the font used for the title.
    
*   **`[subtitle](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#flet.ListTile.subtitle) `**(`[StrOrControl](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.StrOrControl)  | None`) –
    
    Additional content displayed below the title.
    
*   **`[subtitle_text_style](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#flet.ListTile.subtitle_text_style) `**(`[TextStyle](https://docs.flet.dev/types/textstyle/#flet.TextStyle)  | None`) –
    
    The [`TextStyle`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/textstyle/#flet.TextStyle)
     for the `subtitle` control.
    
*   **`[text_color](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#flet.ListTile.text_color) `**(`[ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None`) –
    
    The color used for texts in [`title`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#flet.ListTile.title)
    , [`subtitle`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#flet.ListTile.subtitle)
    , [`leading`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#flet.ListTile.leading)
    , and [`trailing`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#flet.ListTile.trailing)
    .
    
*   **`[title](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#flet.ListTile.title) `**(`[StrOrControl](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.StrOrControl)  | None`) –
    
    A control to display as primary content of the list tile.
    
*   **`[title_alignment](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#flet.ListTile.title_alignment) `**(`[ListTileTitleAlignment](https://docs.flet.dev/types/listtiletitlealignment/#flet.ListTileTitleAlignment)  | None`) –
    
    Defines how `leading` and `trailing` are vertically aligned relative to the titles (`title` and `subtitle`).
    
*   **`[title_text_style](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#flet.ListTile.title_text_style) `**(`[TextStyle](https://docs.flet.dev/types/textstyle/#flet.TextStyle)  | None`) –
    
    The [`TextStyle`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/textstyle/#flet.TextStyle)
     for the [`title`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#flet.ListTile.title)
    
*   **`[toggle_inputs](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#flet.ListTile.toggle_inputs) `**(`[bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool) `) –
    
    Whether clicking on a list tile should toggle the state of [`Radio`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/radio/#flet.Radio)
    , [`Checkbox`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/checkbox/#flet.Checkbox)
     or [`Switch`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/switch/#flet.Switch)
     inside this tile.
    
*   **`[trailing](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#flet.ListTile.trailing) `**(`[IconDataOrControl](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.IconDataOrControl)  | None`) –
    
    A control to display after the [`title`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#flet.ListTile.title)
    .
    
*   **`[url](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#flet.ListTile.url) `**(`[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)  | [Url](https://docs.flet.dev/types/url/#flet.Url)  | None`) –
    
    The URL to open when this button is clicked.
    
*   **`[visual_density](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#flet.ListTile.visual_density) `**(`[VisualDensity](https://docs.flet.dev/types/visualdensity/#flet.VisualDensity)  | None`) –
    
    Defines how compact the control's layout will be.
    

Events

*   **`[on_click](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#flet.ListTile.on_click) `**(`[ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[ListTile](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#flet.ListTile) ] | None`) –
    
    Called when a user clicks or taps the list tile.
    
*   **`[on_long_press](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#flet.ListTile.on_long_press) `**(`[ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[ListTile](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#flet.ListTile) ] | None`) –
    
    Called when the user long-presses on this list tile.
    

Examples[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#examples "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[Live example](https://flet-controls-gallery.fly.dev/layout/listtile)
