Properties[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#flet.ListTile-properties "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### autofocus `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#flet.ListTile.autofocus "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#__codelineno-0-1) autofocus: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)  = False`

`True` if the control will be selected as the initial focus. If there is more than one control on a page with autofocus set, then the first one added to the page will get focus.

### bgcolor `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#flet.ListTile.bgcolor "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#__codelineno-0-1) bgcolor: [ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None = None`

The list tile's background color.

### content\_padding `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#flet.ListTile.content_padding "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#__codelineno-0-1) content_padding: [PaddingValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.PaddingValue)  | None = None`

The tile's internal padding. It insets the contents of this tile.

its `leading`, `title`, `subtitle`, and `trailing` controls.

### dense `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#flet.ListTile.dense "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#__codelineno-0-1) dense: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)  | None = None`

Whether this list tile is part of a vertically dense list.

Dense list tiles default to a smaller height.

### enable\_feedback `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#flet.ListTile.enable_feedback "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#__codelineno-0-1) enable_feedback: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)  | None = None`

Whether detected gestures should provide acoustic and/or haptic feedback. On Android, for example, setting this to `True` produce a click sound and a long-press will produce a short vibration.

### horizontal\_spacing `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#flet.ListTile.horizontal_spacing "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#__codelineno-0-1) horizontal_spacing: [Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None = None`

The horizontal gap between the `title` and the [`leading`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#flet.ListTile.leading)
 and [`trailing`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#flet.ListTile.trailing)
 controls.

### hover\_color `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#flet.ListTile.hover_color "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#__codelineno-0-1) hover_color: [ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None = None`

The tile's color when hovered. Only takes effect if [`toggle_inputs`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#flet.ListTile.toggle_inputs)
 is True or if [`on_click`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#flet.ListTile.on_click)
 is provided.

### icon\_color `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#flet.ListTile.icon_color "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#__codelineno-0-1) icon_color: [ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None = None`

Defines the default color for the icons present in [`leading`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#flet.ListTile.leading)
 and [`trailing`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#flet.ListTile.trailing)
.

### is\_three\_line `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#flet.ListTile.is_three_line "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#__codelineno-0-1) is_three_line: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)  | None = None`

Whether this list tile is intended to display three lines of text.

If `True`, then subtitle must be non-null (since it is expected to give the second and third lines of text).

If `False`, the list tile is treated as having one line if the subtitle is null and treated as having two lines if the subtitle is non-null.

When using a Text control for title and subtitle, you can enforce line limits using [`Text.max_lines`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/text/#flet.Text.max_lines)
.

### leading `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#flet.ListTile.leading "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#__codelineno-0-1) leading: [IconDataOrControl](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.IconDataOrControl)  | None = None`

A control to display before the [`title`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#flet.ListTile.title)
.

### leading\_and\_trailing\_text\_style `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#flet.ListTile.leading_and_trailing_text_style "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#__codelineno-0-1) leading_and_trailing_text_style: [TextStyle](https://docs.flet.dev/types/textstyle/#flet.TextStyle)  | None = None`

The [`TextStyle`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/textstyle/#flet.TextStyle)
 for the `leading` and `trailing` controls.

### min\_height `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#flet.ListTile.min_height "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#__codelineno-0-1) min_height: [Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None = None`

The minimum height allocated for this control.

If `None` or not set, default tile heights are `56.0`, `72.0`, and `88.0` for one, two, and three lines of text respectively. If [`dense`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#flet.ListTile.dense)
 is `True`, these defaults are changed to `48.0`, `64.0`, and `76.0`.

Note that, a visual density value or a large title will also adjust the default tile heights.

### min\_leading\_width `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#flet.ListTile.min_leading_width "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#__codelineno-0-1) min_leading_width: [Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None = None`

The minimum width allocated for the `leading` control.

### min\_vertical\_padding `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#flet.ListTile.min_vertical_padding "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#__codelineno-0-1) min_vertical_padding: [Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None = None`

The minimum padding on the top and bottom of the `title` and `subtitle` controls.

### mouse\_cursor `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#flet.ListTile.mouse_cursor "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#__codelineno-0-1) mouse_cursor: [MouseCursor](https://docs.flet.dev/types/mousecursor/#flet.MouseCursor)  | None = None`

The cursor to be displayed when a mouse pointer enters or is hovering over this control. The value is [`MouseCursor`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/mousecursor/#flet.MouseCursor)
 enum.

### selected `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#flet.ListTile.selected "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#__codelineno-0-1) selected: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)  = False`

If this tile is also enabled then icons and text are rendered with the same color. By default the selected color is the theme's primary color.

### selected\_color `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#flet.ListTile.selected_color "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#__codelineno-0-1) selected_color: [ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None = None`

Defines the color used for icons and text when `selected=True`.

### selected\_tile\_color `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#flet.ListTile.selected_tile_color "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#__codelineno-0-1) selected_tile_color: [ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None = None`

Defines the background color of ListTile when `selected=True`.

### shape `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#flet.ListTile.shape "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#__codelineno-0-1) shape: [OutlinedBorder](https://docs.flet.dev/types/outlinedborder/#flet.OutlinedBorder)  | None = None`

The tile's shape.

### splash\_color `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#flet.ListTile.splash_color "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#__codelineno-0-1) splash_color: [ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None = None`

The list tile's splash color after the control has been tapped.

### style `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#flet.ListTile.style "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#__codelineno-0-1) style: [ListTileStyle](https://docs.flet.dev/types/listtilestyle/#flet.ListTileStyle)  | None = None`

Defines the font used for the title.

Defaults to `ListTileStyle.LIST`.

### subtitle `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#flet.ListTile.subtitle "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#__codelineno-0-1) subtitle: [StrOrControl](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.StrOrControl)  | None = None`

Additional content displayed below the title.

If [`is_three_line`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#flet.ListTile.is_three_line)
 is `False`, this should not wrap. If `is_three_line` is `True`, this should be configured to take a maximum of two lines. For example, you can use [`Text.max_lines`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/text/#flet.Text.max_lines)
 to enforce the number of lines.

Typically a [`Text`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/text/#flet.Text)
 control.

### subtitle\_text\_style `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#flet.ListTile.subtitle_text_style "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#__codelineno-0-1) subtitle_text_style: [TextStyle](https://docs.flet.dev/types/textstyle/#flet.TextStyle)  | None = None`

The [`TextStyle`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/textstyle/#flet.TextStyle)
 for the `subtitle` control.

### text\_color `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#flet.ListTile.text_color "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#__codelineno-0-1) text_color: [ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None = None`

The color used for texts in [`title`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#flet.ListTile.title)
, [`subtitle`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#flet.ListTile.subtitle)
, [`leading`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#flet.ListTile.leading)
, and [`trailing`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#flet.ListTile.trailing)
.

### title `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#flet.ListTile.title "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#__codelineno-0-1) title: [StrOrControl](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.StrOrControl)  | None = None`

A control to display as primary content of the list tile.

Typically a [`Text`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/text/#flet.Text)
 control. This should not wrap. To enforce the single line limit, use [`Text.max_lines`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/text/#flet.Text.max_lines)
.

### title\_alignment `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#flet.ListTile.title_alignment "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#__codelineno-0-1) title_alignment: [ListTileTitleAlignment](https://docs.flet.dev/types/listtiletitlealignment/#flet.ListTileTitleAlignment)  | None = None`

Defines how `leading` and `trailing` are vertically aligned relative to the titles (`title` and `subtitle`).

Defaults to `ListTileTitleAlignment.THREE_LINE` in Material 3 or `ListTileTitleAlignment.TITLE_HEIGHT` in Material 2.

### title\_text\_style `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#flet.ListTile.title_text_style "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#__codelineno-0-1) title_text_style: [TextStyle](https://docs.flet.dev/types/textstyle/#flet.TextStyle)  | None = None`

The [`TextStyle`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/textstyle/#flet.TextStyle)
 for the [`title`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#flet.ListTile.title)
 control.

### toggle\_inputs `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#flet.ListTile.toggle_inputs "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#__codelineno-0-1) toggle_inputs: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)  = False`

Whether clicking on a list tile should toggle the state of [`Radio`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/radio/#flet.Radio)
, [`Checkbox`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/checkbox/#flet.Checkbox)
 or [`Switch`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/switch/#flet.Switch)
 inside this tile.

### trailing `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#flet.ListTile.trailing "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#__codelineno-0-1) trailing: [IconDataOrControl](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.IconDataOrControl)  | None = None`

A control to display after the [`title`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#flet.ListTile.title)
.

Typically an [`Icon`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/icon/#flet.Icon)
 control.

### url `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#flet.ListTile.url "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#__codelineno-0-1) url: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)  | [Url](https://docs.flet.dev/types/url/#flet.Url)  | None = None`

The URL to open when this button is clicked.

Additionally, if [`on_click`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#flet.ListTile.on_click)
 event callback is provided, it is fired after that.

### visual\_density `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#flet.ListTile.visual_density "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#__codelineno-0-1) visual_density: [VisualDensity](https://docs.flet.dev/types/visualdensity/#flet.VisualDensity)  | None = None`

Defines how compact the control's layout will be.

Events[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#flet.ListTile-events "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### on\_click `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#flet.ListTile.on_click "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#__codelineno-0-1) on_click: [ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[ListTile](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#flet.ListTile) ] | None = None`

Called when a user clicks or taps the list tile.

### on\_long\_press `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#flet.ListTile.on_long_press "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#__codelineno-0-1) on_long_press: [ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[ListTile](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#flet.ListTile) ] | None = None`

Called when the user long-presses on this list tile.

Back to top

---

# Slider - Flet

 

[Skip to content](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#flet.Slider)

Slider
======

A slider provides a visual indication of adjustable content, as well as the current setting in the total range of content.

Use a slider when you want people to set defined values (such as volume or brightness), or when people would benefit from instant feedback on the effect of setting changes.

Example:

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#__codelineno-0-1) ft.Slider(label="Slider", value=0.3)`

[![Slider](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/material/golden/macos/slider/image_for_docs.png)](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/material/golden/macos/slider/image_for_docs.png)

Basic slider

Inherits: `[LayoutControl](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/layoutcontrol/#flet.LayoutControl) `, `[AdaptiveControl](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/adaptivecontrol/#flet.AdaptiveControl) `

Properties

*   **`[active_color](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#flet.Slider.active_color) `**(`[ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None`) –
    
    The color to use for the portion of the slider track that is active.
    
*   **`[autofocus](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#flet.Slider.autofocus) `**(`[bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool) `) –
    
    True if the control will be selected as the initial focus. If there is more than one control on a page with autofocus set, then the first one added to the page will get focus.
    
*   **`[divisions](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#flet.Slider.divisions) `**(`[int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int)  | None`) –
    
    The number of discrete divisions.
    
*   **`[inactive_color](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#flet.Slider.inactive_color) `**(`[ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None`) –
    
    The color for the inactive portion of the slider track.
    
*   **`[interaction](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#flet.Slider.interaction) `**(`[SliderInteraction](https://docs.flet.dev/types/sliderinteraction/#flet.SliderInteraction)  | None`) –
    
    The allowed way for the user to interact with this slider.
    
*   **`[label](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#flet.Slider.label) `**(`[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)  | None`) –
    
    A label to show above the slider when the slider is active. The value of `label` may contain `{value}` which will be dynamically replaced with a current slider value. For example, `"Volume: {value}"`.
    
*   **`[max](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#flet.Slider.max) `**(`[Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number) `) –
    
    The maximum value the user can select.
    
*   **`[min](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#flet.Slider.min) `**(`[Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number) `) –
    
    The minimum value the user can select.
    
*   **`[mouse_cursor](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#flet.Slider.mouse_cursor) `**(`[MouseCursor](https://docs.flet.dev/types/mousecursor/#flet.MouseCursor)  | None`) –
    
    The cursor to be displayed when a mouse pointer enters or is hovering over this control.
    
*   **`[overlay_color](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#flet.Slider.overlay_color) `**(`[ControlStateValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlStateValue) [[ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue) ] | None`) –
    
    The highlight color that's typically used to indicate that the range slider thumb is in [`ControlState.HOVERED`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/controlstate/#flet.ControlState.HOVERED)
     or [`ControlState.DRAGGED`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/controlstate/#flet.ControlState.DRAGGED)
     states.
    
*   **`[padding](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#flet.Slider.padding) `**(`[PaddingValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.PaddingValue)  | None`) –
    
    Determines the padding around this slider.
    
*   **`[round](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#flet.Slider.round) `**(`[int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int) `) –
    
    The number of decimals displayed on the [`label`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#flet.Slider.label)
    
*   **`[secondary_active_color](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#flet.Slider.secondary_active_color) `**(`[ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None`) –
    
    The color to use for the portion of the slider track between the thumb and the [`secondary_track_value`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#flet.Slider.secondary_track_value)
    .
    
*   **`[secondary_track_value](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#flet.Slider.secondary_track_value) `**(`[Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None`) –
    
    The secondary track value for this slider.
    
*   **`[thumb_color](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#flet.Slider.thumb_color) `**(`[ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None`) –
    
    The color of the thumb.
    
*   **`[value](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#flet.Slider.value) `**(`[Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None`) –
    
    The currently selected value for this slider.
    
*   **`[year_2023](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#flet.Slider.year_2023) `**(`[bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)  | None`) –
    
    If this is set to `False`, this slider will use the latest Material Design 3 appearance, which was introduced in December 2023.
    

Events

*   **`[on_blur](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#flet.Slider.on_blur) `**(`[ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[Slider](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#flet.Slider) ] | None`) –
    
    Called when this slider has lost focus.
    
*   **`[on_change](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#flet.Slider.on_change) `**(`[ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[Slider](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#flet.Slider) ] | None`) –
    
    Called when the state of this slider is changed.
    
*   **`[on_change_end](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#flet.Slider.on_change_end) `**(`[ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[Slider](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#flet.Slider) ] | None`) –
    
    Called when the user is done selecting a new value for this slider.
    
*   **`[on_change_start](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#flet.Slider.on_change_start) `**(`[ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[Slider](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#flet.Slider) ] | None`) –
    
    Called when the user starts selecting a new value for this slider.
    
*   **`[on_focus](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#flet.Slider.on_focus) `**(`[ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[Slider](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#flet.Slider) ] | None`) –
    
    Called when this slider has received focus.
    

Examples[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#examples "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[Live example](https://flet-controls-gallery.fly.dev/input/slider/basic)

### Basic Example[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#basic-example "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#__codelineno-0-1) import flet as ft [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#__codelineno-0-2) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#__codelineno-0-3)[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#__codelineno-0-4) def main(page: ft.Page):     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#__codelineno-0-5)     page.add(        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#__codelineno-0-6)         ft.Text("Default slider:"),        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#__codelineno-0-7)         ft.Slider(),        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#__codelineno-0-8)         ft.Text("Default disabled slider:"),        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#__codelineno-0-9)         ft.Slider(disabled=True),    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#__codelineno-0-10)     ) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#__codelineno-0-11) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#__codelineno-0-12)[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#__codelineno-0-13) if __name__ == "__main__":     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#__codelineno-0-14)     ft.run(main)`

[![basic](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/material/golden/macos/slider/basic.png)](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/material/golden/macos/slider/basic.png)

### Setting a custom label[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#setting-a-custom-label "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#__codelineno-1-1) import flet as ft [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#__codelineno-1-2) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#__codelineno-1-3)[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#__codelineno-1-4) def main(page: ft.Page):     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#__codelineno-1-5)     page.add(        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#__codelineno-1-6)         ft.Text("Slider with value:"),        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#__codelineno-1-7)         ft.Slider(value=0.3),        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#__codelineno-1-8)         ft.Text("Slider with a custom range and label:"),        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#__codelineno-1-9)         ft.Slider(min=0, max=100, divisions=10, label="{value}%"),    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#__codelineno-1-10)     ) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#__codelineno-1-11) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#__codelineno-1-12)[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#__codelineno-1-13) if __name__ == "__main__":     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#__codelineno-1-14)     ft.run(main)`

[![custom-label](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/material/golden/macos/slider/custom_label.png)](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/material/golden/macos/slider/custom_label.png)

### Handling events[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#handling-events "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#__codelineno-2-1) import flet as ft [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#__codelineno-2-2) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#__codelineno-2-3)[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#__codelineno-2-4) def main(page: ft.Page):     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#__codelineno-2-5)     def slider_changed(e: ft.Event[ft.Slider]):        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#__codelineno-2-6)         message.value = f"Slider changed to {e.control.value}"        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#__codelineno-2-7)         message.update() [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#__codelineno-2-8)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#__codelineno-2-9)     page.add(        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#__codelineno-2-10)         ft.Text("Slider with 'on_change' event:"),        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#__codelineno-2-11)         ft.Slider(            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#__codelineno-2-12)             key="slider",            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#__codelineno-2-13)             min=0,            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#__codelineno-2-14)             max=100,            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#__codelineno-2-15)             divisions=10,            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#__codelineno-2-16)             label="{value}%",            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#__codelineno-2-17)             on_change=slider_changed,        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#__codelineno-2-18)         ),        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#__codelineno-2-19)         message := ft.Text(),    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#__codelineno-2-20)     ) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#__codelineno-2-21) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#__codelineno-2-22)[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#__codelineno-2-23) if __name__ == "__main__":     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#__codelineno-2-24)     ft.run(main)`

[![handling-events](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/material/golden/macos/slider/handling_events.png)](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/material/golden/macos/slider/handling_events.png)

Properties[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#flet.Slider-properties "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### active\_color `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#flet.Slider.active_color "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#__codelineno-0-1) active_color: [ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None = None`

The color to use for the portion of the slider track that is active.

The "active" side of the slider is the side between the thumb and the minimum value.

### autofocus `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#flet.Slider.autofocus "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#__codelineno-0-1) autofocus: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)  = False`

True if the control will be selected as the initial focus. If there is more than one control on a page with autofocus set, then the first one added to the page will get focus.

### divisions `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#flet.Slider.divisions "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#__codelineno-0-1) divisions: [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int)  | None = None`

The number of discrete divisions.

Typically used with [`label`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#flet.Slider.label)
 to show the current discrete value.

If `None`, this slider is continuous.

### inactive\_color `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#flet.Slider.inactive_color "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#__codelineno-0-1) inactive_color: [ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None = None`

The color for the inactive portion of the slider track.

The "inactive" side of the slider is the side between the thumb and the maximum value.

### interaction `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#flet.Slider.interaction "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#__codelineno-0-1) interaction: [SliderInteraction](https://docs.flet.dev/types/sliderinteraction/#flet.SliderInteraction)  | None = None`

The allowed way for the user to interact with this slider.

If `None`, [`SliderTheme.interaction`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/slidertheme/#flet.SliderTheme.interaction)
 is used. If that's is also `None`, defaults to [`SliderInteraction.TAP_AND_SLIDE`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/sliderinteraction/#flet.SliderInteraction.TAP_AND_SLIDE)
.

### label `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#flet.Slider.label "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#__codelineno-0-1) label: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)  | None = None`

A label to show above the slider when the slider is active. The value of `label` may contain `{value}` which will be dynamically replaced with a current slider value. For example, `"Volume: {value}"`.

It is used to display the value of a discrete slider, and it is displayed as part of the value indicator shape.

If not set, then the value indicator will not be displayed.

### max `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#flet.Slider.max "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#__codelineno-0-1) max: [Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  = 1.0`

The maximum value the user can select.

If the [`min`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#flet.Slider.min)
 is equal to the `max`, then this slider is disabled.

Raises:

*   `[ValueError](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ValueError) ` –
    
    If it is not greater than or equal to [`min`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#flet.Slider.min)
    .
    
*   `[ValueError](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ValueError) ` –
    
    If it is not greater than or equal to [`value`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#flet.Slider.value)
    , when [`value`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#flet.Slider.value)
     is set.
    

### min `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#flet.Slider.min "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#__codelineno-0-1) min: [Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  = 0.0`

The minimum value the user can select.

If the [`max`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#flet.Slider.max)
 is equal to the `min`, then this slider is disabled.

Raises:

*   `[ValueError](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ValueError) ` –
    
    If it is not less than or equal to [`max`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#flet.Slider.max)
    .
    
*   `[ValueError](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ValueError) ` –
    
    If it is not less than or equal to [`value`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#flet.Slider.value)
    , when [`value`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#flet.Slider.value)
     is set.
    

### mouse\_cursor `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#flet.Slider.mouse_cursor "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#__codelineno-0-1) mouse_cursor: [MouseCursor](https://docs.flet.dev/types/mousecursor/#flet.MouseCursor)  | None = None`

The cursor to be displayed when a mouse pointer enters or is hovering over this control.

### overlay\_color `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#flet.Slider.overlay_color "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#__codelineno-0-1) overlay_color: [ControlStateValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlStateValue) [[ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue) ] | None = None`

The highlight color that's typically used to indicate that the range slider thumb is in [`ControlState.HOVERED`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/controlstate/#flet.ControlState.HOVERED)
 or [`ControlState.DRAGGED`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/controlstate/#flet.ControlState.DRAGGED)
 states.

### padding `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#flet.Slider.padding "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#__codelineno-0-1) padding: [PaddingValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.PaddingValue)  | None = None`

Determines the padding around this slider.

### round `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#flet.Slider.round "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#__codelineno-0-1) round: [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int)  = 0`

The number of decimals displayed on the [`label`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#flet.Slider.label)
 containing [`value`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#flet.Slider.value)
.

Defaults to `0`, which displays value rounded to the nearest integer.

### secondary\_active\_color `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#flet.Slider.secondary_active_color "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#__codelineno-0-1) secondary_active_color: [ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None = None`

The color to use for the portion of the slider track between the thumb and the [`secondary_track_value`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#flet.Slider.secondary_track_value)
.

### secondary\_track\_value `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#flet.Slider.secondary_track_value "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#__codelineno-0-1) secondary_track_value: [Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None = None`

The secondary track value for this slider.

If not null, a secondary track using `secondary_active_color` is drawn between the thumb and this value, over the inactive track. If less than `value`, then the secondary track is not shown.

It can be ideal for media scenarios such as showing the buffering progress while the `value` shows the play progress.

### thumb\_color `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#flet.Slider.thumb_color "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#__codelineno-0-1) thumb_color: [ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None = None`

The color of the thumb.

### value `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#flet.Slider.value "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#__codelineno-0-1) value: [Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None = None`

The currently selected value for this slider.

The slider's thumb is drawn at a position that corresponds to this value.

Defaults to value of [`min`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#flet.Slider.min)
.

Raises:

*   `[ValueError](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ValueError) ` –
    
    If it is not greater than or equal to [`min`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#flet.Slider.min)
    .
    
*   `[ValueError](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ValueError) ` –
    
    If it is not less than or equal to [`max`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#flet.Slider.max)
    .
    

### year\_2023 `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#flet.Slider.year_2023 "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#__codelineno-0-1) year_2023: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)  | None = None`

If this is set to `False`, this slider will use the latest Material Design 3 appearance, which was introduced in December 2023.

When `True`, the Slider will use the 2023 Material Design 3 appearance.

If not set, then the [`SliderTheme.year_2023`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/slidertheme/#flet.SliderTheme.year_2023)
 will be used, which is `False` by default.

If [`Theme.use_material3`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/theme/#flet.Theme.use_material3)
 is `False`, then this property is ignored.

Events[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#flet.Slider-events "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### on\_blur `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#flet.Slider.on_blur "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#__codelineno-0-1) on_blur: [ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[Slider](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#flet.Slider) ] | None = None`

Called when this slider has lost focus.

### on\_change `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#flet.Slider.on_change "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#__codelineno-0-1) on_change: [ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[Slider](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#flet.Slider) ] | None = None`

Called when the state of this slider is changed.

### on\_change\_end `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#flet.Slider.on_change_end "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#__codelineno-0-1) on_change_end: [ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[Slider](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#flet.Slider) ] | None = None`

Called when the user is done selecting a new value for this slider.

### on\_change\_start `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#flet.Slider.on_change_start "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#__codelineno-0-1) on_change_start: [ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[Slider](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#flet.Slider) ] | None = None`

Called when the user starts selecting a new value for this slider.

### on\_focus `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#flet.Slider.on_focus "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#__codelineno-0-1) on_focus: [ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[Slider](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/slider/#flet.Slider) ] | None = None`

Called when this slider has received focus.

# ResponsiveRow - Flet

 

[Skip to content](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/responsiverow/#flet.ResponsiveRow)

ResponsiveRow
=============

Allows aligning child controls to virtual columns.

By default, a virtual grid has 12 columns, but that can be customized with [`columns`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/responsiverow/#flet.ResponsiveRow.columns)
 property.

Similar to `expand` property, every control has [`col`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control.col)
 property which allows specifying how many columns a control should span.

Example:

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/responsiverow/#__codelineno-0-1) ft.ResponsiveRow(     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/responsiverow/#__codelineno-0-2)     controls=[        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/responsiverow/#__codelineno-0-3)         ft.Button(            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/responsiverow/#__codelineno-0-4)             f"Button {i}",            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/responsiverow/#__codelineno-0-5)             color=ft.Colors.BLUE_GREY_300,            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/responsiverow/#__codelineno-0-6)             col={                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/responsiverow/#__codelineno-0-7)                 ft.ResponsiveRowBreakpoint.XS: 12,                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/responsiverow/#__codelineno-0-8)                 ft.ResponsiveRowBreakpoint.MD: 6,                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/responsiverow/#__codelineno-0-9)                 ft.ResponsiveRowBreakpoint.LG: 3,            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/responsiverow/#__codelineno-0-10)             },        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/responsiverow/#__codelineno-0-11)         )        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/responsiverow/#__codelineno-0-12)         for i in range(1, 6)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/responsiverow/#__codelineno-0-13)     ], [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/responsiverow/#__codelineno-0-14) )`

[![ResponsiveRow](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/core/golden/macos/responsive_row/image_for_docs.png)](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/core/golden/macos/responsive_row/image_for_docs.png)

Responsive row

Inherits: `[LayoutControl](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/layoutcontrol/#flet.LayoutControl) `, `[AdaptiveControl](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/adaptivecontrol/#flet.AdaptiveControl) `

Properties

*   **`[alignment](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/responsiverow/#flet.ResponsiveRow.alignment) `**(`[MainAxisAlignment](https://docs.flet.dev/types/mainaxisalignment/#flet.MainAxisAlignment) `) –
    
    Defines how the child [`controls`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/responsiverow/#flet.ResponsiveRow.controls)
     should be placed horizontally.
    
*   **`[breakpoints](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/responsiverow/#flet.ResponsiveRow.breakpoints) `**(`[dict](https://docs.python.org/3/reference/expressions.html#dict) [[ResponsiveRowBreakpoint](https://docs.flet.dev/types/responsiverowbreakpoint/#flet.ResponsiveRowBreakpoint)  | [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str) , [Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number) ]`) –
    
    Defines the minimum widths (in px) for each breakpoint key used by responsive properties such as [`col`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control.col)
    , [`spacing`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/responsiverow/#flet.ResponsiveRow.spacing)
    , and [`run_spacing`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/responsiverow/#flet.ResponsiveRow.run_spacing)
    .
    
*   **`[columns](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/responsiverow/#flet.ResponsiveRow.columns) `**(`[ResponsiveNumber](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ResponsiveNumber) `) –
    
    The number of virtual columns to layout children.
    
*   **`[controls](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/responsiverow/#flet.ResponsiveRow.controls) `**(`[list](https://docs.python.org/3/glossary.html#term-list) [[Control](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control) ]`) –
    
    A list of Controls to display.
    
*   **`[run_spacing](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/responsiverow/#flet.ResponsiveRow.run_spacing) `**(`[ResponsiveNumber](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ResponsiveNumber) `) –
    
    The spacing between runs.
    
*   **`[spacing](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/responsiverow/#flet.ResponsiveRow.spacing) `**(`[ResponsiveNumber](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ResponsiveNumber) `) –
    
    The spacing between controls in a row in virtual pixels.
    
*   **`[vertical_alignment](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/responsiverow/#flet.ResponsiveRow.vertical_alignment) `**(`[CrossAxisAlignment](https://docs.flet.dev/types/crossaxisalignment/#flet.CrossAxisAlignment) `) –
    
    Defines how the child [`controls`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/responsiverow/#flet.ResponsiveRow.controls)
     should be placed vertically.
    

Examples[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/responsiverow/#examples "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[Live example](https://flet-controls-gallery.fly.dev/layout/responsiverow)

[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/responsiverow/#__codelineno-0-80)[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/responsiverow/#__codelineno-0-81) if __name__ == "__main__":     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/responsiverow/#__codelineno-0-82)     ft.run(main)`

[![basic.gif](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/core/golden/macos/responsive_row/basic.gif)](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/core/golden/macos/responsive_row/basic.gif)

### Custom breakpoints[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/responsiverow/#custom-breakpoints "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/responsiverow/#__codelineno-1-1) import flet as ft [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/responsiverow/#__codelineno-1-2) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/responsiverow/#__codelineno-1-3)[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/responsiverow/#__codelineno-1-4) def main(page: ft.Page): page.padding = 16 [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/responsiverow/#__codelineno-1-7)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/responsiverow/#__codelineno-1-8)     breakpoints = { key=lambda item: item[1]) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/responsiverow/#__codelineno-1-15)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/responsiverow/#__codelineno-1-16)     breakpoint_labels = { [![custom_breakpoint.gif](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/core/golden/macos/responsive_row/custom_breakpoint.gif)](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/core/golden/macos/responsive_row/custom_breakpoint.gif)

Properties[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/responsiverow/#flet.ResponsiveRow-properties "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### alignment `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/responsiverow/#flet.ResponsiveRow.alignment "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/responsiverow/#__codelineno-0-1) alignment: [MainAxisAlignment](https://docs.flet.dev/types/mainaxisalignment/#flet.MainAxisAlignment)  = [START](https://docs.flet.dev/types/mainaxisalignment/#flet.MainAxisAlignment.START)`

Defines how the child [`controls`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/responsiverow/#flet.ResponsiveRow.controls)
 should be placed horizontally.

### breakpoints `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/responsiverow/#flet.ResponsiveRow.breakpoints "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/responsiverow/#__codelineno-0-1) breakpoints: [dict](https://docs.python.org/3/reference/expressions.html#dict) [[ResponsiveRowBreakpoint](https://docs.flet.dev/types/responsiverowbreakpoint/#flet.ResponsiveRowBreakpoint)  | [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str) , [Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number) ] = (     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/responsiverow/#__codelineno-0-2)     [field](https://docs.python.org/3/library/dataclasses.html#dataclasses.field "<code>dataclasses.field</code>") (        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/responsiverow/#__codelineno-0-3)         default_factory=lambda: {            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/responsiverow/#__codelineno-0-4)             [XS](https://docs.flet.dev/types/responsiverowbreakpoint/#flet.ResponsiveRowBreakpoint.XS) : 0,            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/responsiverow/#__codelineno-0-5)             [SM](https://docs.flet.dev/types/responsiverowbreakpoint/#flet.ResponsiveRowBreakpoint.SM) : 576,            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/responsiverow/#__codelineno-0-6)             [MD](https://docs.flet.dev/types/responsiverowbreakpoint/#flet.ResponsiveRowBreakpoint.MD) : 768,            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/responsiverow/#__codelineno-0-7)             [LG](https://docs.flet.dev/types/responsiverowbreakpoint/#flet.ResponsiveRowBreakpoint.LG) : 992,            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/responsiverow/#__codelineno-0-8)             [XL](https://docs.flet.dev/types/responsiverowbreakpoint/#flet.ResponsiveRowBreakpoint.XL) : 1200,            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/responsiverow/#__codelineno-0-9)             [XXL](https://docs.flet.dev/types/responsiverowbreakpoint/#flet.ResponsiveRowBreakpoint.XXL) : 1400,        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/responsiverow/#__codelineno-0-10)         }    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/responsiverow/#__codelineno-0-11)     ) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/responsiverow/#__codelineno-0-12) )`

Defines the minimum widths (in px) for each breakpoint key used by responsive properties such as [`col`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control.col)
, [`spacing`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/responsiverow/#flet.ResponsiveRow.spacing)
, and [`run_spacing`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/responsiverow/#flet.ResponsiveRow.run_spacing)
.

Keys can be [`ResponsiveRowBreakpoint`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/responsiverowbreakpoint/#flet.ResponsiveRowBreakpoint)
 values or custom strings. Breakpoint names in responsive values must match the names used here.

The default mirrors Bootstrap breakpoints.

### columns `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/responsiverow/#flet.ResponsiveRow.columns "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/responsiverow/#__codelineno-0-1) columns: [ResponsiveNumber](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ResponsiveNumber)  = 12`

The number of virtual columns to layout children.

### controls `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/responsiverow/#flet.ResponsiveRow.controls "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/responsiverow/#__codelineno-0-1) controls: [list](https://docs.python.org/3/glossary.html#term-list) [[Control](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control) ] = [field](https://docs.python.org/3/library/dataclasses.html#dataclasses.field "<code>dataclasses.field</code>") (default_factory=[list](https://docs.python.org/3/glossary.html#term-list) )`

A list of Controls to display.

### run\_spacing `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/responsiverow/#flet.ResponsiveRow.run_spacing "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/responsiverow/#__codelineno-0-1) run_spacing: [ResponsiveNumber](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ResponsiveNumber)  = 10`

The spacing between runs.

### spacing `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/responsiverow/#flet.ResponsiveRow.spacing "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/responsiverow/#__codelineno-0-1) spacing: [ResponsiveNumber](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ResponsiveNumber)  = 10`

The spacing between controls in a row in virtual pixels.

Note

Has effect only when [`alignment`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/responsiverow/#flet.ResponsiveRow.alignment)
 is set to [`MainAxisAlignment.START`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/mainaxisalignment/#flet.MainAxisAlignment.START)
, [`MainAxisAlignment.END`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/mainaxisalignment/#flet.MainAxisAlignment.END)
, or [`MainAxisAlignment.CENTER`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/mainaxisalignment/#flet.MainAxisAlignment.CENTER)
.

### vertical\_alignment `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/responsiverow/#flet.ResponsiveRow.vertical_alignment "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/responsiverow/#__codelineno-0-1) vertical_alignment: [CrossAxisAlignment](https://docs.flet.dev/types/crossaxisalignment/#flet.CrossAxisAlignment)  = [START](https://docs.flet.dev/types/crossaxisalignment/#flet.CrossAxisAlignment.START)`

Defines how the child [`controls`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/responsiverow/#flet.ResponsiveRow.controls)
 should be placed vertically.

Back to top

---

# Shimmer - Flet

 

[Skip to content](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/shimmer/#flet.Shimmer)

Shimmer
=======

Applies an animated shimmering effect to its [`content`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/shimmer/#flet.Shimmer.content)
.

Use it to create lightweight loading placeholders or to add motion to otherwise static layouts.

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/shimmer/#__codelineno-0-1) ft.Shimmer(     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/shimmer/#__codelineno-0-2)     base_color=ft.Colors.with_opacity(0.3, ft.Colors.GREY_400),    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/shimmer/#__codelineno-0-3)     highlight_color=ft.Colors.WHITE,    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/shimmer/#__codelineno-0-4)     content=ft.Column(        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/shimmer/#__codelineno-0-5)         controls=[            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/shimmer/#__codelineno-0-6)             ft.Container(height=80, bgcolor=ft.Colors.GREY_300),            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/shimmer/#__codelineno-0-7)             ft.Container(height=80, bgcolor=ft.Colors.GREY_300),            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/shimmer/#__codelineno-0-8)             ft.Container(height=80, bgcolor=ft.Colors.GREY_300),        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/shimmer/#__codelineno-0-9)         ],    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/shimmer/#__codelineno-0-10)     ), [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/shimmer/#__codelineno-0-11) )`

[![Shimmer](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/core/golden/macos/shimmer/image_for_docs.gif)](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/core/golden/macos/shimmer/image_for_docs.gif)

Basic shimmer

Inherits: `[LayoutControl](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/layoutcontrol/#flet.LayoutControl) `

Properties

*   **`[base_color](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/shimmer/#flet.Shimmer.base_color) `**(`[ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None`) –
    
    Base color used when no [`gradient`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/shimmer/#flet.Shimmer.gradient)
     is provided.
    
*   **`[content](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/shimmer/#flet.Shimmer.content) `**(`[Control](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control) `) –
    
    The control to render with the shimmer effect.
    
*   **`[direction](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/shimmer/#flet.Shimmer.direction) `**(`ShimmerDirection`) –
    
    Direction of the shimmering animation.
    
*   **`[gradient](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/shimmer/#flet.Shimmer.gradient) `**(`[Gradient](https://docs.flet.dev/types/gradient/#flet.Gradient)  | None`) –
    
    Custom gradient that defines the shimmer colors.
    
*   **`[highlight_color](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/shimmer/#flet.Shimmer.highlight_color) `**(`[ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None`) –
    
    Highlight color used when no [`gradient`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/shimmer/#flet.Shimmer.gradient)
     is provided.
    
*   **`[loop](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/shimmer/#flet.Shimmer.loop) `**(`[int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int)  | None`) –
    
    Number of times the animation should repeat. `0` means infinite.
    
*   **`[period](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/shimmer/#flet.Shimmer.period) `**(`[DurationValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.DurationValue) `) –
    
    Duration of a shimmer cycle in milliseconds.
    

Examples[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/shimmer/#examples "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Basic[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/shimmer/#basic "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/shimmer/#__codelineno-0-1) import flet as ft [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/shimmer/#__codelineno-0-2) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/shimmer/#__codelineno-0-3)[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/shimmer/#__codelineno-0-4) def main(page: ft.Page):     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/shimmer/#__codelineno-0-5)     page.add(        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/shimmer/#__codelineno-0-6)         ft.Shimmer(            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/shimmer/#__codelineno-0-7)             base_color=ft.Colors.with_opacity(0.3, ft.Colors.GREY_400),            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/shimmer/#__codelineno-0-8)             highlight_color=ft.Colors.WHITE,            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/shimmer/#__codelineno-0-9)             content=ft.Column(                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/shimmer/#__codelineno-0-10)                 controls=[                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/shimmer/#__codelineno-0-11)                     ft.Container(height=80, bgcolor=ft.Colors.GREY_300),                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/shimmer/#__codelineno-0-12)                     ft.Container(height=80, bgcolor=ft.Colors.GREY_300),                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/shimmer/#__codelineno-0-13)                     ft.Container(height=80, bgcolor=ft.Colors.GREY_300),                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/shimmer/#__codelineno-0-14)                 ],            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/shimmer/#__codelineno-0-15)             ),        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/shimmer/#__codelineno-0-16)         )    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/shimmer/#__codelineno-0-17)     ) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/shimmer/#__codelineno-0-18) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/shimmer/#__codelineno-0-19)[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/shimmer/#__codelineno-0-20) if __name__ == "__main__":     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/shimmer/#__codelineno-0-21)     ft.run(main)`

[![custom-label](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/core/golden/macos/shimmer/image_for_docs.gif)](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/core/golden/macos/shimmer/image_for_docs.gif)

padding=ft.Padding.all(20),        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/shimmer/#__codelineno-2-17)         bgcolor=ft.Colors.with_opacity(0.1, ft.Colors.BLACK),          ft.Row(spacing=10, controls=[metric(60, 10), metric(90, 10)]),                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/shimmer/#__codelineno-2-24)                 ft.Container( [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/shimmer/#__codelineno-2-61)             ),        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/shimmer/#__codelineno-2-62)         ],    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/shimmer/#__codelineno-2-63)     ) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/shimmer/#__codelineno-2-64)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/shimmer/#__codelineno-2-65)     page.add(cards) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/shimmer/#__codelineno-2-66) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/shimmer/#__codelineno-2-67)[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/shimmer/#__codelineno-2-68) if __name__ == "__main__":     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/shimmer/#__codelineno-2-69)     ft.run(main)`

[![custom-label](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/core/golden/macos/shimmer/custom_gradient.png)](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/core/golden/macos/shimmer/custom_gradient.png)

Properties[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/shimmer/#flet.Shimmer-properties "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### base\_color `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/shimmer/#flet.Shimmer.base_color "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/shimmer/#__codelineno-0-1) base_color: [ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None = None`

Base color used when no [`gradient`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/shimmer/#flet.Shimmer.gradient)
 is provided.

### content `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/shimmer/#flet.Shimmer.content "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/shimmer/#__codelineno-0-1) content: [Control](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control)`

The control to render with the shimmer effect.

Raises:

*   `[ValueError](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ValueError) ` –
    
    If it is not visible.
    

### direction `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/shimmer/#flet.Shimmer.direction "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/shimmer/#__codelineno-0-1) direction: ShimmerDirection = LTR`

Direction of the shimmering animation.

### gradient `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/shimmer/#flet.Shimmer.gradient "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/shimmer/#__codelineno-0-1) gradient: [Gradient](https://docs.flet.dev/types/gradient/#flet.Gradient)  | None = None`

Custom gradient that defines the shimmer colors.

### highlight\_color `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/shimmer/#flet.Shimmer.highlight_color "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/shimmer/#__codelineno-0-1) highlight_color: [ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None = None`

Highlight color used when no [`gradient`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/shimmer/#flet.Shimmer.gradient)
 is provided.

### loop `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/shimmer/#flet.Shimmer.loop "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/shimmer/#__codelineno-0-1) loop: [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int)  | None = 0`

Number of times the animation should repeat. `0` means infinite.

Raises:

*   `[ValueError](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ValueError) ` –
    
    If it is not greater than or equal to `0`.
    

### period `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/shimmer/#flet.Shimmer.period "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/shimmer/#__codelineno-0-1) period: [DurationValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.DurationValue)  = 1500`

Duration of a shimmer cycle in milliseconds.

Back to top

---

# TextButton - Flet

 

[Skip to content](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/textbutton/#flet.TextButton)

TextButton
==========

Text buttons are used for the lowest priority actions, especially when presenting multiple options. Text buttons can be placed on a variety of backgrounds. Until the button is interacted with, its container isn’t visible.

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/textbutton/#__codelineno-0-1) ft.TextButton(     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/textbutton/#__codelineno-0-2)     content="Text Button",    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/textbutton/#__codelineno-0-3)     icon=ft.Icons.STAR_BORDER,    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/textbutton/#__codelineno-0-4)     icon_color=ft.Colors.BLUE_300, [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/textbutton/#__codelineno-0-5) )`

[![TextButton](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/material/golden/macos/text_button/image_for_docs.png)](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/material/golden/macos/text_button/image_for_docs.png)

Simple text button

Inherits: `[LayoutControl](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/layoutcontrol/#flet.LayoutControl) `, `[AdaptiveControl](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/adaptivecontrol/#flet.AdaptiveControl) `

Properties

*   **`[autofocus](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/textbutton/#flet.TextButton.autofocus) `**(`[bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool) `) –
    
    True if the control will be selected as the initial focus.
    
*   **`[clip_behavior](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/textbutton/#flet.TextButton.clip_behavior) `**(`[ClipBehavior](https://docs.flet.dev/types/clipbehavior/#flet.ClipBehavior) `) –
    
    Defines how the content of this button is clipped.
    
*   **`[content](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/textbutton/#flet.TextButton.content) `**(`[StrOrControl](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.StrOrControl)  | None`) –
    
    A Control representing custom button content.
    
*   **`[icon](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/textbutton/#flet.TextButton.icon) `**(`[IconDataOrControl](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.IconDataOrControl)  | None`) –
    
    An icon to show in this button.
    
*   **`[icon_color](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/textbutton/#flet.TextButton.icon_color) `**(`[ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None`) –
    
    Icon color.
    
*   **`[style](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/textbutton/#flet.TextButton.style) `**(`[ButtonStyle](https://docs.flet.dev/types/buttonstyle/#flet.ButtonStyle)  | None`) –
    
    Defines the style of this button.
    
*   **`[url](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/textbutton/#flet.TextButton.url) `**(`[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)  | [Url](https://docs.flet.dev/types/url/#flet.Url)  | None`) –
    
    The URL to open when this button is clicked.
    

Events

*   **`[on_blur](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/textbutton/#flet.TextButton.on_blur) `**(`[ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[TextButton](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/textbutton/#flet.TextButton) ] | None`) –
    
    Called when this button has lost focus.
    
*   **`[on_click](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/textbutton/#flet.TextButton.on_click) `**(`[ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[TextButton](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/textbutton/#flet.TextButton) ] | None`) –
    
    Called when a user clicks this button.
    
*   **`[on_focus](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/textbutton/#flet.TextButton.on_focus) `**(`[ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[TextButton](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/textbutton/#flet.TextButton) ] | None`) –
    
    Called when this button has received focus.
    
*   **`[on_hover](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/textbutton/#flet.TextButton.on_hover) `**(`[ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[TextButton](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/textbutton/#flet.TextButton) ] | None`) –
    
    Called when a mouse pointer enters or exists this button's response area.
    
*   **`[on_long_press](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/textbutton/#flet.TextButton.on_long_press) `**(`[ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[TextButton](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/textbutton/#flet.TextButton) ] | None`) –
    
    Called when this button is long-pressed.
    

Methods

*   **`[focus](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/textbutton/#flet.TextButton.focus) `** –
    
    Requests focus for this control.
    

Examples[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/textbutton/#examples "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[Live example](https://flet-controls-gallery.fly.dev/buttons/textbutton)

### Basic Example[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/textbutton/#basic-example "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/textbutton/#__codelineno-0-1) import flet as ft [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/textbutton/#__codelineno-0-2) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/textbutton/#__codelineno-0-3)[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/textbutton/#__codelineno-0-4) def main(page: ft.Page):     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/textbutton/#__codelineno-0-5)     page.title = "Basic text buttons" [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/textbutton/#__codelineno-0-6)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/textbutton/#__codelineno-0-7)     page.add(        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/textbutton/#__codelineno-0-8)         ft.TextButton(content="Text button"),        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/textbutton/#__codelineno-0-9)         ft.TextButton(content="Disabled button", disabled=True),    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/textbutton/#__codelineno-0-10)     ) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/textbutton/#__codelineno-0-11) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/textbutton/#__codelineno-0-12)[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/textbutton/#__codelineno-0-13) if __name__ == "__main__":     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/textbutton/#__codelineno-0-14)     ft.run(main)`

[![basic](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/material/golden/macos/text_button/basic.png)](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/material/golden/macos/text_button/basic.png)

### Icons[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/textbutton/#icons "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/textbutton/#__codelineno-1-1) import flet as ft [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/textbutton/#__codelineno-1-2) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/textbutton/#__codelineno-1-3)[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/textbutton/#__codelineno-1-4) def main(page: ft.Page):     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/textbutton/#__codelineno-1-5)     page.title = "TextButtons with icons" [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/textbutton/#__codelineno-1-6)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/textbutton/#__codelineno-1-7)     page.add(        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/textbutton/#__codelineno-1-8)         ft.TextButton(content="Button with icon", icon=ft.Icons.WAVES_OUTLINED),        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/textbutton/#__codelineno-1-9)         ft.TextButton(            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/textbutton/#__codelineno-1-10)             content="Button with colorful icon",            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/textbutton/#__codelineno-1-11)             icon=ft.Icons.PARK_ROUNDED,            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/textbutton/#__codelineno-1-12)             icon_color=ft.Colors.GREEN_400,        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/textbutton/#__codelineno-1-13)         ),    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/textbutton/#__codelineno-1-14)     ) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/textbutton/#__codelineno-1-15) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/textbutton/#__codelineno-1-16)[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/textbutton/#__codelineno-1-17) if __name__ == "__main__":     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/textbutton/#__codelineno-1-18)     ft.run(main)`

### Handling clicks[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/textbutton/#handling-clicks "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/textbutton/#__codelineno-2-1) import flet as ft [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/textbutton/#__codelineno-2-2) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/textbutton/#__codelineno-2-3)[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/textbutton/#__codelineno-2-4) def main(page: ft.Page):     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/textbutton/#__codelineno-2-5)     page.title = "TextButton with 'click' event" [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/textbutton/#__codelineno-2-6)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/textbutton/#__codelineno-2-7)     def button_clicked(e):        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/textbutton/#__codelineno-2-8)         button.data += 1        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/textbutton/#__codelineno-2-9)         message_text.value = f"Button clicked {button.data} time(s)"        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/textbutton/#__codelineno-2-10)         page.update() [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/textbutton/#__codelineno-2-11)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/textbutton/#__codelineno-2-12)     message_text = ft.Text()    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/textbutton/#__codelineno-2-13)     page.add(        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/textbutton/#__codelineno-2-14)         button := ft.TextButton(            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/textbutton/#__codelineno-2-15)             key="TextButton",            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/textbutton/#__codelineno-2-16)             content="Button with 'click' event",            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/textbutton/#__codelineno-2-17)             data=0,            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/textbutton/#__codelineno-2-18)             on_click=button_clicked,        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/textbutton/#__codelineno-2-19)         ),        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/textbutton/#__codelineno-2-20)         message := ft.Container(content=message_text, padding=ft.Padding(left=12)),    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/textbutton/#__codelineno-2-21)     ) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/textbutton/#__codelineno-2-22) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/textbutton/#__codelineno-2-23)[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/textbutton/#__codelineno-2-24) if __name__ == "__main__":     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/textbutton/#__codelineno-2-25)     ft.run(main)`

[![handling-clicks](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/material/golden/macos/text_button/handling_clicks.png)](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/material/golden/macos/text_button/handling_clicks.png)

### Custom content[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/textbutton/#custom-content "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/textbutton/#__codelineno-3-1) import flet as ft [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/textbutton/#__codelineno-3-2) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/textbutton/#__codelineno-3-3)[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/textbutton/#__codelineno-3-4) def main(page: ft.Page):     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/textbutton/#__codelineno-3-5)     page.title = "TextButtons with custom content" [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/textbutton/#__codelineno-3-6)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/textbutton/#__codelineno-3-7)     page.add(        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/textbutton/#__codelineno-3-8)         ft.TextButton( ft.Icon(ft.Icons.FAVORITE, color=ft.Colors.PINK),                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/textbutton/#__codelineno-3-14)                     ft.Icon(ft.Icons.AUDIOTRACK, color=ft.Colors.GREEN),                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/textbutton/#__codelineno-3-15)                     ft.Icon(ft.Icons.BEACH_ACCESS, color=ft.Colors.BLUE), [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/textbutton/#__codelineno-3-18)         ),        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/textbutton/#__codelineno-3-19)         ft.TextButton(            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/textbutton/#__codelineno-3-20)             content=ft.Container(                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/textbutton/#__codelineno-3-21)                 padding=ft.Padding.all(10),                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/textbutton/#__codelineno-3-22)                 content=ft.Column(                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/textbutton/#__codelineno-3-23)                     alignment=ft.MainAxisAlignment.CENTER,                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/textbutton/#__codelineno-3-24)                     spacing=5,                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/textbutton/#__codelineno-3-25)                     controls=[                        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/textbutton/#__codelineno-3-26)                         ft.Text(value="Compound button", size=20),                        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/textbutton/#__codelineno-3-27)                         ft.Text(value="This is secondary text"),                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/textbutton/#__codelineno-3-28)                     ],                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/textbutton/#__codelineno-3-29)                 ),            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/textbutton/#__codelineno-3-30)             ),        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/textbutton/#__codelineno-3-31)         ),
