# ScrollableControl - Flet

 

[Skip to content](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/scrollablecontrol/#flet.ScrollableControl)

ScrollableControl
=================

Inherits: `[Control](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control) `

Shared scroll behavior for controls that expose a scrollable viewport.

This mixin-style control is inherited by controls such as [`Column`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#flet.Column)
, [`Row`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/row/#flet.Row)
, [`View`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/view/#flet.View)
, [`ListView`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listview/#flet.ListView)
, and [`GridView`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/gridview/#flet.GridView)
. It provides a common API for:

*   enabling/disabling scrolling and scrollbar visibility via [`scroll`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/scrollablecontrol/#flet.ScrollableControl.scroll)
    ;
*   receiving throttled scroll notifications via [`on_scroll`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/scrollablecontrol/#flet.ScrollableControl.on_scroll)
     and [`scroll_interval`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/scrollablecontrol/#flet.ScrollableControl.scroll_interval)
    ;
*   imperatively changing position with [`scroll_to()`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/scrollablecontrol/#flet.ScrollableControl.scroll_to)
    .

Properties

*   **`[auto_scroll](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/scrollablecontrol/#flet.ScrollableControl.auto_scroll) `**(`[bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool) `) –
    
    Whether the scrollbar should automatically move its position to the end when children updated.
    
*   **`[scroll](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/scrollablecontrol/#flet.ScrollableControl.scroll) `**(`[ScrollMode](https://docs.flet.dev/types/scrollmode/#flet.ScrollMode)  | [Scrollbar](https://docs.flet.dev/types/scrollbar/#flet.Scrollbar)  | None`) –
    
    Defines the scroll bar configuration of this control.
    
*   **`[scroll_interval](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/scrollablecontrol/#flet.ScrollableControl.scroll_interval) `**(`[Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number) `) –
    
    Throttling in milliseconds for [`on_scroll`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/scrollablecontrol/#flet.ScrollableControl.on_scroll)
     event.
    

Events

*   **`[on_scroll](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/scrollablecontrol/#flet.ScrollableControl.on_scroll) `**(`[EventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.EventHandler) [[OnScrollEvent](https://docs.flet.dev/types/onscrollevent/#flet.OnScrollEvent) ] | None`) –
    
    Called when scroll position is changed by a user.
    

Methods

*   **`[scroll_to](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/scrollablecontrol/#flet.ScrollableControl.scroll_to) `** –
    
    Moves the scroll position.
    

Properties[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/scrollablecontrol/#flet.ScrollableControl-properties "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### auto\_scroll `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/scrollablecontrol/#flet.ScrollableControl.auto_scroll "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/scrollablecontrol/#__codelineno-0-1) auto_scroll: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)  = False`

Whether the scrollbar should automatically move its position to the end when children updated.

Note

Must be `False` for [`scroll_to()`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/scrollablecontrol/#flet.ScrollableControl.scroll_to)
 method to work.

### scroll `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/scrollablecontrol/#flet.ScrollableControl.scroll "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/scrollablecontrol/#__codelineno-0-1) scroll: [ScrollMode](https://docs.flet.dev/types/scrollmode/#flet.ScrollMode)  | [Scrollbar](https://docs.flet.dev/types/scrollbar/#flet.Scrollbar)  | None = None`

Defines the scroll bar configuration of this control.

Can be a [`Scrollbar`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/scrollbar/#flet.Scrollbar)
 instance for full control over the appearance of the scrollbar, or a [`ScrollMode`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/scrollmode/#flet.ScrollMode)
 value, for ready-made scrollbar behaviors.

### scroll\_interval `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/scrollablecontrol/#flet.ScrollableControl.scroll_interval "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/scrollablecontrol/#__codelineno-0-1) scroll_interval: [Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  = 10`

Throttling in milliseconds for [`on_scroll`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/scrollablecontrol/#flet.ScrollableControl.on_scroll)
 event.

Events[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/scrollablecontrol/#flet.ScrollableControl-events "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### on\_scroll `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/scrollablecontrol/#flet.ScrollableControl.on_scroll "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/scrollablecontrol/#__codelineno-0-1) on_scroll: [EventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.EventHandler) [[OnScrollEvent](https://docs.flet.dev/types/onscrollevent/#flet.OnScrollEvent) ] | None = None`

Called when scroll position is changed by a user.

Methods[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/scrollablecontrol/#flet.ScrollableControl-methods "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### scroll\_to `async` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/scrollablecontrol/#flet.ScrollableControl.scroll_to "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/scrollablecontrol/#__codelineno-0-1) scroll_to(     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/scrollablecontrol/#__codelineno-0-2)     offset: [float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float)  | None = None,    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/scrollablecontrol/#__codelineno-0-3)     delta: [float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float)  | None = None,    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/scrollablecontrol/#__codelineno-0-4)     scroll_key: [ScrollKey](https://docs.flet.dev/types/scrollkey/#flet.ScrollKey)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/scrollablecontrol/#__codelineno-0-5)     | [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/scrollablecontrol/#__codelineno-0-6)     | [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/scrollablecontrol/#__codelineno-0-7)     | [float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/scrollablecontrol/#__codelineno-0-8)     | [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/scrollablecontrol/#__codelineno-0-9)     | None = None,    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/scrollablecontrol/#__codelineno-0-10)     duration: [DurationValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.DurationValue)  = 0,    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/scrollablecontrol/#__codelineno-0-11)     curve: [AnimationCurve](https://docs.flet.dev/types/animationcurve/#flet.AnimationCurve)  = [EASE](https://docs.flet.dev/types/animationcurve/#flet.AnimationCurve.EASE) , [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/scrollablecontrol/#__codelineno-0-12) )`

Moves the scroll position.

Parameters:

*   **`offset`** (`[float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float)  | None`, default: `None` ) –
    
    Absolute scroll target in pixels. A negative value is interpreted relative to the end (e.g. `-1` to jump to the very end).
    
*   **`delta`** (`[float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float)  | None`, default: `None` ) –
    
    Relative scroll change in pixels. Positive values scroll forward, negative values scroll backward.
    
*   **`scroll_key`** (`[ScrollKey](https://docs.flet.dev/types/scrollkey/#flet.ScrollKey)  | [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)  | [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int)  | [float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float)  | [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)  | None`, default: `None` ) –
    
    Key of the target control to scroll to.
    
*   **`duration`** (`[DurationValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.DurationValue) `, default: `0` ) –
    
    The scroll animation duration.
    
*   **`curve`** (`[AnimationCurve](https://docs.flet.dev/types/animationcurve/#flet.AnimationCurve) `, default: `[EASE](https://docs.flet.dev/types/animationcurve/#flet.AnimationCurve.EASE) ` ) –
    
    The scroll animation curve.
    

Notes

*   Exactly one of `offset`, `delta` or `scroll_key` should be provided.
*   [`auto_scroll`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/scrollablecontrol/#flet.ScrollableControl.auto_scroll)
     must be `False`.
*   This method is ineffective for controls (e.g. [`ListView`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listview/#flet.ListView)
    , [`GridView`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/gridview/#flet.GridView)
    ) that build items dynamically.

Examples:

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/scrollablecontrol/#__codelineno-0-1) await products.scroll_to(offset=100, duration=1000) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/scrollablecontrol/#__codelineno-0-2) await products.scroll_to(offset=-1, duration=1000)  # to the end [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/scrollablecontrol/#__codelineno-0-3) await products.scroll_to(delta=50)  # forward 50px [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/scrollablecontrol/#__codelineno-0-4) await products.scroll_to(scroll_key="item_20", duration=500)`

Back to top

---

# TimePicker - Flet

 

[Skip to content](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#flet.TimePicker)

TimePicker
==========

A Material-style time picker dialog.

Can be opened by calling the [`Page.show_dialog()`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#flet.BasePage.show_dialog)
 method.

Depending on the [`entry_mode`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#flet.TimePicker.entry_mode)
, it will show either a Dial or an Input (hour and minute text fields) for picking a time.

Example:

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-0-1) ft.TimePicker(     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-0-2)     open=True,    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-0-3)     value=time(1, 2),    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-0-4)     entry_mode=ft.TimePickerEntryMode.INPUT_ONLY, [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-0-5) )`

[![TimePicker](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/material/golden/macos/time_picker/image_for_docs.png)](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/material/golden/macos/time_picker/image_for_docs.png)

Time picker

Inherits: `[DialogControl](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/dialogcontrol/#flet.DialogControl) `

Properties

*   **`[barrier_color](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#flet.TimePicker.barrier_color) `**(`[ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None`) –
    
    The color of the modal barrier that darkens everything below this picker's dialog.
    
*   **`[cancel_text](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#flet.TimePicker.cancel_text) `**(`[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)  | None`) –
    
    The text that is displayed on the cancel button.
    
*   **`[confirm_text](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#flet.TimePicker.confirm_text) `**(`[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)  | None`) –
    
    The text that is displayed on the confirm button.
    
*   **`[entry_mode](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#flet.TimePicker.entry_mode) `**(`[TimePickerEntryMode](https://docs.flet.dev/types/timepickerentrymode/#flet.TimePickerEntryMode) `) –
    
    The initial mode of time entry method for this picker.
    
*   **`[error_invalid_text](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#flet.TimePicker.error_invalid_text) `**(`[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)  | None`) –
    
    The error message displayed below the input text field if the input is not a valid hour/minute.
    
*   **`[help_text](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#flet.TimePicker.help_text) `**(`[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)  | None`) –
    
    The text that is displayed at the top of the header.
    
*   **`[hour_format](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#flet.TimePicker.hour_format) `**(`[TimePickerHourFormat](https://docs.flet.dev/types/timepickerhourformat/#flet.TimePickerHourFormat) `) –
    
    Defines the hour format of this time picker.
    
*   **`[hour_label_text](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#flet.TimePicker.hour_label_text) `**(`[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)  | None`) –
    
    The text that is displayed below the hour input text field.
    
*   **`[locale](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#flet.TimePicker.locale) `**(`[Locale](https://docs.flet.dev/types/locale/#flet.Locale)  | None`) –
    
    The locale for this time picker dialog. It is intended for (rare) cases where this dialog should be localized differently from the rest of the page.
    
*   **`[minute_label_text](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#flet.TimePicker.minute_label_text) `**(`[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)  | None`) –
    
    The text that is displayed below the minute input text field.
    
*   **`[modal](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#flet.TimePicker.modal) `**(`[bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool) `) –
    
    Whether this picker cannot be dismissed by clicking the area outside of it.
    
*   **`[orientation](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#flet.TimePicker.orientation) `**(`[Orientation](https://docs.flet.dev/types/orientation/#flet.Orientation)  | None`) –
    
    The orientation of the dialog when displayed.
    
*   **`[switch_to_input_icon](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#flet.TimePicker.switch_to_input_icon) `**(`[IconData](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.IconData)  | None`) –
    
    The icon displayed in the corner of this picker's dialog when [`entry_mode`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#flet.TimePicker.entry_mode)
     is [`TimePickerEntryMode.DIAL`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/timepickerentrymode/#flet.TimePickerEntryMode.DIAL)
    .
    
*   **`[switch_to_timer_icon](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#flet.TimePicker.switch_to_timer_icon) `**(`[IconData](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.IconData)  | None`) –
    
    The icon displayed in the corner of this picker's dialog when [`entry_mode`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#flet.TimePicker.entry_mode)
     is [`TimePickerEntryMode.INPUT`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/timepickerentrymode/#flet.TimePickerEntryMode.INPUT)
    .
    
*   **`[value](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#flet.TimePicker.value) `**(`[time](https://docs.python.org/3/library/datetime.html#datetime.time "<code>datetime.time</code>")  | None`) –
    
    The selected time that this picker should display.
    

Events

*   **`[on_change](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#flet.TimePicker.on_change) `**(`[ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[TimePicker](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#flet.TimePicker) ] | None`) –
    
    Called when user clicks confirm button.
    
*   **`[on_entry_mode_change](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#flet.TimePicker.on_entry_mode_change) `**(`[EventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.EventHandler) [[TimePickerEntryModeChangeEvent](https://docs.flet.dev/types/timepickerentrymodechangeevent/#flet.TimePickerEntryModeChangeEvent) ] | None`) –
    
    Called when the [`entry_mode`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#flet.TimePicker.entry_mode)
     is changed through the time picker dialog.
    

Examples[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#examples "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[Live example](https://flet-controls-gallery.fly.dev/dialogs/timepicker)

### Basic Example[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#basic-example "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-0-1) from datetime import time [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-0-2)[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-0-3) import flet as ft [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-0-4) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-0-5)[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-0-6) def main(page: ft.Page):     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-0-7)     page.horizontal_alignment = ft.CrossAxisAlignment.CENTER [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-0-8)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-0-9)     def handle_change(e: ft.Event[ft.TimePicker]):        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-0-10)         selection.value = f"Selection: {time_picker.value}"        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-0-11)         page.show_dialog(ft.SnackBar(f"TimePicker change: {time_picker.value}")) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-0-12)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-0-13)     def handle_dismissal(e: ft.Event[ft.DialogControl]):        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-0-14)         page.show_dialog(ft.SnackBar("TimePicker dismissed!")) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-0-15)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-0-16)     def handle_entry_mode_change(e: ft.TimePickerEntryModeChangeEvent):        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-0-17)         page.show_dialog(ft.SnackBar(f"Entry mode changed: {time_picker.entry_mode}")) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-0-18)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-0-19)     time_picker = ft.TimePicker(        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-0-20)         value=time(hour=19, minute=30),        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-0-21)         confirm_text="Confirm",        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-0-22)         error_invalid_text="Time out of range",        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-0-23)         help_text="Pick your time slot",        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-0-24)         entry_mode=ft.TimePickerEntryMode.DIAL,        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-0-25)         on_change=handle_change,        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-0-26)         on_dismiss=handle_dismissal,        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-0-27)         on_entry_mode_change=handle_entry_mode_change,    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-0-28)     ) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-0-29)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-0-30)     page.add(        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-0-31)         ft.Button(            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-0-32)             content="Pick time",            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-0-33)             icon=ft.Icons.TIME_TO_LEAVE,            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-0-34)             on_click=lambda: page.show_dialog(time_picker),        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-0-35)         ),        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-0-36)         selection := ft.Text(weight=ft.FontWeight.BOLD),    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-0-37)     ) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-0-38) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-0-39)[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-0-40) if __name__ == "__main__": ### Hour Formats[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#hour-formats "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-1-1) from datetime import time [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-1-2)[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-1-3) import flet as ft [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-1-4) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-1-5)[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-1-6) def main(page: ft.Page):     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-1-7)     page.horizontal_alignment = ft.CrossAxisAlignment.CENTER [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-1-8)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-1-9)     def get_system_hour_format():        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-1-10)         """Returns the current system's hour format."""        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-1-11)         return "24h" if page.media.always_use_24_hour_format else "12h" [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-1-12)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-1-13)     def format_time(value: time) -> str:        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-1-14)         """Returns a formatted time string based on TimePicker and system settings."""        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-1-15)         use_24h = time_picker.hour_format == ft.TimePickerHourFormat.H24 or (            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-1-16)             time_picker.hour_format == ft.TimePickerHourFormat.SYSTEM            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-1-17)             and page.media.always_use_24_hour_format        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-1-18)         )        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-1-19)         return value.strftime("%H:%M" if use_24h else "%I:%M %p") [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-1-20)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-1-21)     def handle_change(e: ft.Event[ft.TimePicker]):        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-1-22)         selection.value = f"Selection: {format_time(time_picker.value)}" [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-1-23)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-1-24)     time_picker = ft.TimePicker(        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-1-25)         value=time(hour=19, minute=30),        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-1-26)         help_text="Choose your meeting time",        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-1-27)         on_change=handle_change,    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-1-28)     ) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-1-29)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-1-30)     def open_picker(e: ft.Event[ft.Button]):        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-1-31)         choice = format_dropdown.value        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-1-32)         hour_format_map = {            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-1-33)             "system": ft.TimePickerHourFormat.SYSTEM,            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-1-34)             "12h": ft.TimePickerHourFormat.H12,            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-1-35)             "24h": ft.TimePickerHourFormat.H24,        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-1-36)         }        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-1-37)         time_picker.hour_format = hour_format_map[choice]        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-1-38)         page.show_dialog(time_picker) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-1-39)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-1-40)     page.add(        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-1-41)         ft.Row(            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-1-42)             alignment=ft.MainAxisAlignment.CENTER,            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-1-43)             controls=[                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-1-44)                 format_dropdown := ft.Dropdown(                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-1-45)                     label="Hour format",                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-1-46)                     value="system",                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-1-47)                     width=260,                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-1-48)                     key="dd",                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-1-49)                     options=[                        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-1-50)                         ft.DropdownOption(                            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-1-51)                             key="system",                            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-1-52)                             text=f"System default ({get_system_hour_format()})",                        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-1-53)                         ), "Open TimePicker",                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-1-60)                     icon=ft.Icons.SCHEDULE, [![hour_formats.gif](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/material/golden/macos/time_picker/hour_formats.gif)](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/material/golden/macos/time_picker/hour_formats.gif)

Properties[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#flet.TimePicker-properties "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### barrier\_color `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#flet.TimePicker.barrier_color "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-0-1) barrier_color: [ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None = None`

The color of the modal barrier that darkens everything below this picker's dialog.

If `None`, the [`DialogTheme.barrier_color`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/dialogtheme/#flet.DialogTheme.barrier_color)
 is used. If it is also `None`, then [`Colors.BLACK_54`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/colors/#flet.Colors.BLACK_54)
 is used.

### cancel\_text `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#flet.TimePicker.cancel_text "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-0-1) cancel_text: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)  | None = None`

The text that is displayed on the cancel button.

The default value is `"Cancel"`.

### confirm\_text `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#flet.TimePicker.confirm_text "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-0-1) confirm_text: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)  | None = None`

The text that is displayed on the confirm button.

The default value is `"OK"`.

### entry\_mode `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#flet.TimePicker.entry_mode "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-0-1) entry_mode: [TimePickerEntryMode](https://docs.flet.dev/types/timepickerentrymode/#flet.TimePickerEntryMode)  = [DIAL](https://docs.flet.dev/types/timepickerentrymode/#flet.TimePickerEntryMode.DIAL)`

The initial mode of time entry method for this picker.

Defaults to [`TimePickerEntryMode.DIAL`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/timepickerentrymode/#flet.TimePickerEntryMode.DIAL)
.

### error\_invalid\_text `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#flet.TimePicker.error_invalid_text "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-0-1) error_invalid_text: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)  | None = None`

The error message displayed below the input text field if the input is not a valid hour/minute.

The default value is `"Enter a valid time"`.

### help\_text `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#flet.TimePicker.help_text "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-0-1) help_text: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)  | None = None`

The text that is displayed at the top of the header.

This is used to indicate to the user what they are selecting a time for. The default value is `"Enter time"`.

### hour\_format `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#flet.TimePicker.hour_format "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-0-1) hour_format: [TimePickerHourFormat](https://docs.flet.dev/types/timepickerhourformat/#flet.TimePickerHourFormat)  = [SYSTEM](https://docs.flet.dev/types/timepickerhourformat/#flet.TimePickerHourFormat.SYSTEM)`

Defines the hour format of this time picker.

### hour\_label\_text `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#flet.TimePicker.hour_label_text "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-0-1) hour_label_text: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)  | None = None`

The text that is displayed below the hour input text field.

The default value is `"Hour"`.

### locale `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#flet.TimePicker.locale "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-0-1) locale: [Locale](https://docs.flet.dev/types/locale/#flet.Locale)  | None = None`

The locale for this time picker dialog. It is intended for (rare) cases where this dialog should be localized differently from the rest of the page.

It overrides the locale used by the page (see [`Page.locale_configuration`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#flet.BasePage.locale_configuration)
), but does not participate in page-level locale resolution.

If set to `None` (the default) or an inexistent/unsupported locale, the [`current_locale`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/localeconfiguration/#flet.LocaleConfiguration.current_locale)
 of the [`Page.locale_configuration`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#flet.BasePage.locale_configuration)
 is used as fallback.

### minute\_label\_text `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#flet.TimePicker.minute_label_text "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-0-1) minute_label_text: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)  | None = None`

The text that is displayed below the minute input text field.

The default value is `"Minute"`.

### modal `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#flet.TimePicker.modal "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-0-1) modal: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)  = False`

Whether this picker cannot be dismissed by clicking the area outside of it.

### orientation `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#flet.TimePicker.orientation "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-0-1) orientation: [Orientation](https://docs.flet.dev/types/orientation/#flet.Orientation)  | None = None`

The orientation of the dialog when displayed.

### switch\_to\_input\_icon `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#flet.TimePicker.switch_to_input_icon "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-0-1) switch_to_input_icon: [IconData](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.IconData)  | None = None`

The icon displayed in the corner of this picker's dialog when [`entry_mode`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#flet.TimePicker.entry_mode)
 is [`TimePickerEntryMode.DIAL`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/timepickerentrymode/#flet.TimePickerEntryMode.DIAL)
.

Clicking on icon changes the [`entry_mode`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#flet.TimePicker.entry_mode)
 to [`TimePickerEntryMode.INPUT`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/timepickerentrymode/#flet.TimePickerEntryMode.INPUT)
.

If `None`, defaults to `Icons.KEYBOARD_OUTLINED`.

### switch\_to\_timer\_icon `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#flet.TimePicker.switch_to_timer_icon "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-0-1) switch_to_timer_icon: [IconData](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.IconData)  | None = None`

The icon displayed in the corner of this picker's dialog when [`entry_mode`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#flet.TimePicker.entry_mode)
 is [`TimePickerEntryMode.INPUT`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/timepickerentrymode/#flet.TimePickerEntryMode.INPUT)
.

Clicking on this icon changes the [`entry_mode`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#flet.TimePicker.entry_mode)
 to [`TimePickerEntryMode.DIAL`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/timepickerentrymode/#flet.TimePickerEntryMode.DIAL)
.

If `None`, defaults to `Icons.ACCESS_TIME`.

### value `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#flet.TimePicker.value "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-0-1) value: [time](https://docs.python.org/3/library/datetime.html#datetime.time "<code>datetime.time</code>")  | None = [field](https://docs.python.org/3/library/dataclasses.html#dataclasses.field "<code>dataclasses.field</code>") (default_factory=lambda: [time](https://docs.python.org/3/library/time.html#module-time) ())`

The selected time that this picker should display.

The default value is equal to the current time.

Events[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#flet.TimePicker-events "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### on\_change `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#flet.TimePicker.on_change "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-0-1) on_change: [ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[TimePicker](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#flet.TimePicker) ] | None = None`

Called when user clicks confirm button.

[`value`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#flet.TimePicker.value)
 property is updated with selected time. Additionally, the [`data`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/event/#flet.Event.data)
 property of the event handler argument also contains the selected time.

### on\_entry\_mode\_change `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#flet.TimePicker.on_entry_mode_change "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-0-1) on_entry_mode_change: (     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-0-2)     [EventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.EventHandler) [[TimePickerEntryModeChangeEvent](https://docs.flet.dev/types/timepickerentrymodechangeevent/#flet.TimePickerEntryModeChangeEvent) ] | None [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#__codelineno-0-3) ) = None`

Called when the [`entry_mode`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/timepicker/#flet.TimePicker.entry_mode)
 is changed through the time picker dialog.

Back to top

# SnackBar - Flet

 

[Skip to content](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#flet.SnackBar)

SnackBar
========

A lightweight message with an optional action which briefly displays at the bottom of the screen.

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#__codelineno-0-1) page.show_dialog(ft.SnackBar(ft.Text("Opened snack bar")))`

[![SnackBar](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/material/golden/macos/snack_bar/image_for_docs.png)](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/material/golden/macos/snack_bar/image_for_docs.png)

Opened snack bar

Inherits: `[DialogControl](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/dialogcontrol/#flet.DialogControl) `

Properties

*   **`[action](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#flet.SnackBar.action) `**(`[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)  | [SnackBarAction](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#flet.SnackBarAction)  | None`) –
    
    An optional action that the user can take based on the snack bar.
    
*   **`[action_overflow_threshold](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#flet.SnackBar.action_overflow_threshold) `**(`[Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None`) –
    
    The percentage threshold for [`action`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#flet.SnackBar.action)
    's width before it overflows to a new line.
    
*   **`[behavior](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#flet.SnackBar.behavior) `**(`[SnackBarBehavior](https://docs.flet.dev/types/snackbarbehavior/#flet.SnackBarBehavior)  | None`) –
    
    This defines the behavior and location of the snack bar.
    
*   **`[bgcolor](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#flet.SnackBar.bgcolor) `**(`[ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None`) –
    
    SnackBar background color.
    
*   **`[clip_behavior](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#flet.SnackBar.clip_behavior) `**(`[ClipBehavior](https://docs.flet.dev/types/clipbehavior/#flet.ClipBehavior) `) –
    
    The [`content`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#flet.SnackBar.content)
     will be clipped (or not) according to this option.
    
*   **`[close_icon_color](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#flet.SnackBar.close_icon_color) `**(`[ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None`) –
    
    The color of the close icon, if [`show_close_icon`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#flet.SnackBar.show_close_icon)
     is `True`.
    
*   **`[content](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#flet.SnackBar.content) `**(`[StrOrControl](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.StrOrControl) `) –
    
    The primary content of the snack bar.
    
*   **`[dismiss_direction](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#flet.SnackBar.dismiss_direction) `**(`[DismissDirection](https://docs.flet.dev/types/dismissdirection/#flet.DismissDirection)  | None`) –
    
    The direction in which the SnackBar can be dismissed.
    
*   **`[duration](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#flet.SnackBar.duration) `**(`[DurationValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.DurationValue) `) –
    
    The amount of time this snack bar should stay open for.
    
*   **`[elevation](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#flet.SnackBar.elevation) `**(`[Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None`) –
    
    The z-coordinate at which to place the snack bar. This controls the size of the shadow below the snack bar.
    
*   **`[margin](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#flet.SnackBar.margin) `**(`[MarginValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.MarginValue)  | None`) –
    
    Empty space to surround the snack bar.
    
*   **`[padding](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#flet.SnackBar.padding) `**(`[PaddingValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.PaddingValue)  | None`) –
    
    The amount of padding to apply to the snack bar's content and optional action.
    
*   **`[persist](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#flet.SnackBar.persist) `**(`[bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)  | None`) –
    
    Whether the snack bar will stay or auto-dismiss after timeout.
    
*   **`[shape](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#flet.SnackBar.shape) `**(`[OutlinedBorder](https://docs.flet.dev/types/outlinedborder/#flet.OutlinedBorder)  | None`) –
    
    The shape of this snack bar.
    
*   **`[show_close_icon](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#flet.SnackBar.show_close_icon) `**(`[bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool) `) –
    
    Whether to include a "close" icon widget.
    
*   **`[width](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#flet.SnackBar.width) `**(`[Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None`) –
    
    The width of the snack bar.
    

Events

*   **`[on_action](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#flet.SnackBar.on_action) `**(`[ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[SnackBar](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#flet.SnackBar) ] | None`) –
    
    Called when action button is clicked.
    
*   **`[on_visible](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#flet.SnackBar.on_visible) `**(`[ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[SnackBar](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#flet.SnackBar) ] | None`) –
    
    Called the first time that the snackbar is visible within the page.
    

Examples[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#examples "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[Live example](https://flet-controls-gallery.fly.dev/dialogs/snackbar)

### Basic Example[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#basic-example "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#__codelineno-0-1) import flet as ft [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#__codelineno-0-2) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#__codelineno-0-3)[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#__codelineno-0-4) def main(page: ft.Page):     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#__codelineno-0-5)     def on_click(e: ft.Event[ft.Button]):        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#__codelineno-0-6)         page.show_dialog(ft.SnackBar(ft.Text("Hello, world!"))) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#__codelineno-0-7)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#__codelineno-0-8)     page.add(ft.Button("Open SnackBar", on_click=on_click)) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#__codelineno-0-9) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#__codelineno-0-10)[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#__codelineno-0-11) if __name__ == "__main__":     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#__codelineno-0-12)     ft.run(main)`

[![basic](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/material/golden/macos/snack_bar/basic.png)](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/material/golden/macos/snack_bar/basic.png)

### Counter[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#counter "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#__codelineno-1-1) import flet as ft [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#__codelineno-1-2) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#__codelineno-1-3)[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#__codelineno-1-4) class Data:     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#__codelineno-1-5)     def __init__(self) -> None:        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#__codelineno-1-6)         self.counter = 0 [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#__codelineno-1-7)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#__codelineno-1-8)     def increment(self):        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#__codelineno-1-9)         self.counter += 1 [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#__codelineno-1-10)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#__codelineno-1-11)     def decrement(self):        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#__codelineno-1-12)         self.counter -= 1 [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#__codelineno-1-13) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#__codelineno-1-14)[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#__codelineno-1-15) data = Data() [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#__codelineno-1-16) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#__codelineno-1-17)[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#__codelineno-1-18) def main(page: ft.Page):     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#__codelineno-1-19)     page.title = "SnackBar Example" [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#__codelineno-1-20)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#__codelineno-1-21)     snack_bar = ft.SnackBar(        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#__codelineno-1-22)         content=ft.Text("You did it!"),        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#__codelineno-1-23)         action="Undo it!",        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#__codelineno-1-24)         on_action=lambda e: data.decrement(),    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#__codelineno-1-25)     ) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#__codelineno-1-26)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#__codelineno-1-27)     def handle_button_click(e: ft.Event[ft.Button]):        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#__codelineno-1-28)         data.increment()        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#__codelineno-1-29)         snack_bar.content.value = f"You did it x {data.counter}"        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#__codelineno-1-30)         if not snack_bar.open:            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#__codelineno-1-31)             page.show_dialog(snack_bar)        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#__codelineno-1-32)         page.update() [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#__codelineno-1-33)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#__codelineno-1-34)     page.add(ft.Button("Open SnackBar", on_click=handle_button_click)) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#__codelineno-1-35) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#__codelineno-1-36)[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#__codelineno-1-37) if __name__ == "__main__":     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#__codelineno-1-38)     ft.run(main)`

Snack bar with counter

### Action[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#action "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#__codelineno-2-1) import flet as ft [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#__codelineno-2-2) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#__codelineno-2-5)     def open_simple_action(e: ft.Event[ft.Button]):        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#__codelineno-2-6)         page.show_dialog(            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#__codelineno-2-7)             ft.SnackBar(                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#__codelineno-2-8)                 ft.Text("The file has been deleted."),                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#__codelineno-2-9)                 action="Undo",                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#__codelineno-2-10)                 on_action=lambda e: print("Simple Undo clicked"),            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#__codelineno-2-11)             )        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#__codelineno-2-12)         )   [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#__codelineno-2-17)                 ft.Text("The directory has been deleted."),                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#__codelineno-2-18)                 persist=False,                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#__codelineno-2-19)                 action=ft.SnackBarAction(                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#__codelineno-2-20)                     label="Undo delete",                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#__codelineno-2-21)                     text_color=ft.Colors.YELLOW,                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#__codelineno-2-22)                     bgcolor=ft.Colors.BLUE,                    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#__codelineno-2-23)                     on_click=lambda e: print("Custom Undo clicked"),                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#__codelineno-2-24)                 ),            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#__codelineno-2-25)             ) action", on_click=open_simple_action),        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#__codelineno-2-30)         ft.Button("Open SnackBar with a Custom action", on_click=open_custom_action), Snack bar with a simple action

[![Snack bar with a custom action](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/material/golden/macos/snack_bar/action_custom.png)](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/material/golden/macos/snack_bar/action_custom.png)

Snack bar with a custom action

Properties[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#flet.SnackBar-properties "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### action `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#flet.SnackBar.action "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#__codelineno-0-1) action: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)  | [SnackBarAction](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#flet.SnackBarAction)  | None = None`

An optional action that the user can take based on the snack bar.

For example, the snack bar might let the user undo the operation that prompted the snackbar. Snack bars can have at most one action.

The action should not be "dismiss" or "cancel".

### action\_overflow\_threshold `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#flet.SnackBar.action_overflow_threshold "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#__codelineno-0-1) action_overflow_threshold: [Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None = 0.25`

The percentage threshold for [`action`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#flet.SnackBar.action)
's width before it overflows to a new line.

If the width of the snackbar's [`content`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#flet.SnackBar.content)
 is greater than this percentage of the width of the snackbar minus the width of its `action`, then the `action` will appear below the [`content`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#flet.SnackBar.content)
.

At a value of `0.0`, the `action` will not overflow to a new line.

Raises:

*   `[ValueError](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ValueError) ` –
    
    If it is not between `0.0` and `1.0`, inclusive.
    

### behavior `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#flet.SnackBar.behavior "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#__codelineno-0-1) behavior: [SnackBarBehavior](https://docs.flet.dev/types/snackbarbehavior/#flet.SnackBarBehavior)  | None = None`

This defines the behavior and location of the snack bar.

Defines where a SnackBar should appear within a page and how its location should be adjusted when the page also includes a [`FloatingActionButton`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#flet.FloatingActionButton)
 or a [`NavigationBar`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/navigationbar/#flet.NavigationBar)
.

If `None`, [`SnackBarTheme.behavior`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/snackbartheme/#flet.SnackBarTheme.behavior)
 is used. If that's is also `None`, defaults to [`SnackBarBehavior.FIXED`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/snackbarbehavior/#flet.SnackBarBehavior.FIXED)
.

Note

*   If [`behavior`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#flet.SnackBar.behavior)
     is [`SnackBarBehavior.FLOATING`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/snackbarbehavior/#flet.SnackBarBehavior.FLOATING)
    , the length of the bar is defined by either [`width`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#flet.SnackBar.width)
     and [`margin`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#flet.SnackBar.margin)
    , and if both are specified, `width` takes precedence over `margin`.
*   [`width`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#flet.SnackBar.width)
     and [`margin`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#flet.SnackBar.margin)
     are ignored if [`behavior`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#flet.SnackBar.behavior)
     is not [`SnackBarBehavior.FLOATING`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/snackbarbehavior/#flet.SnackBarBehavior.FLOATING)
    .

### bgcolor `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#flet.SnackBar.bgcolor "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#__codelineno-0-1) bgcolor: [ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None = None`

SnackBar background color.

### clip\_behavior `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#flet.SnackBar.clip_behavior "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#__codelineno-0-1) clip_behavior: [ClipBehavior](https://docs.flet.dev/types/clipbehavior/#flet.ClipBehavior)  = [HARD_EDGE](https://docs.flet.dev/types/clipbehavior/#flet.ClipBehavior.HARD_EDGE)`

The [`content`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#flet.SnackBar.content)
 will be clipped (or not) according to this option.

### close\_icon\_color `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#flet.SnackBar.close_icon_color "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#__codelineno-0-1) close_icon_color: [ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None = None`

The color of the close icon, if [`show_close_icon`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#flet.SnackBar.show_close_icon)
 is `True`.

### content `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#flet.SnackBar.content "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#__codelineno-0-1) content: [StrOrControl](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.StrOrControl)`

The primary content of the snack bar.

Typically a [`Text`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/text/#flet.Text)
 control.

Raises:

*   `[ValueError](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ValueError) ` –
    
    If it is neither a string nor a visible `Control`.
    

### dismiss\_direction `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#flet.SnackBar.dismiss_direction "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#__codelineno-0-1) dismiss_direction: [DismissDirection](https://docs.flet.dev/types/dismissdirection/#flet.DismissDirection)  | None = None`

The direction in which the SnackBar can be dismissed.

If `None`, [`SnackBarTheme.dismiss_direction`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/snackbartheme/#flet.SnackBarTheme.dismiss_direction)
 is used. If that's is also `None`, defaults to [`DismissDirection.DOWN`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/dismissdirection/#flet.DismissDirection.DOWN)
.

### duration `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#flet.SnackBar.duration "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#__codelineno-0-1) duration: [DurationValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.DurationValue)  = [field](https://docs.python.org/3/library/dataclasses.html#dataclasses.field "<code>dataclasses.field</code>") (     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#__codelineno-0-2)     default_factory=lambda: [Duration](https://docs.flet.dev/types/duration/#flet.Duration) (milliseconds=4000) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#__codelineno-0-3) )`

The amount of time this snack bar should stay open for.

### elevation `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#flet.SnackBar.elevation "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#__codelineno-0-1) elevation: [Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None = None`

The z-coordinate at which to place the snack bar. This controls the size of the shadow below the snack bar.

Raises:

*   `[ValueError](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ValueError) ` –
    
    If it is not greater than or equal to `0`.
    

### margin `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#flet.SnackBar.margin "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#__codelineno-0-1) margin: [MarginValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.MarginValue)  | None = None`

Empty space to surround the snack bar.

Has effect only when `behavior=SnackBarBehavior.FLOATING` and will be ignored if `width` is specified.

### padding `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#flet.SnackBar.padding "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#__codelineno-0-1) padding: [PaddingValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.PaddingValue)  | None = None`

The amount of padding to apply to the snack bar's content and optional action.

### persist `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#flet.SnackBar.persist "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#__codelineno-0-1) persist: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)  | None = None`

Whether the snack bar will stay or auto-dismiss after timeout.

If `True`, the snack bar remains visible even after the timeout, until the user taps the action button or the close icon.

If `False`, the snack bar will be dismissed after the timeout.

If not provided, but the snackbar [`action`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#flet.SnackBar.action)
 is not null, the snackbar will persist as well.

### shape `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#flet.SnackBar.shape "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#__codelineno-0-1) shape: [OutlinedBorder](https://docs.flet.dev/types/outlinedborder/#flet.OutlinedBorder)  | None = None`

The shape of this snack bar.

### show\_close\_icon `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#flet.SnackBar.show_close_icon "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#__codelineno-0-1) show_close_icon: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)  = False`

Whether to include a "close" icon widget.

Tapping the icon will close the snack bar.

### width `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#flet.SnackBar.width "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#__codelineno-0-1) width: [Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None = None`

The width of the snack bar.

If width is specified, the snack bar will be centered horizontally in the available space.

Note

Has effect only when [`behavior`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#flet.SnackBar.behavior)
 is [`SnackBarBehavior.FLOATING`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/snackbarbehavior/#flet.SnackBarBehavior.FLOATING)
. It can not be used if `margin` is specified.

Events[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#flet.SnackBar-events "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### on\_action `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#flet.SnackBar.on_action "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#__codelineno-0-1) on_action: [ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[SnackBar](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#flet.SnackBar) ] | None = None`

Called when action button is clicked.

### on\_visible `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#flet.SnackBar.on_visible "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#__codelineno-0-1) on_visible: [ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[SnackBar](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#flet.SnackBar) ] | None = None`

Called the first time that the snackbar is visible within the page.

Inherits: `[Control](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control) `

A button that can be used as an action in a [`SnackBar`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#flet.SnackBar)
.

An action button for a [`SnackBar`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#flet.SnackBar)
.

Note

*   Snack bar actions are always enabled. Instead of disabling a snack bar action, avoid including it in the snack bar in the first place.
*   Snack bar actions will only respond to first click. Subsequent clicks/presses are ignored.

Properties

*   **`[bgcolor](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#flet.SnackBarAction.bgcolor) `**(`[ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None`) –
    
    The button background fill color.
    
*   **`[disabled_bgcolor](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#flet.SnackBarAction.disabled_bgcolor) `**(`[ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None`) –
    
    The button disabled background color.
    
*   **`[disabled_text_color](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#flet.SnackBarAction.disabled_text_color) `**(`[ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None`) –
    
    The button disabled label color.
    
*   **`[label](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#flet.SnackBarAction.label) `**(`[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str) `) –
    
    The button's label.
    
*   **`[text_color](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#flet.SnackBarAction.text_color) `**(`[ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None`) –
    
    The button label color.
    

Events

*   **`[on_click](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#flet.SnackBarAction.on_click) `**(`[ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[SnackBarAction](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#flet.SnackBarAction) ] | None`) –
    
    Called when this action button is clicked.
    

Properties[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#flet.SnackBarAction-properties "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### bgcolor `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#flet.SnackBarAction.bgcolor "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#__codelineno-0-1) bgcolor: [ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None = None`

The button background fill color.

If `None`, [`SnackBarTheme.action_bgcolor`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/snackbartheme/#flet.SnackBarTheme.action_bgcolor)
 is used.

### disabled\_bgcolor `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#flet.SnackBarAction.disabled_bgcolor "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#__codelineno-0-1) disabled_bgcolor: [ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None = None`

The button disabled background color. This color is shown after the action is dismissed.

If `None`, [`SnackBarTheme.disabled_action_bgcolor`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/snackbartheme/#flet.SnackBarTheme.disabled_action_bgcolor)
 is used.

### disabled\_text\_color `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#flet.SnackBarAction.disabled_text_color "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#__codelineno-0-1) disabled_text_color: [ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None = None`

The button disabled label color. This color is shown after the action is dismissed.

### label `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#flet.SnackBarAction.label "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#__codelineno-0-1) label: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)`

The button's label.

### text\_color `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#flet.SnackBarAction.text_color "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#__codelineno-0-1) text_color: [ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None = None`

The button label color.

If `None`, [`SnackBarTheme.action_text_color`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/snackbartheme/#flet.SnackBarTheme.action_text_color)
 is used.

Events[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#flet.SnackBarAction-events "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### on\_click `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#flet.SnackBarAction.on_click "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#__codelineno-0-1) on_click: [ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[SnackBarAction](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/snackbar/#flet.SnackBarAction) ] | None = None`

# PrecisionType - Flet

 

[Skip to content](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/types/precisiontype/#flet_ads.types.PrecisionType)

PrecisionType
=============

Inherits: `[Enum](https://docs.python.org/3/library/enum.html#enum.Enum "<code>enum.Enum</code>") `

Describes how accurately a paid ad value is reported.

Properties

*   **`[ESTIMATED](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/types/precisiontype/#flet_ads.types.PrecisionType.ESTIMATED) `** –
    
    An ad value estimated from aggregated data.
    
*   **`[PRECISE](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/types/precisiontype/#flet_ads.types.PrecisionType.PRECISE) `** –
    
    The precise value paid for this ad.
    
*   **`[PUBLISHER_PROVIDED](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/types/precisiontype/#flet_ads.types.PrecisionType.PUBLISHER_PROVIDED) `** –
    
    A publisher-provided ad value, such as manual CPMs in a mediation group.
    
*   **`[UNKNOWN](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/types/precisiontype/#flet_ads.types.PrecisionType.UNKNOWN) `** –
    
    An ad value with unknown precision.
    

Properties[#](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/types/precisiontype/#flet_ads.types.PrecisionType-properties "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### ESTIMATED `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/types/precisiontype/#flet_ads.types.PrecisionType.ESTIMATED "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/types/precisiontype/#__codelineno-0-1) ESTIMATED = 'estimated'`

An ad value estimated from aggregated data.

### PRECISE `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/types/precisiontype/#flet_ads.types.PrecisionType.PRECISE "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/types/precisiontype/#__codelineno-0-1) PRECISE = 'precise'`

The precise value paid for this ad.

### PUBLISHER\_PROVIDED `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/types/precisiontype/#flet_ads.types.PrecisionType.PUBLISHER_PROVIDED "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/types/precisiontype/#__codelineno-0-1) PUBLISHER_PROVIDED = 'publisherProvided'`

A publisher-provided ad value, such as manual CPMs in a mediation group.

### UNKNOWN `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/types/precisiontype/#flet_ads.types.PrecisionType.UNKNOWN "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/ads/types/precisiontype/#__codelineno-0-1) UNKNOWN = 'unknown'`

An ad value with unknown precision.

Back to top
