# SearchBar - Flet

 

[Skip to content](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#flet.SearchBar)

SearchBar
=========

Manages a "search view" route that allows the user to select one of the suggested completions for a search query.

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#__codelineno-0-1) ft.SearchBar(bar_hint_text="Search...")`

[![SearchBar](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/material/golden/macos/search_bar/image_for_docs.png)](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/material/golden/macos/search_bar/image_for_docs.png)

Basic search bar

Inherits: `[LayoutControl](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/layoutcontrol/#flet.LayoutControl) `

Properties

*   **`[autofocus](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#flet.SearchBar.autofocus) `**(`[bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool) `) –
    
    Whether the text field should focus itself if nothing else is already focused.
    
*   **`[bar_bgcolor](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#flet.SearchBar.bar_bgcolor) `**(`[ControlStateValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlStateValue) [[ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue) ] | None`) –
    
    Defines the background color of the search bar in all or specific [`ControlState`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/controlstate/#flet.ControlState)
     states.
    
*   **`[bar_border_side](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#flet.SearchBar.bar_border_side) `**(`[ControlStateValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlStateValue) [[BorderSide](https://docs.flet.dev/types/borderside/#flet.BorderSide) ] | None`) –
    
    The color and weight of the search bar's outline.
    
*   **`[bar_elevation](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#flet.SearchBar.bar_elevation) `**(`[ControlStateValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlStateValue) [[Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None] | None`) –
    
    The elevation of the search bar.
    
*   **`[bar_hint_text](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#flet.SearchBar.bar_hint_text) `**(`[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)  | None`) –
    
    Defines the text to be shown in the search bar when it is empty and the search view is close.
    
*   **`[bar_hint_text_style](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#flet.SearchBar.bar_hint_text_style) `**(`[ControlStateValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlStateValue) [[TextStyle](https://docs.flet.dev/types/textstyle/#flet.TextStyle) ] | None`) –
    
    The style to use for the [`SearchBar.bar_hint_text`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#flet.SearchBar.bar_hint_text)
    .
    
*   **`[bar_leading](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#flet.SearchBar.bar_leading) `**(`[Control](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control)  | None`) –
    
    A control to display before the text input field when the search view is close.
    
*   **`[bar_overlay_color](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#flet.SearchBar.bar_overlay_color) `**(`[ControlStateValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlStateValue) [[ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue) ] | None`) –
    
    Defines the highlight color that's typically used to indicate that the search bar is in [`FOCUSED`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/controlstate/#flet.ControlState.FOCUSED)
    , [`HOVERED`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/controlstate/#flet.ControlState.HOVERED)
    , or [`PRESSED`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/controlstate/#flet.ControlState.PRESSED)
     states.
    
*   **`[bar_padding](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#flet.SearchBar.bar_padding) `**(`[ControlStateValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlStateValue) [[PaddingValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.PaddingValue) ] | None`) –
    
    The padding between the search bar's boundary and its contents.
    
*   **`[bar_scroll_padding](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#flet.SearchBar.bar_scroll_padding) `**(`[PaddingValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.PaddingValue) `) –
    
    Configures the padding around a Scrollable when the text field scrolls into view.
    
*   **`[bar_shadow_color](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#flet.SearchBar.bar_shadow_color) `**(`[ControlStateValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlStateValue) [[ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue) ] | None`) –
    
    The shadow color of the search bar.
    
*   **`[bar_shape](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#flet.SearchBar.bar_shape) `**(`[ControlStateValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlStateValue) [[OutlinedBorder](https://docs.flet.dev/types/outlinedborder/#flet.OutlinedBorder) ] | None`) –
    
    The shape of the search bar.
    
*   **`[bar_size_constraints](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#flet.SearchBar.bar_size_constraints) `**(`[BoxConstraints](https://docs.flet.dev/types/boxconstraints/#flet.BoxConstraints)  | None`) –
    
    Optional size constraints for the search bar.
    
*   **`[bar_text_style](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#flet.SearchBar.bar_text_style) `**(`[ControlStateValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlStateValue) [[TextStyle](https://docs.flet.dev/types/textstyle/#flet.TextStyle) ] | None`) –
    
    The style to use for the text being edited.
    
*   **`[bar_trailing](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#flet.SearchBar.bar_trailing) `**(`[list](https://docs.python.org/3/glossary.html#term-list) [[Control](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control) ] | None`) –
    
    A list of controls to display after the text input field when the search view is close.
    
*   **`[capitalization](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#flet.SearchBar.capitalization) `**(`[TextCapitalization](https://docs.flet.dev/types/textcapitalization/#flet.TextCapitalization)  | None`) –
    
    Enables automatic on-the-fly capitalization of entered text.
    
*   **`[controls](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#flet.SearchBar.controls) `**(`[list](https://docs.python.org/3/glossary.html#term-list) [[Control](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control) ]`) –
    
    The list of controls to be displayed below the search bar when in search view.
    
*   **`[divider_color](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#flet.SearchBar.divider_color) `**(`[ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None`) –
    
    The color of the divider when in search view.
    
*   **`[full_screen](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#flet.SearchBar.full_screen) `**(`[bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool) `) –
    
    Defines whether the search view grows to fill the entire screen when the search bar is tapped.
    
*   **`[keyboard_type](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#flet.SearchBar.keyboard_type) `**(`[KeyboardType](https://docs.flet.dev/types/keyboardtype/#flet.KeyboardType) `) –
    
    The type of action button to use for the keyboard.
    
*   **`[shrink_wrap](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#flet.SearchBar.shrink_wrap) `**(`[bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)  | None`) –
    
    Whether the search view should shrink-wrap its contents.
    
*   **`[value](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#flet.SearchBar.value) `**(`[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str) `) –
    
    The text in the search bar.
    
*   **`[view_bar_padding](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#flet.SearchBar.view_bar_padding) `**(`[PaddingValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.PaddingValue)  | None`) –
    
    The padding to use for the search view's search bar.
    
*   **`[view_bgcolor](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#flet.SearchBar.view_bgcolor) `**(`[ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None`) –
    
    Defines the background color of the search view.
    
*   **`[view_elevation](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#flet.SearchBar.view_elevation) `**(`[Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None`) –
    
    Defines the elevation of the search view.
    
*   **`[view_header_height](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#flet.SearchBar.view_header_height) `**(`[Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None`) –
    
    The height of the search field on the search view.
    
*   **`[view_header_text_style](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#flet.SearchBar.view_header_text_style) `**(`[TextStyle](https://docs.flet.dev/types/textstyle/#flet.TextStyle)  | None`) –
    
    Defines the text style of the text being edited on the search view.
    
*   **`[view_hint_text](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#flet.SearchBar.view_hint_text) `**(`[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)  | None`) –
    
    Defines the text to be displayed when the search bar's input field is empty.
    
*   **`[view_hint_text_style](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#flet.SearchBar.view_hint_text_style) `**(`[TextStyle](https://docs.flet.dev/types/textstyle/#flet.TextStyle)  | None`) –
    
    Defines the text style of [`view_hint_text`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#flet.SearchBar.view_hint_text)
    .
    
*   **`[view_leading](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#flet.SearchBar.view_leading) `**(`[Control](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control)  | None`) –
    
    A `Control` to display before the text input field when the search view is open.
    
*   **`[view_padding](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#flet.SearchBar.view_padding) `**(`[PaddingValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.PaddingValue)  | None`) –
    
    The padding to use for the search view.
    
*   **`[view_shape](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#flet.SearchBar.view_shape) `**(`[OutlinedBorder](https://docs.flet.dev/types/outlinedborder/#flet.OutlinedBorder)  | None`) –
    
    Defines the shape of the search view.
    
*   **`[view_side](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#flet.SearchBar.view_side) `**(`[BorderSide](https://docs.flet.dev/types/borderside/#flet.BorderSide)  | None`) –
    
    Defines the color and weight of the search view's outline.
    
*   **`[view_size_constraints](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#flet.SearchBar.view_size_constraints) `**(`[BoxConstraints](https://docs.flet.dev/types/boxconstraints/#flet.BoxConstraints)  | None`) –
    
    Optional size constraints for the search view.
    
*   **`[view_trailing](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#flet.SearchBar.view_trailing) `**(`[list](https://docs.python.org/3/glossary.html#term-list) [[Control](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control) ] | None`) –
    
    A list of `Control`s to display after the text input field when the search view is open.
    

Events

*   **`[on_blur](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#flet.SearchBar.on_blur) `**(`[ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[SearchBar](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#flet.SearchBar) ] | None`) –
    
    Fired when the search bar loses focus.
    
*   **`[on_change](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#flet.SearchBar.on_change) `**(`[ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[SearchBar](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#flet.SearchBar) ] | None`) –
    
    Called when the typed input in the search bar has changed.
    
*   **`[on_focus](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#flet.SearchBar.on_focus) `**(`[ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[SearchBar](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#flet.SearchBar) ] | None`) –
    
    Fired when the search bar gains focus.
    
*   **`[on_submit](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#flet.SearchBar.on_submit) `**(`[ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[SearchBar](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#flet.SearchBar) ] | None`) –
    
    Called when user presses ENTER while focus is on SearchBar.
    
*   **`[on_tap](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#flet.SearchBar.on_tap) `**(`[ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[SearchBar](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#flet.SearchBar) ] | None`) –
    
    Called when the search bar is tapped.
    
*   **`[on_tap_outside_bar](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#flet.SearchBar.on_tap_outside_bar) `**(`[ControlEventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlEventHandler) [[SearchBar](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#flet.SearchBar) ] | None`) –
    
    Fired when the user taps outside the search bar while the search view is open.
    

Methods

*   **`[close_view](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#flet.SearchBar.close_view) `** –
    
    Closes an opened search view.
    
*   **`[focus](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#flet.SearchBar.focus) `** –
    
    Requests focus for this control.
    
*   **`[open_view](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#flet.SearchBar.open_view) `** –
    
    Opens the search view.
    

Examples[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#examples "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[Live example](https://flet-controls-gallery.fly.dev/input/searchbar)

### Basic Example[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#basic-example "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#__codelineno-0-1) import flet as ft [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#__codelineno-0-2)[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#__codelineno-0-3) colors = [     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#__codelineno-0-4)     "Amber",    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#__codelineno-0-5)     "Blue Grey",    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#__codelineno-0-6)     "Brown",    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#__codelineno-0-7)     "Deep Orange",    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#__codelineno-0-8)     "Green",    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#__codelineno-0-9)     "Light Blue",    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#__codelineno-0-10)     "Orange",    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#__codelineno-0-11)     "Red", [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#__codelineno-0-12) ] [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#__codelineno-0-13) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#__codelineno-0-14)[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#__codelineno-0-15) def main(page: ft.Page):     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#__codelineno-0-16)     page.horizontal_alignment = ft.CrossAxisAlignment.CENTER [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#__codelineno-0-17)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#__codelineno-0-18)     def build_tiles(items: list[str]) -> list[ft.Control]:        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#__codelineno-0-19)         return [            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#__codelineno-0-20)             ft.ListTile(                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#__codelineno-0-21)                 title=ft.Text(item),                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#__codelineno-0-22)                 data=item,                [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#__codelineno-0-23)                 on_click=handle_tile_click,            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#__codelineno-0-24)             )            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#__codelineno-0-25)             for item in items        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#__codelineno-0-26)         ] [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#__codelineno-0-27)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#__codelineno-0-28)     async def handle_tile_click(e: ft.Event[ft.ListTile]):        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#__codelineno-0-29)         await anchor.close_view() [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#__codelineno-0-30)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#__codelineno-0-31)     async def handle_change(e: ft.Event[ft.SearchBar]):        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#__codelineno-0-32)         query = e.control.value.strip().lower()        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#__codelineno-0-33)         matching = (            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#__codelineno-0-34)             [color for color in colors if query in color.lower()] if query else colors        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#__codelineno-0-35)         )        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#__codelineno-0-36)         anchor.controls = build_tiles(matching) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#__codelineno-0-37)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#__codelineno-0-38)     def handle_submit(e: ft.Event[ft.SearchBar]):        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#__codelineno-0-39)         print(f"Submit: {e.data}") [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#__codelineno-0-40)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#__codelineno-0-41)     async def handle_tap(e: ft.Event[ft.SearchBar]):        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#__codelineno-0-42)         await anchor.open_view() [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#__codelineno-0-43)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#__codelineno-0-44)     page.add(        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#__codelineno-0-45)         anchor := ft.SearchBar(            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#__codelineno-0-46)             view_elevation=4,            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#__codelineno-0-47)             divider_color=ft.Colors.AMBER,            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#__codelineno-0-48)             bar_hint_text="Search colors...",            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#__codelineno-0-49)             view_hint_text="Choose a color from the suggestions...",            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#__codelineno-0-50)             on_change=handle_change,            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#__codelineno-0-51)             on_submit=handle_submit,            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#__codelineno-0-52)             on_tap=handle_tap,            [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#__codelineno-0-53)             controls=build_tiles(colors),        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#__codelineno-0-54)         ),    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#__codelineno-0-55)     ) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#__codelineno-0-56) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#__codelineno-0-57)[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#__codelineno-0-58) if __name__ == "__main__":     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#__codelineno-0-59)     ft.run(main)`

[![basic](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/material/golden/macos/search_bar/basic.png)](https://docs.flet.dev/test-images/examples/material/golden/macos/search_bar/basic.png)

Properties[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#flet.SearchBar-properties "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### autofocus `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#flet.SearchBar.autofocus "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#__codelineno-0-1) autofocus: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)  = False`

Whether the text field should focus itself if nothing else is already focused.

### bar\_bgcolor `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#flet.SearchBar.bar_bgcolor "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#__codelineno-0-1) bar_bgcolor: [ControlStateValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlStateValue) [[ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue) ] | None = None`

Defines the background color of the search bar in all or specific [`ControlState`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/controlstate/#flet.ControlState)
 states.

### bar\_border\_side `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#flet.SearchBar.bar_border_side "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#__codelineno-0-1) bar_border_side: [ControlStateValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlStateValue) [[BorderSide](https://docs.flet.dev/types/borderside/#flet.BorderSide) ] | None = None`

The color and weight of the search bar's outline.

This value is combined with [`bar_shape`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#flet.SearchBar.bar_shape)
 to create a shape decorated with an outline.

### bar\_elevation `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#flet.SearchBar.bar_elevation "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#__codelineno-0-1) bar_elevation: [ControlStateValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlStateValue) [[Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None] | None = (     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#__codelineno-0-2)     None [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#__codelineno-0-3) )`

The elevation of the search bar.

### bar\_hint\_text `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#flet.SearchBar.bar_hint_text "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#__codelineno-0-1) bar_hint_text: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)  | None = None`

Defines the text to be shown in the search bar when it is empty and the search view is close.

Usually some text that suggests what sort of input the field accepts.

### bar\_hint\_text\_style `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#flet.SearchBar.bar_hint_text_style "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#__codelineno-0-1) bar_hint_text_style: [ControlStateValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlStateValue) [[TextStyle](https://docs.flet.dev/types/textstyle/#flet.TextStyle) ] | None = (     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#__codelineno-0-2)     None [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#__codelineno-0-3) )`

The style to use for the [`SearchBar.bar_hint_text`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#flet.SearchBar.bar_hint_text)
.

### bar\_leading `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#flet.SearchBar.bar_leading "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#__codelineno-0-1) bar_leading: [Control](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control)  | None = None`

A control to display before the text input field when the search view is close.

Typically an [`Icon`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/icon/#flet.Icon)
 or an [`IconButton`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#flet.IconButton)
.

### bar\_overlay\_color `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#flet.SearchBar.bar_overlay_color "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#__codelineno-0-1) bar_overlay_color: [ControlStateValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlStateValue) [[ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue) ] | None = (     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#__codelineno-0-2)     None [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#__codelineno-0-3) )`

Defines the highlight color that's typically used to indicate that the search bar is in [`FOCUSED`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/controlstate/#flet.ControlState.FOCUSED)
, [`HOVERED`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/controlstate/#flet.ControlState.HOVERED)
, or [`PRESSED`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/controlstate/#flet.ControlState.PRESSED)
 states.

### bar\_padding `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#flet.SearchBar.bar_padding "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#__codelineno-0-1) bar_padding: [ControlStateValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlStateValue) [[PaddingValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.PaddingValue) ] | None = None`

The padding between the search bar's boundary and its contents.

### bar\_scroll\_padding `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#flet.SearchBar.bar_scroll_padding "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#__codelineno-0-1) bar_scroll_padding: [PaddingValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.PaddingValue)  = 20`

Configures the padding around a Scrollable when the text field scrolls into view.

If the bar's text field is partially off-screen or covered (e.g., by the keyboard), it scrolls into view, ensuring it is positioned at the specified distance from the Scrollable edges.

### bar\_shadow\_color `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#flet.SearchBar.bar_shadow_color "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#__codelineno-0-1) bar_shadow_color: [ControlStateValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlStateValue) [[ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue) ] | None = (     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#__codelineno-0-2)     None [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#__codelineno-0-3) )`

The shadow color of the search bar.

### bar\_shape `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#flet.SearchBar.bar_shape "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#__codelineno-0-1) bar_shape: [ControlStateValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlStateValue) [[OutlinedBorder](https://docs.flet.dev/types/outlinedborder/#flet.OutlinedBorder) ] | None = None`

The shape of the search bar.

This shape is combined with [`bar_border_side`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#flet.SearchBar.bar_border_side)
 to create a shape decorated with an outline.

### bar\_size\_constraints `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#flet.SearchBar.bar_size_constraints "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#__codelineno-0-1) bar_size_constraints: [BoxConstraints](https://docs.flet.dev/types/boxconstraints/#flet.BoxConstraints)  | None = None`

Optional size constraints for the search bar.

### bar\_text\_style `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#flet.SearchBar.bar_text_style "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#__codelineno-0-1) bar_text_style: [ControlStateValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ControlStateValue) [[TextStyle](https://docs.flet.dev/types/textstyle/#flet.TextStyle) ] | None = None`

The style to use for the text being edited.

### bar\_trailing `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#flet.SearchBar.bar_trailing "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#__codelineno-0-1) bar_trailing: [list](https://docs.python.org/3/glossary.html#term-list) [[Control](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control) ] | None = None`

A list of controls to display after the text input field when the search view is close.

These controls can represent additional modes of searching (e.g voice search), an avatar, or an overflow menu and are usually not more than two.

### capitalization `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#flet.SearchBar.capitalization "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#__codelineno-0-1) capitalization: [TextCapitalization](https://docs.flet.dev/types/textcapitalization/#flet.TextCapitalization)  | None = None`

Enables automatic on-the-fly capitalization of entered text.

### controls `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#flet.SearchBar.controls "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#__codelineno-0-1) controls: [list](https://docs.python.org/3/glossary.html#term-list) [[Control](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control) ] = [field](https://docs.python.org/3/library/dataclasses.html#dataclasses.field "<code>dataclasses.field</code>") (default_factory=[list](https://docs.python.org/3/glossary.html#term-list) )`

The list of controls to be displayed below the search bar when in search view.

Typically [`ListTile`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listtile/#flet.ListTile)
s and will be displayed in a [`ListView`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/listview/#flet.ListView)
.

### divider\_color `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#flet.SearchBar.divider_color "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#__codelineno-0-1) divider_color: [ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None = None`

The color of the divider when in search view.

### full\_screen `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#flet.SearchBar.full_screen "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#__codelineno-0-1) full_screen: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)  = False`

Defines whether the search view grows to fill the entire screen when the search bar is tapped.

### keyboard\_type `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#flet.SearchBar.keyboard_type "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#__codelineno-0-1) keyboard_type: [KeyboardType](https://docs.flet.dev/types/keyboardtype/#flet.KeyboardType)  = [TEXT](https://docs.flet.dev/types/keyboardtype/#flet.KeyboardType.TEXT)`

The type of action button to use for the keyboard.

### shrink\_wrap `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#flet.SearchBar.shrink_wrap "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#__codelineno-0-1) shrink_wrap: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)  | None = None`

Whether the search view should shrink-wrap its contents.

### value `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#flet.SearchBar.value "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#__codelineno-0-1) value: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)  = ''`

The text in the search bar.

### view\_bar\_padding `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#flet.SearchBar.view_bar_padding "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#__codelineno-0-1) view_bar_padding: [PaddingValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.PaddingValue)  | None = None`

The padding to use for the search view's search bar.

If null, then the default value is 8.0 horizontally.

### view\_bgcolor `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#flet.SearchBar.view_bgcolor "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#__codelineno-0-1) view_bgcolor: [ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None = None`

Defines the background color of the search view.

### view\_elevation `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#flet.SearchBar.view_elevation "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#__codelineno-0-1) view_elevation: [Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None = None`

Defines the elevation of the search view.

### view\_header\_height `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#flet.SearchBar.view_header_height "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#__codelineno-0-1) view_header_height: [Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None = None`

The height of the search field on the search view.

### view\_header\_text\_style `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#flet.SearchBar.view_header_text_style "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#__codelineno-0-1) view_header_text_style: [TextStyle](https://docs.flet.dev/types/textstyle/#flet.TextStyle)  | None = None`

Defines the text style of the text being edited on the search view.

### view\_hint\_text `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#flet.SearchBar.view_hint_text "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#__codelineno-0-1) view_hint_text: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)  | None = None`

Defines the text to be displayed when the search bar's input field is empty.

### view\_hint\_text\_style `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#flet.SearchBar.view_hint_text_style "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#__codelineno-0-1) view_hint_text_style: [TextStyle](https://docs.flet.dev/types/textstyle/#flet.TextStyle)  | None = None`

Defines the text style of [`view_hint_text`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#flet.SearchBar.view_hint_text)
.

### view\_leading `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#flet.SearchBar.view_leading "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#__codelineno-0-1) view_leading: [Control](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control)  | None = None`

A `Control` to display before the text input field when the search view is open.

Typically an [`Icon`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/icon/#flet.Icon)
 or an [`IconButton`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/iconbutton/#flet.IconButton)
.

Defaults to a back button which closes/pops the search view.

### view\_padding `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#flet.SearchBar.view_padding "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#__codelineno-0-1) view_padding: [PaddingValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.PaddingValue)  | None = None`

The padding to use for the search view.

Has no effect if the search view is full-screen.

### view\_shape `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#flet.SearchBar.view_shape "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#__codelineno-0-1) view_shape: [OutlinedBorder](https://docs.flet.dev/types/outlinedborder/#flet.OutlinedBorder)  | None = None`

Defines the shape of the search view.

### view\_side `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#flet.SearchBar.view_side "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#__codelineno-0-1) view_side: [BorderSide](https://docs.flet.dev/types/borderside/#flet.BorderSide)  | None = None`

Defines the color and weight of the search view's outline.

### view\_size\_constraints `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#flet.SearchBar.view_size_constraints "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#__codelineno-0-1) view_size_constraints: [BoxConstraints](https://docs.flet.dev/types/boxconstraints/#flet.BoxConstraints)  | None = None`

Optional size constraints for the search view.

By default, the search view has the same width as the search bar and is ⅔ the height of the screen. If the width and height of the view are within the view\_size\_constraints, the view will show its default size. Otherwise, the size of the view will be constrained by this property.

### view\_trailing `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#flet.SearchBar.view_trailing "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#__codelineno-0-1) view_trailing: [list](https://docs.python.org/3/glossary.html#term-list) [[Control](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control) ] | None = None`

A list of `Control`s to display after the text input field when the search view is open.

Defaults to a close button which closes/pops the search view.

Called when user presses ENTER while focus is on SearchBar.

### on\_tap `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#flet.SearchBar.on_tap "Permanent link")

Called when the search bar is tapped.

### on\_tap\_outside\_bar `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#flet.SearchBar.on_tap_outside_bar "Permanent link")

Fired when the user taps outside the search bar while the search view is open.

### close\_view `async` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#flet.SearchBar.close_view "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#__codelineno-0-1) close_view(text: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)  | None = None)`

Closes an opened search view.

Parameters:

*   **`text`** (`[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)  | None`, default: `None` ) –
    
    The text to set in the search bar when closing the view. If not provided, the current [`value`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/searchbar/#flet.SearchBar.value)
     of the search bar will be used.

# BasePage - Flet

 

[Skip to content](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#flet.BasePage)

BasePage
========

Inherits: `[AdaptiveControl](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/adaptivecontrol/#flet.AdaptiveControl) `

A visual container representing a top-level view in a Flet application.

`BasePage` serves as the base class for [Page](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/page/#flet.Page)
 and [`MultiView`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/multiview/#flet.MultiView)
, and provides a unified surface for rendering application content, app bars, navigation elements, dialogs, overlays, and more. It manages one or more [`View`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/view/#flet.View)
 instances and exposes high-level layout, scrolling, and theming properties.

Unlike lower-level layout controls (e.g., [`Column`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/column/#flet.Column)
, [`Container`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/container/#flet.Container)
), [`BasePage`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#flet.BasePage)
 represents an entire logical view or screen of the app. It provides direct access to view-level controls such as [`AppBar`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#flet.AppBar)
, [`NavigationBar`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/navigationbar/#flet.NavigationBar)
, [`FloatingActionButton`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#flet.FloatingActionButton)
, and supports system-level events like window resizing and media changes.

This class is not intended to be used directly in most apps; instead, use [`Page`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/page/#flet.Page)
 or [`MultiView`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/multiview/#flet.MultiView)
, which extend this base functionality.

Properties

*   **`[appbar](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#flet.BasePage.appbar) `**(`[AppBar](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#flet.AppBar)  | [CupertinoAppBar](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinoappbar/#flet.CupertinoAppBar)  | None`) –
    
    Gets or sets the top application bar ([AppBar](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#flet.AppBar)
     or [CupertinoAppBar](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinoappbar/#flet.CupertinoAppBar)
    ) for the view.
    
*   **`[auto_scroll](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#flet.BasePage.auto_scroll) `**(`[bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool) `) –
    
    Whether root view should auto-scroll to the end on content changes.
    
*   **`[bgcolor](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#flet.BasePage.bgcolor) `**(`[ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None`) –
    
    Background color of the root view.
    
*   **`[bottom_appbar](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#flet.BasePage.bottom_appbar) `**(`[BottomAppBar](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/bottomappbar/#flet.BottomAppBar)  | None`) –
    
    Bottom app bar displayed in the root view.
    
*   **`[controls](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#flet.BasePage.controls) `**(`[list](https://docs.python.org/3/glossary.html#term-list) [[BaseControl](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basecontrol/#flet.BaseControl) ]`) –
    
    Root view content controls displayed by this page.
    
*   **`[dark_theme](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#flet.BasePage.dark_theme) `**(`[Theme](https://docs.flet.dev/types/theme/#flet.Theme)  | None`) –
    
    Customizes the theme of the application when in dark theme mode.
    
*   **`[decoration](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#flet.BasePage.decoration) `**(`[BoxDecoration](https://docs.flet.dev/types/boxdecoration/#flet.BoxDecoration)  | None`) –
    
    Background decoration of the root view container.
    
*   **`[drawer](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#flet.BasePage.drawer) `**(`[NavigationDrawer](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/navigationdrawer/#flet.NavigationDrawer)  | None`) –
    
    Navigation drawer opened from the leading edge.
    
*   **`[enable_screenshots](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#flet.BasePage.enable_screenshots) `**(`[bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool) `) –
    
    Enable taking screenshots of the entire page with `take_screenshot` method.
    
*   **`[end_drawer](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#flet.BasePage.end_drawer) `**(`[NavigationDrawer](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/navigationdrawer/#flet.NavigationDrawer)  | None`) –
    
    Navigation drawer opened from the trailing edge.
    
*   **`[floating_action_button](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#flet.BasePage.floating_action_button) `**(`[FloatingActionButton](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#flet.FloatingActionButton)  | None`) –
    
    Floating action button shown for the root view.
    
*   **`[floating_action_button_location](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#flet.BasePage.floating_action_button_location) `**(`[FloatingActionButtonLocation](https://docs.flet.dev/types/floatingactionbuttonlocation/#flet.FloatingActionButtonLocation)  | [OffsetValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.OffsetValue)  | None`) –
    
    Placement of the floating action button in the root view.
    
*   **`[foreground_decoration](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#flet.BasePage.foreground_decoration) `**(`[BoxDecoration](https://docs.flet.dev/types/boxdecoration/#flet.BoxDecoration)  | None`) –
    
    Foreground decoration painted above root view content.
    
*   **`[height](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#flet.BasePage.height) `**(`[Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None`) –
    
    Page height in logical pixels.
    
*   **`[horizontal_alignment](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#flet.BasePage.horizontal_alignment) `**(`[CrossAxisAlignment](https://docs.flet.dev/types/crossaxisalignment/#flet.CrossAxisAlignment) `) –
    
    Horizontal alignment of root view child controls.
    
*   **`[locale_configuration](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#flet.BasePage.locale_configuration) `**(`[LocaleConfiguration](https://docs.flet.dev/types/localeconfiguration/#flet.LocaleConfiguration)  | None`) –
    
    Configures supported locales and the current locale.
    
*   **`[media](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#flet.BasePage.media) `**(`[PageMediaData](https://docs.flet.dev/types/pagemediadata/#flet.PageMediaData) `) –
    
    The current environmental metrics of the page or window.
    
*   **`[navigation_bar](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#flet.BasePage.navigation_bar) `**(`[NavigationBar](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/navigationbar/#flet.NavigationBar)  | [CupertinoNavigationBar](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinonavigationbar/#flet.CupertinoNavigationBar)  | None`) –
    
    Bottom navigation bar for the root view.
    
*   **`[overlay](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#flet.BasePage.overlay) `**(`[list](https://docs.python.org/3/glossary.html#term-list) [[BaseControl](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basecontrol/#flet.BaseControl) ]`) –
    
    The list of overlay controls rendered above page content.
    
*   **`[padding](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#flet.BasePage.padding) `**(`[PaddingValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.PaddingValue)  | None`) –
    
    Inner padding for the root view content.
    
*   **`[scroll](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#flet.BasePage.scroll) `**(`[ScrollMode](https://docs.flet.dev/types/scrollmode/#flet.ScrollMode)  | [Scrollbar](https://docs.flet.dev/types/scrollbar/#flet.Scrollbar)  | None`) –
    
    Scroll behavior mode for root view content.
    
*   **`[services](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#flet.BasePage.services) `**(`[list](https://docs.python.org/3/glossary.html#term-list) [[Service](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/service/#flet.Service) ]`) –
    
    Service instances attached to the root view lifecycle.
    
*   **`[show_semantics_debugger](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#flet.BasePage.show_semantics_debugger) `**(`[bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)  | None`) –
    
    Whether to turn on an overlay that shows the accessibility information reported by the framework.
    
*   **`[spacing](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#flet.BasePage.spacing) `**(`[Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number) `) –
    
    Default spacing between root view child controls.
    
*   **`[theme](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#flet.BasePage.theme) `**(`[Theme](https://docs.flet.dev/types/theme/#flet.Theme)  | None`) –
    
    Customizes the theme of the application when in light theme mode. Currently, a theme can only be automatically generated from a "seed" color. For example, to generate light theme from a green color.
    
*   **`[theme_mode](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#flet.BasePage.theme_mode) `**(`[ThemeMode](https://docs.flet.dev/types/thememode/#flet.ThemeMode)  | None`) –
    
    The page's theme mode.
    
*   **`[title](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#flet.BasePage.title) `**(`[str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)  | None`) –
    
    Page or window title.
    
*   **`[vertical_alignment](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#flet.BasePage.vertical_alignment) `**(`[MainAxisAlignment](https://docs.flet.dev/types/mainaxisalignment/#flet.MainAxisAlignment) `) –
    
    Vertical alignment of root view child controls.
    
*   **`[views](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#flet.BasePage.views) `**(`[list](https://docs.python.org/3/glossary.html#term-list) [[View](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/view/#flet.View) ]`) –
    
    A list of views managed by the page.
    
*   **`[width](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#flet.BasePage.width) `**(`[Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None`) –
    
    Page width in logical pixels.
    

Events

*   **`[on_media_change](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#flet.BasePage.on_media_change) `**(`[EventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.EventHandler) [[PageMediaData](https://docs.flet.dev/types/pagemediadata/#flet.PageMediaData) ] | None`) –
    
    Called when [`media`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#flet.BasePage.media)
     has changed.
    
*   **`[on_resize](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#flet.BasePage.on_resize) `**(`[EventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.EventHandler) [[PageResizeEvent](https://docs.flet.dev/types/pageresizeevent/#flet.PageResizeEvent) ] | None`) –
    
    Called when a user resizes a browser or native OS window containing Flet app
    

Methods

*   **`[add](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#flet.BasePage.add) `** –
    
    Adds controls to the page.
    
*   **`[clean](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#flet.BasePage.clean) `** –
    
    Remove all root view controls and send update to the client.
    
*   **`[close_drawer](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#flet.BasePage.close_drawer) `** –
    
    Close the drawer.
    
*   **`[close_end_drawer](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#flet.BasePage.close_end_drawer) `** –
    
    Close the end drawer.
    
*   **`[insert](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#flet.BasePage.insert) `** –
    
    Inserts controls at specific index of `page.controls` list.
    
*   **`[pop_dialog](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#flet.BasePage.pop_dialog) `** –
    
    Closes the most recently opened dialog.
    
*   **`[remove](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#flet.BasePage.remove) `** –
    
    Removes specific controls from `page.controls` list.
    
*   **`[remove_at](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#flet.BasePage.remove_at) `** –
    
    Remove controls from `page.controls` list at specific index.
    
*   **`[scroll_to](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#flet.BasePage.scroll_to) `** –
    
    Moves scroll position to either absolute `offset`, relative `delta` or jump to the control with specified `scroll_key`.
    
*   **`[show_dialog](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#flet.BasePage.show_dialog) `** –
    
    Displays a dialog and manages its dismissal lifecycle.
    
*   **`[show_drawer](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#flet.BasePage.show_drawer) `** –
    
    Show the drawer.
    
*   **`[show_end_drawer](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#flet.BasePage.show_end_drawer) `** –
    
    Show the end drawer.
    
*   **`[take_screenshot](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#flet.BasePage.take_screenshot) `** –
    
    Captures a screenshot of the entire page with overlays.
    
*   **`[update](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#flet.BasePage.update) `** –
    
    Push pending updates to the connected client.
    

Properties[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#flet.BasePage-properties "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### appbar `property` `writable` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#flet.BasePage.appbar "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#__codelineno-0-1) appbar: [AppBar](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#flet.AppBar)  | [CupertinoAppBar](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinoappbar/#flet.CupertinoAppBar)  | None`

Gets or sets the top application bar ([AppBar](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/appbar/#flet.AppBar)
 or [CupertinoAppBar](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinoappbar/#flet.CupertinoAppBar)
) for the view.

The app bar typically displays the page title and optional actions such as navigation icons, menus, or other interactive elements.

### auto\_scroll `property` `writable` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#flet.BasePage.auto_scroll "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#__codelineno-0-1) auto_scroll: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)`

Whether root view should auto-scroll to the end on content changes.

### bgcolor `property` `writable` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#flet.BasePage.bgcolor "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#__codelineno-0-1) bgcolor: [ColorValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.ColorValue)  | None`

Background color of the root view.

### bottom\_appbar `property` `writable` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#flet.BasePage.bottom_appbar "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#__codelineno-0-1) bottom_appbar: [BottomAppBar](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/bottomappbar/#flet.BottomAppBar)  | None`

Bottom app bar displayed in the root view.

### controls `property` `writable` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#flet.BasePage.controls "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#__codelineno-0-1) controls: [list](https://docs.python.org/3/glossary.html#term-list) [[BaseControl](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basecontrol/#flet.BaseControl) ]`

Root view content controls displayed by this page.

### dark\_theme `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#flet.BasePage.dark_theme "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#__codelineno-0-1) dark_theme: [Theme](https://docs.flet.dev/types/theme/#flet.Theme)  | None = None`

Customizes the theme of the application when in dark theme mode.

### decoration `property` `writable` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#flet.BasePage.decoration "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#__codelineno-0-1) decoration: [BoxDecoration](https://docs.flet.dev/types/boxdecoration/#flet.BoxDecoration)  | None`

Background decoration of the root view container.

### drawer `property` `writable` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#flet.BasePage.drawer "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#__codelineno-0-1) drawer: [NavigationDrawer](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/navigationdrawer/#flet.NavigationDrawer)  | None`

Navigation drawer opened from the leading edge.

### enable\_screenshots `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#flet.BasePage.enable_screenshots "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#__codelineno-0-1) enable_screenshots: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)  = False`

Enable taking screenshots of the entire page with `take_screenshot` method.

### end\_drawer `property` `writable` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#flet.BasePage.end_drawer "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#__codelineno-0-1) end_drawer: [NavigationDrawer](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/navigationdrawer/#flet.NavigationDrawer)  | None`

Navigation drawer opened from the trailing edge.

### floating\_action\_button `property` `writable` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#flet.BasePage.floating_action_button "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#__codelineno-0-1) floating_action_button: [FloatingActionButton](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/floatingactionbutton/#flet.FloatingActionButton)  | None`

Floating action button shown for the root view.

### floating\_action\_button\_location `property` `writable` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#flet.BasePage.floating_action_button_location "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#__codelineno-0-1) floating_action_button_location: (     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#__codelineno-0-2)     [FloatingActionButtonLocation](https://docs.flet.dev/types/floatingactionbuttonlocation/#flet.FloatingActionButtonLocation)  | [OffsetValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.OffsetValue)  | None [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#__codelineno-0-3) )`

Placement of the floating action button in the root view.

### foreground\_decoration `property` `writable` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#flet.BasePage.foreground_decoration "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#__codelineno-0-1) foreground_decoration: [BoxDecoration](https://docs.flet.dev/types/boxdecoration/#flet.BoxDecoration)  | None`

Foreground decoration painted above root view content.

### height `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#flet.BasePage.height "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#__codelineno-0-1) height: [Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None = None`

Page height in logical pixels.

Note

*   This property is read-only.
*   To get or set the full window height including window chrome (e.g., title bar and borders) when running a Flet app on desktop, use the [`height`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/window/#flet.Window.height)
     property of [`Page.window`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/page/#flet.Page.window)
     instead.

### horizontal\_alignment `property` `writable` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#flet.BasePage.horizontal_alignment "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#__codelineno-0-1) horizontal_alignment: [CrossAxisAlignment](https://docs.flet.dev/types/crossaxisalignment/#flet.CrossAxisAlignment)`

Horizontal alignment of root view child controls.

### locale\_configuration `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#flet.BasePage.locale_configuration "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#__codelineno-0-1) locale_configuration: [LocaleConfiguration](https://docs.flet.dev/types/localeconfiguration/#flet.LocaleConfiguration)  | None = None`

Configures supported locales and the current locale.

### media `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#flet.BasePage.media "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#__codelineno-0-1) media: [PageMediaData](https://docs.flet.dev/types/pagemediadata/#flet.PageMediaData)  = [field](https://docs.python.org/3/library/dataclasses.html#dataclasses.field "<code>dataclasses.field</code>") (     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#__codelineno-0-2)     default_factory=lambda: [PageMediaData](https://docs.flet.dev/types/pagemediadata/#flet.PageMediaData) (        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#__codelineno-0-3)         padding=[zero](https://docs.flet.dev/types/padding/#flet.Padding.zero) (),        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#__codelineno-0-4)         view_padding=[zero](https://docs.flet.dev/types/padding/#flet.Padding.zero) (),        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#__codelineno-0-5)         view_insets=[zero](https://docs.flet.dev/types/padding/#flet.Padding.zero) (),        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#__codelineno-0-6)         device_pixel_ratio=0,        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#__codelineno-0-7)         orientation=[PORTRAIT](https://docs.flet.dev/types/orientation/#flet.Orientation.PORTRAIT) ,        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#__codelineno-0-8)         always_use_24_hour_format=False,    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#__codelineno-0-9)     ) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#__codelineno-0-10) )`

The current environmental metrics of the page or window.

This data is updated whenever the platform window or layout changes, such as when rotating a device, resizing a browser window, or adjusting system UI elements like the keyboard or safe areas.

### navigation\_bar `property` `writable` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#flet.BasePage.navigation_bar "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#__codelineno-0-1) navigation_bar: (     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#__codelineno-0-2)     [NavigationBar](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/navigationbar/#flet.NavigationBar)  | [CupertinoNavigationBar](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/cupertinonavigationbar/#flet.CupertinoNavigationBar)  | None [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#__codelineno-0-3) )`

Bottom navigation bar for the root view.

### overlay `property` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#flet.BasePage.overlay "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#__codelineno-0-1) overlay: [list](https://docs.python.org/3/glossary.html#term-list) [[BaseControl](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basecontrol/#flet.BaseControl) ]`

The list of overlay controls rendered above page content.

### padding `property` `writable` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#flet.BasePage.padding "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#__codelineno-0-1) padding: [PaddingValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.PaddingValue)  | None`

Inner padding for the root view content.

### scroll `property` `writable` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#flet.BasePage.scroll "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#__codelineno-0-1) scroll: [ScrollMode](https://docs.flet.dev/types/scrollmode/#flet.ScrollMode)  | [Scrollbar](https://docs.flet.dev/types/scrollbar/#flet.Scrollbar)  | None`

Scroll behavior mode for root view content.

### services `property` `writable` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#flet.BasePage.services "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#__codelineno-0-1) services: [list](https://docs.python.org/3/glossary.html#term-list) [[Service](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/service/#flet.Service) ]`

Service instances attached to the root view lifecycle.

### show\_semantics\_debugger `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#flet.BasePage.show_semantics_debugger "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#__codelineno-0-1) show_semantics_debugger: [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)  | None = None`

Whether to turn on an overlay that shows the accessibility information reported by the framework.

### spacing `property` `writable` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#flet.BasePage.spacing "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#__codelineno-0-1) spacing: [Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)`

Default spacing between root view child controls.

### theme `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#flet.BasePage.theme "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#__codelineno-0-1) theme: [Theme](https://docs.flet.dev/types/theme/#flet.Theme)  | None = None`

Customizes the theme of the application when in light theme mode. Currently, a theme can only be automatically generated from a "seed" color. For example, to generate light theme from a green color.

### theme\_mode `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#flet.BasePage.theme_mode "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#__codelineno-0-1) theme_mode: [ThemeMode](https://docs.flet.dev/types/thememode/#flet.ThemeMode)  | None = [SYSTEM](https://docs.flet.dev/types/thememode/#flet.ThemeMode.SYSTEM)`

The page's theme mode.

### title `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#flet.BasePage.title "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#__codelineno-0-1) title: [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)  | None = None`

Page or window title.

### vertical\_alignment `property` `writable` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#flet.BasePage.vertical_alignment "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#__codelineno-0-1) vertical_alignment: [MainAxisAlignment](https://docs.flet.dev/types/mainaxisalignment/#flet.MainAxisAlignment)`

Vertical alignment of root view child controls.

### views `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#flet.BasePage.views "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#__codelineno-0-1) views: [list](https://docs.python.org/3/glossary.html#term-list) [[View](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/view/#flet.View) ] = [field](https://docs.python.org/3/library/dataclasses.html#dataclasses.field "<code>dataclasses.field</code>") (default_factory=lambda: [[View](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/view/#flet.View) ()])`

A list of views managed by the page.

Each [`View`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/view/#flet.View)
 represents a distinct navigation state or screen in the application.

The first view in the list is considered the active one by default.

### width `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#flet.BasePage.width "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#__codelineno-0-1) width: [Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None = None`

Page width in logical pixels.

Note

*   This property is read-only.
*   To get or set the full window height including window chrome (e.g., title bar and borders) when running a Flet app on desktop, use the [`width`](https://docs.flet.dev/types/window/#flet.Window.width)
     property of [`Page.window`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/page/#flet.Page.window)
     instead.

Events[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#flet.BasePage-events "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### on\_media\_change `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#flet.BasePage.on_media_change "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#__codelineno-0-1) on_media_change: [EventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.EventHandler) [[PageMediaData](https://docs.flet.dev/types/pagemediadata/#flet.PageMediaData) ] | None = None`

Called when [`media`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#flet.BasePage.media)
 has changed.

### on\_resize `class-attribute` `instance-attribute` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#flet.BasePage.on_resize "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#__codelineno-0-1) on_resize: [EventHandler](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.EventHandler) [[PageResizeEvent](https://docs.flet.dev/types/pageresizeevent/#flet.PageResizeEvent) ] | None = None`

Called when a user resizes a browser or native OS window containing Flet app

Example

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#__codelineno-0-1) def main(page: ft.Page):     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#__codelineno-0-2)     def handle_page_size(e):        [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#__codelineno-0-3)         print("New page size:", page.window.width, page.window_height) [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#__codelineno-0-4)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#__codelineno-0-5)     page.on_resize = handle_page_size`

Methods[#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#flet.BasePage-methods "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### add [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#flet.BasePage.add "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#__codelineno-0-1) add(*controls: [Control](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control) ) -> None`

Adds controls to the page.

Example:

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#__codelineno-0-1) page.add(ft.Text("Hello!"), ft.FilledButton("Button"))`

### clean [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#flet.BasePage.clean "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#__codelineno-0-1) clean() -> None`

Remove all root view controls and send update to the client.

### close\_drawer `async` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#flet.BasePage.close_drawer "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#__codelineno-0-1) close_drawer()`

Close the drawer.

### close\_end\_drawer `async` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#flet.BasePage.close_end_drawer "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#__codelineno-0-1) close_end_drawer()`

Close the end drawer.

### insert [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#flet.BasePage.insert "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#__codelineno-0-1) insert(at: [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int) , *controls: [Control](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control) ) -> None`

Inserts controls at specific index of `page.controls` list.

### pop\_dialog [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#flet.BasePage.pop_dialog "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#__codelineno-0-1) pop_dialog() -> [DialogControl](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/dialogcontrol/#flet.DialogControl)  | None`

Closes the most recently opened dialog.

This method searches the active dialog stack for the topmost dialog that is currently open, marks it as closed, updates its state, and returns the closed dialog.

Returns:

*   `[DialogControl](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/dialogcontrol/#flet.DialogControl)  | None` –
    
    The closed dialog instance if one was found, otherwise `None`.
    

### remove [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#flet.BasePage.remove "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#__codelineno-0-1) remove(*controls: [Control](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control) ) -> None`

Removes specific controls from `page.controls` list.

### remove\_at [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#flet.BasePage.remove_at "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#__codelineno-0-1) remove_at(index: [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int) ) -> None`

Remove controls from `page.controls` list at specific index.

### scroll\_to `async` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#flet.BasePage.scroll_to "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#__codelineno-0-1) scroll_to(     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#__codelineno-0-2)     offset: [Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None = None,    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#__codelineno-0-3)     delta: [Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None = None,    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#__codelineno-0-4)     scroll_key: [ScrollKey](https://docs.flet.dev/types/scrollkey/#flet.ScrollKey)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#__codelineno-0-5)     | [str](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#str)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#__codelineno-0-6)     | [int](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#int)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#__codelineno-0-7)     | [float](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#float)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#__codelineno-0-8)     | [bool](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#bool)    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#__codelineno-0-9)     | None = None,    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#__codelineno-0-10)     duration: [DurationValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.DurationValue)  | None = None,    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#__codelineno-0-11)     curve: [AnimationCurve](https://docs.flet.dev/types/animationcurve/#flet.AnimationCurve)  | None = None, [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#__codelineno-0-12) ) -> None`

Moves scroll position to either absolute `offset`, relative `delta` or jump to the control with specified `scroll_key`.

See [`Column.scroll_to()`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/scrollablecontrol/#flet.ScrollableControl.scroll_to)
 for method details and examples.

### show\_dialog [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#flet.BasePage.show_dialog "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#__codelineno-0-1) show_dialog(dialog: [DialogControl](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/dialogcontrol/#flet.DialogControl) ) -> None`

Displays a dialog and manages its dismissal lifecycle.

This method adds the specified `dialog` to the active dialog stack and renders it on the page. The [`on_dismiss`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/dialogcontrol/#flet.DialogControl.on_dismiss)
 handler of the dialog is temporarily wrapped to ensure the dialog is removed from the stack and its dismissal event is triggered appropriately.

Parameters:

*   **`dialog`** (`[DialogControl](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/dialogcontrol/#flet.DialogControl) `) –
    
    The dialog instance to display. Must not yet be open.
    

Raises:

*   `[RuntimeError](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#RuntimeError) ` –
    
    If the specified dialog is already open.
    

### show\_drawer `async` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#flet.BasePage.show_drawer "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#__codelineno-0-1) show_drawer()`

Show the drawer.

Raises:

*   `[ValueError](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ValueError) ` –
    
    If no [`drawer`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#flet.BasePage.drawer)
     is defined.
    

### show\_end\_drawer `async` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#flet.BasePage.show_end_drawer "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#__codelineno-0-1) show_end_drawer()`

Show the end drawer.

Raises:

*   `[ValueError](https://docs.python.org/3/library/exceptions.html#ValueError) ` –
    
    If no [`end_drawer`](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#flet.BasePage.end_drawer)
     is defined.
    

### take\_screenshot `async` [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#flet.BasePage.take_screenshot "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#__codelineno-0-1) take_screenshot(     [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#__codelineno-0-2)     pixel_ratio: [Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None = None,    [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#__codelineno-0-3)     delay: [DurationValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.DurationValue)  | None = None, [](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#__codelineno-0-4) ) -> [bytes](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#bytes)`

Captures a screenshot of the entire page with overlays.

Parameters:

*   **`pixel_ratio`** (`[Number](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.Number)  | None`, default: `None` ) –
    
    A pixel ratio of the captured screenshot. If `None`, device-specific pixel ratio will be used.
    
*   **`delay`** (`[DurationValue](https://docs.flet.dev/types/aliases/#flet.DurationValue)  | None`, default: `None` ) –
    
    A delay before taking a screenshot. The delay will be 20 milliseconds if not specified.
    

Returns:

*   `[bytes](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#bytes) ` –
    
    Screenshot in PNG format.
    

### update [#](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#flet.BasePage.update "Permanent link")

`[](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/basepage/#__codelineno-0-1) update(*controls: [Control](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control) ) -> None`

Push pending updates to the connected client.

Parameters:

*   **`*controls`** (`[Control](https://docs.flet.dev/controls/control/#flet.Control) `, default: `()` ) –
    
    Specific controls to update. When omitted, updates this page object.
